title: Dynamic Simulation and Performance Evaluation of an Electric Two-Wheeler Powertrain
authors: Kumar Amartya Dev, Aldrin Claytus Vaz, Vinaya Basavaraaj Koradoor, Navya Thirumaleshwar Hegde
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: India, as one of the largest consumers in the two-wheeler market, heavily depends on motorcycles for daily transportation. While conventional petrol-powered bikes offer extended range and efficient fuel use, they are associated with high fuel costs, environmental pollution, and frequent maintenance. In response to these challenges, the electric two-wheeler segment has gained substantial traction over the past decade. This paper presents a comprehensive design and simulation of electric two-wheelers aimed at improving efficiency, enhancing performance, and reducing maintenance demands. Multiple configurations were evaluated to identify the most effective setup, using MATLAB's Simscape and Powertrain Design Blockset for modeling. The powertrain simulation incorporates data-driven modeling of key components, including a mapped BLDC motor, battery specifications, wheel and brake dynamics, and vehicle body parameters. Regenerative braking enables energy recovery in generator mode, enhancing overall efficiency. Performance metrics such as range, speed, battery state of charge (SOC), and powertrain efficiency are analyzed under standard drive cycles. The entire study is executed within the MATLAB environment using SIMULINK and Simscape tools.

title: Shunt Active Power Filter Based on DETOGI for Self-Excited Induction Generator Under Unbalanced Loaded Condition
authors: Jotirmoy Samanta, Ralli Sangno, Rajen Pudur
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Power quality issues (PQIs) pose a critical challenge in edge-of-grid renewable systems, especially under dynamic and unbalanced loading conditions. These issues are primarily characterized by harmonic distortion arising from nonlinear loads, poor power factor (PF), and voltage degradation at the terminals of self-excited induction generators (SEIGs). This paper proposes a shunt active power filter (SAPF)-based SEIG system to mitigate PQIs in edge-of-grid applications effectively. A Double-Enhanced Third-Order Generalized Integrator (DETOGI) is employed within a Dual Fundamental Component Extraction (DFCE) control framework to precisely extract the fundamental components necessary for generating the reference compensation current in the PWM control of the SAPF. Additionally, a nonlinear PID (NPID) controller is designed to regulate the DC-link voltage of the SAPF, ensuring stable operation under varying load conditions. The proposed control strategy not only stabilizes the SEIG terminal voltage but also significantly reduces harmonic distortion and improves the overall power factor of the system. The performance of the proposed SEIG-SAPF configuration is validated through detailed simulations conducted in MATLAB/Simulink, demonstrating its effectiveness in enhancing power quality at the grid edge.

title: Urban Traffic Intelligence: Merging Computer Vision and Reinforcement Learning for Proactive Congestion Control
authors: Aadithyaa Eeswaran, Sravya Duvvuri, Bhavana Naidu, N Lalithamani N
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion significantly impacts travel time, fuel use, and air quality in rapidly growing cities. This paper proposes an integrated framework combining computer vision and reinforcement learning for proactive traffic-signal control and emergency-vehicle prioritization. The system uses YOLOv8-based image analysis and Mel-spectrogram audio classification to detect and classify vehicles in real time, while a Proximal-Policy-Optimization (PPO) controller dynamically optimizes signal phases across multiple intersections. SUMO- based experiments show a 94 % cut in ambulance waiting time, 96 % in overall vehicle waiting, and a 41 % drop in emissions versus timer-based control. These results highlight the approach's potential to improve urban mobility, lower environmental impact, and speed emergency response.

title: Bandgap Prediction in Mixed $\text{Sn}-\text{Pb}$ Perovskites via Regression Models
authors: Shikha Marwaha, Debajyoti Biswas
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this work, we have predicted the bandgap of mixed tin-lead <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{Sn}-\text{Pb})$</tex> perovskites using machine learning techniques. Here, we apply machine learning techniques including NuSVR (with RBF kernel), Random Forest Regressor, and Linear Regression to predict the bandgap of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Cs}_{\mathrm{a}} \text{FA}_{\mathrm{b}} \text{MA}_{(1-\mathrm{a}-}$</tex> b) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Sn}_{\mathrm{m}} \text{Pb}_{(1-\mathrm{m})}(\text{Cl}_{\mathrm{x}} \text{Br}_{\mathrm{y}} \mathrm{I}_{(1-\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y})})_{3}$</tex> perovskites. The models are evaluated via <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> metrics, while compositional impacts on bandgap tuning are examined. Our results align well with experimental data, demonstrating machine learning's efficacy in guiding material optimization for scalable PSC development.

title: Design and Control of High Power Density Power Electronics for Fuel Cell-Based Energy Storage Systems
authors: Rajesh Kumar Mahto, Aditya Kant, Pallavi Bharadwaj
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This work presents the design and control strategy for the development of a 5 kW high power-density DC/DC converter used to power loads from fuel cell energy storage systems. The proposed converter can maintain a fixed voltage at the DC bus despite of wide variation in input voltage produced by fuel cells. This is ensured under varying load demand. The fuel cell DC/DC converter (FDC) consists of two H-bridges with a common high-frequency AC link. The primary H-bridge has four active semiconductor switches controlled through a single microcontroller. The FDC is operated in the boost mode at a switching frequency of 25 kHz. The validity of the converter control and performance is confirmed through simulation as well as experimental verification on developed hardware prototypes. The efficiency of the proposed converter varies between 88 and 98% as per operating conditions.

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Efficiency-Based Fault Detection in Ev Batteries
authors: Bhagya Satya Sri Vutukuri, Shanmukha Krishna Chaitanya M, Gowreesh Raja Dandamudi, D S R S L Avanthika, Ramanjaneya Reddy Udumula
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The widespread adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) has significantly increased the demand for reliable and efficient Battery Management Systems (BMS), which are essential for ensuring safety, high performance, and extended battery life. BMS rely on parameters such as voltage, current, temperature, and charge-discharge cycles to estimate the battery's State of Charge (SOC) and State of Health (SOH). However, efficiency degradation and fault conditions remain key challenges. This study presents a machine learning (ML)-based framework aimed at detecting efficiency-related anomalies in BMS data. The proposed approach emphasizes a structured preprocessing pipeline, incorporating missing value imputation, outlier correction, skewness handling, and feature scaling to enhance data quality. Unsupervised anomaly detection using Isolation Forest is employed to identify deviations from normal behavior, followed by classification of these anomalies using supervised ML algorithms: Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, and Random Forest. Model performance is rigorously evaluated using Accuracy, F1-Score, and AUC-ROC metrics to benchmark effectiveness. This work contributes a scalable and data-driven diagnostic methodology for intelligent battery systems, addressing real-time fault detection in evolving EV ecosystems.

title: Modern Hybrid Cross-Tied BIPV Interconnections for Smart Infrastructures With Improved Global MPP Under Extreme Non-Uniform Shading
authors: Nikhil Arunrao Bodkhe, Debayan Sarkar, Risha Mal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Modern hybrid cross-tied interconnections offer promising solutions to the persistent challenge of Global Maximum Power Point (GMPP) enhancement in BuildingIntegrated Photovoltaic (BIPV) systems under extreme NonUniform Shading Patterns (NU-SPs). This study evaluates three existing configurations-Alternate Bridge Linked-Honey Comb (A-BL-HC), Series Parallel-Triple Tied Cross Linked (SP-TTCL), and Total Cross Tied-Bridge Link-Total Cross Tied (TCT-BL-TCT-alongside two new interconnections: Proposed Triple Tied-Total Cross Tied (Prop. TT-TCT) and Proposed Series Parallel-Total Cross Tied-Series Parallel (Prop. SP-TCT-SP). Simulations performed in MATLAB R2023b examine key performance metrics including GMPP, Mismatch Loss (ML), Cross Ties, Open-Circuit Voltage (OCV), Short-Circuit Current (SCC), and Local Maximum Power Points (LMPP1 & LMPP2) under high, low, and very low NU-SP scenarios. Results indicate that the Prop. SP-TCT-SP topology delivers the highest GMPP and most effective shade dispersion, while A-BL-HC suffers from the lowest power output and highest shading sensitivity. The emergence of multiple LMPPs at varying irradiance levels further emphasizes the role of modern hybrid topologies in maximizing energy yield. This study underscores the value of strategically engineered cross-tied hybrid BIPV interconnections for enhancing reliability and performance in smart building infrastructures under real-world shading conditions.

title: Enhanced Ultra Gain Cubic DC-DC Converter for EV and Grid Applications Using Dual Switch Operation
authors: Arun Polasa, Roshini Gaini, Pavani Guvvala, Phanindra Kumar Samudrala, Manikanta Kuraganti, Ramulu Chinthamalla
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In order to integrate photovoltaic (PV) and fuel cell sources into the grid with a substantially higher voltage gain, this paper suggests a novel Ultra Gain Cubic (UGC) DC-DC converter. Even at a low duty cycle, the suggested converter achieves ultra high gain, making it extremely effective for applications involving renewable energy. With a focus on its gain performance at lower duty cycles, the converter's operation is examined in Continuous Conduction Mode (CCM). Using the PSIM simulation platform, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \text{KW}, 800 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> converter model is developed and tested, demonstrating its reliability and suitability for high voltage, high efficiency power conversion.

title: Life Cycle Assessment of Prismatic Lithium Iron Phosphate Batteries for Autonomous Vehicles in India: Tool Benchmarking and Environmental Impact Mitigation Strategies
authors: Aarushi Chatterjee, Souvik Ganguli
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles (AVs) in India use a lot of Prismatic Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP) batteries because they are more stable, last longer, and are safer. But most of the environmental studies on these batteries use international datasets that don't show how India's electricity grid and manufacturing ecosystem are changing and how much carbon they use. This work contributes to the development of an India-specific Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) model for prismatic LFP cells, incorporating localized electricity emission factors, material supply chains, and process-specific parameters. Using the Central Electricity Authority (CEA) emission factor of 0.716-0.757 kg <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} / \text{kWh}$</tex>, the cradle-to-gate Global Warming Potential (GWP) was found to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$67.1 ~\text{kg} \text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e} / \text{kWh}$</tex>. The battery model was implemented using TCS Product Life Cycle Profiler (PLCP) and compared with openLCA, SimaPro, and GaBi. The results were within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 6 \%$</tex> of each other based on the same assumptions. The case study showed that using an aqueous binder, adding renewable energy, and using hydrometallurgical recycling more effectively could all lower GWP by 15-20%. These studies show that India needs LCA frameworks, new ways of doing things, and clear policy measures to really reduce the environmental impact of different battery models throughout their entire life cycle.

title: Optimal Placement of Electric Vehicle Rapid Charging Stations with V2G strategies
authors: Sandip S. Yeole, Prakash G. Burade, Fareed Ahmad
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are gaining substantial consideration from both government and the automotive industry due to their potential for reducing carbon emissions, lower maintenance requirements. With the growing adoption of EVs as a sustainable alternative to fossil fuel-based vehicles, the growth of sustainable charging infrastructure has become a global significance. One of the foremost concerns in this area is determining the optimal number of charging piles and the locations of EV rapid charging stations (RCSs), which is necessary for effective policy and infrastructure planning. This study presents a mathematical model designed to forecast the required number of RCSs in the proposed area. Additionally, an optimisation framework is established to identify the optimal locations for proposed RCSs within the power distribution network, incorporating vehicle-to-grid (V2G). The problem formulation considers two main objective functions: minimizing the total power loss and reducing the transportation cost incurred by EVs traveling from demand points to nearest RCS. To evaluate the efficiency of the proposed framework, simulations are conducted on the IEEE 34-buses test system using the backward/forward sweep load flow technique. Performance is analyzed under three scenarios: the base case, optimal placement of RCSs, and optimal placement with integrated V2G strategy.

title: Reduction of Harmonic Distortion in Five-Phase VSI Using Modified Pentagonal Space Vector PWM
authors: Harsh Ramesh Moon, Ramsha Karampuri
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper discusses an improved pentagonal space vector pulse width modulation (SVPWM) method for a five-phase voltage source inverter (VSI). Conventional pentagonal SVPWM techniques often face limitations in achieving good harmonic performance while keeping the output voltage quality high. In this work, the conventional switching sequences have been modified to create a new pattern that makes better use of the available dc bus voltage. The proposed method provides a stronger fundamental component in the output voltage and smoother current waveforms. Simulation results show that the load current total harmonic distortion (THD) is reduced compared to the standard pentagonal approach. These improvements highlight the effectiveness of the proposed scheme, making it a promising option for high-performance motor drives and multiphase energy conversion systems.

title: Frequency Control of Multi-Area Power Systems Using a Novel Hippopotamus Optimization-Based Cascaded Controller Incorporating Renewable Energy Sources and Electric Vehicles
authors: Hiramani Shukla, Shailendra Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Ensuring constant system frequency is essential for the reliable functioning of contemporary power systems, particularly with the increasing integration of renewable energy sources. This research examines load frequency management in a multi-area, multi-source power system using a cascaded proportional-integral and proportional-integral-derivative with filter (C-PI-PIDN) controller, optimized by the Hippopotamus Optimization Algorithm (HOA). The suggested HOA-based method is contrasted with several known optimization strategies to assess its efficacy. Simulation findings reveal that the C-PI-PIDN controller exhibits superior performance compared to the traditional PIDN controller in alleviating frequency deviations and tie-line power variations, attaining diminished overshoot, minimized undershoot, and expedited settling time. The system model includes traditional thermal generating units with renewable energy sources, including a solar thermal power plant, wind energy conversion systems, and solar photovoltaic production. The involvement of electric cars functioning in vehicle-to-grid (V2G) mode is seen as beneficial for grid stability and for mitigating carbon emissions. Moreover, energy storage technologies, such as a redox flow battery and an ultracapacitor, are included to improve system dynamics and mitigate oscillatory behavior. The efficacy of the suggested control technique is substantiated by simulation experiments conducted in MATLAB/Simulink, with the findings further corroborated using the OPAL-RT (OP4510) real-time simulation platform.

title: Comparative Analysis of Different Rotor-Shaped IPMSM for Two-Wheeler Electric Vehicles
authors: Lourembam Ranjita Devi, Sreenu Sreekumar, Anoubam Delin Sharma, Stalin Meitei Athokpam, Kilangleima Chingsubam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The rapid rise in vehicle numbers has intensified concerns over air pollution and global warming. Electric vehicles (EVs) present a sustainable solution, with electric motors playing a critical role in their performance. Among various motor types, Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (IPMSMs) are widely adopted in electric two-wheelers due to their high torque output and high efficiency. This study investigates the impact of rotor geometry on the performance of IPMSMs, focusing on enhancing output power, power factor, torque, and efficiency characteristics through rotor shape optimization. Particular attention is given to minimize undesirable cogging torque, which can arise from poor rotor design. Comparative analysis shows that a delta-shaped rotor offers improved performance, with the lowest cogging torque (4.4329 Nm), highest average torque (25.47 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N m}$</tex>), and minimal torque ripple (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 2 8 5 \%}$</tex>), outperforming the V-shaped rotor by 4.3 % in torque and reducing torque ripple by 60 %. Furthermore, the application of rotor skewing in the V-shaped design significantly reduces cogging torque from 5.2388 Nm to 0.0248 Nm and torque ripple from 1.29 % to 0.53 %, achieving a 58.9 % improvement. These findings demonstrate that rotor shape optimization, particularly skewing and delta configurations, enhances overall motor performance, leading to smoother and more efficient EV operation.

title: Optimized Bidirectional Dual-Input Single-Output DC-DC Converter for Enhanced Electric Vehicle Integration
authors: V. Suryanarayanareddy, Arun Kumar Thulasi, P. Dhanaselvi, Busireddy Hemanth Kumar, P. Vishnuvardhan, E. Parimalasindar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study helps to completely examine an optimized Bidirectional Dual-Input Single-Output (BDISO) DC-DC converter tailored for Electric Vehicle (EV) applications. Incorporating Photovoltaic (PV) technology with advanced Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) functionality. The converter's dual functionality proves instrumental in managing power flow between various sources, optimising energy efficiency in EVs. The presentation covers the converter's operational principles, application in EVs, advantages, and analysis methods. Real-world case studies highlight successful implementations, while discussions on challenges and innovative solutions provide a comprehensive overview. This research not only contributes to the efficiency of EVs but also underscores the pivotal role of bidirectional converters in shaping the future of electric transportation. Utilising MATLAB software, comprehensive simulations were conducted to investigate the behaviour of the converters under different loading conditions, including various load scenarios and different input voltage scenarios.

title: Efficient Architecture for Cubesat Electric Power System
authors: Anil Jakhar, Pravin Kumar, Amarendra Edpuganti
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a new architecture for CubeSat Electric power system (EPS) to achieve higher efficiency compared to the state-of-the-art architectures. In the literature, several architectures are presented for the CubeSat EPS, and among them, two architectures are utilized in commercial products. The efficiency of the architectures that depend on the number of power converters between the source and load is critical, as it affects the cycle life of the battery. The proposed architecture achieves only one power conversion stage between source and load in all modes of operation and thus, it achieves higher efficiency compared to commercial EPS architectures. Further, the simulation tests are performed for the proposed EPS architecture based on the 1 U CubeSat specifications, and results are presented to demonstrate the performance of the proposed EPS architecture.

title: Loss Minimization Through Non-Dispatchable Distributed Generators in Distribution System
authors: Aditya, Narayanan Krishnan, Alexander Águila Téllez, Anurag Sharma, Gulshan Sharma, Tomonobu Senjyu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy sources within distribution networks such as the IEEE 33-Bus and IEEE 118-Bus systems offers a practical route toward affordable and sustainable electricity access. This paper presents a detailed techno-economic analysis of renewable energy technologies, focusing mainly on solar and wind generation. Key performance indicators—including the Voltage Stability Index (VSI), total system loss, voltage profile, and operational cost—are evaluated using load-flow analysis. The study examines the influence of incorporating Distributed Generators (DGs) of uniform capacity into the system. Using DGs of equal rating ensures consistent investment requirements for customers and simplifies comparative evaluation at the distribution level. Results from the simulated test systems provide valuable guidance for planners and utilities in designing system reinforcements that accommodate both future load growth and the increasing penetration of electric vehicles (EVs).

title: Advanced Incentive-Based Demand Response Strategies for Optimal Energy Management in Microgrids
authors: Neha Yadav, Amit Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Microgrids use energy management strategies to reduce costs and maximise resource use. This research proposes an energy management system incorporating demand response (DR) to optimise power output and load demand. This study incorporates an incentive-based DR strategy, where consumers are incentivized to reduce or shift their electricity usage. Consumers receive incentives based on their load curtailment, which helps the microgrid operator (MGO) balance supply and demand efficiently. The MGO offers incentives to service providers (SPs), who in turn compensate consumers for their participation, thus creating a financial balance in the resource trading process. Additionally, an advanced Coati Optimization Algorithm (ACOA) is utilized to optimize the cost and incentives. The results demonstrate that the proposed ACOA technique, combined with the incentive-based DR strategy, effectively reduces operational costs by up to 5.040 %.

title: Cyber Resilient Protection Functionality of Modern Protective Relays
authors: Rahul Pujari, Mahamad Nabab Alam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The advancements in technology have significantly enhanced and strengthened the reliability of modern power system network. With the integration of modern advanced tools like Internet of Things (IoT), Information and Communication Technology (ICT), and standard communication protocols, the traditional power grid is rapidly evolving into a digital and more sophisticated intelligent smart grid. This offers improved controllability, stability, resiliency and efficiency. However, this digital transformation introduces new risks in particularly to cyber threats. The increasing dependence on digital infrastructure unmask the grid to potential cyber-attacks, which can target critical protection infrastructure and can cause faulty conditions without any actual physical fault type scenario. Such attacks pose challenges, as they can compromise the security, dependability, and integrity of fault detection and protection systems. Thus recent research focused on distinguishing between cyber-attacks, faults, and faults occurring during a cyber-attack. In this regard, the authors of this paper propose a method that utilizes digital data with local analog measurements of voltage, current, and frequency—and integrates them with existing protection relay logic. Which aims to reliably detect and differentiate between complex scenarios and thus enhancing grid resilience.

title: IoT Node Synchronized with Indian Constellation for Phasor Measurement in Power System
authors: Pratik Sharma, Ankush Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The commitment to reduce carbon emission is encouraging the integration of renewable energy generation sources in the power system. However, the intermittency of these renewable resources adds stress to the power system and compels its components to operate close to their operating limits. This situation pose threat to the security and stability of the power system. Thus it is important to implement real-time monitoring techniques in power systems. The real-time monitoring in the power system is carried out using wide area monitoring system (WAMS) which intends to cover the monitoring of power systems spread over a huge geographical area using phasor measurement units (PMUs). This paper describes the construction of a low-cost, open-source hardware based, phasor measurement apparatus. It acquires 64 digital samples per power cycle and can publish up to 50 phasor measurement. It employs the Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System (IRNSS) for time stamping, and streams reports following IEEE C37.118.2011 standard. The results indicate that a low-cost, standards-compliant PMU can provide actionable situational awareness for power grid.

title: Fuzzy Logic Controlled Hybrid EV Charging Station Utilizing Solar Power and Diesel Generator
authors: Chidurala Saiprakash, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Srikanth Velpula, Masadi Prashanth Kumar, Kalyani Thota
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The Range-Anxiety is the major concern influencing consumers' use of Electric Vehicles (EVs). In this context, designing an EV charging station (CS) has become more important and plays a vital role in EV technology. Especially the designing of EVs CS using different energy sources that are in demand, which helps in increasing the use of renewable energy sources. In this paper, the CS for EV is designed using solar energy, batteries, along with a diesel generator and verified its performance. This charging station employs the use of a grid-operated CS for continuous load disjunct, grid-operated, and DG-operated modes. A power storage unit, a DG, and photovoltaic (PV) technology are used for EV charging. The DG set in this project has a FUZZY controller that can control the voltage and frequency. Recharging the EV energy storage unit by making use of solar PV and BES saves money by reducing the cost per unit at a charging station. In the event of an empty storage battery and the unavailability of solar PV array production, the CS is conceptually powered by electricity from the power grid or diesel generators. To ensure maximum fuel efficiency, the DG aggregation power is drawn in a way that typically operates at 80 to 90 percent charge in all charging conditions. The charger manages the generator's output voltage and frequency in addition to the storage battery in the absence of a mechanical speed control. Furthermore, the tension of the PCC (Point Common connector) synchronises with the grid/generator voltage for continuous loading. Further, to maximise the vehicle's working efficiency, the CS directs the EV toward the grid's isolated/connected transmission, its domestic, and the transference of vehicle power. The proposed CS operation is verified using Matlab/Simulink.

title: DMSA: A Decentralized Microservice Architecture for Edge Networks
authors: Yuang Chen, Chengdi Lu, Yongsheng Huang, Chang Wu, Fengqian Guo, Hancheng Lu, Chang Wen Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The dispersed node locations and complex topologies of edge networks, combined with intricate dynamic microservice dependencies, render traditional centralized microservice architectures (MSAs) unsuitable. In this paper, we propose a decentralized microservice architecture (DMSA), which delegates scheduling functions from the control plane to edge nodes. DMSA redesigns and implements three core modules of microservice discovery, monitoring, and scheduling for edge networks to achieve precise awareness of instance deployments, low monitoring overhead and measurement errors, and accurate dynamic scheduling, respectively. Particularly, DMSA has customized a microservice scheduling scheme that leverages multi-port listening and zero-copy forwarding to guarantee high data forwarding efficiency. Moreover, a dynamic weighted multi-level load balancing algorithm is proposed to adjust scheduling dynamically with consideration of reliability, priority, and response delay. Finally, we have implemented a physical verification platform for DMSA. Extensive empirical results demonstrate that compared to state-of-the-art and traditional scheduling schemes, DMSA effectively counteracts link failures and network fluctuations, improving the service response delay and execution success rate by approximately <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$60\% \sim 75\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10\%\sim 15\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>, respectively.

title: Attitude Estimation on SO(3) Under Biased, Scaled and Misaligned Rate Gyro Measurements
authors: Joel Reis, Carlos Silvestre
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This technical note presents a nonlinear attitude observer formulated directly on the special orthogonal group (SO(3)). The observer uses body-fixed measurements from at least two non-collinear inertial reference directions, along with readings from a triaxial rate gyro. The rate gyro outputs are subject to nonidealities modeled as an affine transformation of the input. Under standard and mild assumptions, such as the requirement for persistent excitation of the true angular velocity and its derivative, the proposed estimator ensures almost global asymptotic stability while simultaneously estimating attitude and intrinsic parameters of the rate gyro, including scaling factors, installation misalignment, and offset. The observer's performance and effectiveness are demonstrated through extensive and realistic numerical simulations.

title: A Digital Slope Modulation Technique for a PFM-based Series Resonant LED Driver
authors: Arnab Kumar Pal, Amit Kumar Singha
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: The envelope slope-controlled hysteretic pulse frequency modulation (HPFM) scheme can be an efficient and low-cost alternative over the traditional PI-controller based single-loop/multi-loop resonant LED drivers. With the help of two slopes, the current mode HPFM scheme can regulate a wide range of output loads. However, the flickering issues and large output ripples under light load conditions remain major limitations of this scheme. To address these issues, this paper proposes a load-dependent slope reference to precisely control the rising and falling slopes of the main envelope. The impact of the envelope reference slope on frequency deviations is thoroughly analyzed and analytical expressions for different control slopes are derived. Furthermore, a C-L filter is proposed to alleviate the impact of LED dynamic resistance variation due to thermal effects. The proposed scheme is implemented using a field-programmable gate array (FPGA) based digital platform and the experimental results are obtained using a 170 W rated in-house series resonant converter (SRC)-based LED driver prototype. Results show that the proposed scheme is capable of reducing the LED current ripple and output flicker significantly with improved line regulation and load transient performances compared to the reported schemes.

title: The Landscape of GPU-Centric Communication
authors: Didem Unat, Ilyas Turimbetov, Mohammed Issa, Dogan Sagbili, Flavio Vella, Daniele Sensi, Ismayil Ismayilov
publish: ACM Computing Surveys
abstract: None

title: HierMine: Accelerating Graph Pattern Mining via Hierarchical Sampling
authors: Guang Wu, Xinbiao Gan, Songzhu Mei, Zhengbin Pang, Hongxu Jin
publish: ACM Transactions on Architecture and Code Optimization
abstract: None

title: MConAI: Multilingual Conversational AI for Low-Cost Early Alzheimer's Detection
authors: Srihith Chennareddy, Charisse N. Winston
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 8th Artificial Intelligence and Cloud Computing Conference
abstract: None

title: Risk-Augmented Loss With Attention Mechanisms: Enhancing Proximal Policy Optimization for Safer Autonomous Driving
authors: Safae Belkhyr, Jaouad Boumhidi, Nikola S. Nikolov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Autonomous driving (AD) on highways presents significant challenges due to dynamic environments, high speeds, and uncertainties arising from sensor noise or abrupt vehicle behavior. This paper explores enhancing decision-making for autonomous vehicles (AVs) using an improved Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) based on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). The improvement is achieved through a novel risk-augmented loss function. Additionally, attention mechanisms are integrated to enhance performance. Unlike traditional approaches embedding risk in reward functions, our method integrates probabilistic risk assessment directly into the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm’s loss function, ensuring a clear separation between safety and performance objectives. Adaptive and robust decision-making are realized by paying more attention to important driving characteristics and punishing unsafe actions with risk-augmented loss. The experimental results show successes in safety, efficiency, and stability, proving that the proposed approach is effective in realistic highway driving scenarios. This system allows us to develop AV technology and address some of its major hurdles like dynamic environments, reliability and safety.

title: SpecAug-DM: Few-Shot Radar Spectrogram Augmentation via Diffusion Model for Human Motion Recognition
authors: Yue Lang, Wenjie Xia, Yuqi Fang, Yajie Fu, Shuang Li, Yang Yang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Radar-based human motion recognition provides a nonintrusive and reliable solution for a wide range of applications, such as smart living environments, health monitoring, and human–machine interaction. However, the scarcity of radar measurements and the limited diversity of available spectrograms often hinder the performance of data-driven models. Existing augmentation strategies, especially those based on generative adversarial networks (GANs), suffer from instability and insufficient diversity, which restrict their applicability in few-shot scenarios. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes SpecAug-DM, a diffusion-based framework for few-shot radar spectrogram augmentation. The framework incorporates a progressive sample regularization module to enhance intra-class diversity and a local-alignment mechanism to mitigate regional distribution discrepancies, thereby generating high-fidelity and semantically consistent spectrograms from limited data. Experimental validations are conducted on a measured ultra-wide bandwidth (UWB) radar dataset as well as a public frequency modulated continuous wave (FMCW) radar dataset. Extensive results demonstrate that SpecAug-DM outperforms several state-of-the-art comparative algorithms, and substantially improves motion recognition performance, highlighting its potential for reliable radar-based human motion perception.

title: Joint Magnetometer-IMU Calibration via Maximum A Posteriori Estimation
authors: Chuan Huang, Gustaf Hendeby, Isaac Skog
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper presents a new method for jointly calibrating a magnetometer and inertial measurement unit (IMU), focusing on balancing calibration accuracy and computational efficiency. The proposed method is based on a maximum a posteriori estimation framework, treating both the calibration parameters and orientation trajectory of the sensors as unknowns. This method enables efficient optimization of the calibration parameters using analytically derived derivatives. The performance of the proposed method is compared against that of two state-of-the-art methods. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves the lowest root mean square error in calibration parameters, increasing the calibration accuracy by 20–30%, while maintaining competitive computational efficiency. Further validation through real-world experiments confirms the practical benefits of the proposed method. The proposed method calibrated 30 magnetometer-IMU pairs in under two minutes on a consumer-grade laptop, which is one order of magnitude faster than the most accurate state-of-the-art algorithm as implemented in this work. Moreover, when calibrated using the proposed method, a magnetic-field-aided inertial navigation system achieved positioning performance comparable to when it is calibrated with the state-of-the-art method. These results demonstrate that the proposed method is a reliable and effective choice for jointly calibrating magnetometer-IMU pairs.

title: AGI-CTFormer: An efficient architecture with attention-based ghost inception ResNet and a convolutional transformer for the detection of Exons in eukaryotic DNA
authors: K. Jayasree, Malaya Kumar Hota
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In bioinformatics, accurately detecting exons in eukaryotic DNA is a challenging task. In this work, an efficient inception and transformer-based deep learning model, namely AGI-CTFormer, is introduced for the enhanced detection of exons. Here, a new Attention-based Ghost Inception ResNet (AGIR) module is proposed that incorporates the attention module with Ghost Inception ResNet to generate multi-scale local features. Further, a Convolutional Transformer block is proposed to capture the global features. In Convolutional Transformer, the Depth-wise Multi-Head Self-Attention module extracts more salient features and provides high-level information, while a new Gated Feed Forward Network effectively enhances the model’s capability by extracting deeper and richer information. The proposed AGI-CTFormer model integrates the AGIR module with the Convolutional Transformer block to enhance feature extraction capability. Furthermore, the SVM classifier is employed by optimizing its parameters using a Bayesian optimization algorithm to enhance the overall classification performance. The efficacy of the proposed model is evaluated in comparison with a few pretrained networks using two benchmark datasets. In the 10-fold cross-validation experiment, the proposed model achieves a superior performance, obtaining an accuracy of 96.58% for the HMR 195 dataset and 98.27% for the Genscan training set.

title: Bridging the Trust-Latency Gap in 6G NTN V2X: An Edge-Native Architecture and Standardisation Perspective
authors: Muhammad Toaha Raza Khan, Enver Ever, Omer Chughtai, Yoney Kirsal Ever
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: The integration of non-terrestrial networks (NTNs) into sixth-generation (6G) vehicle-to-everything (V2X) systems promises ubiquitous connectivity. However, the high latency of satellite links is incompatible with the real-time requirements of centralised security verification. This article proposes an edge-native trust architecture to resolve this latency-trust trade-off by relocating critical misbehaviour detection to the vehicle. In the proposed architecture, vehicles perform local trust assessment and trust scoring on received V2X messages, enabling real-time safety decisions independent of high-latency remote verification. We explore the standardisation implications of this shift and validate its feasibility through a practical implementation on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano platform. Experimental results demonstrate that a complete end-to-end trust assessment, including cryptographic verification and parsing, can be performed in approximately 7.14 ms. Furthermore, by utilising optimised power profiles, energy consumption is reduced by up to 68% (to 50 mJ/inference) in Eco-mode, achieving response times an order of magnitude faster than centralised NTN architectures. These findings underscore the need to incorporate distributed edge intelligence into future 3GPP and ETSI standards to ensure resilient, energy-efficient 6G V2X operations.

title: Wearable Textile Microwave Sensor for Wrist Pulse Wave and Heart Rate Monitoring
authors: Amirhossein Karami-Horestani, Bahareh Moradi, Ferran Paredes, Ferran Martín
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper presents a contact bracelet-type microwave sensor, implemented on fabric, able to monitor the wrist pulse wave (WPW) and, consequently, the heart rate of the individual under analysis. The device operates in transmission, and the working principle is the variation of the frequency response as consequence of pressure changes exerted on a diaphragm caused by blood pumping in the radial artery. The associated electronics to implement a stand-alone device (avoiding the use of a vector network analyzer) are also included. Specifically, by using a phase or an envelope detector, the system provides either the phase or the magnitude of the output signal at a specific frequency, namely, the WPW. The main relevant contribution of the paper is the implementation and validation of the sensor in a textile (and hence flexible and conformal) substrate, using also textile conductors. By this means, the device features a low cost, profile, and weight, making it ergonomic and user-friendly for real usage. Comparison of the recorded signal of the sensor on multiple individuals (at rest, under motion, and by wetting their wrist skin) with Photoplethysmography (PPG) further validates the approach.

title: FANE: FPGA-Based FP8 Approximate Neural Network Engine
authors: Han Bao, Shidi Tang, Jingdong Li, Ahmed Sadaqa, Ruiqi Chen, Ming Ling, Bruno da Silva
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: The 8-bit floating-point (FP8) format has gained growing interest in neural networks (NNs) for its superior dynamic range over traditional INT8. However, multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations remain a major source of power consumption during inference of NNs, which makes DSP-free design important, especially for edge FPGAs with few or no DSPs. Therefore, this brief presents FPGA-based FP8 approximate neural network engine (FANE), an FPGA-based approximate NN engine for FP8. We first introduce a novel approximation method that replaces the multiplications by linear additions. This approximate method reduces power consumption while maintaining high accuracy, outperforming the latest FP8 approximate multiplier by 53.15%. Based on this design, we construct an FP8 MAC unit and integrate it into both a convolution engine and a matrix–vector multiplication (MVM) unit. Finally, we integrate our design into a large language model (LLM). The result shows 61.5% higher efficiency (TOPS/W) than the previous design, demonstrating the superiority of FANE in terms of performance and power efficiency. The code of FANE is available on our <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/hanbao04/FANE-FPGA-based-FP8-Approximate-Neural-Network-Engine.git</uri>

title: Shaping the Q-U Droop Characteristic of Grid Forming Converters from the Perspective of Constant Voltage Control
authors: Chenhui Fan, Xiaohui Qin, Xiangyu Zhang, Cheng Wang, Hui Liu, Lei Qi, Jun Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy
abstract: With the increasing penetration rate of variable renewable energy (VRE) and other inverter-based resources (IBR) in power systems, system inertia and strength are being continuously weakened. Grid-forming (GFM) technology, adaptable to weak grids, has attracted widespread attention. The GFM Q-U droop control is widely adopted in practice, but it is challenging to tune key parameters, such as initial voltage reference value and droop coefficient, therefore, the adaptability and effectiveness of voltage support is difficult to ensure. To take the advantages of GFM converters as voltage sources, adopting constant voltage control (CVC) seems to be a better choice. This paper clarifies the implicit internal connection between CVC and droop control, indicating that the droop characteristic does not inherently depend on droop control, but should be regarded as a natural external port performance under internal CVC. Based on above analysis, a novel shaping method for Q-U droop characteristic of GFM converters from the perspective of CVC using virtual impedance is proposed. The virtual impedance in the proposed method is straightforward to set due to its explicit physical meaning, and the method performs well in strong adaptability and robustness for both islanded and grid-connected modes. Finally, the conclusions and the control effectiveness are verified by theoretical analysis, simulation, and experimental results.

title: Experimental Demonstration of Grid-Gate LDMOS with Ultra-Low R on,sp and High HCI Robustness on 55nm BCD Platform
authors: Jiawei Wang, Ming Qiao, Fanyi Zeng, Hanwen Bai, Wenliang Liu, Dingxiang Ma, Yangjie Liao, Zhaoji Li, Bo Zhang
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: In this letter, a grid-gate LDMOS (GG-LDMOS) is experimentally demonstrated on a 55-nm Bipolar-CMOS-DMOS (BCD) platform to relax the drift-region doping limit of scaled finger-LDMOS under breakdown-voltage (BVOFF) and hot-carrier injection (HCI) constraints. A sloping body formed by a dual-implantation process enables an ultra-short channel, making the channel resistance sufficiently low that the drift-region resistance dominates the on-resistance. Multidimensional depletion in the GG-LDMOS homogenizes the drift-region surface electric field and allows higher drift doping at a given BVOFF, lowering the specific on-resistance (Ron,sp). Circular and hexagonal GG-LDMOS devices are fabricated and analyzed, and the hexagonal cell spatially separates the peaks of ionization coefficient (αn) and vertical electric field, resulting in improved HCI robustness. The fabricated 9 V GG-LDMOS achieves BVOFF > 18 V, sub-1-μm cell pitch, and a 25% reduction in Ron,sp (1.76 mΩ·mm2) with a finger-LDMOS-comparable HCI lifetime in the hexagonal cells, providing an architecture for scaling in advanced BCD nodes.

title: RAID-Crypt: Rounding Alignment of Intermediate Differentials for Cryptanalysis of Lightweight Encryption
authors: Jonathan Cook, Sabih Ur Rehman, M. Arif Khan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The first lightweight encryption algorithms suitable for the Internet of Things (IoT), SIMON and SPECK, were introduced in 2013 and were immediately subjected to security evaluations utilising cryptanalysis techniques. However, cryptanalysis is inefficient, and while heuristic and sampling methods have improved efficiency, the random nature of heuristics hinders reproducibility and impedes further advances. While Machine Learning (ML) techniques, such as Deep Neural Networks (DNN), have had limited success against SPECK by creating an effective Difference Distribution Table (DDT), they remain less efficient than heuristic strategies. Furthermore, DDT and partial DDTs (pDDTs), which are often utilised in differential cryptanalysis, contain limited information, which hinders the ability of ML models to predict high-probability differentials accurately. To improve model accuracy, researchers often utilise feature engineering techniques to enhance data quality and extract additional features. However, additional features, such as Boolean data, can introduce a floating-point centroid with ML models such as k-means. Identifying these limitations, we introduce a technique utilising Rounding Alignment of Intermediate Differentials, which we have called RAID-Crypt. We begin by enriching the information in a pDDT by leveraging the elegant, intricate, and predictable nature of powers of two to feature engineer additional differential features. Utilising the additional features enables us to introduce a method for centroid rounding in k-means clustering, which increases the number of rounds to 20 while reducing time by up to 65%, producing state-of-the-art results. Through a process of analysing and comparing the performance of several ML models, we identify five key features crucial for selecting an ML model, which is further validated through Bayesian Optimisation. Our technique presents robust and reproducible results, demonstrating the effectiveness of feature engineering differentials and centroid rounding. The code utilised in this research will be available at: https://github.com/johncook1979/simon-kmeans-cryptanalysis once published.

title: A Multi-modal Iterative Guided Framework for Fake News Detection
authors: Shouxin Liu, Junfeng Guo, Hongran Zeng, Chongyang Zhang, In-Kwon Lee, Yushu Zhang, Xiaowei Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: In recent years, multi-modal fake news detection methods have been widely used and have shown good detection performance in identifying common fake news scenarios, i.e., different tweets are accompanied by different images. However, many fake news stories involve multiple tweets using the same image. Existing detection methods do not fully consider this feature, resulting in unsatisfactory performance in this case. To address this omission problem, we proposed a Multi-modal Iterative Guided Framework (MIGF), where the entire detection process is completed in the latent space. Specifically, first, we construct a latent space at the feature level to alleviate the problem of inconsistent multi-modal feature space and distribution. Then, at the multi-feature interaction level, a multi-modal cross-guided attention mechanism is designed for the interaction of text and visual features. Finally, in terms of multi-view feature extraction and learning, an iterative guided mixture-of-expert system is used to guide the extraction of multi-modal features. Comparative experiments show that the comprehensive performance of MIGF surpasses the baseline models, verifying its effectiveness and superiority. Ablation experiments further quantify the contribution of each module, proving that the overall architecture is indispensable.

title: Cross-Domain Self-Training Network with Prototype Rectification for Few-Shot Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Qingyan Wang, Peng Luo, Junping Zhang, Zhenhang Yao, Yujing Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: In recent years, the classification research of hyperspectral image (HSI) with scarce labeled samples has made significant progress, demonstrating strong performance when labeled samples are limited. However, current methods often overlook the importance of class prototypes. Additionally, while Conditional Domain Adversarial Adaptation (CDAA) is employed to mitigate domain shift, it fails to account for the varying degrees of data distribution discrepancies across different classes, leading to suboptimal alignment. To address these issues, a Cross-domain Self-Training Network with Prototype Rectification (CSNPR) for few-shot HSI classification is proposed. First, a Three-dimensional Ghost Attention Network with Brownian distance covariance (TGAN-B) is introduced to improve the representation learning of HSI, generating more effective class prototypes. Next, an adaptive weighted conditional domain adversarial adaptation (ACDAA) strategy is proposed, which assigns weights based on the domain discrepancy for better alignment. Finally, a Pseudo Label Selection (PLS) module is developed to select the most confident pseudo-labeled samples from each class in the target domain, thereby enriching the training set. The experimental findings derived from the analysis of three publicly available HSI datasets, prove that the CSNPR method outperforms existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods, achieving superior and stable performance even with sparse labeled samples. Our source code is available at https://github.com/Yzh190-hue/CSNPR.

title: A Multi-groove Ceramic-packaged Sapphire-Diaphragm Fiber Sensor for High-temperature Vibration
authors: Zhifeng Wang, Guangze Yao, Xingkui Pang, Xuecheng Liu, Heming Wei, Mengshi Zhu, Liang Zhang, Fufei Pang
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: A sapphire-diaphragm fiber-optic vibration sensor for high-temperature applications is proposed and experimentally demonstrated. Its sensing unit consists solely of a monolithic sapphire sensitive diaphragm encapsulated in a multi-groove ceramic housing, forming a stable Fabry-Pérot interferometer. Experimental shows that the sensor achieves reliable vibration measurements up to 10 g across a temperature range of 17°C to 800°C, with a room-temperature sensitivity of 5.18 nm/g and a resonant frequency of 9.65 kHz. After being subjected to a 237 g impact at room temperature, the amplitude–frequency response error within the 20-3000 Hz band remains approximately 6.2%. Experimental results demonstrate that the sensor features a compact structure and robust performance, offering a reliable solution for vibration monitoring in harsh environments.

title: Research on the Pattern Design of Doors and Windows in Tuanshan Village Based on AIGC and Entropy Weight TOPSIS Method
