title: Grid Resilience and Renewable Integration: India's Transition to a Sustainable Power System
authors: Shubhra Shah, Prakash Chand Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The Indian power sector is committed to fulfill its goal to achieve a 500 GW non-fossil fuel-based generation capacity by 2030, targeting 54 % on-grid renewables by 2030 and 80 % by 2040. As of Aug'2025, renewable energy accounts for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$38.8 \%(192 \text{GW})$</tex> of the nation's grid network. This extensive integration of inverter-based resources (IBRs) are posing numerous challenges which must be addressed promptly to ensure a safe and reliable power system. The absence of synchronous generators in the grid has led to reduced system inertia and short circuit power, which in turn impacts transient and dynamic stability. Automatic Generation Control (AGC) is being implemented in India's grid to support and enhance frequency response and inertia support. The challenges associated with high inverter-based resource (IBR) integration & decreased system inertia can be mitigated through the increased deployment of pumped storage projects (PSPs), synchronous condensers, and battery energy storage systems (BESS) within the grid. This paper provides an overview of the methodology and findings from a study conducted to assess the power system inertia of the Indian grid. It discusses the various challenges posed by large-scale IBR integration, their impacts on the grid, and the mitigation strategies being employed from both technological and infrastructural perspectives.

title: Series Cascade Control Loop for Double Integrating Processes Based on Smith Predictor
authors: Prabir Singha, Sudipta Chakraborty, Vipin Chandra Pal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Cascade double integrating processes, often found in industrial applications such as fluid level regulation and thermal control, display second-order dynamics without inherent damping, resulting in marginal stability and uncontrolled responses. This paper examines their dynamic behavior and suggests effective control methods, including cascade control systems and PID controllers with derivative action. Simulation results show significant improvements in stability and system performance with these control strategies. The well-known IMC method and the maximum sensitivity function are used to derive the controller gains. A modified Smith Predictor structure is adopted in the proposed design to mitigate delays and enhance robustness.

title: Extended Kalman Filter Based Control of Coupled Inductor Buck-Boost Converter for Grid Connected System
authors: Debarchita Mishra, Krithik M, Ananth S, Kathir A
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study introduces a Photovoltaic (PV) based sophisticated control approach for a Coupled Inductor Buck-Boost Converter (CIBBC) for grid tied system. As the main renewable energy source, PV module uses the CIBBC topology to efficiently boost up the DC-link voltage in the face of varying solar irradiation with better efficiency. By dynamically estimating and compensating for nonlinearities and unmodeled disturbances in the converter operation, Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) based controller improves voltage regulation and the grid's power quality. Additionally, a battery energy storage system (BESS) with a bidirectional converter interface is included to guarantee grid side flexibility and energy reliability. For power balancing and grid support tasks, this system makes charging and discharging smooth. In order to ensure high-efficiency energy conversion, robust transient response and intelligent energy management, the proposed structure permits coordinated power flow between the PV, converter, battery and grid. The EKF-controlled CIBBC's improved dynamic and steady-state performance is validated by MATLAB/simulation, and its results attained a minimized Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) of 0.59% and the highest State of Charge (SOC) of 80%, proving its feasibility for applications in the next generation of sustainable energy needs.

title: IoT-Based Fault Diagnosis of AFE Using Harmonic Distortion and Waveform Asymmetry for EV Charging Applications
authors: S. Ranesh, B. Jyothilakshmi, G. Arun Senthil, R. Raja Singh, Yong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The active front-end (AFE) rectifier is an essential component of electric vehicle (EV) rapid charging stations. Due to its dependability and cost-effectiveness, the two-level power converter is the most prevalent among power converter topologies. The power switches are key components of the AFE rectifier for controlling the power conversion process. A failure in these switches results in poor power quality, lower efficiency, or even a system outage. To improve the dependability of power converters, many ways are being used in the present research. Power converter reliability can be improved by incorporating fault diagnosis and real-time condition monitoring. This paper introduces a technique for detecting and localising open circuit switch faults by analysing the current harmonic distortion and waveform asymmetry. A 30 kW EV charging unit with an AFE rectifier is considered for this study. Furthermore, an IoT application has been built and linked with the EV charging AFE power converter system, enabling user notifications by app or email in the event of a switch fault. Overall, the suggested approach provides more benefits for establishing EV charging stations in terms of power conditioning and dependability.

title: Performance Enhancement and Excess Energy Minimization in Hybrid Renewable Energy System for Cold Desert Regions
authors: Neelam Bora, Swapnajit Pattnaik, Baidyanath Bag
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The Ladakh region experiences power mismatch issues due to varying load demand throughout the day. According to HOMER Pro optimization results, solar photo voltaic (PV) generates high power during the daytime when demand is low, whereas at night, the demand increases significantly, but solar generation is absent. To address this, a Python-based dispatch strategy is developed to reduce excess energy in the PV-battery-DG (Diesel generator) system, initially optimized using HOMER Pro. The dispatch logic activates a dump load in response to real-time PV surplus, a higher state of charge (SOC) and time-of-day constraints using post-processed HOMER data. The results show a significant reduction in excess energy along with Net Present Cost (NPC) and Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE), showcasing the benefits of smart dispatch integration in a hybrid energy system, further enhanced by parameter optimization using Genetic Algorithm (GA).

title: An Imposed Distributed Generation for Resynchronization Methodology for Microgrid Seamless Transitions
authors: Shikhar Jagram Singh, Suman Murugesan, Asheesh Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Microgrids (MG) can work in grid connected (GC) mode or islanding mode, with distributed energy resources (DERs) controlled using grid-forming or grid-following strategies. In GC mode, DERs typically use grid-following control, while in islanding mode; at least one DER operates in grid-forming mode. This paper proposes an integrated synchronization (SZN) control technique designed for a grid forming and grid following inverter operating within a MG. The aim of this technique is to enhance the MG's transients during the transition operations of the MG. The developed control loop includes both disconnection and reconnection process. The results demonstrate the effective performance of this integrated SZN control, ensuring a smooth and seamless operation during MG transitions. The proposed re-SZN methodology (IDGR) involves the allocation of two voltage source converters (VSC) at the substation point (SSP) for seamless mode switching. One VSC is dedicated to primary control, while the other serves as a backup. Additionally, a dedicated DG is included in the system as part of the IDGR. The re-SZN process, when transitioning from islanded mode to GC mode, can have a significant impact on the electrical parameters of the MG and utility grid. The SZN of voltage, frequency, and phase angle can potentially lead to system collapse. To mitigate these issues and ensure a seamless transition, a ReSZN Process Model IDGR has been developed. The IDGR facilitates fast SZN of electrical parameters, thereby achieving a smooth transition. The effectiveness of this phenomenon is demonstrated through the modelling and explanation of three cases in the result section using MATLAB R2024b.

title: Securing the Smart Drive: AI-Powered Anomaly Detection in EVs
authors: Shubhreet Dhaliwal, Pratham Sharma, Samrath Singh Kharbanda, Danish Sharma, Ashima Anand, Amit Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: With the advent of technology, the growth of the automobile industry has led to a rapid increase in the production of electric vehicles (EV). Manufacturers, in the hope of a brighter future, are shifting towards the development of EVs. However, following this transition, there awaits a daunting challenge of cyber threats in EVs. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of various AI-based anomaly detection techniques, focusing on the detection of noise injection attacks targeting the Battery Management System (BMS) in EVs. The study explores both machine learning and deep learning approaches, evaluating their effectiveness, accuracy, and suitability for real-time deployment. To ensure a realistic simulation of attack scenarios, a representative dataset was generated using Simulink in combination with the Virtual Vehicle Composer. By applying and analyzing different models on this dataset, the paper uncovers key performance metrics and highlights the strengths and limitations of each method. The insights gained from this analysis contribute to the development of more robust and predictive diagnostic systems, ultimately enhancing the safety and reliability of EVs.

title: Parameter Estimation of a PV Model Through Multi-Objective Optimization: Towards an Economically Viable Identification
authors: Arabinda Ghosh, Kukil Khanikar, Ankita Sarmah, Arjab Roy, Omkar Singh, Anjan Kumar Ray
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) modules offer a sustainable energy alternative, but developing reliable models for their performance remains challenging because of their sensitivity to environmental conditions and limited manufacturer data. This paper presents a multi-objective particle swarm optimization (MOPSO) based approach for estimating the parameters considering the two-diode PV model. Unlike existing methods that focus solely on minimizing current error, the proposed framework incorporates both current and power deviations as objective functions to ensure estimations that are physically consistent and economically viable. The proposed method is validated using experimental data from the Photowatt-PWP-201 PV module under standard test conditions. The sets of resulting parameters yield low estimation errors in both current and power. A comparative analysis with several popular optimization methods confirmed that the proposed approach provides improved performance and consistent parameter estimation.

title: Advancing Grid-Forming Converter Control Using Transformer-Based Artificial Intelligence Model: A Performance Evaluation
authors: Abhay Kumar, Dushyant Sharma, Mayukha Pal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative performance evaluation of a grid-forming converter (GFC) controlled via a transformer-based data-driven approach against a conventional control strategy. The proposed transformer-based framework leverages machine learning principles to enhance both steady-state accuracy and dynamic response without the need for extensive mathematical modeling or precise parameter tuning. Performance assessment is conducted using key error metrics—Integral of Absolute Error (IAE), Integral of Squared Error (ISE), Integral of Time-weighted Absolute Error (ITAE), and Integral of Time-weighted Squared Error (ITSE)—during transient periods, revealing consistently lower error magnitudes for the transformer-based controller compared to its traditional counterpart. Additionally, the Rate of Change of Voltage (RO-COV) analysis demonstrates reduced peak deviations, while voltage deviation results indicate significant improvement from 0.023 pu in traditional control to 0.008 pu with transformer-based control during the transient period. These findings highlight the superior adaptability and robustness of transformer-based GFC control during the transient phase, making it a promising solution for modern power systems subject to variable operating conditions and dynamic events.

title: Fault Diagnosis and Identification in Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Using Temporal Convolutional Network
authors: Anukriti Sharma, Khushboo Ramawat, Bharat Singh Rajpurohit, Adil Usman, Pramod J. Chaudhary
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to diagnose inter-turn short circuit (ITSC) fault in permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) based on machine learning (ML) models. A temporal convolution network (TCN) model has been used for the detection and classification of faults that utilize the time series current dataset. PMSM dataset includes healthy and faulty conditions with fault severity levels classified as low (2.26%), medium (2.70%), and high (12.17%). A comparative analysis has been performed between TCN and 1D-convolution neural network-long short-term memory (1DCNN-LSTM) to show the effectiveness of the proposed model. The performance evaluation of the proposed model has been done using accuracy, confusion matrices, and receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curves. The simulation results show that TCN model consistently achieved better accuracy of 99.60% for phase <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A$</tex> and 99.68% for phases B and C, and superior area under the curve (AUC) values across all severity levels, indicating efficient classification ability. The architectural advantages of TCN, including causal dilated convolutions, batch normalization, and parallel processing, enable it to extract temporal features more effectively than the recurrent-based CNN-LSTM model. This study show that TCN is a robust and efficient approach for real-time fault diagnosis in PMSM.

title: Enhanced Sensorless PMSM Drive Control with Data-Driven ANFIS-SMO Integration
authors: Shilpa Ranjan, Brijendra Sangar, Madhusudan Singh, Mini Sreejeth
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study presents an enhanced sensorless control technique for Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) drive. The proposed method combines an Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) with a Sliding Mode Observer (SMO), yielding a hybrid ANFIS-SMO architecture for precise rotor angle estimation. This fusion enables real-time adaptation of observer parameters via fuzzy logic rules, thereby helping suppress high-frequency chattering, improve estimation precision, and enhance resilience to motor parameter drifts and external disturbances. Performance was benchmarked against conventional sensorless strategies, including SMO and Phase-Locked Loop (PLL)-based approaches. Simulation and experimental results across diverse operating regimes, including varying reference speeds, load transients, and startup transients, confirm the superiority of the proposed ANFIS-SMO. Key improvements include a torque ripple reduction of up to 40%, a 25% gain in speed regulation precision, and rotor position estimation errors consistently kept under 2 electrical degrees. The method also achieves quicker transient settling and reduced overshoot in speed response relative to benchmark techniques, making it well-suited for demanding industrial PMSM drive applications.

title: GGM-LDMAF Control and Seamless Operation of a Solar-Powered EV Charger
authors: Pavan Kumar Singh, Divyank Srivastava, Bhim Singh, Arunima Verma, Saurabh Mani Tripathi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Rapid, high-power EV charging events create transient stress on distribution networks; this work addresses controller-level solutions that mitigate stability and powerquality impacts during steep charging ramps. To address these problems, this paper presents a grid-interactive, fast DC EV charging architecture that synergistically integrates a photovoltaic (PV) array and a battery for optimal energy management and enhanced grid reliability. This architecture is interfaced through a three-phase voltage source converter (VSC), which operates as a multifunctional power conditioning unit. It coordinates power exchange among grid, EV charger, local nonlinear loads, and PV array. Presented system ensures continuous and seamless power delivery for EV charger and nonlinear load in grid-connected mode and islanded mode and in mode transfer from GCM to islanded mode, with VSC operating under current control mode. To achieve precise current referencing and mitigate power quality issues, a novel Geman-McClure logistic distance metric adaptive filter (GGMLDMAF) is to control VSC. This sparsity-aware control algorithm demonstrates fast convergence characteristics, enhanced noise immunity, and robust harmonic suppression capabilities under dynamic conditions. It enables accurate extraction of fundamental current components for VSC reference current generation, ensuring minimal total harmonics distortion (THD) and compliance with IEEE-519 standard. We validate controller in a MATLAB/Simulink. Results demonstrate improved current-tracking, harmonic attenuation, and stable DC-link behavior under tested disturbances, and seamless transitions between operating modes under fluctuating grid and load conditions.

title: Real-Time Power Balancing in Bidirectional Vehicle-to-Vehicle Wireless Power Transfer Modulation and Control Strategy Development
authors: R. Nakkeeran, C. Bharatiraja
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The paper presents the development of advanced modulation and control strategies for real-time power balancing in Bidirectional Vehicle-To-Vehicle Wireless Power Transfer (V2V-WPT) systems with the increasing number of Electric Vehicles (EVs), the demand for efficient, wireless, and bidirectional power transfer methods is increasing, especially when one EV can act as a power source for another. Achieving stable and efficient power transfer while dynamically managing the bidirectional power flow is a major challenge in V2V-WPT systems. To address this, proposed an integrated control framework that integrates optimal modulation techniques with real-time adaptive control strategies. The electromagnetic behavior of the system is analyzed with ANSYS, which allows for the estimation of the magnetic field distribution and the optimization of the coil coupling. Meanwhile, PLECS simulations are used to model the dynamics of power transfer and validate the control algorithms under different load conditions. The proposed approach is experimentally validated using a prototype V2V-WPT configuration. Simulation and hardware test results confirm that the system maintains stable and efficient power flow under different operating conditions. This research demonstrates the feasibility of intelligent modulation and control schemes that enable real-time bidirectional power transfer, contributing to the advancement of V2V charging infrastructure.

title: Finite Element Analysis of a Five Phase PMSM Machine with Open-Circuit Fault and Its Mitigation
authors: Shinoy K. S, R. Sudharshan Kaarthik, Anish Gopinath
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The multiphase winding structure makes any machines fault tolerant, even with one or more of its stator phases open-circuited or isolated. In this paper a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \phi$</tex> Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) is designed for an aerospace application. Finite Element Analysis (FEA) of five <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\phi$</tex> PMSM operating with a single phase open-circuit fault, with and without compensation for mitigating low frequency torque ripple, that are inherent in machines operated with faults, is presented. During fault, there is an unbalance generated within the motor, which modifies magnetic fields with in the airgap of the motor. This unbalance can be avoided by modifying the currents in the healthy coils to generate the same rotating magneto-motive force (mmf), similar to the healthy motor. A compensated current profile was given to run the motor, to obtain a ripple free torque. Based on the FEA, it can be predicted how much the motor can be uprated under faulty conditions to achieve the same performance. In this work, FEA simulations provide more insight into the system especially on how the flux waveform in air gap varies during healthy, one coil failure and under compensation are presented. This work proves through FEA simulations that with compensation of currents, the flux shape is restored in the machine.

title: Unifying Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average Model, Poisson Regression, Internet of Things and Smart Cameras to Alleviate Traffic
authors: Wellington Makondo, Tawanda Mudawarima, Gladman Jekese, Wellington Manjoro
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Traffic violations represent an enormous challenge to road safety and economic growth in Zimbabwe and they contribute a major percentage of accidents, deaths and costs. This research paper discusses how possible it is for emerging technologies such as Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) Model, Poisson Regression, Internet of Things (IoT) and smart camera systems to combat these issues successfully. It presents the existing state of traffic enforcement in Zimbabwe, analyses the limitations of current infrastructure and legal frameworks and learns from successful rollout of technology in other developing nations. The paper succeeds in presenting a full architecture for the rollout of smart technologies into Zimbabwe's traffic management system, graphically portraying concrete applications in real-time observation, automation of enforcement and policy-making based on data. The paper also addresses the expected gains, possible challenges and policy implications of such an adoption. Through a detailed methodology pictorial diagrams and possible visualizations of influence, the aim of this study is to present a Zimbabwean template for improving road safety, minimizing traffic offenses and creating an improved and safer transport industry through technological advancement.

title: Facial Recognition in Online Examinations: Harnessing Converging Technologies to Redefine Online Exam Integrity
authors: Isheanesu Tafirenyika, Eva Tsitsi Chigodo
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: This paper presents a facial recognition system for real-time identity verification in online exams to uphold academic integrity. The hybrid architecture utilizes Haar Cascade classifiers for efficient face detection and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for feature encoding. Testing yielded 92.5% accuracy on benchmarks and 87.3% in realistic exam conditions. Beyond addressing deployment challenges like device heterogeneity and ethical privacy concerns, the system is designed for future integration with quantum computing, robotics, and advanced cybersecurity.

title: Secure Exam+: A Privacy-Conscious AI and Biometric Framework for Online Assessment Integrity
authors: Tariro Claudia Mhiribidi, Prudence Kadebu
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Secure online assessments have become a growing challenge in remote education. Existing proctoring systems face limitations such as high operational costs, intrusiveness, and privacy risks. This paper introduces Secure Exam+ an integrated platform that combines biometric fingerprint authentication, AI-powered gaze tracking, and browser lockdown to enhance exam integrity. Users are verified at login and intermittently during the test using fingerprint scans, while gaze behavior is monitored using computer vision models. A secure browser enforces restrictions to prevent misconduct. Evaluation is in a simulated environment with 20 participants showed 99% biometric accuracy and 92% gaze tracking precision. Secure Exam+ offers a scalable, privacy conscious alternative for modern online assessment environments.

title: Leveraging AI and IoT for Sustainable Food Processing and Manufacturing in Developing Countries: A Review
authors: Kurai Gareth Mawoneke, Takudzwa Rusike, Joel Mukwidigwi, Humphrey Masimba Makumbirofa
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Incorporating Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) in food processing and manufacturing offers transformative opportunities for improving sustainability, particularly in developing nations. This paper explores the potential of AIoT tools to improve agricultural productivity, ensure food safety, and optimize resource use across the supply chain. AI is essential for automating quality control, implementing predictive analytics, and enhancing supply chain management, while IoT enables real-time monitoring and data-informed decision-making. These technologies collectively address significant challenges, including supply paucity, inefficiencies, and post-harvest losses. Despite the promising applications, significant barriers impede widespread adoption, such as inadequate infrastructure, restricted access to funding, and skilled personnel scarcity. Case studies from India and Kenya highlight successful implementations, and the potential within the Southern African Development Community (SADC) is examined. These examples demonstrate the potential for AIoT to revolutionize food processing and agricultural practices, contributing to food security and economic development. The findings emphasize the necessity for collaborative efforts among governments, industry stakeholders, and academic institutions to overcome existing challenges and promote tailored solutions that align with local needs. Recommendations include fostering public-private partnerships, investing in capacity-building initiatives, and developing supportive regulatory frameworks. By leveraging AI and IoT technologies, developing countries can advance toward more resilient, efficient, and sustainable food systems, ultimately ensuring food sustainability and stimulating economic growth. This research enhances the comprehension of the critical role AIoT can play in transforming the agro-food sector, underscoring the importance of addressing barriers to completely leverage these tools for sustainable development.

title: Movable Antenna Enhanced Multiuser Communications with Mutual Coupling
authors: Wei Xu, Lipeng Zhu, Wenyan Ma, An Liu, Rui Zhang
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Movable antenna (MA) systems enhance wireless communication performance by dynamically adjusting antennas’ positions. However, most existing MA studies treat antennas’ mutual coupling as detrimental and neglect it by enforcing interantenna spacing larger than half-wavelength, which may result in model inaccuracy. This letter investigates the effect and potential benefits of mutual coupling in MA arrays for multi-user downlink communications. Specifically, we allow inter-antenna spacing to be less than half-wavelength and establish a mutual coupling-aware system model. The sum-rate maximization problem is formulated by jointly optimizing antennas’ positions and beamforming vectors with the position-dependent antenna mutual coupling matrix considered. To solve this highly non-convex problem, we propose a nested Greedy Search and Gradient Descent with Fractional Programming (GS-GD-FP) algorithm. It employs GS-GD in the outer loop to optimize antennas’ positions, while the Fractional Programming (FP) method is utilized in the inner loop to optimize beamforming vectors and attain the maximum achievable sum-rate for given antennas’ positions. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed coupling-aware MA array achieves significant sum-rate gains over conventional MA arrays optimized without considering mutual coupling in various scenarios.

title: High Power Large Mode Area Solid-Core with Nano Structure Antireflective Surface to Antiresonant Hollow-Core Fiber Splice
authors: Timothy Bate, Joseph Wahlen, Mark Whitledge, Ryan A. Lane, J. E. Antonio-Lopez, Md Selim Habib, Rodrigo Amezcua-Correa
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Antiresonant hollow-core fibers (AR-HCFs) have reduced their losses below traditional solid-core fiber and are beginning to scale production up for large-scale adoption. To achieve this, AR-HCFs must be able to be spliced into existing infrastructure with low loss and low back reflection. For high power fiber amplifiers, this back reflection not only steals gain but also seeds deleterious nonlinear effects like stimulated Brillouin scattering, and the splice losses lead to significant heating challenges. Here, we demonstrate for the first time to the best of our knowledge a motheye surface-treated large mode area fiber fusion spliced to a fundamental mode matched AR-HCF with an insertion efficiency of 93.4 % and a measured back reflection below <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$-32.3$</tex-math></inline-formula> dB at above 790 W. These results provide a springboard for next-generation remote material processing, power beaming over fiber, and directed energy applications.

title: Distributed Safe Frequency Control for Networked Microgrids Using Control Barrier Functions
authors: Zhiyuan Tang, Dongjian Li, Yue Xiang, Hongjun Gao, Youbo Liu, Junyong Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: In this letter, we consider the problem of keeping the system frequency of the off-grid networked microgrid system (NMG) within a safe set during the whole control process. The control problem is formulated as a state-dependent quadratic program using control barrier functions (CBFs). By introducing a set of auxiliary variables, the proposed controller can be implemented in a distributed manner while satisfying the same safety constraints at all times. Disturbance observers are also introduced in the control framework to eliminate the impact of external disturbances on safety guarantees established by CBFs.

title: Life with an Experimental Brain Implant: Early Users Reveal what the Technology Gives—And what it Takes
authors: Edd Gent
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: Scott Imbrie vividly remembers the first time he used a robotic arm to shake someone's hand and felt the robotic limb as if it were his own. “I still get goosebumps when I think about that initial contact,” he says. “It's just unexplainable.” The moment came courtesy of a brain implant: an array of electrodes that let him control a robotic arm and receive tactile sensations back to the brain. ¶ Getting there took decades. In 1985, Imbrie had woken up in the hospital after a car accident with a broken neck and a doctor telling him he'd never use his hands or legs again. His response was an expletive, he says—and a decision. “I'm not going to allow someone to tell me what I can and can't do.” With the determination of a head-strong 22-year-old, Imbrie gradually regained the ability to walk and some limited arm movement. Aware of how unusual his recovery was, the Illinois native wanted to help others in similar situations and began looking for research projects related to spinal cord injuries. For decades, though, he wasn't the right fit, until in 2020 he was finally accepted into a University of Chicago trial.

title: Ads
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Spectrum

title: Stochastic Gradient-Biased Traversal: a Heuristic Approach for Efficient Leak Source Localization
authors: Atharva Tilak, Prajakta Salunkhe, Ninad Mehendale
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this paper, we propose Stochastic GradientBiased Traversal: an algorithm for autonomous leak source discovery in unknown environments. Our approach uses local concentration gradients to guide the robot toward the leak source. The algorithm gives priority to movement toward higher gas concentration while also taking movement inertia to maintain direction in case of a tie in concentration values. This behavior prevents oscillation during traversal. We conducted 100 simulations for experimental evaluation in an simulated environment to demonstrate that the algorithm's effectiveness. It was able to consistently locate leak sources while requiring an average of only 259.17 steps in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$400 \times 200$</tex> field. Our approach aims not only to discover the source rapidly, but also continue exploration. The algorithm covers approximately 59.21 % of high-concentration regions. Concentration-time analysis shows effective prioritization of high-value areas early in traversal, making the algorithm particularly suitable for applications with strict time requirements.

title: Application of Golden Eagle Optimization in Reduced Order Modeling
authors: Aswant K. Sharma, Mangesh S. Modak, Sandeep Chawda, Dhanesh K. Sambariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The increasing complexity of modern engineering systems necessitates the development of efficient modeling techniques that reduce computational costs while maintaining accuracy. Reduced order modeling (ROM) is a powerful approach that simplifies high-order models into lower-order representations, preserving essential system dynamics. This paper explores the application of Golden Eagle Optimization (GEO), a nature-inspired algorithm, in the context of ROM for complex systems. GEO is inspired by the hunting behavior of golden eagles, particularly their ability to intelligently locate prey by balancing exploration and exploitation in dynamic environments. In this paper, GEO is employed to optimize the Single Input Single-Output System [S.I.S.O.] model achieving effective order reduction without significant loss of system characteristics. The performance of GEO-based ROM approach is compared with traditional techniques, demonstrating its potential to provide accurate approximations with reduced computational effort. The results highlights effectiveness of GEO in improving the accuracy and efficiency of reduced order models(ROM). The paper concludes with a discussion on the advantages and limitations of GEO in ROM applications, along with future research directions to enhance its robustness and versatility in real-world engineering problems.

title: Grid-Connected PV System Using Isolated DC-DC Converter for MPPT with Power Quality Enhancement
authors: Rahul Kumar Verma, Rajan Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The development of grid-linked photovoltaic (PV) schemes has been driven by the growing emphasis on nonconventional energy integration worldwide, particularly for the sustainable distribution of power to various load types. In order to efficiently handle both linear and nonlinear loads, this work provides a thorough design of a grid-linked PV scheme that makes use of an isolated DC-DC converter. To enhance energy extraction from the PV module in a variety of environmental circumstances, the suggested architecture integrates a perturb and observe (P&O) maximum power point tracking (MPPT) technique. Galvanic isolation and regulated DC output are ensured by the isolated DC-DC converter, which is controlled by a pulse width modulation (PWM) generator whose duty cycle is set by the MPPT output. An LCL filter is used to reduce harmonics and then convert this output to AC using a 3-phase inverter before it is connected to the utility grid and used to power local loads. To provide effective active and reactive power flow under dynamic loading situations, a unit vector control approach is used to synchronize the inverter with the grid voltage and current. The system is simulated using MATLAB for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ k W ~ P V}$</tex> array and tested under two scenarios-linear and nonlinear load conditions. The simulation results reveal that the system maintains voltage stability and effectively supplies constant load power even during fluctuations in solar irradiance.

title: A Triboelectric Nanogenerator for Wind Energy Harvesting and Battery Charging Applications
authors: Suvobrata Sil, Arunangshu Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This work presents the design and fabrication of a flexible wind-actuated triboelectric nanogenerator (W-TENG) that employs polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) as the negative triboelectric layer and polyurethane <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(P U)$</tex> as the positive counterpart for harvesting energy from surrounding airflow. The fabricated W-TENG exhibits a linear rise in voltage and current outputs as the wind velocity increases from 1 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. Among the tested devices, the W-TENG pair delivered the peak power density of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$41.43 ~\text{mW} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>, along with current and voltage sensitivities of 0.5 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu A /(\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s})$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28.51 ~\mathrm{V} /(\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s})$</tex>, respectively. The material selection was based on their high triboelectric contrast, flexibility, mechanical robustness, and insulating nature, all of which contribute to efficient charge generation and extended device lifespan. The devices also exhibited stable operation over 80,000 cycles, achieved without relying on complex fabrication methods. To demonstrate real-world applicability, a battery-charging circuit was simulated in MATLAB/Simulink, showing that the W-TENG attained a state-of-charge (SOC) increase of 5.74 %. These findings highlight the capability of the W-TENG to serve as an energy source for low-power electronics in autonomous and selfpowered systems, with future work focusing on optimizing power management circuitry to enhance energy storage efficiency.

title: Investigation of Flow Induced Variation in Vanadium Redox Flow Battery With Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy
authors: Jusmita Das, Rajdeep Dasgupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS) is a valuable technique for analysing the complex electrochemical phenomena occurring within Vanadium Redox Flow Batteries (VRFBs). It facilitates the investigation of key components such as electrodes, electrolytes, and membrane interfaces, as well as redox reactions and interfacial processes. To model the dynamic behaviour of a battery, electrical equivalent circuit models (ECMs) are commonly employed for their simplicity. This study presents an analysis of the optimal flow rate in VRFBs using EIS. By evaluating the impedance characteristics under different flow rates, the work identifies how changes in flow rate impact charge transfer resistance, capacitance behaviour, and overall battery performance. The impedance is modelled using an ECM comprising resistive, capacitive, inductive, and Warburg elements. The findings demonstrate that increased flow rate enhances mass transport, reduces impedance, and improves electrochemical kinetics, validating the ECM approach through experimental data.

title: Frequency Domain Dielectric Response of Aged Transformer Oil-Paper Insulation Retrofilled with Natural Ester Oil
authors: Brahma Swarup Laha, Soumya Chatterjee, Arup Kumar Das
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In the present work, frequency domain dielectric response of mineral oil (MO) impregnated paper insulation retrofilled with natural ester (NE) oils has been investigated. In this study, insulation samples resembling real-life transformer insulation were prepared using mineral oil, kraft paper and pressboard. Following this, the samples were dried and were subsequently immersed in fresh MO and the composite oil-paper insulation samples were placed inside an oven and thermal ageing was done <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> for constant duration of 400 hours, to emulate thermal aging of a real-life power transformer. Following this, frequency domain dielectric response (real part of complex capacitance and loss tangent) of the aged sample was measured using IDAX-300. Then, aged MO is replaced completely with fresh NE (FR3) and FDS measurement of oil-paper insulation was again performed. From the measured dielectric response, ac conductivity has been computed for NE filled oil-paper sample and were compared with that obtained using MO. Further, to unmask the effect of dielectric relaxation from the influence of conductivity, (charge transport), dielectric modulus (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M^{\prime}(\omega)$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M^{\prime \prime}(\omega)$</tex>) spectrum has been computed. It has been observed that the relaxation peak and the relaxation time constant showed significant variations with thermal ageing. Besides, it has been observed that the dielectric relaxation peak of oil-paper insulation is altered when the aged MO is replaced with fresh NE. The investigations reported in this work can be used for condition monitoring of MO transformers retrofilled with ester oils.

title: Frequency Response Analysis of Transformer Winding Faults Using Circuit-Based Modeling and Driving Point Impedance
authors: Manoj Samal, Mithun Mondal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive circuit-based methodology for diagnosing transformer winding faults using frequency response analysis (FRA) with driving point impedance (DPI) measurements. A detailed ladder network model is developed and simulated in LTspice to investigate seven critical fault scenarios: axial displacement (AD), radial deformation (RD), clamping pressure variation (CPV), inter-disc faults (IDF), short-circuit faults (SC), dielectric leakage (DL), and bushing degradation (BF). Each fault condition is characterized through systematic variations of equivalent circuit parameters, including inter-winding capacitance, mutual inductance, and ground capacitance. DPI measurements across the frequency range of 1 Hz to 1 MHz reveal distinct fault signatures: mechanical deformations primarily affect mid-to-high frequencies (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.05-0.2 \text{MHz}$</tex>), IDF manifest as deep attenuation valleys in the low-frequency range <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.001-0.02 \text{MHz})$</tex>, while BF produce broad attenuation across mid-frequencies (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 1 - 0. 1 0 ~ M H z}$</tex>). The simulation results provide quantitative correlations between the severity of the fault and the deviations of the impedance, establishing a robust framework for the assessment of transformer condition and predictive maintenance strategies.

title: Modeling and Simulation of Single-Phase Shift Power Modulation in Isolated Bi-directional Dual Active Bridge DC-DC Converter
authors: Nivedika Kher, Arun Rathore, Bhukya Nageswar Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a control scheme for an Isolated Bi-directional Dual Active Bridge (IBDAB) DC-DC converter where power regulation in the system is achieved using Single-Phase-Shift (SPS) modulation scheme. The operation entails managing the flow of power in IBDAB DC-DC converter in both directions which includes forward as well as reverse direction. The objective of this study is to achieve and analyze the high-power transfer efficiency throughout the entire range of operation. This work is carried out by using different values of power transfer for its successful operation. Finally, it validates the efficiency of the IBDAB DC-DC converter by using MATLAB/SIMULINK software.

title: Intensively Quantized Integrated Fuzzy Neural Network for High-Accuracy Image Classification
authors: Jianhong Yao, Yangming Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: Modern vision networks achieve high accuracy but depend on millions of weights and offer limited insight into prediction confidence. Fuzzy reasoning produces graded rule strengths that quantify uncertainty, and variational quantum circuits add compact expressive power, yet quantum neural networks and fuzzy reasoning rarely appear together in a unified model. We present the Intensively Quantized Integrated Fuzzy Neural Network (IQI-FNN), a quantized architecture that replaces the membership and defuzzification blocks of a classical fuzzy neural network with a shallow parameterized quantum circuit while retaining only minimal classical layers. Its quantum-fuzzy module merges single-qubit membership encoding, angle reuploading, a lightweight rule layer and a clustered-CNOT defuzzifier, which keeps gate count linear in feature dimension. Across standard image classification benchmarks IQI-FNN surpasses relevant state-of-the-art models and attains peak accuracy with modest hyperparameter tuning. Complexity analysis shows gate depth and parameter size grow gently with input dimension, the network stays lightweight, and fidelity remains high under representative gate-level noise in simulation. IQI-FNN therefore offers a compact, interpretable and noise-resilient alternative to conventional vision backbones and advances quantum-native fuzzy learning.

title: Efficiency-Enhanced Polarization-Reconfigurable Transparent Antenna Using Reversed-Stacked ITO Films
authors: Xiao-Xue Tan, Ming-Li Sun, Lei Zhu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents an efficiency-enhanced and reconfigurable optically transparent patch antenna (OTPA). A quality-factor (Q-factor)-based analysis is first conducted to quantitatively identify conduction loss of indium tin oxide (ITO) films as the dominant factor limiting radiation efficiency. To address this issue, a reversed-stacked indium tin oxide (ITO) configuration is proposed, which effectively increases the conductive thickness to enhance radiation efficiency while maintaining high optical transparency. Measured results at 2.4 GHz show that the proposed structure achieves a radiation efficiency of 41%, which is twice that of a conventional single-layer OTPA (20%). Based on this validated configuration, a reconfigurable OTPA is further proposed. By mechanically rotating the top patch, the antenna switches between right-hand circular polarization (RHCP) and linear polarization (LP) across the 2.39–2.6 GHz band. The prototype achieves a peak efficiency of 40.3% and an optical transparency of 71.1%, with a 3.7% 3-dB axial-ratio bandwidth (2.37-2.46 GHz) in the RHCP state.

title: Super-Resolution Channel Estimation for RIS-Aided Multi-User mmWave Systems: Dictionary Adaptation and Error Modeling
authors: Zhendong Peng, Gui Zhou, Cunhua Pan, Maged Elkashlan, Cyril Leung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: To achieve effective channel estimation for reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-aided multi-user (MU) millimeter wave (mmWave) systems, existing methods commonly exploit the shared RIS-base station (BS) channel characteristic; however, doing so inevitably decomposes estimation of cascaded channels into multiple stages, causing error propagation. To this end, this paper proposes a novel two-stage error-aware super-resolution channel estimation method that leverages the ambiguity and correlation arising from the shared channel, featuring performance enhancement, pilot overhead reduction, and a unique error-aware compensation mechanism. Specifically, in Stage I, the method estimates the common ambiguous RIS-BS channel by exploiting the MU diversity gain. In Stage II, the measurement matrix is constructed using the Stage I estimates, which enables joint space-time measurements and is perturbed by the estimation errors propagated from Stage I. Owing to these two distinctive characteristics, Stage II can use a significantly reduced number of pilots to not only estimate the ambiguous user-RIS channels of all users but, importantly, explicitly and adaptively estimate and then compensate for the shared errors propagated from Stage I via MU diversity gain, thereby effectively addressing error propagation and enhancing overall estimation performance. Accordingly, the MU super-resolution cascaded channel estimation is formulated into two types of tractable nonconvex optimizations, namely nonlinear approximations and joint sparse recovery and dictionary adaptation problems. Grid search-based and majorization-minimization-based solution procedures are developed to efficiently address these two types, respectively. Theoretical analysis of the proposed method is provided, and simulation results are presented to validate its effectiveness, further confirming the performance gains achieved by the unique error-aware compensation mechanism.

title: Model Vectorization and Low Fluctuation Method for Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer
authors: Xiaoning Li, Yuxi Gu, Fei Xu, Xian Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer (DWPT) is a pivotal technology for future mobile electrical energy interaction. However, its widespread adoption is challenged by significant transfer fluctuations induced by spatial and temporal factors. This paper proposes a vectorization method of variables to reconstruct the controlled scalar variables into space vector variables for analyzing fluctuations in both spatial and temporal factors. A vector-based model is further established for the multi-transmitter (Tx)-single-receiver (Rx) DWPT system. To improve the performance, the model is constrained and ultimately simplified into a single-Tx-single-Rx model. Based on this model, a stable output of the Rx is achieved just by maintaining the amplitude of the input voltage vector, which can track the Rx position synchronously. This paper further investigates the impact of the coil design parameters on the electrical parameters, cross‑coupling, fundamental content and offset of coupling coefficient curves, which can impact the model and system performance through the analysis. Compared with two existing advanced methods, the proposed approach demonstrates superior performance in experiments: power fluctuations remain around ±1.2% across all tested positions, and average efficiency exceeds 90%, peaking at 92.3%.

title: Consumer Responses to Ethical and Privacy Concerns in AI Chatbots: Evidence From Offensive Language, Gender Bias, and Digital Privacy
authors: Jikhan Jeong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Due to the rapid innovation of artificial intelligence (AI) and big data, AI chatbots have become increasingly influential computational social systems (CSS) in everyday life. However, the expansion of interactions between AI chatbots and consumers has raised significant ethical and privacy concerns. Using data from an online survey, this study empirically examines how consumer concerns about AI ethics and privacy influence their intention to use AI chatbots. These concerns are categorized into three types: 1) offensive language (generated by AI chatbots or users); 2) gender-biased language (from either source); and 3) digital privacy violations. The findings suggest that prior experience with AI chatbots is the most significant positive influence on respondents’ intention to use them. In contrast, all concern variables negatively influence respondents’ intention to use AI chatbots. Moreover, this intention varies depending on the type and source of unethical behavior. Specifically, concerns about user-generated offensive language directed at AI chatbots have a stronger negative effect than concerns about offensive language from AI directed at users. Conversely, concerns about AI-generated gender-biased language toward users have a stronger negative impact than concerns about such language from users toward AI. Finally, respondents express greater sensitivity to AI chatbots’ violations of their own digital privacy than to violations affecting other users.

title: Space-Forensic Tool Coverage Derivation through Satellite System Profiling
authors: Bae Geun Cho, Arpita Dinesh Sarang, Jun-Ho Hong, Ki-Woong Park
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With the expanding commercialization of reusable launch vehicles, ventures into the space industry appear feasible, increasing the number of satellite systems. As a consequence, based on recent incidents of ground station compromise, satellite link disruption, and terminal vulnerabilities, these systems are facing a variety of cyber threats. This highlights the necessity for a satellite forensics system to determine and prevent the cause of incidents. According to the space environment resources constraint, the conventional information technology (IT) forensics for terrestrial IT environments is nearly inapplicable in a satellite environment. Due to long propagation delays, restricted visibility, and the use of space-specific protocols like CCSDS, they employ embedded RTOS-based architectures that require real-time control. This is implemented through a multi-layered architecture. This necessitates a cross-analysis system based on flow between segments, indirect state reconstruction, signal-based restoration, and protocols. Also, a combination of specialized and general IT tools and time synchronization-based correlation analysis. Therefore, we analyzed relevant research trends, hierarchically profiled, codified the artifacts generated, and moved according to the structural and operational characteristics of the Space-Link-Ground segment. We systematically linked the functionality of satellite signal tools and IT forensics tools with profiling code to propose a forensic tool coverage model for each artifact in each segment. We constructed and simulated scenarios to examine applicability and procedural validity. This significantly improves the visibility of evidence acquisition and appropriate tool selection in satellite systems. Furthermore, forensic procedures allow reconstruction of a spacecraft’s internal states to counter security threats.

title: Coupling Structure Optimization and Efficiency Analysis in Ultra-Thin Sapphire Fiber
authors: Jie Xiong, Wen Zhang, Jinlong Li, Guanghui Chen, Lianqing Zhu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: A multi-cascaded coupling technique for Φ30 μm ultra-thin sapphire fibers is proposed and experimentally validated, demonstrating enhanced fundamental mode coupling efficiency and effective suppression of higher-order modes. Based on the overlap integral algorithm for coupling efficiency and phase-matching theory, a multi-cascaded transmission model for mode coupling in sapphire fibers is established. The model analyzes the coupling efficiencies of different modes under varying core diameters and input field conditions, while simulating axial field distribution, normalized fundamental mode power, and transverse output field profiles. An end-face processing method using hollow-core fiber nesting and auxiliary polishing is developed to address the challenges posed by the high hardness, single-crystal structure, and ultra-thin quasi-cylindrical geometry of sapphire fibers. High-quality splicing between heterogeneous fibers is achieved under low-arc misalignment fusion conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to direct coupling, the proposed technique nearly triples the output optical field intensity and produces a more concentrated energy distribution. This proposal provides a simple and effective solution to the challenge of ensuring high-quality fundamental mode transmission in ultra-thin sapphire fibers.

title: ECG Wavelet Graph for IoT-Enabled Well-Being Monitoring
authors: Aurora Silvi, Tiziana Cattai, Gaetano Scarano, Stefania Colonnese
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: IoT-based ECG monitoring can support well-being assessment across a range of applications, from emotion recognition to arrhythmia detection. In this work, we propose a novel graph–wavelet-based representation of the ECG signal for classification under different emotional and health conditions. The proposed wavelet-graph encodes the persistence of ECG components across multiple wavelet sub-bands, effectively discriminating structurally different signals both in affective computing and arrhythmia detection tasks. The proposed wavelet-graph approach applies a single unified framework across diverse tasks — a characteristic that remains relatively uncommon in the current literature — outperforming task-specific methods by up to 4% and maintaining competitive performance across all evaluated settings, despite those methods being individually optimized for a single classification problem. Furthermore, the proposed approach relies on substantially fewer training samples and a markedly reduced number of trainable parameters compared to existing methods, thereby supporting near–real-time IoT-based wellbeing monitoring applications.

title: Adaptive Detectors for Channel Matrix-based MIMO Radar
authors: Touseef Ali, Christ D. Richmond
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: In conventional radar signal processing, a structured model is typically assumed for the target response, while the co variance matrix of the radar data distribution characterizes clutter and interference. In contrast, the channel matrix-based model treats target and clutter returns as responses to corresponding channels, yielding a more generic and versatile radar data model. Leveraging this framework, and assuming the availability of primary and secondary data sets, this paper derives Generalized Likelihood Ratio Test (GLRT) statistics for adaptive target detection in a multiple input multiple-output (MIMO) radar system under the assumption of a complex multivariate elliptically symmetric (CMES) data distribution. We consider two scenarios: one where the covariance of waveform-independent colored noise (WICN) is known and another where it is unknown. The statistical performance of the resulting GLRTs is also analyzed. Additionally, Monte Carlo (MC) simulations and the saddle point approximation (SPA) are employed to generate receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curves for a simple numerical example, with experimental results and high f idelity simulations further substantiating our findings.

title: An Enhanced Golden Jackal Optimizer for Multi-Criteria DAG Task Scheduling in a Heterogeneous 6G Air-Ground-Space Integrated Network
authors: Ahmed I. Awad, Marwa M. Khashaba, Mostafa M. Fouda, Zubair Md Fadlullah, Khalid M. Hosny
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Next-generation computing paradigms are redefining the 6G ecosystem by enabling distributed intelligence over an integrated Space–Air–Ground Network (SAGIN) continuum. Despite these advances, scheduling complex applications modeled as Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) within this high-fidelity, three-dimensional infrastructure remains an NP-hard challenge, exacerbated by stochastic mobility, intermittent connectivity, and multi-tenancy resource contention. In this study, we present the Enhanced Golden Jackal Optimizer (EGJO). This training-free metaheuristic jointly optimizes four key objectives: execution time (makespan), local energy consumption, probabilistic task reliability, and operational costs under non-linear congestion-aware pricing. To navigate the non-convex fitness landscape and mitigate premature convergence, a stagnation-aware hybrid strategy is introduced that integrates a Frame Shifting operator for global exploration with a load-balancing intensification heuristic. The performance of EGJO is assessed using high-fidelity simulations that incorporate Gauss-Markov mobility models and LEO orbital constraints. Comprehensive evaluations against six metaheuristics (HHO, WOA, GTO, PSO, GA, and standard GJO) and domain-specific state-of-the-art approaches—including Modified HEFT and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)—demonstrate that EGJO attains a superior Pareto-optimal trade-off and a broader solution spread. Experimental results reveal that EGJO achieves nearly a 33x reduction in makespan compared to GA on large-scale 97-task workloads and reduces handover penalties to 3.0 s, significantly outperforming the 8.5–15.0 s range reported by baseline algorithms. Overall, EGJO achieves up to a 60.5% reduction in the global cost function, with average improvements of 57.67% in makespan and 62.83% in operational costs, ensuring robust and adaptive scheduling in dynamic 6G computing environments.

title: Strategic 3D Printing Technology Authorization for Spare Parts Under Demand Uncertainty
authors: Jiajia Nie, Qian Nie, Xiaoxuan Xu, Yu Zhou, Lijuan Wan, Yu Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: 3D printing technology enables on-demand production of spare parts, but its implementation requires technology authorization from the supplier. This study investigates whether a supplier would opt to authorize 3D printing under uncertain market demand and how this decision is influenced by spare parts-related costs and market conditions. To address these questions, we develop a game-theoretic model involving a supplier, two symmetric original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), and a 3D printing service provider to examine the equilibrium decisions of stakeholders. The impact of technology authorization is evaluated and validated through sensitivity analysis, and the outcomes are connected to practice. Although technology authorization enhances the supply chain's adaptability to market fluctuations, we find that the supplier should avoid granting authorization under the following conditions: the cost of 3D-printed spare parts is high, or market uncertainty is low. Interestingly, in the competitive setting, we uncover a free-riding effect under exclusive authorization, where the supplier's strategy can lead to a win-win-win outcome for all three parties. Furthermore, the results indicate that, under specific conditions, the supplier and the 3D printing service provider adopt opposing pricing strategies to sustain the OEMs' incentives to procure. Finally, robustness analyses confirm that our main conclusions remain consistent. These findings provide new insights into the strategic interactions among suppliers, OEMs, and 3D printing service providers, highlighting the complex trade-offs in technology authorization under demand uncertainty.

title: Stochastic Geometry Analysis and Optimization for Multi-LEO Satellite-Assisted Communications
authors: Xu Hu, Bin Lin, Ping Wang, Xiao Lu, Weihua Zhuang, Shuang Qi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites are vital in pro viding seamless connectivities to ground users (GUs). How ever, complex satellite-to-ground propagation and non-negligible satellite failure probabilities make single-satellite transmission unreliable. To address this issue, we consider a multi-LEO satellite communication network (mLEO-CN), where a typical GU is served by multiple LEO satellites. The satellite locations are modeled as a binomial point process on a sphere and ordered according to their distances to the GU. To improve communication reliability, a serial communication scheme is adopted, in which the subsequent satellite is activated only when all preceding satellites fail to satisfy the target signal to-noise ratio threshold. By employing the tool of stochastic geometry, we derive key performance metrics, including overall transmission success probability, average transmission rate, and end-to-end delay under the serial communication scheme by incorporating both shadowed Rician (SR) fading and satellite failure probabilities. To improve analytical tractability, we derive asymptotic expressions for the end-to-end transmission success probability in two special cases by leveraging the asymptotic expression of the SR fading model and a Gamma-distribution, and further propose a cluster-based approximation approach for the overall transmission success probability. Building on these analyses, we formulate an optimization problem to maximize the ratio of the overall transmission success probability to the end to-end delay, and obtain its solution using a one-dimensional search approach. Numerical results validate the analysis, reveal the impact of key parameters, and identify the optimal number of serving LEO satellites under different constellation altitudes.

title: Semantic-Aware Resource Allocation for Uplink RSMA via Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Chujun Zhang, Linyu Huang, Qian Ning
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Semantic communications (SemCom) and uplink rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) are promising enablers for efficient 6G systems, yet their integration poses new resource allocation challenges due to the intrinsic coupling between semantic compression and multi-user interference management. This letter investigates semantic-aware resource allocation for an uplink RSMA system. Joint optimization of semantic compression, bandwidth allocation, and transmit power is formulated to maximize long-term task transmission efficiency. The problem is modeled as a Markov decision process and solved using a Soft Actor-Critic framework. The simulation results demonstrate robust performance gains over baseline schemes under various resource constraints.

title: Enhancing Stock Price Forecasting through GA-SVR: An Evaluation of Three Crossover Strategies
authors: Shekhar Joshi, Anchit Bijalwan
