title: Multi-Criteria Collaborative GNSS Integrity Monitoring: Optimization, Spoofing/Jamming Detection, and Performance Evaluation
authors: Victor Vince, Dominique Heurguier, Alexandre Vervisch Picois, Jose Manuel Rubio Hernan
publish: IEEE Journal of Indoor and Seamless Positioning and Navigation
abstract: This study evaluates the performance of a collaborative, multi-criteria integrity control method in the presence of GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System) jamming and spoofing. By simultaneously leveraging several sources of information, it enables the determination of whether the position of the receiver has been compromised by anattack. In particular, it combines PVT (Position, Velocity, Time) residuals with the carrier-to-noise density ratio (C/N0) to ensure a robust assessment of integrity across all available data. Its main strength lies in collaboration between receivers through comparison and information sharing. This approach is purely software-based, requiring no additional hardware, and is scalable to receiver populations of varying sizes, from small to large. This study uses real jamming data and simulated spoofing data. In both cases, the data used for PVT calculation, originating from different satellites, are real. The presented results based on data combining simulation and real data demonstrate that the method is promising.

title: Design of Compact Wilkinson Power Divider Based on Suspended Mushroom Metamaterial
authors: Hanyong Wang, Yongqiang Wang, Kaixue Ma
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a compact Wilkinson power divider (WPD) implemented using a suspended mushroom structure. By exploiting the low loss and slow wave characteristics of the proposed unit under two excitation modes, the design simultaneously achieves low transmission loss and compact size. An inverse design approach based on an internal multiport method (IMPM) is introduced for rapid optimization. A 5 GHz prototype was designed, fabricated, and measured. The measured results show an insertion loss of 0.34 dB, an isolation of 24.7 dB, return losses better than 20 dB at all ports, and a 122% 10-dB fractional bandwidth. The core size is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.1\times 0.1~\lambda _{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>.

title: A Grid Connected PV Inverter with Subarray Level Control in MPP and non-MPP Mode
authors: Ayush Chauhan, Hiruteamlak G. Kidanemaria, Arpit Tiwari, Subhendu Dutta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a new power circuit topology and a dual-mode control strategy of a single-phase grid-connected solar PV inverter. This inverter is capable of seamlessly switching from MPP mode to non-MPP mode for independent regulation of active and reactive power of two series-connected PV subarrays. The inverter ensures that each of the two PV subarrays operates at their respective MPP points during MPP mode, enabling it to yield higher power from the PV array, especially under varying and mismatched atmospheric conditions. Apart from this, the inverter can also inject or absorb reactive power within its kVA limit. In non-MPP mode, the operating points of the subarrays are set by external active and reactive power references. The ability to control active power injection gives the inverter a flexibility to maximize its reactive power support (injection or absorption) without exceeding its total kVA rating. This seamless transition between MPP and non-MPP modes enhances operational flexibility, broadens the stability of the grid, and improves overall reliability of the power system. The description of the power circuit, operating principle, and control strategy of the proposed inverter are explained. A comprehensive simulation study has been carried out with a 2.5 kVA MATLAB-Simulink model of the proposed inverter to show its effectiveness.

title: Comprehensive Study Paper on the Role of Additive Manufacturing in Production of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor
authors: Asma Raza, Ujjwal Kumar Kalla
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Additive Manufacturing (AM) is revolutionizing the production of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) by offering exceptional design flexibility, material efficiency, and rapid prototyping capabilities. This paper reviews key AM technologies, including Selective Laser Sintering (SLS), Selective Laser Melting (SLM), Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM), and Electron Beam Melting (EBM), and various other technologies highlighting their applications in PMSM manufacturing. These technologies enable the creation of complex geometries, lightweight structures, and optimized cooling systems that enhance motor performance and reduce material waste. Case studies, such as the Fraunhofer Institute's development of 3D-printed stators for electric vehicle (EV) motors and CRP Technology's customized rotors for drones, illustrate the practical applications and performance improvements achieved through AM. Despite its advantages, challenges like material limitations and precision issues remain. However, with ongoing advancements in materials, multi-material printing, and digital twin integration, AM holds great promise for the future of PMSM production, offering the potential for more efficient, custoomized, and sustainable motors for a wide range of applications.

title: Optimal Charge Scheduling for Economic Operation of Electric Vehicle Charging Station
authors: Samarendra Pratap Singh, Bishwajit Dey, Neeraj Kanwar, Srikant Misra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The load demand in the distribution system has been on the rise as a result of the deployment of electric vehicles (EVs) in the automobile sector in recent days. The charging management must be enhanced in order to address this burden demand. However, it requires several hours to fully charge an electric vehicle (EV). The promotion of electric vehicles (EVs) over conventional vehicles is a significant competition that involves the reduction of energy consumption and charging time. The energy consumption of the EV may be influenced by the condition of the itineraries, which must be considered prior to satisfying the energy demand. This work aims to minimize the total operating cost (TOC) for the owner of an electric vehicle charging station (EVCS) owner also termed as EV aggregator by optimally scheduling the charging capabilities of up to 200 EVs based on their cost-effective grid to vehicle (G2V) and vehicle to grid (V2G) operation. TOC includes the V2G cost, G2V cost and battery degradation cost (BDC) of the EVs. Five different types of EV are used for the study and four case studies solved using CONOPT solver of GAMS environment analyses the best charging schedule with minimum TOC.

title: Enhanced Automation Using FPGA and IoT for Multi-Environment Applications
authors: Parvathy Harikumar, Harshavardhan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This work presents the design and implementation of a hybrid home and industrial automation system using FPGA board and NodeMCU. The system features the LM35 for temperature monitoring and the LM393 for sensing ambient light. The system includes actuators including a DC motor to mimic a fan and three LEDs representing tube light, bulb and air conditioner. For industrial setting, a servo motor demonstrates to mimic the movement of robotic arm by rotating from 0° to 90°. The system prototype is demonstrated using Xilinx Vivado for FPGA implementation, along with Arduino IDE and Blynk IoT for NodeMCU-based control and monitoring. This implementation demonstrates the adaptability and parallel capability of FPGAbased automation in a variety of applications, including those in home and industrial sector.

title: Comparative Analysis of Phase Shift Modulation Strategies for Dual Active Bridge Converters
authors: T. Dheepaswarupan, R. Nakkeeran, C. Bharatiraja
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: World moves towards the path of electric vehicles, we have a drastically higher usage of electric vehicles, which has opened a new problem called higher electricity usage. Dual Active Bridge (DAB) theatres a pivotal part is a solution for this problem. The DAB is nothing but a converter it seen as one that can attain a bidirectional power supply. It is very popular among researchers these days. It's popular because of its capability of attaining Zero switching voltage (ZVS), Low switching losses, and also an easy-to-understand circuit design. Even though it is easily understandable, the DAB converter features a wide spectrum of topologies, which arises a challenge. There is a need for a wide variety of control and modulation strategies which is been customized for specific applications. Various methods of modulation techniques, but in this paper, we are focusing on the phase shift modulation techniques, which include Single phase shift control (SPS), Extended phase shift control (EPS), Dual phase shift control (DPS), and Triple Phase Shift control (TPS). This work, a Conventional DAB converter is been modelled using PLECS software, where a numerous amount of simulations has been run for each of the advanced techniques listed above, and the resulting performance metrics like voltage, current, switching losses, conduction losses, efficiencies, and more importantly temperature of all 8 power switches which have been used are been compared and evaluated to list their effectiveness.

title: Real-Time Optimization of Partially Shaded Solar Photovoltaic Modules Using Parameter-Optimized Global Maximum Power Point Tracking
authors: N S Ramanathan, Devangi, Naran M. Pindoriya, Pallavi Bharadwaj
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Solar photovoltaic (PV) emerges as a promising energy source to reduce carbon emissions. However, the performance of solar panels deteriorates during partial shading conditions. Therefore, tracking global maximum power point to maximize the power output of PV systems amidst changing environment conditions is crucial. This work proposes an advanced parameter-optimized global maximum power point tracking (PGMPPT) algorithm, with stable operation under varying ambient conditions. It follows the underlying principles of bio-inspired algorithms to track the global maxima. The parameter optimization involved in the proposed method paves the way to optimally track the global maximum power point with low processing time, high speed, low CPU utilization factor (CUF) and low computational memory requirement (CMR). The proposed optimization enables faster convergence with a speed improvement of 62.5-70%. It is implemented and validated in real-time using OPAL-RT simulator using existing cuckoo search optimization (CSO) as a baseline, yielding a high tracking speed of 440μs over other bio-inspired algorithms for the given conditions in the study.

title: Economic Operation of an Islanded Microgrid Using PSO-Tuned Droop-Controlled Distributed Generations Alongside Solar and Wind Generations
authors: Sk Mahammad Sorif, Supriya Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The operation of an islanded microgrid is critical to maintain the system frequency and node voltages within acceptable limits around the clock as the load and generation change throughout the day. The generations from solar PV and wind generators are not even available for a long time of the day, and vary significantly during their availability. So, droop settings of droop-controlled distributed generations are controlled precisely in the presence of solar PV and wind generators to balance the generation and load. The active and reactive powers of droop-controlled distributed generations are optimized for each hour of a day by PSO algorithm based on the economic dispatch of power from the generators while satisfying the system frequency, node voltages, and power constraints. The effectiveness of the methodology is tested through a 33-node radial islanded network.

title: Fault-Tolerant Radio Communication Framework for TCAS: Design, Implementation and Validation
authors: Raj Singh, Sandeep Kumar, Sanjay Motia, Manpreet Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Reliable and fail-safe radio communication forms the backbone of safety-critical railway operations, especially for system like the Train Collision Avoidance System (TCAS). However, achieving resilience in radio networks demands solutions to challenges such as wireless instability, real-time failover, secure session handling, and redundancy management. This paper presents a robust Fault-Tolerant UHF Radio Communication Framework that ensures uninterrupted train-to-trackside communication using full-duplex UHF radios, hot-standby dualmodem architecture, and application-layer packet switching. GPS-synchronized TDMA scheduling provides time determinism but serves as a supporting layer to the core radio infrastructure. The framework is validated through diverse failure simulations, demonstrating seamless switchover among redundant radios and time sources (GPS, RTC, CPU). It fulfils TCAS safety specifications and lays a scalable foundation for integrating emerging technologies like 5G and software-defined radios in future railway communication systems. The framework architecture also accommodates cross-layer optimizations to reduce handover delays and packet retransmissions. Its modular and interoperable design ensures backward compatibility with legacy TCAS deployments while being adaptable for future digital signalling paradigms.

title: Real-Time Vital Sign Monitoring with Dashboard Integration
authors: Harinath Aireddy, Sudhir R, Marriwada Harshavardhan, Nanditha D N, Javvaji Pavan Kumar, Namitha Papishetty, Sheshadri K, Dappili Vijay Kumar Reddy, Shravani N
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The development of real-time vital sign monitoring systems has become a key advancement in healthcare, focusing on the continuous tracking of health metrics such as SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, heart rate, blood pressure, and body temperature. The system employs advanced sensors integrated with a microcontroller to continuously collect data, which is then wirelessly transmitted to a central dashboard for real-time visualization. The dashboard provides a user-friendly interface to monitor health trends and detect any irregularities, enhancing the efficiency of healthcare delivery. With low power consumption and high accuracy, this system can improve patient outcomes, reducing the reliance on manual processes, and enhancing the accessibility of healthcare services.

title: Twin-Delayed DDPG-Tuned PIDF Controller for Load Frequency Control in Power Grid with Distributed Energy Resources
authors: Tumuluri Datta Venkata Sai, Sukriti Tiwari, Devesh Shukla
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study of metaheuristic, deep reinforcement learning, and proposed hybrid approaches for Load Frequency Control in a multirenewable integrated two-area power system. A traditional Proportional Integral Derivative controller with a Filter is first implemented to tune the controller gains, along with metaheuristic algorithms such as Particle Swarm Optimization and Genetic Algorithm optimization. Particle Swarm Optimization and Genetic Algorithm optimization exhibited limitations in their ability to minimize steady-state error and achieve a shorter settling time. To address this, Deep Reinforcement Learning algorithms, Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient, and Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient, were integrated into the control loop. The controller using the TD3 algorithm delivered the most effective dynamic performance, providing quicker frequency recovery, minimizing tie-line variations, and enhancing resilience to load changes.

title: Coupled-Inductor Based Non-Isolated High Gain DC-DC Converter
authors: Raj Moyal, Jahangeer A. Dar, M.k. Pathak
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This research introduces a new non-isolated DC-DC converter topology that combines a coupled active-switched inductor network with a passive switched-capacitor configuration. The proposed configuration offers multiple benefits, such as an increased voltage gain, reduced voltage stress and current stress across the switching devices, and a lower component count when compared to similar high-voltage performance topologies. A comprehensive theoretical analysis is provided, accompanied by a performance comparison with existing designs. The converter's operation is comprehensively analyzed under both continuous conduction mode (CCM) and discontinuous conduction mode (DCM) conditions. Analytical findings are supported by simulation results acquired using PLECS software.

title: MAC: Multiple-Source Autoencoder-Based Cyberattack Detection System for Electric Vehicle Charging Communications
authors: Son Duong, Secil Ercan, Mahdi Zargayouna
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The integration of electric vehicle charging stations into smart grids introduces new cyber-security vulnerabilities (e.g., within vehicle-to-charger and charger-to-grid communication). While recent research focuses on attack detection in single-source settings or specific data-model pairs for multi-source tasks, these approaches face limitations regarding generalizability and scalability, particularly when operating across heterogeneous sources. To solve this problem, we propose a novel Multiple-Source AutoEncoder-based Cyberattack Detection (MAC) model to transform heterogeneous inputs into a shared hyperspherical latent space with geometry-aware constraints and classify the cyberattacks. A compositional loss is introduced to balance representation encoding, geometric regularization, and supervised classification. Furthermore, we provide a formal domain adaptation risk analysis, proving that MAC’s compositional loss mathematically bounds the target risk. Our experiments on the multi-source CICEVSE2024 datasets show that MAC not only outperforms state-of-the-art methods under source heterogeneity and domain shift but also demonstrates robustness against targeted white-box adversarial attacks (e.g., PGD attacks).

title: Matrix Representation-Based Fault-Tolerant Control for Boolean Control Networks Subject to Bridging Fault
authors: Zhaoqi Liu, Dan Ma, Haitao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: Fault-tolerant control is an essential and intricate area within the control community, with broad applications across aerospace, chemical engineering, nuclear engineering and so on. Notably, the study of faulty Boolean control networks (BCNs) is of considerable importance in the field of genetic engineering. This paper investigates the fault-tolerant control of BCNs subject to bridging fault in the framework of semi-tensor product (STP) of matrices. Firstly, the concrete algebraic form of faulty BCNs is presented based on the fault transformation matrices. Secondly, several criteria are proposed for the fault detectability and fault tolerance of BCNs subject to bridging fault by constructing the fault combination matrices and indicative vectors. Based on the criteria, an algorithm is given for designing the fault-tolerant control. Finally, a Boolean model of faulty p53 pathways is used to validate the obtained results.

title: Highly Sensitive Ammonia Gas Sensors Based on Unidirectional Graphene Oxide Polymer Nanocomposite Thin Films
authors: Radhe Shyam, Bhola Nath Pal, Takaaki Manaka, Rajiv Prakash
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: In this work, we demonstrate a highly sensitive ammonia (NH3) gas sensor based on organic field-effect transistors (OFETs) with a polymer nanocomposite of graphene oxide (GO) and the conjugated polymer Poly[N-9’-heptadecanyl-2,7-carbazole-alt-5,5-(4’,7’-di-2-thienyl-2’,1’,3’-benzothiadiazole)] (PCDTBT) as the active semiconductor channel. The nanocomposite semiconductor was fabricated using the unidirectional floating film transfer method (UFTM) at the liquid-air interface, enabling the preparation of highly oriented thin films with good molecular ordering. Our approach leverages UFTM-induced molecular orientation in a GO–conjugated polymer nanocomposite to simultaneously achieve ultrafast response, high sensitivity at low NH3 concentrations, and exceptional operational robustness under harsh conditions. The resulting OFET sensors exhibit an outstanding sensing response of approximately 80% at a ammonia concentration of 20 ppm, with response and recovery times of 3 and 5 s, respectively, under ambient conditions (~30 °C). Moreover, the devices showed superior stability under harsh environmental conditions, maintaining their sensing performance at temperatures up to 100 oC and relative humidity of 85%. The sensors also maintained their performance over an extended period of 90 days. The enhanced orientation and molecular packing of the polymer films achieved by UFTM are responsible for the high sensitivity, reliability, and stability of the fabricated OFET-based ammonia sensors.

title: Optimization Design of Load-Independent Class-E$^{2}$ DC-DC Converter with Performance Visualization
authors: T. Osato, X. Wei, H. Sekiya
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes an analysis and design method for the load-independent class-E<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> DC-DC converter, which is composed of the load-independent class-E inverter and the load-independent class-E synchronous rectifier. By combining the newly derived analytical equations for the load-independent class-E synchronous rectifier with the analytical equation for the load-independent class-E inverter, the detailed circuit characteristics of the load-independent class-E<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> DC-DC converter are analyzed. From the analysis equations, the power output capability, which is the ratio of the power dissipated in the switching devices to the output power, is derived to visualize the circuit characteristics. The parameter space of the visualized data reveals the points where the power output capability is maximized concerning the input and output inductors, enabling the selection of low-cost switching devices. The quantitative agreement with experimental results confirms the validity of the analysis.

title: Electromagnetic Characterization of Electrically Thin Low-Loss Materials Through Uncalibrated Frequency-Domain Free-Space Measurements
authors: Ugur C. Hasar, Huseyin Korkmaz, Yunus Kaya
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: An extraction procedure is proposed for electrically thin (W < λ/4 where W is the sample thickness and λ is the wavelength) low-loss samples taking into account of an antenna error model. Different from previous models, the examined error model considers the more general case of networks for transmitting and receiving antennas with non-zero and non-identical reflection terms, arising due to impedance mismatches and ringing effects, which are hard to be eliminated for wideband frequency-domain measurements. An objective function free from all error network terms and reference plane transformation factors is first derived and then specialized for unique relative permittivity (εr) determination of electrically thin low-loss samples only. Our method produces multiple-solutions for thicker samples (W > λ/4). Minimum W and minimum and maximum εr values that our method is applicable are also analyzed. Sensitivity and uncertainty analyses were followed to examine the performance of our method. Free-space measurements of standard electrically thin low-loss Rogers 4350B and FR-4 samples were then performed over X-band (8.2 − 12.4 GHz) for validation of our method.

title: Movable Antenna Enhanced Cellular-Connected UAV Communication with Trajectory Planning
authors: Tianshi Ren, Xianchao Zhang, Wenyan Ma, Lipeng Zhu, Xiaozheng Gao, Rui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: The sixth-generation (6G) mobile communication systems are expected to provide seamless connectivity for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to support them in fulfilling various tasks. However, the line-of-sight (LoS)-dominated channels of cellular-connected UAVs expose them to severe co-channel interference from nearby base stations (BSs), which significantly degrades communication reliability. To address this challenge, this paper investigates a movable antenna (MA)-enhanced cellular-connected UAV communication system, where the additional spatial degrees of freedom (DoFs) offered by MAs are exploited for the interference-aware UAV trajectory planning. Specifically, we formulate an optimization problem to minimize the UAV mission completion time by jointly optimizing the UAV beamforming matrix, antenna position vector (APV), UAV trajectory, and UAV–BS association, subject to constraints on signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) requirements, UAV mobility, and MA mobility. To overcome the inherent challenges of the continuous-time formulation, we discretize both the flight region and trajectory of the UAV, thereby reformulating the problem into a tractable discrete optimization problem. A selective uniform cost search (SUCS) algorithm is then developed for UAV trajectory planning, where the feasibility of candidate grid points is evaluated by jointly optimizing beamforming, APV, and UAV–BS association to maximize the expected SINR. Simulation results show that, compared with benchmark schemes, the proposed MA-enhanced design significantly improves the expected SINR of cellular-connected UAVs along the optimized trajectory, thereby reducing UAV mission completion time while ensuring reliable communication links.

title: A Novel Multibeam Time-Division ISAC Approach for Accurate Sensing Parameter Estimation
authors: Tingting Zhang, Taejoon Kim, Sergiy A. Vorobyov, David J. Love
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: A novel multibeam time-division (TD) multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) approach is proposed to achieve a balanced tradeoff between sensing and communication functionalities and accurate sensing parameter estimation with a wide field-of-view. Firstly, the TD strategy is introduced to address the simultaneous high demands for sensing performance and communication rate. By allocating time resources between sensing and communications, this approach can reach a desired balance between them while avoiding spectrum and spatial interference, as well as competition in power allocation. Next, a new multibeam method is developed to achieve wide-area sensing for TD MIMO ISAC. Conventional multibeam methods typically rely on beam scanning for direction estimation, suffering from limited accuracy. Inspired by Doppler division multiple access (DDMA) approach, the proposed method divides the Doppler spectrum into more subbands, generating more beams than the number of transmit antenna elements using only phase modulation. Beyond enabling flexible control over the sensing coverage location through beam selection, the proposed method also improves parameter estimation accuracy by fully leveraging the inter-beam relationships, particularly for targets located at null directions. Specifically, for such targets, the proposed method achieves a significantly higher maximum unambiguous velocity, mitigating the velocity ambiguity inherent in conventional DDMA. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in enhancing both the performance tradeoff between sensing and communication, and the accuracy of sensing parameter estimation.

title: Development of an X-ray fluorescence spectral imaging instrument, based on Timepix3 counting chip, adapted for electronic waste sorting.
authors: Aurore Vazzoler, Adrien Stolidi, Thibault Rosier, Jean-Christophe P. Gabriel, Guillaume Amoyal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: With the increasing production of electronic devices, the recycling of Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) poses significant environmental, economic, and strategic challenges. However, technical difficulties in material recovery as well as the limited economic viability of existing recycling methods remain major obstacles to its widespread implementation. A promising approach to address these challenges is the concentration of chemical elements through inline sorting of electronic components. This approach could significantly improve both the efficiency and economic viability of WEEE recycling. The present work describes a sorting approach based on X-ray fluorescence spectral imaging. A prototype, integrating a Timepix3 pixelated detector with collimators designed for X-ray imaging has been developed, enabling the localization and discrimination of the different chemical elements on a sorting bench under both static and on-the-fly conditions. These results highlight the potential of X-ray fluorescence-based spectral imaging for WEEE sorting, while identifying critical parameters for its further development and optimization.

title: SSDFNet: A State Space Guided Network for Multimodal Building Extraction and Solar Modeling
authors: Yonghui Tan, Yumin Chen, Rui Zhu, Man Sing Wong, Xuequan Zhang, Tiantian Ran, Daoyong Zhu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate building extraction (BE) from multimodal remote sensing imagery is critical to urban management and sustainable development. Although the integration of optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data can provide complementary information, large-scale deployment is difficult due to blurring object boundaries, severe modality disparities, and expensive computing costs. To overcome these concerns, we proposed SSDFNet, a state space driven multimodal fusion framework for the Optical-SAR BE task. By involving VMamba into the backbone, SSDFNet captures long-term contextual dependencies with linear computing complexity, enabling efficient global modeling. To improve extraction quality, we proposed the Semantic Detail Fusion Module (SDFM), which injects high-level semantic cues into high-resolution features for finer structural delineation; the Dual Stream Fusion Module (DSFM), which adaptively aligns heterogeneous optical and SAR representations; and the Frequency Domain Enhancement Fusion Module (FDEFM), which improves edge sharpness and topological consistency through frequency decomposition and recombination. Extensive experiments on the DFC23 Track 2, MSAW, and INRIA datasets demonstrate the superiority of SSDFNet, achieving IoU scores of 85.31%, 80.70%, and 84.38%, respectively. In addition, we construct a 0.5 m resolution Optical-SAR rooftop dataset covering Singapore, on which SSDFNet attains an IoU of 80.41%. Furthermore, we quantitatively evaluate the effectiveness of the extracted building rooftops in a downstream solar photovoltaic (PV) potential estimation task by integrating rooftops with height information. The results demonstrate that improved geometric accuracy of building extraction directly leads to more reliable PV potential estimation, highlighting the practical significance of the proposed method for renewable energy planning.

title: MSP-MAE: Multi-Scale Perceptive Masked Autoencoder for Self-Supervised SAR Target Recognition
authors: Xunyang Wan, Xiaolin Du, Xiaolong Chen, Guolong Cui, Jibin Zheng, Mengjiao Tang, Wenming Ma, Kaige Hou, Jinwei Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The masked autoencoder (MAE) is a self-supervised learning model that reconstructs masked patches of an input image. Its pre-trained encoder significantly enhances downstream tasks, even surpassing supervised methods. This is particularly important for synthetic aperture radar (SAR) target recognition because labeled SAR images are scarce. Leveraging large amounts of unlabeled SAR data for network pre-training mitigates this challenge. Therefore, by combining the scale-fusion spatial-channel attention (SF-SCA) and multi-head self-attention (MHSA) modules, the multi-scale perceptive convolutional transformer (MSP-CT) is proposed as the backbone of the multi-scale perceptive MAE (MSP-MAE) for SAR target recognition tasks. The MHSA module exhibits locality and sparsity in shallow features, thereby introducing redundancy in global dependency modeling. In contrast, the SF-SCA module facilitates the interaction of localized and sparse patches through a sliding window. Therefore, the MSP-CT architecture employs alternating stacks of SF-SCA and MHSA blocks to balance the ability of the model to extract local and global features. The experimental results show that MSP-MAE pre-training can effectively improve the performance of the downstream task and can greatly alleviate the problem of insufficient labeled samples.

title: Linear-Complexity Unified Defense Against Deception Attacks in Distributed Economic Dispatch Using Cryptography and Machine Learning
authors: Md. Mainul Islam, Abdulrahman Takiddin, Muhammad Ismail, Hasan Kurban, Erchin Serpedin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Deception attacks, including false data injection, replay, and Byzantine manipulation, can corrupt broadcast price signals in distributed economic dispatch (DED), breaking fairness and increasing generation costs while remaining stealthy. Existing defenses are often attack-specific and rely on inter-supplier cross-verification, incurring O(n2) communication overhead, and many provide probabilistic detection that can cause false alarms or missed attacks. To deterministically detect multiple deception attacks with O(n) complexity, this paper proposes a unified security layer for DED based on threshold Schnorr signatures, ensuring data integrity, freshness, and global consistency of price broadcasts. Upon detection, the framework triggers a machine-learning-based robust post-attack recovery mechanism that estimates the true marginal price from historical demand–price correlations, enabling suppliers to continue near-optimal updates. Supplier privacy is preserved via lightweight pairwise masking that reveals only aggregate supply without sacrificing accuracy. The proposed scheme tolerates up to ⌊(n−1)/3⌋ malicious suppliers among n. Experiments on the IEEE 14-bus system quantify efficiency loss under representative attacks. Large-scale studies on the IEEE 118-bus system demonstrate that Byzantine manipulation can increase the system cost by up to 47%. In contrast, the proposed scheme limits the resulting efficiency loss to 0.3% by leveraging an offline-trained LightGBM predictor that achieves an R2 score of 0.985 on the test set.

title: Ka-Band Radar Interferometer High Power Amplifier for Surface Water Ocean and Topography Mission
authors: Matthew Keyawa, Michael Settember, Wayne Harvey, Igor Botvinnik, Jerry Dalton, Randy Ballat, Raul Perez, Michael Agnew, Michael Bodendorfer, Carl Spurgers, Nacer Chahat
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine
abstract: The Surface Water and Ocean Topography (SWOT) mission, launched in December 2022, aims to advance our understanding of ocean circulation and freshwater dynamics by providing the first global measurements of surface water elevation, stream slopes, and inundation extents in rivers, lakes, and floodplains. Utilizing a wide-swath altimeter and the Ka-Band Radar Interferometer (KaRIn), SWOT achieves unprecedented spatial resolution of 15-25 kilometers, significantly improving upon previous satellite capabilities. Central to the mission's success is the High Power Amplifier (HPA) in the KaRIn instrument, which amplifies radar chirp pulses to meet the stringent signal-to-noise ratio requirements for centimeter-level accuracy. This paper details the HPA's design, which consists of an Extended Interaction Klystron (EIK) and a High Voltage Power Supply (HVPS) to bias it. Concepts of EIK operation and details of the HVPS design are discussed in depth, with particular focus on the HVPS architecture and technical challenges faced with the design, which necessitated innovations in electronics design and packaging.

title: Scenario-Adaptive Device-Free Target Detection Using mmWave Signals
authors: Jingmiao Wu, Heteng Cao, Kai Sun, Wei Huang, Haijun Zhang, Victor C. M. Leung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Device-free target detection plays a crucial role in wireless sensing, enabling non-intrusive monitoring of human presence and activities, which is vital for healthcare, security, and smart home applications. Current research commonly adopts CFAR detection schemes to ensure stable false alarm probability under varying noise and clutter conditions. However, such schemes suffer from two major limitations: 1) employing a global false alarm rate often leads to degraded detection of distant or weak targets due to severe signal attenuation, and 2) employing a fixed thresholding mechanism in practical scenarios is vulnerable to strong interference and non-stationary noise, which distorts threshold estimation and results in unstable detection performance. To address these issues, we propose a scenario adaptive device-free target detection method using mmWave signals, which reconfigures CFAR detection through scenario adaptive false alarm rate setting and threshold estimation strategy. Specifically, we propose a sub-region adaptive false alarm rate setting algorithm, in which the optimal false alarm rate of each sub-region is determined through detection performance analysis. Meanwhile, we develop a reference window adaptive adjustment algorithm to ensure that threshold estimation is conducted within homogeneous window through homogeneity analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves the detection accuracy of multiple targets under arbitrary scenarios.

title: Fully Metallic Slant-Polarized Linear Antenna Array with Two-Way Elliptical Curvatures
authors: Hae Soo Eun, Jin Myeong Heo, Byeonggwi Mun, Changho Choi, Tae Jung Kim, Gangil Byun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper proposes a fully metallic slant-polarized linear antenna array with two-way elliptical curvatures for enhancing cross-polarization discrimination (XPD). The linear array comprises 18 elements, including 16 active elements and one dummy element at each side. Each element, designed as a tapered slot antenna rotated by 45◦, allows for wideband operation and signal reception of both vertical and horizontal polarizations. Unlike conventional slant linear arrays, the two-way elliptical curvatures are employed to enhance XPD degradation caused by field coupling between adjacent elements. Both elliptical curvatures in the outward and upward directions introduce tapered spacing between adjacent elements, thereby effectively suppressing cross-polarized radiation in the lower and higher frequency bands, respectively. To validate the proposed design, a fully metallic 18 × 1 array with two dummy elements is fabricated and measured. The proposed antenna array shows measured active VSWR < 2 over 12 GHz (6−18 GHz) with an average boresight gain of 3.61 dBi for the centermost element. The simulated average XPD of the proposed antenna array is improved by 10.61 dB compared to the conventional slant antenna array. Also, the array exhibits a beamforming capability of up to ±45◦, showing its effectiveness for high-power electronic warfare systems.

title: Stabilizing the Memristive Liu System Using PI-Based Sliding Mode Control: Implications for Autonomous Vehicle Dynamics
authors: Pallav, Rani Kumari
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In modern years, there has been a rising focus in the study of chaotic systems, predominantly in exploring methods to stabilize their unpredictable behavior. Chaos theory, known for its ability to describe the dynamics of highly complex and nonlinear systems, has emerged as a valuable tool in modeling real-world phenomena such as traffic flow where irregular and sudden changes are common. This work explores the stabilization of a memristive Liu system as a conceptual analog for modeling the complex and unpredictable dynamics of urban traffic flow. By applying PI-based sliding mode control (PI-SMC), the chaotic dynamics are stabilized in simulation, providing insights into robust control strategies for autonomous vehicles navigating uncertain and nonlinear traffic environments. This approach bridges chaos theory and adaptive vehicle control, offering a foundation for future advancement of intelligent transport systems competent of handling practical-world traffic complexities.

title: A Current Sensor Fault Tolerant Technique for Pole-Changing Induction Motors
authors: Shubham Dabral, Saptarshi Basak
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Pole-changing induction motors (PCIMs), have been considered as potential drives for electric vehicle (EV) applications due to their ability to function at a wide constant power speed range along with higher starting torque. Vector control techniques are commonly implemented for PCIM drives where accurate value of stator current is essential for proper control and decoupling of flux and torque. Therefore, identifying and mitigating current sensor faults in PCIMs offers significant challenges, particularly during pole transitions and under varying loads. This is because the motors exhibit a complex and dynamic behavior, which makes it difficult to detect and mitigate such sensor faults. A failure detection and mitigation approach for PCIMs is provided in this work. The technique is based on the unique features of the transformation matrices used in a PCIM drive. Through the provision of consistent performance throughout all operational modes, including pole-changing events, the suggested approach enhances the reliability and safety of electric vehicle drive systems. The MATLAB/Simulink software is utilized in order to carry out simulations and validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Ground Fault Detection in RES Integrated Power System Networks Using Clustering Algorithm
authors: Mandadhi Venkateswarlu, Rakesh Kumar Panda, N. Kumaresan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Accurate detection of Line-to-Ground (LG) faults in power systems integrated with Inverter Interfaced Sources (IISs) remains challenging due to their limited contribution to negative and zero-sequence currents. This paper proposes an unsupervised learning approach for LG fault detection using Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) within a time-series K-Means clustering framework. The WSCC 9-bus system is used as the testbed to validate the effectiveness of the algorithm. Various fault scenarios are simulated by varying source combinations and fault locations across different buses. Voltage and current phasors are recorded using Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs), while sequence components are extracted using sequence analyzers. To improve clustering performance, dimensionality reduction techniques—including Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) are employed to map the data into a reduced feature space. MATLAB is utilized for simulation and data generation, while Python is used to implement the unsupervised clustering algorithm. Simulation results confirm that the proposed DTW-based method effectively distinguishes between faulted and non-faulted conditions, even under scenarios involving high IIS penetration.

title: PiGraphNet: A Novel Physics-Informed Graph Neural Network for Remaining Useful Life Prediction of Lithium-Ion Batteries in Electric Vehicles
authors: Manoranjan Gandhudi, Gangadharan G. R
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of the Remaining Useful Life of lithium-ion batteries is essential for enhancing the performance, safety, and reliability of electric vehicles (EVs). Existing model-based approaches often require extensive parameter identification and lose accuracy under unmodeled operating conditions, whereas purely data-driven methods may overlook spatial-temporal dependencies and violate physical constraints. To address these limitations, we propose PiGraphNet, a novel Physics-Informed Graph Neural Network (GNN) that unifies graph-based spatial-temporal modeling with embedded battery degradation physics. PiGraphNet dynamically constructs battery interaction graphs from voltage, current, and temperature data to capture evolving inter-cell dependencies, while integrating electrochemical degradation laws, such as Peukert's law, into the message-passing mechanism for physically consistent predictions. Two domain-specific graph strategies—the Voltage-Current-Temperature (VCT) Graph and the Electrode Degradation Graph (EDG) enable targeted modeling of real-time electro-thermal coupling and long-term aging patterns. The model is trained with a composite loss function combining mean squared error with physics-informed constraints, ensuring both accuracy and interpretability. Experimental results on the NASA lithium-ion battery degradation dataset demonstrate that PiGraphNet outperforms LSTM, GRU, TCN, and physics-informed baselines, achieving an MSE of 251.60 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 93.29%.

title: Performance Analysis of LSVF Equivalent Output Feedback I-PD Control for Non-Minimum Phase Systems
authors: Rajeev Kumar, Jayati Dey, Tapas Kumar Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The advantages of the LSVF (Linear State Variable feedback) control are twofold because the response and loop performance of systems can be improved by virtue of arbitrary closed-loop pole-placement, and no transmission zero arises in the closed-loop transfer function due to controller dynamics. However, LSVF control needs measurement of all the state variables. This work establishes an equivalence between LSVF integral error feedback and output feedback-based I-PD control for second order systems. The performance of the developed IPD control is investigated for non-minimum phase Boost and Buck-Boost Converters, which inherently contain right half plane zeros. The I-PD control necessitates only the output voltage measurement for the converters, and achieves the same desirable performance as LSVF one. The controller is designed to achieve a satisfactory time domain response and robust stability. The I-PD controller parameters are designed with linear quadratic regulator (LQR) control technique to obtain the optimal solution. Gain margin (GM), Phase margin (PM), and Peak of the Sensitivity transfer function are employed here as measures of the robustness. The I-PD control is shown to maintain reference output voltage under varying load and set point conditions. Further, the LQR based I-PD control is shown to achieve a lesser settling time and overshoot as compared to two other output feedback control schemes, Lead-PI and Internal Model Control (IMC).

title: A Comparative Analysis of Various Deep Learning Models for State-of-Charge Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries
authors: Sourabh Joshi, Arghya Mitra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) have emerged as a key technology driving recent advancements in electrical and electronic systems. They are widely used in electric vehicles, renewable energy storage systems, portable electronic devices and even in orbiting satellites due to their high energy density, fast charging capabilities, long operational lifespan, and minimal maintenance requirements. Accurate estimation of the State-of-Charge (SoC) is obligatory to ensure the safe, reliable, and efficient performance of LIBs. Conventional approaches like Coulomb counting and Kalman filters are commonly used, but they often face issues in handling nonlinear battery behaviour, high-dimensional data, and complex operational conditions. In this study, we perform a comparative analysis of three deep learning architectures - Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-LSTM (CNN-LSTM) for datadriven SoC estimation using voltage, current, and temperature as input parameters. Using a publicly available Li-ion battery dataset, we preprocess the time-series data into fixed-length windows and train each model under identical conditions. The models are then evaluated using Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and the coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}})$</tex>. To ensure a statistically sound assessment, each model's performance was evaluated over 10 independent runs. The results show that all three architectures are capable of learning time-based patterns for SoC estimation, but their performance and stability vary significantly.

title: Analysis of Modified Leaky-LMS Algorithm Based Single-Stage Three-Phase Grid-Tied PV System Using Current Error Space Phasor Hysteresis Controller
authors: Urvashiben Rathod, Uday Patel, Siddharthsingh K. Chauhan, M. T. Shah, P. N. Tekwani
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this paper, analysis of Modified leaky-LMS (MLLMS) algorithm based single-stage three-phase grid-tied photovoltaic (SSGTPV) system that employs a current error space phasor (CESP) hysteresis controller (HC) is presented. The system employs an incremental conductance (InC) maximum power point tracking (MPPT) method for extraction of maximum power from the PV array. At the same time, it maintains the grid current stable at the point of common coupling (PCC). The CESP based HC uses two hexagonal hysteresis bands - one for selecting voltage vectors and the another to identify the corresponding sector. During each fundamental cycle, the CESP frequently crosses the fixed hexagonal boundary. It reaches the outer hysteresis band to enable precise sector identification. The CESP has advantages such as easy implementation, fast transient behavior, adjacent voltage vector switching. Additionally, it helps prevent random voltage vector switching by ensuring that only adjacent vectors within the same sector of the voltage vectors are selected. Analysis of MLLMS algorithm based SSGTPV system using CESP HC is evaluated through MATLAB simulation. The system is analyzed for steady-state, phaseout condition, and dynamic behavior for irradiance change.

title: Poisson-Based Modeling of Arc-Induced Spike Events for Low-Voltage Distribution Systems
authors: Ratnakar Nutenki, Haraprasad Badajena, Aurobinda Routray, Ashok Kumar Pradhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Arcing faults in low-voltage distribution systems exhibit stochastic and load-dependent temporal patterns that challenge conventional detection techniques. This paper presents a systematic analysis of arcing currents generated under various household appliances, including rheostat, kettle, fan, LED, and drill machines. A slip-difference-based method is employed to extract transient signatures from the current waveforms, revealing distinct spike trains associated with zero-current periods and rapid excursions. For each load, the inter-spike intervals are statistically analyzed and shown to follow an exponential distribution, validating a Poisson point process model for arc event occurrence. This physically interpretable and data-driven framework supports the development of adaptive, load-aware arc detection schemes suitable for smart circuit breakers and protective devices.

title: Control Strategy of Bidirectional AC-DC Converter using Modified RBFNN Algorithm to Improve PQ in Grid Connected PV-EV based Charger
authors: Sudhanshu Mittal, Manoj Badoni, Prakash Chittora, Gaurav Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: A grid connected EV with a PV charging system is proposed in this paper. An RBFNN is designed to control a bidirectional AC-DC Converter (BADC), and the system is designed for single-phase. The DC link of BADC is connected with a bidirectional DC-DC converter (BDDC) with a battery for smooth operation under charging and discharging conditions. This paper presents a fast charging control during dynamic load conditions in G2V as well as V2G modes. The solar Photovoltaic (SPV) injects power to support the grid, load and battery. Also, it provides an improved power quality by applying the proposed RBFNN controller. The simulation results of G2V and V2G are well presented and analyzed their dynamic waveforms.

title: A Synergistic Neural-Kalman Hybrid Approach for Real-Time State Estimation
authors: Tasadeek Hassan Dar, Satyavir Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study introduces a new framework for estimating the state of charge (SOC). It blends optimization, deep learning, and adaptive filtering techniques. First, the Cuckoo Search Algorithm finds the best settings for a secondorder RC circuit model. This ensures accurate battery characterization. The parameters obtained is fed to the Adaptive Extended Kalman Filter (AEKF) to produce training data for a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network. The BiLSTM model predicts SOC estimation errors. It integrates with a Squared Gain EKF (SGEKF) to adjust SOC estimates in real-time. We assess the BiLSTM-SGEKF method with real driving cycle data. We also compare it to traditional methods like AEKF and EKF. The results show that BiLSTMSGEKF has the lowest RMSE at 0.047 %, MAE at 0.041 %, and ME at 0.14%. It significantly outperforms AEKF and EKF. This hybrid approach adjusts easily to changing conditions. So, it's a great option for real-time SOC estimation.

title: Barrier Method Optimized MTPA and AFSTSMOCDSC-SRF-PLL Techniques for Sensorless Operation of SPV-Battery-Charger Integrated PMaSynRM Based LEV
authors: Sushant Kumar, Shailendra Kumar, Shashank Kurm
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced control framework for a solar photovoltaic (SPV) and battery-powered permanent magnet-assisted synchronous reluctance motor (PMaSynRM) drive, it is designed for the operation of light electric vehicles (LEVs) and battery charging topology. The SPV array is primarily utilized to extend the driving range by providing supplementary energy for battery charging, promoting cleaner transportation. Similarly, a bidirectional DC-DC converter (BDDC) is employed to recover energy during regenerative braking, further enhancing the range of LEV. DC bus voltage is regulated using a second-order sliding mode controller (SOMSC), ensuring system stability under load transients. To achieve optimal torque generation with minimized copper losses, the Maximum Torque Per Ampere (MTPA) strategy is implemented. The associated constrained optimization problem is effectively solved using the Barrier method. For accurate flux estimation under dynamic conditions, an improved active fluxbased supertwisting sliding mode observer (AFSTSMO) is proposed. A cascaded delayed signal cancellation (CDSC)-based filter is applied to eliminate harmonics and enable precise rotor position estimation from the active flux. Furthermore, a gridintegrated charger topology based on a three-phase inverter is incorporated, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{d}-\mathbf{q}$</tex> axis current regulation. The current controllers employ a second-order sliding mode control strategy for high accuracy and robustness. The integration of these advanced control and estimation techniques leads to distortionfree, dynamically stable, and energy-efficient operation of the PMaSynRM drive system in LEV applications.

title: Metaheuristic Optimization of UAV Mission Planning with a Novel Cost Function
authors: Persdeep Kaur, Naresh Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper addresses the complex problem of mission optimization by efficiently assigning tasks to a fleet of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), or drones, operating within an environment that includes predefined threat zones. The primary objective is to minimize the mission makespan, defined as the maximum path length traversed by any single drone, while ensuring complete task coverage, avoidance of threat areas, and efficient utilization of all available drones. The study evaluates the performance of seven widely recognized metaheuristic algorithms, namely, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), Cuckoo Search (CS), Special Forces Algorithm (SFA), Puma Optimizer (PO), Firefly Algorithm (FA), and Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO). Experimental results indicate that the PO achieved the lowest makespan, demonstrating superior solution quality, albeit with a longer execution time. Conversely, the ACO exhibited the fastest execution time, offering a balance between speed and solution quality. The findings provide valuable insights into the suitability of these metaheuristic algorithms for real-world drone mission planning in dynamic and constrained environments.

title: A 13-,19-, and 25-Level Switched-Capacitor Multilevel Inverter with Threefold Gain for Renewable Energy Applications
authors: Md Ashraf Ali Khan, M Saad Bin Arif, Faraz Ahmad, Abu Athar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel asymmetric multilevel inverter (AMLI) topology for photovoltaic (PV) applications, capable of producing 13-, 19-, and 25-level output voltages with a three-fold voltage gain. The proposed design employs 16 switches, 2 diodes, and 4 switched capacitors (SCs), ensuring reduced component count. The SCs achieve natural voltage self-balancing without sensors or complex control circuitry, thereby simplifying implementation. A three-DC-source selection strategy is introduced to enhance operational flexibility across different configurations. The topology is analyzed using sinusoidal pulse width modulation (SPWM) and evaluated for power loss and efficiency through thermal modeling in PLECS. Simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed AMLI under both steady-state and dynamic load conditions, maintaining an efficiency of 96.91 % at 1000 W and 94.41 % at 5 kW. Comparative analysis highlights its superior gain, low device-per-level ratio, and component count, making it a promising solution for high-performance renewable energy conversion systems.

title: A New Converter Topology to Suppress Common-Mode Voltage in Open End Pmsm Drives Based on Three-Phase Unfolder
authors: Brojogopal Majhi, Anandarup Das
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel dual T-type threelevel inverter based unfolder converter topology for Open-End three-phase surface-mounted permanent magnet synchronous motor(PMSM). Novel circuit topology consist of two T-type inverter in a novel fashion with Common-dc link has been proposed in this paper. Existing Unfolder topology possibly eliminate high frequency Common-Mode-Voltage(CMV), with dual T-type inverter the suppression of high frequency CMV concept further extended for Open-End(OE) Drives. Dual T-Type operates over a line-frequency cycle with sector-wise switching sequences to achieve nearly pure sine wave across the motor phase winding with simplified modulation strategy. Using the unfolded voltage waveform in the motor phase windings, the proposed topology inherently eliminate high frequency CMV and almost eliminate motor bearing current. Simulation results show that the proposed approach maintains low harmonic distortion, regulated motor currents, and stable torque production under both dynamic and steady-state conditions.

title: Directional Relaying using Fundamental Harmonic Sensitivity in Phase Currents
authors: Srijita Saha, Biman Kumar Saha Roy, Rajesh Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Estimation of the fault direction is an important feature of protective relays, primarily in power systems where the fault current direction is not fixed. Traditional directional relays are dependent on both current and voltage signals. Although it is a common practice, relying on voltage implies the usage of additional equipment such as potential transformers, making the system more expensive and difficult to maintain. In this paper, a novel method is proposed for directional relaying in which only the current signals at the relay point are used. The timederivative graphs of the fundamental harmonic current have been analyzed to design an algorithm to distinguish between forward and reverse faults in transmission systems. The proposed method is applicable for reverse power flow as well. The efficiency of the scheme has been checked through extensive simulations.

title: Gradient-Based Optimizer for Short-Term Hydrothermal Generation Scheduling
authors: Aman Kumar, Manoj Kumawat, Ujjwal Kumar Kalla, Ashok Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper explores the application of the Gradient-Based algorithm (GBO) to discover the best way to manage and schedule power generation from both hydro and thermal plants over a short period of time, involving the coordinated operation of one thermal unit and two hydro units over a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex>-hour period. The hydro unit operates with a fixed-head water reservoir. The main objective is to reduce the total fuel cost of thermal generators by utilizing the water volume of the reservoir to the maximum extent while satisfying system and operational constraints. The GBO is inspired by gradient-based search strategies to effectively navigate the solution search space and determine optimal hydrothermal power generation schedules for 24 hours. The algorithm balances exploration and exploitation capabilities to handle the complexity of the Short-term Hydro-Thermal Scheduling problem. Comparative results show that the GBO is capable of solving complex hydrothermal power generation problems, providing more economical solutions. Furthermore, statistical analysis confirms the robustness and consistency of the algorithm, demonstrating its suitability and efficiency in addressing the challenges associated with short-term hydrothermal generation scheduling
