title: Theileriosis Monitoring and Management System Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Dafuleya Thembalami, Monica Madyembwa, Tinoziva Hungwe, Emmanuel Nhedzi, Itai Nyerai, Jameson Madzana
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Theileriosis (January Disease), caused by Theileria parva, remains one of the most devastating tick-borne diseases affecting cattle in Zimbabwe. The government has intervened through strategies of dip-tank rehabilitation, acaricide distribution, and vaccination programs to control the outbreak. However, outbreaks continue to threaten livestock productivity. This study presented a comparative analysis of six supervised Machine learning algorithms for predicting the likelihood of Theileriosis occurrence. A dataset comprising of 2,500 epidemiological and climatic records was obtained from the Zimbabwe Veterinary Services Department. The algorithms were Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, Gradient Boosting, AdaBoost, Logistic Regression, and Naïve Bayes. The diverse model groups represented were tree-based, boosting, linear, margin-based, and probabilistic approaches. Algorithm performance was assessed using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix. The data were pre-processed to address missing values, normalise features, and encode categorical variables. Random Forest had the highest accuracy of 89.6 % in determining the likelihood of occurrence of the disease. The correlation analysis indicated that tick infestation strongly increased Theileriosis risk <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(r=0.6)$</tex>, acaricide treatment reduced it <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(r=-0.4)$</tex>, and host age <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(r=0.35)$</tex> and season <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(r=0.3)$</tex> moderately influenced disease occurrence. The findings revealed the ability of Random Forest in identifying Theileriosis-prone conditions under varying climatic and environmental scenarios. The study highlighted the potential of the model in enhancing livestock disease surveillance and targeted intervention planning. This study is relevant as it assists veterinary authorities and policymakers in strengthening disease control strategies and promoting sustainable livestock production.

title: KiF: Accelerating Low-Batch LLM Inference Using In-Flash KV Cache
authors: Haeeun Jung, Jaeyong Lee, Sanggu Lee, Huiwon Yun, Dongsuk Jeon, Nam Sung Kim, Jihong Kim
publish: IEEE Computer Architecture Letters
abstract: Deploying LLMs on edge devices is constrained by the memory-intensive, dynamic KV cache. While offloading the KV cache to SSDs expands capacity, it introduces severe bandwidth bottlenecks. Existing in-flash processing (IFP) solutions, optimized for static weights, fail to address the write-intensive nature and access pattern mismatches inherent to the KV cache. We propose KiF, a novel IFP solution that manages KV cache within flash chips, via reliable pseudo-SLC based write management and specialized in-flash GEMV accelerator. KiF delivers up to 4.1× higher throughput than existing SSD offloading solutions.

title: Cover 2
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Spectrum

title: Approximating Optimal Soft Repairs for Denial Constraints_supp1-3689023.pdf
authors: Dongjing Miao
publish: N/A
abstract: Computing optimal soft repairs of inconsistent relational databases with respect to functional dependencies (rel-fd-OSR) is known to be NP-hard. Constant-ratio approximation algorithms for rel-fd-OSR have been well studied. However, it is still open whether there is a constant ratio approximation algorithm for optimal soft repairs of inconsistent relational databases with respect to denial constraints (rel-dc-OSR). This paper studies an LP-based framework for approximating rel-dc-OSR. Based on this framework, we obtain a polynomial-time (λ + 1)-approximation algorithm for rel-dc-OSR where the constant λ is the maximum number of tuples involved in a violation with respect to the given set of DCs. Moreover, the approximation ratio reduces to 2 when λ = 2, thus immediately improving the state of the art 3-approximation algorithm for rel-fd-OSR.The application of our framework can also be extended to computing optimal soft repairs of inconsistent temporal databases with respect to DCs (temp-dc-OSR). We show that a (λ +1)-approximation algorithm that runs in polynomial time can be easily derived for temp-dc-OSR based on our framework and a polynomial-time λ-approximation can be obtained for temp-dc-OSR of all given DCs are hard constraints. Experiments on five real-world datasets (up to 100K tuples and tens of millions of violations) show that algorithms derived from our framework achieve up to 21% lower repair cost than existing methods while maintaining stable running times and improved discriminative power for query answering.

title: A Thermal Distribution Estimation-Based Thermal Stress Tolerant Control Method for Traction Inverters Under Fault Conditions
authors: Chao Yang, Yanghe Liu, Yansong Xu, Tao Peng, Chunhua Yang, Weihua Gui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: As one of effective ways to improve the reliability and extend the lifetime of power converters, thermal stress control (TSC) methods have drawn increasing attention. However, the existing TSC methods mainly consider the normal condition, but do not take into account the fault conditions, which will increase the risk of secondary faults. For this reason, a thermal distribution estimation-based thermal stress tolerant control method, which is based on two-tier thermal weighting (TTW) finite-control-set model predictive control, is proposed for traction inverters. Using the proposed method without additional sensors or hardware redundancy, better reliability of traction inverters can be achieved under both normal and open-circuit (OC) fault conditions. Firstly, the electrical model and thermal model of the system under fault conditions are built and analyzed. Secondly, the energy loss-based thermal distribution estimator (TDE) is proposed to evaluate the thermal stress distribution within traction inverters. Then, a TTW-based reconfigurable reward function and a TDE-based dynamic thermal weighting model are proposed to dynamically reconfigure the control strategies and their parameters, which is used to improve the thermal distribution of traction inverters under various OC fault conditions. Finally, the effectiveness and merits of the proposed method are verified and discussed.

title: Two-Stage Pseudorange Positioning Framework for eLoran Cold Start Resolution and Coverage Area Expansion
authors: Chaofan Tuo, Xiaoli Xi, Songnan Yang, Jiangfan Liu, Jinsheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The enhanced long-range navigation (eLoran) system serves as a reliable backup to the global navigation satellite system (GNSS) and holds strategic significance in critical applications. To address the limitations of the traditional eLoran system under cold start and incomplete chain conditions, this paper proposes a two-stage pseudorange positioning framework. First, this framework projects the Earth to a standard sphere, enabling the derivation of closed-form solutions based on measured pseudorange values. Second, a Newton-Raphson method is employed for iterative refinement, utilizing closed-form solutions as an initial position to obtain the accurate position. This framework requires less prior position information and supports flexible station combinations. Also, the framework is capable of positioning under cold start conditions and in areas beyond the coverage of the entire chain. Third, the framework is comprehensively validated through theoretical simulations and practical applications. The results demonstrate significant improvements in positioning availability and accuracy, compared to the traditional eLoran system that utilizes time difference of arrival (TDOA) and pseudorange-based local optimization methods. The proposed framework exhibits superior robustness and broader applicability, establishing a solid foundation for eLoran-based resilient navigation.

title: IEEE DataPort
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences

title: Vision-Guided Robotic System for Automatic Fish Quality Grading and Packaging
authors: Mohamed Lamine Mekhalfi, Saigopal Vasudevan, Jorge S. Calado, Anh Dong Le, Pablo Malvido Fresnillo, Jose Ferreira, Pedro Garcia, Carla Macieira, Paul Ian Chippendale, Ricardo Jardim-Gonçalves, Jose L. Martinez Lastra, Fabio Poiesi
publish: IEEE/CAA Journal of Automatica Sinica
abstract: Dear Editor, Automated fish grading and packaging, critical in the seafood industry, have not been simultaneously addressed by prior works using machine vision and robotics. This letter presents a novel proof-of-concept robotic vision system for automatic, size-based fish grading and packaging. Our system classifies frozen fish steaks into two size grades and localizes them on a conveyor belt for robotic pick-and-place via a specialized end-effector. Experiments achieved a grading accuracy of 87.6% and a robotic packaging rate of 87%, demonstrating the potential of vision-guided robotics for automated food quality inspection and handling.

title: OU-CA: A Face–Voice Multimodal Benchmark for Continuous User Authentication
authors: Xiang Li, Chi Xu, Yasushi Yagi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: Unlike conventional login authentication systems, which verify a user’s identity only once at the beginning of a session, continuous authentication enhances security by continuously verifying the user throughout the session. In this paper, we introduce a novel multimodal face-voice benchmark dataset for continuous user authentication. It contains data from 1,169 subjects, collected through web-based conversational games conducted between paired participants. During the games, participants’ facial videos and corresponding voice data were automatically recorded. Specifically, five cameras surrounding the display captured faces from different viewing angles to increase pose variations. Moreover, we provide baseline evaluations using state-of-the-art face and speaker recognition methods on our database. We further propose a novel disentanglement cluster network for continuous face authentication, achieving state-of-the-art performance on both our database and the widely used IJB-B and IJB-C datasets. We expect that our database will facilitate further research on continuous authentication and the development of more secure authentication systems.

title: Corrections to “Shaping the Profile and Dispersion of Waves Guided Between Spatiotemporally Dispersive, Electrically and Magnetically Conductive Metasurface Boundaries”
authors: Odysseas Tsilipakos, Thomas Koschny
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This erratum reports two corrections to our work [1]. They are mere typos and have no further implications on the reported simulation results or on the derived conclusions

title: DSA: A Dual Safety Approach for Test-Time Temporal Risk Removal of Video Generation_supp1-3689683.pdf
authors: Wu Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: With the rapid advancement of diffusion models, AI-generated content (AIGC), particularly in image and video generation, has achieved remarkable progress while simultaneously raising significant concerns about content safety within the community. Compared to image generation, the risks of generated videos remain largely overlooked and more challenging, as harmful content may be concealed at any temporal location or accumulate over time. Therefore, this paper proposes a novel Dual-Safety Approach (DSA) to remove temporal risks in generated videos while preserving their inherent quality at test time. The DSA solves the temporal risks from two aspects: the semantics in the text prompts and the temporal dynamics in the diffusion generation process. Specifically, we introduce a Semantic Adaptive Refinement module, which constructs a prompt-specific harmful subspace for targeted risk mitigation while preserving core semantics. Rather than treating video safety at a coarse, whole-video level, we introduce Temporal Dynamic Adaptive Optimization and Temporal Safety Guidance to dynamically constrain unsafe content over time while preserving temporal consistency. Moreover, to evaluate the effectiveness of our approach, we construct a novel Temporal Safety Benchmark for both Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video models. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DSA can significantly reduce the risks of videos generated by open-source models without degrading video quality and even surpassing commercial models. Additional evaluations on multiple benchmarks, including adversarial attack datasets, further confirm that DSA surpasses existing methods in both effectiveness and robustness. Code and data will be publicly available.

title:
Wafer-Scale Rapid Characterization of Barrier Layer Thickness Uniformity in Nb/Al-AlO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    /Nb Josephson Junctions Using Spectroscopic Ellipsometry
authors: Shiyu Wang, Xiaoyan Yang, Hua Jin, Linxian Ma, Hui Xie, Wei Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: In Nb/Al-AlO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>/Nb Josephson junctions, the AlO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> barrier layer plays a critical role in controlling the critical current density (J<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub>). This study presents an ellipsometry-based technique for characterizing the thickness distribution of the AlO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> barrier in junctions fabricated on 100-mm wafers. An in-situ Al capping layer (Al_cap) was introduced to protect the barrier during thickness analysis. Following dynamic oxidation in an Ar + 15 % O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> atmosphere at 72 Pa·min, spectroscopic ellipsometry measurements were performed on Al_cap covered samples. Fitting results show an average AlO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> barrier thickness of 1.17 nm, with a thickness non-uniformity of 2.6 % across the wafer. The method was further validated by transmission electron microscopy and J<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub> measurements. This ellipsometry-based technique, combined with in-situ barrier protection method, enables non-destructive barrier assessment, offering efficient and rapid process monitoring and oxidation optimization for superconducting integrated circuit fabrication.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning based Access Point Selection in Cell-free Massive MIMO with Behavior Cloning
authors: Dativa K. Tizikara, Daniel K. C. So, Zhiguo Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) utilizes multiple access points (APs) distributed over a large coverage area as distributed antennas to simultaneously serve multiple user equipments (UEs). In order to guarantee its scalability, it is necessary to optimally select AP-UE association clusters, which is a combinatorial problem with high complexity. Although deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has notably emerged as a potential solution, its application to AP-UE association is still challenging due to the difficulty in exploring such large action spaces and slow convergence. Existing work has tackled this challenge by heavily punishing invalid actions in the reward function, which does not scale well. In this paper, an alternative action space shaping approach is proposed, where a tailored mapping algorithm is added to the policy network, transforming invalid actions to feasible ones, thereby ensuring that training time is not wasted in discovering invalid actions. Additionally, we propose to incorporate behavior cloning (BC) with a heuristic teacher to speed up learning. We show that the proposed algorithm can improve the spectral and energy efficiency compared to existing schemes.

title: Decoupled Amplitude and Phase Recalibration for Tiny Object Detection in Remote Sensing
authors: Kai Zheng, Jintao Li, Feng Sun, Lin Li, Shui Yu, Yun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Tiny object detection in remote sensing imagery remains difficult because repeated convolutional downsampling progressively suppresses structurally informative frequency responses, which weakens fine-grained cues and degrades localization accuracy. To address this issue, we present a frequency-aware detection framework built on a YOLO11-style one-stage detector. We first analyze the frequency characteristics of tiny-object representation and show that backbone downsampling increasingly shifts features toward low-frequency responses, while phase plays a more direct role than amplitude alone in preserving tiny-object structure. Motivated by this observation, we propose a Frequency-Aware Amplitude and Phase Recalibration module, which transforms intermediate features into the frequency domain and performs frequency-conditioned decoupled modulation on amplitude and phase. This module provides a compact learnable mechanism for compensating the systematic spectral bias introduced by repeated downsampling. Lightweight context aggregation and head-level refinement are further incorporated as complementary components. Experiments on AI-TOD, DIOR, and USOD demonstrate consistent improvements over strong baselines, achieving 28.7% AP, 48.5% AP, and 36.10% AP, respectively. Further analysis and ablation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed frequency-aware design, especially the benefit of decoupled amplitude-phase compensation for tiny-object representation.

title: A Hybrid Solar-PV Grid-Tied Battery Energy Storage System for Modern EV Charging Applications
authors: Javed Akhtar Ansari, Saran Chaurasia, Shivam Kumar Yadav, Bhim Singh, Kuldeep Sahay
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Rising adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) necessitates establishing a robust charging infrastructure, which significantly enhances through integrating renewable energy sources (RES). Integrating RES with modern EV infrastructure is vital for achieving sustainability in transportation. This paper presents design and implementation of an integrated power system comprising a 2.5 MW grid connected photovoltaic (PV) array, a 1 MW/2 MWh battery energy storage system (BESS), and a 500 kW EV charging station connected to a 33 kV grid. Proposed system utilizes advanced control algorithms to optimize power flow between various components while ensuring grid stability and maximizing renewable energy utilization. Simulation results demonstrate system capability to maintain stable operation under various environmental and load conditions while achieving a peak grid power quality with THD <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<2 \%$</tex>. As demand for renewable energy sources increases, integration of PV solar plants, BESS, and EV charging infrastructure is essential for sustainable energy management. This study includes proposed system design, implementation, and performance evaluation, demonstrating its potential to reduce dependency on fossil fuels and lower greenhouse gas emissions.

title: Robust TDOA localization under NLOS condition using Convex-Concave Programming
authors: Soudeh Zarei, Hannan Lohrasbipeydeh
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Accurate source localization is essential for wireless networks, yet non-line-of-sight (NLOS) propagation can significantly degrade performance. Time-difference-of-arrival (TDOA)-based methods provide high accuracy without clock synchronization; however, their maximum likelihood formulations are nonconvex and sensitive to NLOS errors. In this letter, we propose a robust two-stage TDOA localization framework that explicitly accounts for bounded NLOS errors without assuming their statistical distribution. First, the maximum likelihood TDOA formulation with bounded NLOS errors is recast as a difference-of-convex program for efficient optimization. Next, a convex–concave procedure is applied to obtain a stationary solution, yielding a robust estimator for known NLOS error bounds. The framework is further extended to handle unknown NLOS errors under mixed LOS/NLOS conditions. Numerical results demonstrate accurate and robust localization, particularly at high signal-to-noise ratios.

title: ID-3SPs: Internally Disjoint 3-Steiner Paths Construction in Highly Scalable Data Center Networks with Applications
authors: Wen-Han Zhu, Rong-Xia Hao, Jinn-Shyong Yang, Jou-Ming Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: Cloud computing has become essential to various application services, requiring robust data center networks (DCNs) to support its infrastructure. This paper explores the establishment of a three-party proprietary communication channel and its related applications in a highly scalable data center network (HSDC). This novel research topic involves third-party authentication (TPA) in cloud applications. With the increasing demand for secure and reliable communication, we investigate the implementation of internally disjoint 3-Steiner paths (ID-3SPs), which facilitate message transmission with the involvement of a trusted third party. By explicitly constructing ID-3SPs and developing a definitive algorithm, we incorporate 3-path connectivity with TPA-related applications to enhance transmission efficiency caused by multi-paths while ensuring fault tolerance in the event of network component failures. Extensive experiments conducted in HSDC have shown that our findings significantly improve the reliability and efficiency of communication in DCNs, demonstrating their potential for practical applications that will benefit diverse fields, ranging from e-commerce to supply chains.

title: On the Minimum Distances of Some Families of BCH Codes
authors: Yaqi Chen, Hao Chen, Cunsheng Ding, Huimin Lao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: BCH codes form an important class of cyclic codes, which have applications in communication and data storage systems. Although the BCH bound provides a lower bound on the minimum distance of BCH codes, determining the true minimum distances of BCH codes is a very challenging problem. In this paper, we settle the minimum distances of a number of infinite families of narrow-sense BCH codes. By explicitly constructing the locator polynomials for minimum weight codewords, we obtain many families of primitive and non-primitive BCH codes with d = δ, where d is the minimum distance of a q-ary BCH code of length n, designed distance δ, and offset b, denoted by C(q,n,δ,b). For primitive BCH codes, we obtain infinite families of BCH codes over F3 and F4 satisfying d = δ, where δ 2 {5, 6, 7, 8}. Moreover, we construct several infinite families of q-ary BCH codes with d = δ, where 2 ≤ δ ≤ q −1. For δ = qt +1, we prove that the BCH code C(q,qm−1,qt+1,1) has d = δ for all m satisfying m ≡ 0 (mod pt), where p denotes the characteristic of Fq. In the paper by Ding et al., IEEE Trans. Inf. Theory 61(5): 2351-2356, it was conjectured that the minimum distance of C(q,qm−1,qt+1,1) is always equal to its Bose distance dB. Our result confirms this conjecture for the case m ≡ 0 (mod pt). For non-primitive BCH codes, we construct a family of BCH codes C(q, qp−1/λ ,p+1,1) with d = δ = p + 1, where p is an odd prime, q = pe with p ∤ e and λ | q − 1.

title: Frequency Regulation Enhancement of Multi-Area Hybrid Power System Considering CES Using 3DoF Based Cascaded Controller
authors: Shishu Kumar, Pranav Prakash Singh, Ruchi Agarwal, Ravi Shankar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Load frequency control, or LFC, is essential to maintain power grid stability in advance power systems by balancing load generation and demand. In each control region, the power system being studied combines three different types of energy (biogas, nuclear, and thermal) with a variety of known real-time nonlinearities, including boiler reheat dynamics, Governor Dead Band (GDB), and Generation Rate Constraint (GRC). With a cascade arrangement of a proportional-integral (PI) controller and a three-degree-of-freedom (3DoF) proportional-integral-derivative with a filter (PIDN), this work introduces a novel control structure that is designed for enhanced computing efficiency, robustness, and stability. A new modified Quasi-Opposition-based Crayfish Optimization Technique (QO-COT) technique has been employed to tune the gain of the proposed controllers. The edge of the CQO-COT is shown by comparing it with many popular meta-heuristic Technique. The controller makes it possible for coordinated, constraint-aware operation across interconnected areas by linearizing nonlinear dynamics. Eigen value analysis has also been used to implement the stability study of the proposed controller and power system. According to simulation results, the suggested controller outperforms all other traditional controllers in terms of much faster convergence, reduced frequency deviations, and improved steady-state precision, guaranteeing robust stability in large-scale, multi-region power grids.

title: Three-Level NPC Operation of TS-NPC with Capacitor Voltage Balancing Control
authors: Devwrat, Narendrababu A, Kishor Hirekar, Naveen Yalla
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: With only ten switches, the Ten- Switch Neutral Point Clamped (TS-NPC) retains a split DC-link design, which lowers the number of components and system cost in contrast to the typical NPC configuration, which needs twelve switches. The TS-NPC inverter emulates conventional three-level NPC characteristics in its DC-link operation. A modified space vector PWM (SVPWM) technique is introduced to overcome the problem and preserve near-identical performance. The proposed approach relies on a set of 19 voltage vectors to construct the desired reference vector. By dynamically modifying the duty ratios connected to small vector switching states, a PI controller can be integrated to balance the voltages of DC-link capacitors. Evaluations using simulation verify the feasibility and advantages of the suggested inverter topology and modulation methodology.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Network-based Prediction of Battery Charging Dynamics of Chaotic Energy Harvesters
authors: Rahash Nathasarma, Ankit Tiwari, Binoy Krishna Roy
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Chaotic energy harvesters offer a promising solution for powering micro-systems through their ambient vibrations. However, the chaotic dynamics lead to unpredictable behavior due to sensitive dependence on initial conditions, which makes it difficult to evaluate harvester performance and the prediction of battery charging behavior. In this paper, we propose a physics-informed neural network (PINN) approach to model a coupled mechanical-electrical system (for example, Van der Pol-Duffing oscillator coupled to a piezoelectric mechanism), as a vibration-based energy harvester. The proposed model accurately predicts the voltage across the battery charging dynamics under chaotic base excitation as compared to traditional neural networks. This work demonstrates the effectiveness of PINNs to offer a physics-consistent, generalizable, and data-efficient modeling framework for next-generation energy harvesting systems.

title: Model Predictive Control based Optimal Charging for the Health Improvement in Lithium-ion Batteries
authors: Swastik Acharya, Arijit Guha, Asim Kumar Naskar, Bikky Routh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs), consumer electronics, and other battery-operated applications extensively use lithium-ion batteries (LiBs). A constant current constant voltage (CCCV) charging profile is used in the majority of these applications as a standard charging procedure. However, this approach falls short in addressing LiB's state of health (SoH), which is crucial for extending its lifespan. During the CC charging phase, the battery temperature may increase manifold beyond the safety levels if the charging current is relatively high. It may eventually deteriorate LiB's overall SoH by accelerating the side reactions. In order to address the issues with CCCV, this paper proposes a model predictive control (MPC) based optimal charging procedure to improve the LiB's SoH by monitoring its temperature rise and extending its overall runtime. Compared to CCCV charging, MPC-based charging reduces the capacity loss and solid-electrolyte interphase (SEI) layer's resistance by 18.25% and 2.83%, respectively, which leads to an improvement of 2.34% in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{S o H}$</tex> value.

title: False Data Injection Attack Resilient LFC for Two Area Power System with Optimized Hybrid Fractional Order PID Controller
authors: Anukriti Sharma, Bharat Singh Rajpurohit
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: An advanced LFC system for a modern power system having conventional plants with RESs. High penetration of RESs, characterized by low rotational mass, leads to a significant reduction of the overall inertia of the power system. It causes severe frequency deviation in the power grid. To address this challenge, virtual inertia control is integrated with an energy storage device using a derivative control technique, which effectively emulates the inertia response and enhances frequency regulation. Modern power systems also relies on communication and internet technology for flexible control and operation, but they become susceptible to cyber threats. This paper addresses the false data injection (FDI) attack on frequency regulation. To mitigate the adverse effects of cyber threats, an optimized hybrid fractional-order PID controller (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PI}^{\lambda}-\left(1+\text{PD}^{\mu} \mathrm{F}\right)$</tex>) has been proposed. The proposed technique combines the advantages of fractional calculus and predictive control to ensure robust dynamic performance under both normal and cyber compromised scenarios. The simulation result shows the superiority of the proposed control technique in terms of frequency response, as it minimizes frequency overshoot and tie-line power variation while improving response time. Therefore, the proposed controller provides virtual inertia support and makes the modern interconnected power system cyber resilient.

title: Anomaly-Resilient Control for Automatic Generation Control Systems: Detection and Mitigation of Data Integrity Attacks
authors: Puli Tarun, G. Jawahar Sagar, Harshitha Puli, Sariki Murali, Lokesh Mani Sai Balaji Loya, Tarkeshwar Matho
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The increasing reliance on cyber-physical infrastructure in modern power grids has exposed key components, such as Automatic Generation Control (AGC) systems, to sophisticated cyber threats particularly false data injection attacks that can compromise grid stability and economic operations. While deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has emerged as a promising tool for adaptive, attack-resilient control, it often lacks robust mechanisms for actively detecting and mitigating deceptive inputs in real time. This paper proposes an enhanced DRLbased AGC controller integrated with a novel attack-resilient control framework that combines dynamic anomaly detection and adaptive control reconfiguration. The hybrid architecture enables proactive identification of integrity violations and realtime adjustments to control policies, safeguarding frequency regulation and system reliability during adversarial events. Simulation results confirm that the framework preserves acceptable frequency bounds, minimizes disruption to market operations, and offers a substantial advancement over conventional defenses. By synergizing intelligent learning with systemic resilience, this work charts a new path toward secure and stable smart grid control.

title: Efficient Probabilistic Load Flow Utilising Latin Hypercube with Translational Propagation Algorithm
authors: Neeraj Gupta, B Rajanarayan Prusty
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: For the purpose of conducting an objective analysis of power systems that have a high grid parity of renewable energy sources, stochastic approaches and well-established deterministic methodologies are required. This work presents the Translational Propagation method as a novel approach to achieving optimal or nearly optimal Latin hypercube designs without resorting to formal optimisation. The algorithm is introduced as a novel way to achieve these designs. A minimal amount of computing labour is required for the method, and the results are obtained practically immediately. Extensive simulations are carried out on a modified IEEE-118 bus test system with distributed generation and unknown loads in order to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Speed Sensorless Vector Controlled PMSM Drive Using Cascaded IMC Scheme
authors: Anurag Savarn, Vimlesh Verma, Md Nishat Anwar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study focuses on implementing a model reference adaptive system (MRAS) based speed estimator for sensorless speed control of permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSM) with cascaded internal model controllers(IMC) for controlling speed and current. The proposed cascaded control strategy based on IMC is robust with disturbance rejection, good tracking capability and has simple implementation. A short analysis of IMC method for design of cascaded speed and current loop control is discussed. To construct the MRAS, the instantaneous and steady state fictional quantity (Y) is employed. The suggested speed estimation technique is stable in all four quadrants of operation robust, and has no dependency on integral and differential terms. Speed estimator functions well under a wide range of load torque and speed variations. The suggested work is modelled and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink and its efficacy has been validated from experimental demonstration on a laboratory based hardware setup of PMSM drive using d-SPACE 1104.

title: AI-Driven Automated Design Generator for Smart Mechanical Components in Power and Energy Systems
authors: Tanvi R. Patil, Sanika P. Kamble, Vishvajeet V. Sutar, Poorva D. More, Rohit V. Chitari, Sadhana D. Shendage
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Manual CAD model creation for mechanical components in power and energy systems can take 30 to 60 minutes per part and requires expert skill, creating a major bottleneck for rapid prototyping and iterative design. This paper introduces an AI-driven CAD generation framework that reduces design cycle time by over 99% in less time while maintaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 90 {\%}$</tex> geometric accuracy. This proposed system, where users select components (e.g., ball bearings, flanges, pulleys, hex nuts, shafts) and enter five key parameters as input, enables the proposed ANN model to predict precise geometry and automatically generate DXF files at real-time speeds. This centralized ANN Model Manager ensures obedience with industry constraints, provides explainable parameter sensitivity, and falls back to rulebased formulas when needed. Experimental validation shows a consistent up to 92% prediction accuracy across all component types, speeding up the process versus manual workflows, which is the need of Industry 5.0. By intensely dropping time complexity and removing manual CAD hurdles, our solution empowers engineers in smart grid and industrial applications to accelerate design, optimization, and deployment.

title: A Hybrid STL-LSTM Framework for Data-Driven EV Load Forecasting
authors: Arpana Singh, Poonam Dhaka
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Electric vehicle load forecasting has a crucial role in optimizing energy management and enriching the efficiency of electric vehicle charging stations. In this research, a comprehensive approach for load forecasting using seasonal trend decomposition using Loess and Long short-term memory (LSTM) methods is proposed. First, the dataset is preprocessed by joining date and timedelta data to make a time series dataset. Next, seasonal-trend decomposition is used to find seasonality, trend, and residuals. The LSTM model is then used to forecast by taking advantage of neural networks' innate sequential learning capabilities to forecast electric vehicle load. To further check the efficacy of the proposed method, additional models such as LSTM, Random Forest and Bidirectional-LSTM are employed. Statistical quantities such as the root mean squared error, the mean absolute error, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex>-squared are used to evaluate the forecasting models. The comparison between the performance of proposed methods, along with the extended models, provides the efficacy of the proposed approach by having a minimum root mean square error and mean absolute error, i.e., 0.147 and 0.11, respectively and aims to present the versatility and efficacy of the proposed forecasting approach for the load patterns.

title: Probabilistic Flexibility Enhancement in Active Distribution Networks Under Operational Constraints
authors: T Koteswara Rao, Ankur Singh Rana
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a methodology for assessing the probabilistic flexibility of active distribution networks (ADNs) through coordination of non-dispatchable resources such as, photovoltaic (PV), wind and dispatchable resources like diesel generators (DGs), and battery energy storage systems (BESS). A 33-bus distribution system is modeled using the Pandapower framework, incorporating realistic load, PV, and wind profiles along with operational uncertainties using Monte Carlo simulations. The network operation in consideration with different DER combinations are analyzed to derive feasibility gain and power flexibility metrics. Convex hulls are constructed to visualize and compare the flexibility regions for different DER configurations. Results demonstrate that full coordination of DERs and BESS significantly improves operational feasibility and enhances flexibility at the grid interface, particularly during critical hours. The proposed approach provides a scalable and modular framework for utilities to quantify and visualize flexibility and enhances grid supportive DER operation.

title: A Taxonomy of Data Inconsistencies and their Operational Impact in Automatic Train Operation
authors: Pardeep, Divakar Singh, Meenakshi Chaudhary, Sanjay Motia
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Automatic Train Operation (ATO) systems depend on the seamless integration of real-time data from multiple subsystems including Electronic Interlocking (EI), trackside sensors, communication networks, and onboard control units to ensure safety and operational efficiency. However, inconsistencies arising from timing drift, sensor conflicts, communication errors, or human overrides can disrupt these systems, leading to degraded performance or safety risks. While prior research has focused on hardware faults and anomaly detection, the specific issue of data inconsistency remains underexplored. This paper addresses that gap by proposing a comprehensive taxonomy of data inconsistencies in ATO environments, developed through a combination of literature review, simulation based fault injection, and field validation. We categorize inconsistencies into seven distinct types and analyze their operational impact across safety, efficiency, maintenance, and compliance domains. Our findings highlight the need for inconsistency-aware diagnostics, simulation frameworks, and real-time monitoring tools to improve the resilience of nextgeneration ATO systems.

title: Assessment of Control Strategies and Switching Technologies in DC-DC Power Conversion
authors: Abhishek Raj, Anubrata Dey, Sohom Chakrabarty
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper gives an easy-to-understand summary of DC-DC converters, mainly focusing on how they are controlled and the ways they are switched on and off. DC-DC converters are found in many systems like electric vehicles, solar panels, airplanes, and microgrids. Their job is to change the voltage to the level that the device or system needs. The main goal is to keep the system working efficiently, safely, and smoothly. The paper talks about different control methods such as PID control, sliding mode, fuzzy logic, and model predictive control. It also explains common switching techniques like hard switching, soft switching, and phase-shift modulation. These methods play a big role in how well the system uses energy and how stable it runs. In the end, the paper highlights how digital and AI-based control technologies are becoming more important and are expected to shape the future of DC-DC converter systems.

title: In-Situ State of Health Estimation of Electric Vehicle Batteries Using Partial Driving Data
authors: Jayabrata Maity, Munmun Khanra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Precise State of Health(SOH) estimation of Electric Vehicle(EV) batteries requires precise laboratory experimentation. However, removing the battery pack from the EV for SOH estimation is difficult from the practical point of view. Thus, in-situ SOH estimation of EV battery is necessary. This paper proposes a practical in-situ SOH estimation framework that utilizes only a small segment (5%) of voltage and current data collected during actual EV driving. A total of seventeen timedomain features are extracted from the batteries in two publicly available datasets and the correlation between features and SOH is analyzed. The standard deviation of voltage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{s t d}$</tex> is identified as the most dominant health indicator in both the datasets. Using selected dominant features, multiple machine learning models are trained and tested. Linear Regression demonstrates strong performance in Dataset 1, achieving Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) below 0.85% in most cases, while Support Vector Machine shows the best results in Dataset 2. The proposed method does not require additional sensors, making it highly suitable for real-time implementation in resource-constrained EV systems. The results show that SOH estimation can be achieved using simple models and partial discharge data, enabling practical in-situ implementation.

title: Damping Ratio and PI Integrator Gain Estimation from Generation-Loss Disturbances in the Indian Electricity Grid
authors: Debanjan Mukherjee, Karuna Kalita, Subhash Kumar, Amaresh Mallick, Samar Chandra De
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: India aims to meet 50% of its electricity demand from renewables by 2030. Higher renewable penetration will increase intermittency, raising the risk of generation-demand imbalances and large grid disturbances. This study therefore, estimates damping ratios for generation-loss grid disturbances exceeding 500 MW in the Indian Electricity Grid between December 2020 and September 2022, using 1-second PMU frequency data to analyze frequency response characteristics. The estimation applies a proportional–integrator (PI) control framework to represent primary and secondary frequency control, enabling calculation of PI integrator gains for each disturbance. The novelty lies in a methodology that jointly derives damping ratios, PI integrator gains, and power numbers thereby, quantifying the system frequency stiffness and control responsiveness. Power numbers, in particular, can inform grid operators in scheduling and planning Frequency Control Ancillary Services (FCAS). By capturing temporal and regional variations, the methodology provides insights into how reduced inertia from replacing synchronous generators with inverter-based renewables will affect stability margins. The results offer a replicable framework for tracking trends in frequency control capability, supporting both Indian and international operators in designing resilient, flexible grid operations under high renewable integration. These actionable metrics can directly guide electricity grid expansion, modernization, and reserve allocation strategies.

title: Electrolyser-Fuel Cell-PV-Battery System Connected Grid System with GMBZ Adaptive Filtering for Hybrid EV Charging Infrastructure
authors: Tanu Prasad, Shailendra Kumar, Shashank Kurm
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Production of hydrogen plays an important role in advanced sustainable energy solutions, especially for charging infrastructure in hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs). In this paper, a PV array, a battery, and a fuel cell are connected with an electrolyser to enhance the hydrogen production and provide power during peak load demand, while also supporting the grid. This paper proposes a Generalized Modified Blake-Zisserman (GMBZ) adaptive filter algorithm to optimize system performance within an advanced framework for hydrogen production and energy management. The GMBZ adaptive filter algorithm enhances energy distribution by mitigating power fluctuations, compensating for reactive power, achieving efficient voltage regulation, maximizing power flow, and reducing harmonic content, all while operating at a unity power factor. The addition of a fuel cell ensures a continuous power supply and stability to the system, especially under dynamic load conditions such as variable solar irradiance, battery energy storage (BES) transition, and load dynamics. The proposed architecture is simulated through MATLAB/Simulink, demonstrating continuous hydrogen production with reduced power loss and enhanced energy utilization. The total harmonic distortion (THD) of the grid current is under 5%, which complies with the IEEE 519-2022 grid code.

title: A Novel Passive Islanding Detection Technique with Hybrid DER Technologies
authors: Ankit Mishra, Abhinesh Kumar Sah, Rudrakant Tripathi, S.R. Mohanty, M.K. Verma, A. Maulik
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Unintentional islanding is a significant concern in modern power distribution networks, especially with the increasing integration of renewable energy sources. This paper presents a novel passive islanding detection approach for an inverter-interfaced distributed energy resource comprising photovoltaic (PV) and fuel cell (FC) units. The PV and FC units operate in maximum power point tracking (MPPT) mode, and the voltage source converter (VSC) maintains the DC link voltage. The proposed passive islanding detection method involves obtaining the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex> components of the voltage at the point of common coupling (PCC), followed by computation of the root mean square (RMS) values using a moving window of one cycle. We have proposed a novel index called the square of the inverse of the product of the RMS of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex> components of the PCC voltage (SIPABCV) to detect an islanding phenomenon. The proposed index captures subtle variations in voltage behaviour during islanding events, and can detect islanding even under near-zero power-matched (NZPM) conditions. Therefore, the non-detection zone (NDZ) is reduced compared to other passive islanding detection techniques reported in the literature. The proposed approach can also successfully discriminate between islanding and non-islanding events. The thresholds for reliable classification are established through extensive simulation studies performed in MATLAB R2023a/SIMULINK. The proposed approach is validated by time-domain simulation results on a test system.

title: Dynamic Viscoelastic Modeling Analysis & Nonlinear Control of Smart Material Actuator for Rehabilitation Purposes
authors: Pranjal Sharma, Krishnan Chemmangat
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Dielectric Elastomer Actuators (DEAs) manifest promising characteristics for soft robotic applications due to their high energy density, compliance, and biomimetic behavior. However, their inherent nonlinear properties present significant challenges for precision control. This paper presents an analysis of a dynamic viscoelastic model for DEAs that apprehends the time-dependent mechanical response under electrical excitation. Based on the model, a control strategy is developed by constructing an inverse plant model combined with a proportional-integral (PI) feedback controller. The scheme is evaluated through torque tracking simulations under scenarios involving disturbances and input offsets. Results demonstrate significant improvements in tracking performance. The proposed framework provides a physics-informed basis for accurate and adaptive DEA control, enabling reliable integration into wearable and assistive robotic systems.

title: A Remote Current Injection Based Islanding Detection Technique for Microgrid
authors: Shyamal Das, Pankaj Dilip Achlerkar, Subhransu Ranjan Samantaray
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Islanding detection is an indispensable requirement for the reliable and stable operation of microgrids. Conventional passive methods based on voltage or frequency monitoring often fail to detect islanding events under near-zero power mismatch conditions. Conversely, active methods can compromise power quality due to continuous perturbation injection. Remote islanding detection techniques, which rely on communication infrastructure to detect and relay islanding events, offer high accuracy but at increased complexity and cost. In this work, a remote current-injection-based islanding detection scheme is proposed. The method is applicable to both single-source and multi-source microgrids, regardless of the source type. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach can accurately detect islanding events even under zero power mismatch conditions and effectively eliminates the non-detection zone (NDZ). Furthermore, this method provides a fast detection response, demonstrating its suitability for real-time microgrid applications.

title: Dynamic State of Charge Estimation of Lithium-Ion Batteries Using a Model Switching Approach
authors: Rajkumar Roy, Nilanjan Patra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries meet the essential energy requirements of hybrid and Electric Vehicles. The State of Charge (SoC) is a crucial indicator of battery life and overall safety. Since SoC cannot be measured directly, estimators are typically employed. This paper examines the conventional Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) applied to a well-accepted electrical equivalent circuit of a lithium-based battery, and introduces an adaptive Interacting Multiple Model (IMM) state estimator to handle nonlinearities without sacrificing accuracy. The Monte Carlo simulation results are included to show the efficacy of the proposed estimation technique. The results indicate that the proposed estimator outperforms conventional estimators.

title: Experimental Validation of an Intelligent Dual-Battery Hybrid EV System with PEMFC Range Extension and ML-Based Control Architecture
authors: Navinkumar TM, C. Bharatiraja
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell (PEMFC) is a clean and efficient energy source that helps improve the power and range of battery electric vehicles (BEVs), especially in applications where purely battery-powered systems are not suitable. This work presents a low-voltage energy storage system consisting of a 5kW PEMFC stack (implemented by a PEMFC emulator) and two 60 V, 50 Ah low-voltage (LFP) sources in a twobank configuration. An intelligent switching policy causes Battery 1 (B1) to drive the motor until it drops below 20%, then switches to Battery 2 (B2), while B1 is regenerated by the PEMFC. Charging continues until the (State of Charge) SOC is 80%, and then the batteries switch roles. A DC-DC connector connects the PEMFC to the heat sink, operating at a power range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1600-1800 \mathrm{W}$</tex> to provide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25 A$</tex> of heat, reducing thermal stress and increasing the life of the heat sink. In such a scenario, B1 and B2 travel approximately 36.36 km on the remaining battery power. The PEMFC charges the batteries an average of 2.4 times in a single cycle and adds a range of 50.4 km, making the average range 86.8 km. Tests based on the PEMFC covered 78.6 km, confirming its off-road capability. A Decision Tree-based machine learning model with a rule of thumb of 98% accuracy predicts battery replacement, implemented on an STM32 microchip with an FPGAbased motion controller. The system provides a flexible, intelligent solution for increasing the range of electric vehicles in cities.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Model for Digit Recognition in Electric Vehicle
authors: Fareed Singh Kang, Neeraj Kumar, Ravinder Kumar, Rajesh Kumar Chaudhary
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Hybrid quantum-classical machine learning uses the computational benefits of quantum systems while maintaining the reliability of classical deep learning. This study presents a hybrid model for multiclass image classification, combining a pre-trained ResNet-18 with a quantum-inspired feature transformation layer. A two-qubit parameterized quantum circuit utilizing Qiskit's Aer simulator improves feature extraction through entanglement and superposition. A learnable parameter <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> enhances the integration of quantum and classical characteristics throughout the training process. Adaptive learning rate scheduling and gradient clipping enhance training stability. The model attains a validation accuracy of 97.89 % and an AUC value approaching 1.00 across most classes, indicating robust classification efficacy. The model is further developed for electric vehicle (EV) number plate classification, wherein digit identification is essential. The MNIST-trained architecture effectively generalizes to number plate scenarios, successfully recognizing digits across varying lighting conditions and orientations. Beyond recognition, the proposed strategy also enables vehicle congestion analysis by estimating traffic density based on identified EVs. These findings validate the feasibility of using quantum layers in deep learning, facilitating future studies into complex datasets and advanced quantum representations.

title: Graph-Temporal Actor-Critic: A Hybrid Architecture for UAV Navigation in Dynamic Environments
authors: Selvam Essaky, Surya Raj M, Palaniswamy Shanmugam, Kaushal Kishore
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Autonomous navigation for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) in dynamic, partially observable environments presents a significant challenge requiring sophisticated spatiotemporal reasoning. To address this, we propose the Graph-Temporal Actor-Critic (GTAC) framework, a novel reinforcement learning approach that integrates Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for spatial relationship modeling with transformer encoders for temporal sequence processing. This hybrid architecture enables robust navigation amidst moving obstacles by processing local observations as relational graphs and maintaining temporal context across time steps. Trained using a structured curriculum learning paradigm that progressively increases environmental complexity, GTAC achieves a 92.1% success rate in complex scenarios, decisively outperforming CNNLSTM baselines by 27.1%. Extensive experiments demonstrate superior generalization to unseen obstacle configurations, with a 4.3% collision rate compared to 28.9% for conventional approaches. The fusion of spatial and temporal processing pathways enables sample-efficient learning, requiring 40% fewer training episodes than benchmark methods to reach peak performance.

title: A Lightweight Collaborative Mobile Agent Architecture for Cybersecurity Testing in Industrial Control Systems
