title: DSA: A Dual Safety Approach for Test-Time Temporal Risk Removal of Video Generation_supp2-3689683.mp4
authors: Wu Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: With the rapid advancement of diffusion models, AI-generated content (AIGC), particularly in image and video generation, has achieved remarkable progress while simultaneously raising significant concerns about content safety within the community. Compared to image generation, the risks of generated videos remain largely overlooked and more challenging, as harmful content may be concealed at any temporal location or accumulate over time. Therefore, this paper proposes a novel Dual-Safety Approach (DSA) to remove temporal risks in generated videos while preserving their inherent quality at test time. The DSA solves the temporal risks from two aspects: the semantics in the text prompts and the temporal dynamics in the diffusion generation process. Specifically, we introduce a Semantic Adaptive Refinement module, which constructs a prompt-specific harmful subspace for targeted risk mitigation while preserving core semantics. Rather than treating video safety at a coarse, whole-video level, we introduce Temporal Dynamic Adaptive Optimization and Temporal Safety Guidance to dynamically constrain unsafe content over time while preserving temporal consistency. Moreover, to evaluate the effectiveness of our approach, we construct a novel Temporal Safety Benchmark for both Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video models. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DSA can significantly reduce the risks of videos generated by open-source models without degrading video quality and even surpassing commercial models. Additional evaluations on multiple benchmarks, including adversarial attack datasets, further confirm that DSA surpasses existing methods in both effectiveness and robustness. Code and data will be publicly available.

title: Adaptive Optical Image Enhancement under Transformer Oil via Bright Channel and Statistical Feature Distributions_supp1-3689571.pdf
authors: Hu Qiang
publish: N/A
abstract: Optical images serve as a critical source of information for fault observation under transformer oil. However, the complex and changeable optical imaging conditions lead to various degrees of visual degradation, including color distortion, low contrast, and blurred details, which limits its application in high-level visual tasks. To address the complex degradation problems in images under transformer oil, we propose a multi-source information fusion method for image enhancement based on the bright channel prior of transformer components and statistical feature distribution of image under transformer oil. This method employs different enhancement strategies for color, contrast, and detail to generate three complementary preprocessed images, which are subsequently fused at multiple scales using Laplacian-Gaussian pyramids. For color correction, we introduce a bright channel color compensation algorithm, which can adaptively compensate for the severely attenuated channels based on the degradation level of each channel in the degraded image. Furthermore, an entropy-constrained adaptive gray world algorithm is designed for precise color correction, which dynamically corrects the color information based on the distribution characteristics of entropy in the degraded image. For contrast enhancement, to avoid artifacts, we perform histogram equalization on the Y component in the YUV color space. For detail enhancement, based on the gradient distribution of the color-corrected image, we propose an adaptive nonlinear stretching guided filtering algorithm, which suppresses noise while dynamically enhancing image details. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves image quality. Additionally, the results of object detection in this environment further validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm for high-level visual tasks. The dataset is available at https://drive.google.com/dataset.

title: A Surface-Wave Self-Cancelling Method and Its Application to Vehicle Glass Antenna
authors: Yang Jiao, Zhi Xing Chen, Bai Hao Zhang, Shao Zhang, Jia Jia Chen, Gu Ying Deng, Huafeng Su, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Antenna designed on glass usually suffers from the interference of surface wave, severely degrading the antenna’s radiation performance. To address this issue, the electromagnetic characteristics of the surface wave in glass are studied in depth. The mathematical conditions of surface wave excitation and cancellation are analyzed and deduced. They serve as principles for manipulating surface waves and designing antennas. Based on these principles, it can be proved that the rigorous mathematical conditions can be practically satisfied by generating specific current distributions on antenna, realizing excitation of base mode or cancellation of specific modes surface waves. Then, by following the current distribution of surface wave cancelling, a Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) glass antenna is designed for verification. It realizes surface-wave self-cancelling capability, low profile, and dipole-like radiation characteristics, without introducing metallized vias or extra structures. Simulation and measurement results demonstrate that the proposed glass antenna can not only suppress surface waves, but also realize a vertical polarization and omnidirectional radiation, with a -10dB bandwidth of 5.78–6.25 GHz, a radiation efficiency better than 60%, and a gain exceeding 3.54 dBi in V2X band (5.85-5.925 GHz), exhibiting excellent performance for vehicular communication applications.

title: Spectral Dynamic Attention Network for Hyperspectral Image Super-Resolution
authors: Tengya Zhang, Feng Gao, Lin Qi, Junyu Dong, Qian Du
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Hyperspectral image super-resolution is essential for enhancing the spatial fidelity of HSI data, yet existing deep learning methods often struggle with substantial spectral redundancy and the limited non-linear modeling capacity of standard feed-forward networks (FFNs). To address these challenges, we propose Spectral Dynamic Attention Network (SDANet), a framework designed to adaptively suppress redundant spectral interactions. SDANet integrates two key components: 1) Dynamic Channel Sparse Attention (DCSA) module that computes channel-wise correlations and selectively preserves the most informative attention responses through dynamic and data-dependent sparsification. 2) Frequency-Enhanced Feed-Forward Network (FE-FFN) that jointly models spatial and frequency-domain representations to enhance non-linear expressiveness. Extensive experiments on two benchmark datasets demonstrate that SDANet achieves state-of-the-art HISR performance while maintaining competitive efficiency. The code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/oucailab/SDANet.

title: A Structurally Optimized Planar Two-Dimensional Capacitive Sensor for High-Accuracy Positioning Applications
authors: Kai Peng, Xiaohu Xu, Xiaokang Liu, Qihang Dai, Yuxin Tian
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: The structural parameters of a planar two-dimensional (2-D) capacitive displacement sensor directly affects its measurement accuracy. Based on previous research on planar 2-D capacitive displacement sensors, this article established a 3-D electric field simulation model and a measurement error model to analyze the influence of the structural parameters of the sensor on the measurement error, and the sensor was optimized. First, the changing law of the space harmonic error of the induction electrode under different gaps was investigated, and the optimal gap height for installation was established. Second, the relationship between the error and the area change function was explored. The shape of the induction electrode with four times of the minimum error was obtained using the overlapping area function model. Third, the structural parameters of the excitation electrode were analyzed to cause the first harmonic error, and the optimal structural shape of the excitation electrode was established. Finally, the effect of the mutual crosstalk of the output signals between the sensing electrodes on the measurement error was discussed, and a ground shielding network was used to suppress the coupling error. The simulation results show that after the structural parameter optimization, the first spatial harmonic error, fourth spatial harmonic error, and coupling error of the sensor decreased by 66.9%, 77.5%, and 52.6%, respectively. To minimize the effect of manufacturing errors, a sensor prototype was fabricated using a micro-nano-machining process and verified for comparison. The experimental results show that the peak-to-peak errors of the sensor were ±4.09 and ±4.04 μm in the x and y directions, respectively, over a range of 300 mm × 300 mm. This sensor has approximately double the measurement accuracy of the previous one.

title: A Survey of Cross-Domain Robotics: Classification, Perception, Planning and Control
authors: Chenfei Hou, Henglai Wei, Hui Zhang, Anh-Tu Nguyen, Makoto Iwasaki
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Cross-domain robots (CDRs) represent a distinct category within multimodal robotics, characterized by their ability to operate across diverse domains and environments. These robots strike a balance between the simplicity and energy efficiency of single-domain robots), as well as the performance and exploration capabilities of cross-domain systems that use multiple platforms. They also significantly enhance environmental adaptability. With growing interest in this field, this survey first proposes a symbolic notation for CDRs and presents a review covering their classifications, structures, perception, planning and control. In addition, an online resource repository for CDRs, including 117 prominent state-of-the-art prototypes, is established and visually analyzed to facilitate technological exchange and practical implementation. Finally, we discuss the challenges and future perspectives in the design of both hardware and software for CDRs.

title: PARM: Pipeline-Adapted Reward Model
authors: Xingyu Fan, Wei Shao, Jiacheng Liu, Linqi Song, Pheng Ann Heng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: Reward models (RMs) have become central to aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences, powering both reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and more effective decoding strategies. While most prior work focuses on reward models for single model's text generation, the growing complexity of real-world applications has led to the adoption of multi-stage or pipeline tasks, where LLMs serve as components in a sequential process. However, how to design and train reward models that can effectively guide such pipeline tasks remains underexplored. In this work, we systematically investigate the role of reward models in LLM-based pipelines, focusing on code generation for combinatorial optimization problems as a representative task. We construct a novel pipeline framework that integrates a reward model into both the formulation and solution generation stages, and we analyze the challenges (such as inconsistency between rewards from reward models and results from pipelines) that arise when extending reward guidance beyond single-step generation. To address these challenges, we propose the Pipeline-Adapted Reward Model (PARM), a training method that leverages pipeline-specific data and direct preference optimization to more accurately reflect downstream execution feedback. While the stage-wise principles underlying PARM can in principle extend to general k-stage pipelines, this paper presents and evaluates a two-stage instantiation (formulation → code generation) as a representative and rigorous proof of-concept. We conduct extensive experiments on four public optimization benchmarks, evaluating execution rate and solving accuracy, and compare PARM with baseline reward models and sampling-based methods. We further include a supplementary cross-domain experiment on GSM8K to assess transfer beyond optimization. Our results demonstrate that PARM consistently improves pipeline output quality and stability across all datasets. Further analysis provides new insights into the interplay between reward modeling and multi-stage LLM reasoning. This work highlights the importance of reward model adaptation in complex pipelines and provides a practical framework for their integration, paving the way for more robust LLM applications in real-world, multi-stage tasks.

title: Thyro-LMD: A Benchmark Dataset and Sample-Driven Data Loading, Attention, and Regularization for Long-Tailed Multi-Label Thyroid Ultrasound Diagnosis
authors: Jiansong Zhang, Shunlan Liu, Xiaoling Luo, Guorong Lyu, Linlin Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Developing robust and effective computer-aided diagnostic (CAD) methods for thyroid ultrasound (TUS) remains a key challenge in medical imaging. Prior work has largely focused on binary or multi-class lesion classification, whereas real-world diagnosis follows standardized guidelines based on combinations of lexicon-level descriptors. These combinations naturally exhibit long-tailed distributions due to epidemiological patterns, limiting the robustness and generalizability of existing methods. Motivated by this, we introduce Thyro-LMD, the first long-tailed multi-label dataset for TUS. Using histopathology as the reference, Thyro-LMD provides retrospective, fine-grained annotations aligned with ACR TI-RADS lexicons and reveals a highly imbalanced label distribution. We benchmark representative methods, including end-to-end models, general-purpose multimodal large models (e.g., GPT-4o), and pretrained foundation models. While some methods show reasonable head-class performance, they struggle with body and tail classes. We therefore propose SynTUS-Net, a purpose-built baseline comprising collaborative modules addressing long-tailed multi-label challenges across data loading, feature encoding, and prediction regularization. SynTUS-Net achieves leading performance on Thyro-LMD, outperforming conventional traditional SOTA models by 5.3 Micro-F1 and 11.83 Macro-F1, and exceeding GPT-4o by 42.76 on Tail-F1. Extensive ablation studies confirm the contribution of each module. We believe Thyro-LMD and SynTUS-Net establish a clinically grounded benchmark and a new paradigm for interpretable and generalizable AI in ultrasound. Code and data will be released here.

title: Beyond the Demo: Exoskeletons and Brain-Computer Interfaces May Seem Miraculous, but their True Value is Forged in the Real World
authors: Eliza Strickland
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: I first met Robert Woo in 2011, during his third time walking in a powered exoskeleton. The architect had been paralyzed in a construction accident four years earlier, but he was determined to get back on his feet. Watching him clunk across a rehab room in an exoskeleton prototype, the technology felt astonishing. I had the same reaction when reporting on early brain-computer interfaces (BCIs), which enabled paralyzed people to move robotic arms or communicate by thought alone. Both types of bionic technology seemed to verge on magic.

title: Towards Neurocognitive-Inspired Intelligence: From AI’s Structural Mimicry to Human-Like Functional Cognition
authors: Noorbakhsh Amiri Golilarz, Hassan S. Al Khatib, Shahram Rahimi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Artificial intelligence has advanced significantly through deep learning, reinforcement learning, and large language and vision models. However, these systems often remain task-specific, struggle to adapt to changing conditions, and cannot generalize in ways similar to human cognition. Additionally, they mainly focus on mimicking brain structures, which often leads to black-box models with limited transparency and adaptability. Inspired by the structure and function of biological cognition, this paper introduces the concept of “Neurocognitive-Inspired Intelligence (NII)”, a hybrid approach that combines neuroscience, cognitive science, computer vision, and artificial intelligence to develop more general, adaptive, and robust intelligent systems capable of rapid learning, learning from less data, and handling minimal complexity based on prior experience. These systems aim to emulate the human brain’s ability to flexibly learn, reason, remember, perceive, and act in real-world settings with minimal supervision. We review the limitations of current artificial intelligence methods, define core principles of neurocognitive-inspired intelligence, and propose a modular, biologically inspired architecture that emphasizes integration, embodiment, and adaptability.We also discuss potential implementation strategies and outline various real-world applications, from robotics to education and healthcare. Importantly, this paper offers a hybrid roadmap for future research, laying the groundwork for building intelligent systems that more closely resemble human cognition.

title: Digital Competence Development Through AI-Supported Teaching Models in Higher Education: A Quasi-Experimental Study
authors: Benjamín Maraza-Quispe, Edwin Reyes-Villalba, Victor Hugo Rosas-Iman, Lita Marianela Quispe-Flores, Walter Choquehuanca-Quispe, Olga Melina Alejandro-Oviedo, Giuliana Feliciano-Yucra, Atilio Cesar Martínez-Lopez, Roberto Carlos Pari-Viza
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) has progressively transformed higher education by enabling personalized and interactive learning experiences. However, there is still limited empirical evidence on the effectiveness of structured AI-supported pedagogical models in the development of digital competence. The objective of the study was to determine the impact of an AI-supported teaching model on the development of digital competence in university students. A quasi-experimental design with pretest–posttest and a control group was employed, with a sample of 120 students. The experimental group participated in a structured instructional model based on the guided use of generative AI tools, while the control group followed a traditional approach. A validated instrument based on the DigCompEdu framework was used, evaluating five dimensions: Information literacy, digital communication, content creation, security, and problem solving. The results showed statistically significant improvements in all dimensions in the experimental group compared to the control group (p < 0.001). Mean scores increased from approximately 3.0 to 3.8 in the experimental group, while the control group showed minimal improvements. High effect sizes were identified (Cohen’s d = 1.7–1.8), indicating a significant impact of the intervention. In conclusion, structured AI-supported pedagogical models significantly improve digital competence in higher education, highlighting the importance of integrating AI through guided and well-founded pedagogical approaches.

title: Towards Open Data in Digital Social Protection: A Scoping Review of South Africa's Strategic Needs
authors: Lindiwe Sibiya, Gugulethu Baduza
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: This scoping review examines the strategic needs for implementing open data strategies within South Africa's digital social protection systems. With South Africa experiencing the highest global unemployment rate at 32.1 % and extreme inequality, digital innovations in social protection have become critical for reaching vulnerable populations. The study analyzed 28 articles published between 2019-2025 to identify current digital social protection initiatives, existing open data practices, and barriers to implementation. Key findings reveal that while South Africa has made significant strides in digitizing social grants, particularly during COVID-19 with the Social Relief of Distress (SRD) grant, substantial challenges remain. These include data fragmentation, limited interoperability between government systems, digital exclusion of vulnerable populations, and privacy concerns under the Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA), which governs the processing and sharing of personal information. The research identifies four key themes: the multifaceted nature of data sharing, benefits of data-driven governance, significant implementation barriers, and the importance of user-centric approaches for successful open data strategies in social protection. The study concludes that South Africa requires a strategic shift from broad “open data” concepts toward controlled “data sharing” frameworks that enable government collaboration while protecting sensitive information.

title: Glucocare: “A Web-Based Mhealth Application with Chatbot Technology and Community Support for Enhancing Diabetes Care in Developing Countries”
authors: Sylvia T. Shonhai, Mary Dzinomwa, Belinda Ndlovu, Sibonile Moyo, Catherine Chivasa
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Diabetes is an emerging global health threat affecting over 530 million people worldwide and posing serious problems, especially in developing nations. Traditional chronic disease management systems face problems such as a shortage of specialized personnel, poor infrastructure and overcrowded health services. Diabetic patients in particular often are deprived of timely medical care, support, and locally tailored educational tools that significantly contribute to disease management. To cover this gap, this paper presents GlucoCare, a secure web-based mobile health (mHealth) application designed to address these problems. The system incorporates multiple functional components, including an AI-powered chatbot, diabetes community chatrooms and doctor-patient communication into a single platform. GlucoCare also provides patients with real-time assistance, health education, appointment scheduling and medication tracking at the same time, ensuring data privacy and security. The Build methodology was used to guide the study, while the application was developed using an adapted version of Solo Scrum to support continuous improvement and adaptability to user needs. The GlucoCare system encourages diabetic patients to manage their condition more effectively and reduces pressure on healthcare workers. Its lightweight, low-bandwidth design makes it suitable for both urban and rural use in areas with limited resources. This innovation demonstrates how localized digital health solutions can improve chronic disease management and promote sustainable healthcare practices in developing countries.

title: UHM: Unified Transferring and Pooling over Heterogeneous GPU Memories
authors: Jinyao Liu, Si Wu, Haoyuan Ma, Chaoqun Li, Shaowei Li, Hongjing Yu, Fengxi Zhou, Feng Li, Xiuzhen Cheng, Pengfei Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: While existing far memory and disaggregated memory solutions provide a foundation for addressing limitations of single-node memory capacity and inefficient resource allocation in data centers, they predominantly focus on host memory, overlooking the critical demands of GPU-centric workloads. A key bottleneck in scaling GPU memory is the lack of connectivity and interoperability between GPUs, which is exacerbated by their heterogeneity. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes UHM, a unified data transferring and memory pooling scheme for heterogeneous GPU memories. UHM establishes the communication channels between heterogeneous GPU/host memories and leverages double data buffers for pipelined and reliable transfer. Furthermore, UHM unifies both local and remote memories to build a memory pool. The pooling scheme effectively integrates local and remote resources, performs efficient caching management in local memory, and optimizes memory block management for remote memory resources. Evaluation on a heterogeneous GPU cluster demonstrates that UHM significantly reduces the data transfer latency (up to 87.2%), improves the cache hit ratio, reduces runtime memory allocation latency (up to 94.7%), while enhancing the overall memory utilization (24.7%).

title: Solution of Stochastic Dynamic Economic Load Dispatch Problem Using Pelican Optimization Algorithm
authors: Nibha Rani, Soumyabrata Das, Suraj Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Dynamic Economic Load Dispatch (DELD) is essential for planning economical operation of power systems. However, achieving optimal generating schedule becomes increasingly difficult due to the dynamic load demand and inherent ambiguity associated with renewable energy sources. This study proposes a stochastic DELD approach to minimize cost of electricity production in the presence of uncertain solar and wind power integration. The uncertainties are modeled using a Monte Carlo Simulation (MCS)-based mean value method. To address the DELD problem under source uncertainty, a new Pelican Optimization Algorithm (POA), a new optimization technique, is used. The proposed problem is implemented in MATLAB. Simulation results from multiple case studies demonstrate the effectiveness of the POA approach in solving stochastic DELD problem

title: Intelligent Fault-Tolerant Control Strategy for 5Phase HV Electric Vehicle Traction Inverters
authors: Shreyas Thombare, Anindya Banik, Shantanu Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a fault-tolerant control strategy for electric vehicle (EV) propulsion systems using a five-phase Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) driven by an 800 V highvoltage MOSFET-based five-phase inverter. The primary goal is to ensure continued motor operation under common inverter faults such as open-circuit or short-circuit conditions, thereby enabling the EV to operate in a degraded or “limp” mode and reach its destination safely. A modified Field-Oriented Control (FoC) algorithm is proposed and integrated with intelligent fault detection and isolation mechanisms in MATLAB/Simulink. The proposed control strategy actively monitors phase currents and voltages to detect faults in specific inverter legs or switching devices and isolates the faulty components by disabling gate pulses, preventing further damage or battery drain. The system is capable of maintaining motor operation with the remaining healthy phases, ensuring basic mobility under fault conditions. Detailed simulation results validate the proposed system's effectiveness by comparing healthy and faulty scenarios in terms of speed tracking, battery consumption, torque ripple, phase current behaviour, and rotor angle deviation. The proposed architecture improves drive reliability, system resilience, and overall safety in EV traction applications, establishing a robust solution for fault-tolerant electric propulsion.

title: Adaptive Log-Fractional Terminal Sliding Mode Control for Residual Current Compensation in Bushfire-Prone Power Networks
authors: Tushar Kanti Roy, Md Apel Mahmud
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel adaptive log-fractional terminal sliding mode controller (ALFTSMC) for enhancing safety and reliability in bushfire-prone power distribution networks. The controller is integrated within a residual current compensation inverter system, coordinated with an active arc suppression coil, to mitigate single-line-to-ground faults that can lead to bushfire ignition if not rapidly suppressed. The proposed ALFTSMC employs a hybrid terminal sliding surface that combines fractional-order, logarithmic, and exponential nonlinearities with an adaptive fractional-based reaching law. This structure ensures finite-time convergence, improved transient smoothness, and robustness against system parameter variations. The controller dynamically regulates the injected neutral current to maintain residual current below the critical fire-safe threshold of 0.5 A within 2 s of fault onset. Simulation results under both low- and high-impedance fault conditions demonstrate that ALFTSMC achieves a reduction in steady-state fault current by up to 94%, a 21% improvement in voltage regulation, and a 35% faster settling time compared to a benchmark global terminal sliding mode controller. These findings confirm the ALFTSMC's effectiveness in achieving fire-safe operation under fault disturbances and highlight its practical applicability for real-time fault mitigation in resonant grounded distribution networks.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Bridgeless Zeta Converter for Efficient EV Charging Systems
authors: Vishvanath M, G. Chenna Raghavendra Reddy, Kola Lokesh, Sunkanna Ganiga, Venkadesan A, Sentamizh Selvan S
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a bridgeless Zeta converter's design and performance evaluation for electric vehicle (EV) charging applications. With the rapid proliferation of EVs, there is a growing demand for high-efficiency, compact, and reliable charging systems. Substantial conduction losses and degraded power quality hinder conventional EV chargers employing diode bridge rectifiers. A bridgeless Zeta converter topology is proposed to address these limitations, eliminating the input diode bridge to reduce conduction losses and enhance overall system efficiency. The converter's operational principles, control strategy, and key design parameters are thoroughly analyzed. The topology inherently provides a noninverted, regulated output, making it suitable for isolated and non-isolated charging configurations. The converter is evaluated under input voltages of 120 V and 230 V. A comprehensive design methodology and simulation results are presented using MATLAB/Simulink. Additionally, a comparative analysis of cost-effectiveness and reliability is conducted to validate the practicality of the proposed converter for EV charging applications.

title: Stator Winding Faults Detection Using Symmetrical Components of Stator Fluxes in Three Phase Induction Motor
authors: Tushar G. Vilhekar, Makarand Sudhakar Ballal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In the literature vibrations and current signature analysis based health monitoring methods are reported for the detection of interturn winding faults in electrical machines. In the last decade, flux monitoring based methods are published for the detection of different faults in electrical machines. Because, the flux monitoring based methods are more reliable, accurate and low cost compared to conventional methods. Also, symmetrical components based fault detection methods are mostly used for the detection faults in power system, electrical machines and transformer. Because, symmetrical components based methods are easy to analyzed graphically. Therefore, in this paper new symmetrical components of three phase stator fluxes are monitored for the detection of stator winding fault in three phase induction motor. The main objective of this paper, is to detect, the stator interturn winding fault, by monitoring graphical patterns of symmetrical components of three phase fluxes. Apart from this, the proposed method is also able to detect, the location of stator winding fault that is, the phase in which stator winding fault is occurred. The detection of location of stator winding faults helps to reduce the downtime and cost required for the maintenance. The advantages of the proposed method are most reliable, reduction in computation burden, easy to analyzed, low cost and more accurate.

title: Distributionally Robust Optimization for Virtual Power Plant with Vehicle-to-Grid Integration Under Multi-Source Uncertainties
authors: Jibin Kuriakose, Vinay Kumar Jadoun, Jayalakshmi N. S., Soham Dutta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a distributionally robust optimization (DRO) framework for virtual power plant (VPP) scheduling, integrating photovoltaic (PV) generation, energy storage systems (ESS), and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology under multi-source uncertainty. A Wasserstein distance-based ambiguity set captures PV output variability (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 20 \%$</tex>) and load demand fluctuations <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\pm 15 \%)$</tex> using empirical data from Udupi, India. The mixed-integer program is implemented in Python using CVXPY and validated through 200 Monte Carlo scenarios across three approaches: deterministic, robust, and DRO. Results demonstrate that DRO achieves superior performance, with 98 % constraint feasibility (compared to 76 % for deterministic and 89 % for robust approaches), an 18.8 % cost reduction compared to deterministic approaches (₹2,112 vs. ₹2,600), the lowest value-at-risk (VaR 95% = ₹2,112), and 53.6 % reduction in cost variance. The framework successfully coordinates five electric vehicles across spatially distributed locations (1.8-4.2 km), achieving 58% peak demand reduction through staggered charging, while enabling market access for sub-MW resources and reducing transaction costs by 80 %. Computational analysis confirms near-linear scalability (5-100 EVs), with decomposition strategies enabling deployment to 500+ asset portfolios. The DRO-VPP integration offers a practical and scalable pathway for high-renewable-penetration systems in emerging economies, particularly relevant as India targets 30% EV penetration by 2030.

title: AI-Driven Multi-Model Adaptive Tilt Optimization for Bifacial Solar Arrays Under Spatiotemporal Variability in Process
authors: Radharaman Shaha, Chetan Jambhulkar, Umesh Hiwase, Lata Gidwani
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The need for this work is more important today, which assists in maintaining high efficiency while maximizing bifacial PVs' energy harvest from solar under varying environmental conditions since they offer energy generation capability from both sides. However, in the cases of current designs, the installation or passive adjustment in the angle tilt does not sufficiently capture variable solar irradiation, ground albedo, and real-time meteorological conditions. Such AI-based works today only represent a single-agent system where feedback from some physical domain such as thermal response as well as radiative dynamics is not integrated, thus limiting the generalizability and operational accuracy sets of such models. Here, a complete novel AI-based multi-method adaptive control framework for dynamic tilt optimization in bifacial <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P V$</tex> arrays is introduced. At the heart of the system is a Hierarchical DualAgent Reinforcement Learning with Radiance-Augmented Feedback (HDARL-RAF), which gives rise to real-time tilt angle adjustments fine-tuned through multi-directional irradiance along bifacial gain feedback. An Adaptive Multivariate Entropy-Based Forecasting and Control (AMEFC) model addresses the uncertainty dimension in forecasting by adding entropy-weighted LSTM layers designed to make tilt more predictable and robust in performance. A Bidirectional OpticalThermal Coupled Simulation Engine (BOTCSE) was established for validation of the radiative and thermal outputs during tilt variations. For geo-optimizing these models, the Geospatial-AI Fusion Mapping with Tilt Sensitivity Indexing (GAFM-TSI) creates local tilt models through irradiance and terrain data samples derived from satellites. Lastly, a Hybrid Physics Informed Deep Surrogate Model for Tilt Control (HPDSM-TC) enables lightweight, physics-guided control deployment on embedded systems. Thus, when all put together, they can deliver up to 24 % bifacial gain, a reduction of 15 % of forecast error, and an 11.8 % increase in thermal-optical efficiency, all this while ensuring low-latency and scalable deployment. Overall, this research makes a significant step in integrating intelligent control with physics-informed validation across environmental and geographic domains in intelligent solar adaptive harvesting configuration sets.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Energy Management for Multi-Zone EV Charging Stations
authors: Arpana Singh, M. Rizwan, Uma Nangia
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Electric Vehicle Charging Stations (EVCS) need to be able to manage energy efficiently and effectively because electric automobiles are becoming more prevalent. The integration of renewable sources like photovoltaic systems makes managing charging operations even harder because both electric vehicle demand and solar generation fluctuate constantly and are rarely predictable. Traditional methods of managing energy often depend on established models, forecasts, or rule-based controls. This limits their ability to adapt to changes in real time and grow across different charging zones. This paper proposes a continuous-action deep reinforcement learning approach utilizing the actor-critic policy for real-time energy management in a multi-zone electric vehicle charging station integrated with photovoltaic generation. The three functional zones, residential, commercial, and fleet each have their own PV profiles, trends in arrivals, and costs for electricity. A model-free actor-critic agent learns the best ways to distribute power across zones in order to save money on energy and follow grid rules. The suggested method gets a 23.83% better average episode reward after 300 training episodes. The evaluation results show that adaptive charging behavior leads to lower penalty costs and better use of PV. For EVCS operations in the future under dynamic grid and renewable conditions, the proposed framework offers a scalable, flexible, and sustainable solution.

title: Dual Phase Shift Controlled Dual Active Bridge for 3-Stage MSCC EV Battery Charging
authors: Farha Khan, Shirazul Islam, Lazhar Ben-Brahim, Adel Gastli, Farhad Ilahi Bakhsh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The growing popularity of electric vehicles (EVs) has accelerated the demand for fast-charging systems with high efficiency and the ability to handle battery stress efficiently and deliver the maximum energy transfer. The paper outlines the design and analysis of a Dual Active Bridge (DAB) converter, which is operated in the Dual Phase Shift (DPS) modulation method to achieve a 3-stage Multi-Stage Constant Current (MSCC) charging profile. The proposed system couples the DAB directly with the EV battery, bypassing intermediate gridtied stages. The 3 stages of MSCC operating at different charging currents, where it operates on high (2C), medium (1.5C), and low (1C) charging currents are adapted, depending on the battery State of Charge (SoC) bands sensed, to create a balance between the fast charging, thermal management, and long battery life. The design of control design is created to control the power flow across charging stages and ensure softswitching and minimal RMS current stress. The THD analysis found to be 4.53%, validating the effectiveness of the DPScontrolled architecture. The simulation confirms that the DPSbased DAB has high efficiency, current and behavior stability, and efficient shaping at each stage, and it is a proper architecture when applied to scalable EV fast-charging systems.

title: Experimental Studies on Magnetization Behaviour of Axial Electromagnetic Bearings
authors: Aditya Raj, Kamisetti N V Prasad, G. Narayanan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Electromagnetic bearings (EMBs) provide a frictionfree and lubrication-free support to high-speed rotors. In addition to radial disturbance forces, the rotors experience a significant axial thrust in applications such as compressor drives and turbogenerators. Axial EMBs are used to support such rotors against axial loads. These EMBs typically consist of a pot core stator and a thrust disc, both made up of steel material such as EN8. A solid cored structure made of high conductivity steel leads to significant eddy currents that degrade the load capacity and performance of an axial EMB under time varying excitations. The impact of eddy currents on the magnetic fields in an axial EMB depends on both material as well as geometry of the core. In order to specifically characterize the magnetization behaviour of core material along with its geometry, this paper experimentally studies the flux-linkage to current relation in a prototype axial EMB with zero airgap between stator core and thrust disc. Sinusoidal voltages of varying amplitudes were applied across the stator coil over a frequency range from 0.5 Hz to 50 Hz, spanning two decades. The measured currents showed a peaky nature, which indicated that the EMB core was characterized upto its saturation flux-densities. Flux-linkages produced by currents of similar amplitudes at higher frequencies showed significant phase lag and atttenuation. In order to capture the experimentally obtained non-linearities and time dynamics between coil currents and flux-linkages, the stator coil of the EMB is modeled as a lossy and variable inductor. An approach is developed to evaluate the non-linear frequency-dependent inductance and to predict the magnetizing part of the coil current drawn by this inductor, using experimentally obtained flux-linkage versus current characteristics.

title: Multi-Hypotheses Trajectory Prediction of On-Road Vehicles Using MC-EKF with Map Constraints
authors: Muni Srinvasulu B, Bijo Sebastian, Lelitha Vanajakshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Trajectory prediction plays a critical role in autonomous driving, especially in anticipating the future motion of surrounding traffic participants. In this work, we propose a real-time, multi-modal trajectory prediction framework for surrounding vehicles using a Monte Carlo-Extended Kalman Filter (MC-EKF) informed by road geometry. Our approach generates possible future trajectories by sampling over a range of steering angles and propagating them using a kinematic bicycle model. Road coordinates with directional heuristics derived from map information are used for prediction. Monte Carlo simulation with DBSCAN provides a probabilistic view on the prediction task. Each predicted trajectory is further assigned a confidence score based on trajectory cluster density. The proposed method is validated quantitatively and qualitatively on a miniature Ackermann-steering vehicle platform in a controlled T-junction scenario using AprilTag-based localization. The approach works in real-time without the need for large-scale training data. This lightweight, interpretable framework is well-suited for use in embedded systems and early-stage autonomous vehicle prototypes.

title: Enhancing LFC Systems Against Denial of Service Attacks: Detection and Resilience Strategies
authors: Shaahid Mehmood, Asadur Rahman, Inam ul Haq, Abdullah Ahmad
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The growing dependence on cyber-physical systems (CPS) within power grids has heightened concerns regarding their susceptibility to cyber-attacks. This paper offers an in-depth investigation into the modeling, detection, and mitigation strategies for addressing Denial of Service (DoS) attacks in Load Frequency Control (LFC) systems. A Delay Upper Bound Based Detection (DUBD) method is employed to detect DoS assaults. A robust Load Frequency Control (LFC) architecture has been developed to mitigate these attacks, incorporating a robust controller and a dual-loop communication channel system to effectively minimize the disturbances produced by these types of attacks. Simulations are conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed DUBD detection mechanism and resilient LFC strategy in detecting and mitigating the impacts of DoS attacks. The results indicate a notable enhancement in the dynamic response of the LFC system.

title: A Model and Technical Implementation for Breaking News Information Cocoons and Improving Public Online Literacy
authors: Ju Zhu, Wen Sun
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 2nd International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Digital Media
abstract: None

title: AI-Generated Design of Cultural and Creative Packaging for Regional Paper-Cutting Art
authors: Weishi Peng, Yilan Xie, Qiduan Chen, Mingder Jean
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 2nd International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Digital Media
abstract: None

title: Review of Visible Light Communication Receivers: Standardization, Modulation Techniques, Receiver Technologies and Application Scenarios
authors: Nsikak A. Okono, Vitor L. Padoin, Lucas Teixeira, Marco A. Dalla Costa
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Visible Light Communication (VLC) systems use light from the visible spectrum for safe, interference free, and efficient data transmission, thus providing a strong alternative or complementary technology to conventional radio communications. This work reviews VLC receivers, focusing on their standardization efforts, modulation techniques, receiver types and applications. Key standards regulating the interoperability and scalability of VLC technology at various communication layers were analyzed, including IEEE, ITU, and ETSI/IEC specifications. This study examines multiple modulation techniques, including on-off keying (OOK), Pulse Position Modulation (PPM), Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM), and Color Shift Keying (CSK), while highlighting their respective merits in achieving data efficiency, mitigating flickering effects, and optimizing spectrum utilization. Triage of receiver performance encompasses all its parameters such as response times, bandwidth, signal quality, error rates, and viewing angles with the consideration of different ambient illumination conditions and application specific requirements. The emerging receiver technologies, including micro-LED (detector modes) and advanced photodetectors, were examined based on their potential to overcome some of the disadvantages of low sensitivity, high power consumption, and interference from ambient light. The current trend in research considers hybrid wireless systems, artificial intelligence enhanced filtering, and machine learning driven signal processing as promising avenues of solutions, alongside the advanced development of next generation detectors. The development of VLC technology is expected to go hand in hand with 6G networks and IoT applications, thereby imparting further advantages in terms of network efficiency, security, and connectivity. This paper offers insight that are broad and deep enough to be helpful in the development of more sophisticated optical wireless communication systems by engineers and researchers.

title: Conditional Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with W-Net and Wasserstein-L1-SSIM Loss Function for Single Image Dehazing
authors: Bijaylaxmi Das, Hemanta Kumar Sahu, Ashish Kumar Padhan, Sudipta Mukhopadhyay
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have been widely used to solve different image translation problems. This article proposes an exploration of the Conditional Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network (cWGAN) building blocks to improve its performance. The improvements in the generator, discriminator, and/or loss functions can improve the training of the network and the quality of the outputs. A W-Net has been introduced as the network’s generator to improve performance. A new combination of Wasserstein, L1 loss, and SSIM (WaLSS) loss functions is proposed for network training to improve the quality of generated images. Haze and other atmospheric conditions reduce vision, posing challenges for computer vision applications. The lack of prior knowledge presents difficulties in single-image de-hazing. The proposed refined Conditional Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with W-Net and Wasserstein-L1-SSIM Loss Function (cWGAN-WaLSS) is a complete network that requires no prior knowledge and no further enhancements. Hence, cWGAN-WaLSS is a good candidate for single-image de-hazing. Extensive testing with real and simulated hazy images shows that the proposed cWGAN-WaLSS network demonstrates consistent and competitive performance relative to existing cWGAN-based and other dehazing methods.

title: Energy Consumption Minimization in MEC-empowered CF-mMIMO System Based on Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Boyu Zhang, Yanxiang Jiang, Huiting Li, Yige Huang, Fu-Chun Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: To satisfy strict latency requirements, we consider a mobile edge computing (MEC)-empowered cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) system. To minimize the total energy consumption of user ends under low-latency constraints, an optimization problem is formulated that combines the optimization of offloading decisions, power allocation, and computing resource allocation. By considering the distributed architecture of CF-mMIMO, a partially centralized training multi-agent proximal policy optimization (PC-MAPPO) algorithm is proposed. This algorithm enables the coexistence of both centralized and distributed training in the system, reducing computational complexity while saving communications overhead. Meanwhile, the scheme allows for the existence of different types of agents to jointly optimize the actions of users and access points (APs). The simulation results show that the proposed algorithm reduces energy consumption compared to the considered traditional solutions, while having lower computational complexity and better scalability.

title: MR-HVIL: A Mixed-Reality-Based Human&#x2013;Vehicle-In-the-Loop On-Road Validation Platform for Mixed Traffic Testing_supp1-3626213.mp4
authors: Yunyi Jia
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper introduces MR-HVIL, a novel mixed-reality-based platform designed for on-road human-vehicle-in-the-loop validation in vehicle research. MR-HVIL leverages an optical see-through mixed reality framework to seamlessly integrate real-world driving environments with virtual traffic scenarios. Unlike traditional vehicle-in-the-loop and driving simulators, our platform enhances driver experience by combining real-world physical vehicle dynamic motions with optimized virtual content overlay, ensuring a realistic and immersive validation environment. To address the challenges of synchronization and localization in outdoor environments, we develop a real-time virtual object localization and correction algorithm that integrates vehicle sensor data, enhancing mixed reality accuracy and stability. Experimental validation demonstrates the platform’s efficacy in comparing human responses to real and virtual autonomous vehicles, offering new opportunities for human-centric validation in mixed traffic scenarios. MR-HVIL paves the way for safer, more versatile, and naturalistic testing of mixed traffic systems.

title: State-Aware Hierarchical WiFi Sensing for Continuous and Lightweight Recognition of Motion and Respiration
authors: Yanliang Jin, Mingxin Song, Chenggang Shao, Yuan Gao, Shengli Liu, Junming Ma
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurate and real-time detection of human motion and respiration is critical for applications such as in-home health monitoring and elderly care. However, existing solutions often face challenges in multi-task sensing, continuous behavior recognition, and computational efficiency. This paper proposes CTSMR, a continuous two-stage framework based on wifi channel state information (CSI) for joint motion and respiration monitoring. The system first distinguishes between static and dynamic states, and subsequently performs respiration classification or activity recognition using a unified neural architecture. A differential threshold segmentation algorithm is introduced to enable efficient processing of continuous CSI streams, while a lightweight two-stage network, DualSenseNet, ensures low-latency inference. Experimental results demonstrate that CTSMR achieves 98.79% accuracy in respiration classification and 96.98% in activity recognition. The system maintains stable performance across varying distances and environments, with a total processing latency of 1.156ms per sample, including 0.201ms for model inference. Unlike prior isolated methods, CTSMR introduces a unified framework for continuous multi-task sensing, addressing the trade-off between high-accuracy detection and low-latency deployment in practical settings.

title: Sequential-Scanning Contrast-Enhanced Cerebral Dual-Energy CT Imaging Via Physics-Informed Subject-Specific Deep Image Representations
authors: Qiaoxin Li, Feiyan Zhang, Ruifeng Chen, Peng Wang, Guotao Quan, Yanfeng Du, Dong Liang, Yinsheng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences
abstract: Dual-energy computed tomography (DECT) has been widely used to obtain quantitative elemental composition of imaged subjects for personalized and precise medical diagnosis. Compared with DECT leveraging advanced X-ray source and/or detector technologies, the use of the sequential-scanning data acquisition scheme to implement DECT may make a broader impact on clinical practice because this scheme requires no specialized hardware designs and can be directly implemented with conventional CT systems. However, since the concentration of iodinated contrast agent in the imaged subject varies over time, sequentially scanned data sets acquired at two tube potentials are temporally mismatched. As existing material basis image reconstruction approaches assume that the data sets acquired at two tube potentials are temporally matched, the violation of this assumption results in inaccurate quantification of material concentration. The purpose of this work is to address the challenge caused by temporal mismatch via developing a physics-informed subject-specific deep image representations based temporal matching and material basis image reconstruction technique, FRONTIER in short. FRONTIER has been validated and evaluated using extensive numerical simulation data sets generated from clinical human subject exams and experimental human subject studies. Results demonstrated the improvement of quantification accuracy and image quality using FRONTIER. The source code and data used in this paper are available at https://github.com/SIAT-CT-LAB/FRONTIER.

title: Reciprocal Sinusoidal Vector Modulation in Matrix Converter-Based WPT System
authors: Chang Liu, Wei Liu, Tianyi Liu, Yuchen Wei, Jian Guo, K. T. Chau
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Matrix converters have gained increasing attention as a promising solution for wireless power transfer (WPT) systems due to their capability for single-stage AC-AC conversion and benefit for high efficiency. However, existing three-phase to single-phase matrix converter-based WPT systems face challenges such as grid-side current harmonics, constrained modulation index, and a relatively high component count, which significantly limit their performance and applicability. This paper proposes a brand-new reciprocal sinusoidal vector modulation, which redefines the existing space vector modulation by substituting the fixed-length current space vector with a time-varying sinusoidal one. This approach leverages the inherent sinusoidal resonant current of WPT systems, enabling low-distortion grid-side current and enhanced robustness under detuned conditions. The reciprocal switching pattern achieves single-cycle reactive power cancellation without requiring additional loop compensation. Moreover, the use of gallium-nitride monolithic bidirectional switches further enhances system performance by reducing conduction losses, shortening commutation intervals, and enabling a higher modulation index. The proposed strategy is validated through theoretical analysis and experimental tests, achieving a total harmonic distortion of 2.26% and a peak efficiency of 93.85%.

title: A cross-modal network for facial expression recognition
authors: Chunwei Tian, Jingyuan Xie, Qi Zhang, Chao Li, Wangmeng Zuo, Shichao Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Deep neural networks enriched with structural information have been widely employed for facial expression recognition tasks. However, these methods often depend on hierarchical information rather than face property to finish expression recognition. In this paper, we propose a cross-modal network with strong biological and structural information for facial expression recognition (CMNet). CMNet can respectively learn expression information via face symmetry on a whole face, left and right half faces to extract complementary facial features. To prevent native effect of biological and structural information fusion, a salient facial information refinement module can obtain salient facial expression information to improve stability of an obtained facial expression classifier. To reduce reliance on unilateral facial features, a half-face alignment optimization mechanism is designed to align obtained expression information of learned left and right half faces. Our experimental results demonstrate that CMNet outperforms several novel methods, i.e., SCN and LAENet-SA for facial expression recognition. Codes can be obtained at https://github.com/hellloxiaotian/CMNet.

title: Pinching-Antenna Assisted Short Packet NOMA Communication Systems
authors: Shiv Kumar, Keshav Singh, Chih-Peng Li, Zhiguo Ding
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This letter investigates the performance of a pinching-antenna system (PASS) assisted short-packet downlink non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) network. A dielectric waveguide with a selectable pinching antenna serves two users with heterogeneous propagation, including a line-of-sight (LoS) user in the same room and a non-line-of-sight (NLoS) user in an adjacent room. Closed-form expressions for the average block error rate (ABLER) and goodput are derived under a finite blocklength regime using the Gaussian–Chebyshev quadrature method. An asymptotic analysis further characterizes the high signal-to-noise-ratio (SNR) behavior and diversity order of both users. Simulations validate the analysis and show that PASS-NOMA outperforms both fixed conventional antenna systems (CASS) and PASS-assisted orthogonal multiple access (OMA).

title: Sensorless Control of Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Based on Improved Synchronous Reference Frame Filter in Double Angle Axis
authors: Dingdou Wen, Xiaorui Wei, Xincheng Zhu, Yang Zhang, Taolue Xiao, Yushuang Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: In the medium-to-high-speed speed control system of the interior permanent magnet synchronous motor (IPMSM), to address the issues of large harmonic component of the expanded back electromotive force (EMF) observed by conventional sliding mode observer (SMO) and the phase lag caused by the low-pass filter (LPF), a sensorless control method based on an improved synchronous reference frame filter (ISRFF) in double angle axis is proposed. Firstly, the extended back EMF in double angle and αβ-axis are processed through the LPF with same cut-off frequency to generate leading and lagging phase shifts. Secondly, the phase shifts are transformed to dq-axis, and the harmonics are filtered out by LPF. Then, the phase shifts are eliminated by a triangular transformation. The position observation accuracy of the SMO is improved while the rotor position error signal is constructed, thus the heterodyne method is omitted. Finally, the experimental results confirm that compared with conventional methods, the proposed method effectively filters out harmonics, reduces the maximum position error under steady-state conditions, and mitigates speed fluctuations during dynamic operation.

title: Feasibility of SEU Rate Estimation Using Deposited-Energy Spectra from In-Flight Single-Layer Silicon Detectors
authors: Jieyi Wang, Xinyu Zhu, Guohong Shen, Bin Yuan, Chunqin Wang, Xiaoheng Xu, Longlong Zhang, Yingqi Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: The physical mechanism of single event upset (SEU) is the energy deposited by high-energy particles within a device’s sensitive volume (SV). Single-layer silicon detectors rely on the same principle by measuring energy deposition in silicon. The discrepancy between a detector and an SV arises from geometric differences that produce distinct chord-length distributions for traversing particles. This work presents a mathematical framework that bridges this gap using inverse problem theory. The energy-deposition process is formulated as a Fredholm integral equation of the first kind, with a kernel explicitly derived from the detector’s differential chord-length distribution. The incident linear energy transfer (LET) spectrum is then reconstructed by minimizing a regularized cost functional, which stabilizes the intrinsically ill-posed inversion. The method is validated using a Galactic Cosmic Ray (GCR) model and in-flight data from the Beidou Navigation Satellite System (BDS) satellites M15 and M16. The results demonstrate that the proposed reconstruction yields accurate SEU rate estimates, effectively validating the feasibility of using lightweight single-layer detectors for quantitative in-flight SEU risk monitoring.

title: GSDeformer: Direct, Real-time and Extensible Cage-based Deformation for 3D Gaussian Splatting_supp2-3687062.mp4
authors: Hongchuan YU
publish: N/A
abstract: We present GSDeformer, a method that enables cage-based deformation on 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS). Our approach bridges cage-based deformation and 3DGS by using a proxy point-cloud representation. This point cloud is generated from 3D Gaussians, and deformations applied to the point cloud are translated into transformations on the 3D Gaussians. To handle potential bending caused by deformation, we incorporate a splitting process to approximate it. Our method does not modify or extend the core architecture of 3D Gaussian Splatting, making it compatible with any trained vanilla 3DGS or its variants. Additionally, we automate cage construction for 3DGS and its variants using a render-and-reconstruct approach. Experiments demonstrate that GSDeformer delivers superior deformation results compared to existing methods, is robust under extreme deformations, requires no retraining for editing, runs in real-time, and can be extended to other 3DGS variants. Project Page: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://jhuangbu.github.io/gsdeformer/</uri>
