title: An Entropy Transformed Pareto Distribution for Measuring Wealth Inequality
authors: Stephen Mathew, Ikwuoche J. David, William Bob M. Nkombou, Sadiq S. Abubakar, Yunusa A. Ojirobe
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this study, an Entropy Transformed Pareto Distribution (EnTrPD) was proposed to provide improved flexibility in modelling heavy-tailed behavior observed in data, and was subsequently applied to assess wealth inequalities in Nigeria using quarterly Gross Domestic Product (GDP) data. The derived properties of the distribution include <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n^{\mathrm {th}}$ </tex-math></inline-formula> moment, characteristic function, mean, and variance. The model parameters were estimated using five parameter estimation techniques: the maximum likelihood, least squares, weighted least square, Cramer-von Mises, and Anderson Darling methods. Through simulation, the model was validated to be consistent with large sample inference, and it showed that all five estimators yielded estimates that converged to the true parameter value as sample size increased, reflecting consistency, high accuracy, and stability. On application to GDP data, the results showed that the classical Pareto distribution overestimated inequality. Particularly in the upper tail, it produce inflated Gini coefficients and extreme top-income shares. However, the EnTrPD yielded inequality measures that reflected the Nigerian economy, with Gini coefficients ranging from 0.35 to 0.56 with stable Atkinson and Generalized Entropy indices. In addition, the Lorenz curve and density analyses confirmed that the EnTrPD effectively captured heavy-tail behavior and extreme wealth concentrations without exaggeration, demonstrating its superiority over the classical Pareto method in modelling heavy-tailed economic distributions and assessing inequality.

title: MPPT Performance of P&O and PSO in Tilted Standalone PV Systems with Battery Under Shading
authors: Rushabh Dukare, Narendrababu A, Naveen Yalla
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study presents modelling design, simulation and evaluation of 8.8 kW off-grid rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) system designed for a school located in Eruvapalem, Andhra Pradesh, India. The system includes PV array, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) based Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) charge controller, battery bank and inverter to supply continuous power to AC loads and minimize dependency on non-renewable energy sources. The system also incorporates hydraulic actuator-based tilt mechanism to provide more power output by adjusting tilt angle of PV array based on various weather conditions. A detailed load estimation, PV array sizing and component designing is provided. Unlike conventional work, this paper demonstrates optimization in module connections, component matching and panels orientation validated through MATLAB Simulink results. Novelty lies in the tracking of best possible solar radiations during all weather conditions with realistic design in all loading conditions. A comprehensive comparison with existing literature substantiates the improvements in system performance.

title: Study the Effect of Current Source Driver on the Performance of Traction Inverter
authors: Yashahshree Thombre, Madhuri Chaudhari
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper deals with the performance enhancement of traction inverters in electric vehicles (EVs) through the use of current source gate drivers (CSGD). As inverter power levels increase, efficiency and reliability become increasingly critical. Conventional voltage source gate drivers (VSGD) are limited by high switching losses and slower operation due to the use of external resistors. In contrast, CSGDs deliver a constant gate current, significantly reducing switching losses and improving overall efficiency. These improvements translate into faster charging times and extended driving ranges for EVs. Key findings include a marked reduction in switching losses, enhanced inverter efficiency, and validated performance gains through detailed analytical calculations and simulation results, demonstrating the clear advantages of CSGD over traditional VSGD approaches.

title:
Design and Thermal Characterization of Al6061-T6 Reinforced with Graphene, SiC, and B
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    C Nanoparticles for Electric Vehicle Battery Enclosure
authors: Manimaran S, Bharatiraja C, P Udit Narayan Sai, Rohit Xavier Chatter
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Battery casings are becoming increasingly more durable and thermally efficient due to increasing consumer demand for electric vehicles. Consequently, manufacturers of these casings require advanced materials with improved mechanical and thermal properties. In this study, we developed a computational method to evaluate the effect of various compositions of graphene, silicon carbide (SiC) and boron carbide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$B_{4} \mathrm{C}$</tex>) nanoparticles in an Al6061-T6 matrix, ultimately developing the best composition for battery casing applications. Finite element thermal analysis was conducted for nanoparticle dispersions in various combinations with respect to their thermal conductivity under a simulated battery casing scenario. The results demonstrate that graphene nanoparticle component composition shows better thermal dissipation performance than the other two materials; this study also makes it evident that by varying the composition of graphene, the temperature property is varied.

title: Adaptive Analytical Control and Optimization of STATCOM in Solar-Fed Transmission Lines Under Outlier Scenarios
authors: Chetan Jambhulkar, Umesh Hiwase
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: With power systems reengineered by the increasing injection of renewable energy resources, in particular solar photovoltaic (PV) systems, into modern transmission grids, the time has come when we can study their non-linearity and uncertainty as causes weakening power quality, voltage instability, and harmonic distortions-especially episodes of rapidly changing irradiance or sudden changes in load. The control strategies common to a STATCOM, which are mostly based either on linear or fixed-parameter models, do not adapt to stochastic nonlinear characterizations of grid dynamics and, hence, could not potentially be equipped with robustness, adaptability, and precision in abnormal operating conditions. With such critical disadvantages in mind, this work offers a detailed and analytically sound framework that primarily targets the design, optimization, and validation of a STATCOM system interfaced with solar-feeding transmission line structures in the background of such outlier event scenarios. Five novel methodologies are introduced: (1) Dynamic Uncertainty-Aware Hybrid Fractional Control (DUHFC), incorporating reinforcement learning with fractional PID control to dynamically regulate STATCOM output under uncertain solar and grid conditions; (2) Outlier-Driven Probabilistic Harmonic State Estimation (ODPHSE), leveraging Bayesian convolutional layers and leverage scores for harmonic estimation and control in presence of anomalies; (3) Multivariate Resilience-Based Voltage Stability Predictor (MR-VSP), utilizing graph signal processing to identify voltage collapse-prone nodes; (4) Adaptive Solar-STATCOM Interaction Model using Spatio-Temporal Clustering (ASSIM-STC) for localized and event-specific control adaptation; (5) Metaheuristic-Learned Multi-Objective Damping Index Controller (ML-MODIC) that uses a WOA-TLBO fusion to optimally tune STATCOM controllers across a multitude of dynamic objectives. The impact of this proposed scheme is improved in voltage-control capability (↓ 19.8% deviation), harmonic suppression (↑ 12.5% THD reduction), enhanced damping (↑ 154%), and optimized fault handling suppression (↑ 92% accuracy of class identification), ensuring resilient and high-quality power transmission from thereon for the process. This sets a concrete footing for the next age of smart grids, where intelligent, data-driven, and adaptive deployment of STATCOMs would be possible in process.

title: Investigations on Effect of Shaft-Mounted Position Sensor Unbalance on SRM Performance and Its Remedy
authors: Debamalya Rakshit, Devraj Roy, Aparajita Sengupta, Mainak Sengupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper deals with investigations on the performance of a 4 phase radial flux SRM with a shaft mounted rotary encoder. The detrimental effects of the mechanical imperfections in a slotted dice encoder on motor phase current and hence torque have been studied using Finite Element Method (FEM) based analysis, considering two extreme cases. A digital calibration has been proposed to mitigate the errors in quadrature encoder output (QEP) signal. The correctness of the proposed logic has been verified both in FEM simulation and on a real-time digital platform.

title: Power Quality Enhancement of Hybrid Pv-Wind-Battery-Fcs-Ae Based Standalone Microgrid Under the Operation With Various Loads
authors: K Lokeswara Rao, Usha A, R. S. Geetha
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Power supply systems especially standalone Microgrids that relies on hybrid renewable sources are more viable solutions for many problems. Hybrid system consisting of Photovoltaic (PV), PMSG based wind conversion system, Aqua-Electrolyzer (AE), Fuel Cell Stack (FCS), battery bank is developed in this paper for supplying AC power to various loads through an inverter. All components of PV, wind system, FCS, AE, battery banks are integrated to a common point through required dc/dc converters to establish a stable dc-link. Different loads such are constant current, impedance, power loads are operated from dc-link through an inverter. Novel control methods are developed on all dc/dc converters to make power flow between respective component and dc-link as well as to maintain a stable voltage at dc-link <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{V}_{\text{dc}}\right)$</tex>. In order to supply a stable voltage to various loads, a novel inverter controller is also implemented in this paper. An effective energy management mechanism is also developed among all components involved in the Standalone Microgrid System (SMS). Various responses of the system are presented through Simulink on MATLAB platform to validate the proposed method.

title: MQTT-Enabled Smart Net Metering for V2G with Predictive Analytic
authors: Ashiq Nazir Bhat, Mukesh Singh, Singara Singh Kasana
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The rise of electric vehicles (EVs) and the advent of Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technologies necessitate an advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) that can monitor, communicate, and analyse bidirectional energy flow. Conventional meters typically analyse unidirectional energy flow, and existing bidirectional meters do not provide real-time predictive analysis. The evolving energy landscape requires more versatile and interactive solutions for V2G applications. This paper proposes the design and development of a three-phase, automotive-grade, bi-directional smart net meter with MQTT-based communication for efficient, scalable data transmission. The system incorporates real-time energy analysis, utilising a C2000 DSP, and facilitates asynchronous data streaming through the MQTT. The system integrates real-time energy monitoring in accordance with IEEE 1459-2010 standards and includes predictive load forecasting algorithms. The solution improves grid resilience through modern security protocols and facilitates easy integration. Experimental validation at a functional V2G-enabled EV charging station exhibits enhanced metering accuracy, minimal delay in data transmission, and effective predictive forecasting.

title: Semantic Spatial Object Interpretation for Indoor Environments using Sensor Fusion
authors: Deep Senchowa, Deepjyoti Kalita, Priyakshi Bordoloi, Abhijit Boruah, Debajit Sarma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Scene understanding is a critical component of vision-based decision-making systems, often requiring an effective interpretation of spatial relationships among objects in the environment. This work prioritizes spatial object interpretation by implementing an indoor scene understanding framework that integrates sensor fusion, combining 2D LiDAR and RGB cameras. A proximity-based 2D-3D object alignment approach is proposed to extract spatial semantics and visually interpret indoor environments with enhanced detail. This method incorporates spatial relationships, deducing relative object positions, distances, and hierarchical arrangements to provide a comprehensive understanding of indoor scenes. The model achieved a high precision (0.97) and recall (0.97) for object detection and segmentation, alongside a mean average precision (mAP50) of 0.98. Quantitative results demonstrated superior segmentation and spatial relationship deduction, with distances between objects calculated with an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{\pm 0. 0 5 ~ m}$</tex>, facilitating semantic segmentation that offers meaningful insights into object positioning within indoor environments.

title: High Voltage Conversion Ratio Non-Isolated Common-Ground Bidirectional DC-DC Converter
authors: Kaibalya Prasad Panda, Preeti Sharma, Jinisha Singhal, Vedant Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative, non-isolated, common-ground bidirectional DC-DC converter (NCBDDC) developed for applications requiring a high step-up gain and compact configuration, such as renewable energy systems with battery storage. The suggested topology exhibits dual-mode (boost/buck) operations that yield a very high voltage conversion feature with fewer components and low voltage/current stresses on switches. Analytical modelling, steady-state operation, and the derivation of voltage gain are presented for both modes. A detailed comparison is carried out against existing topologies, placing emphasis on the low gain, ripple, and discontinuity to ground problems that the suggested topology remedies. Theoretical predictions are verified through MATLAB/Simulink-based simulations, revealing stable voltage profiles and good robustness to load transients. It attains a peak efficiency of 95.5% in boost operating mode and 95.9% in buck operating mode, with the power losses mainly due to conduction and inductor losses. Hence, the suggested converter becomes an attractive solution for today's bidirectional energy conversion systems requiring a lot from a small envelope.

title: Solid-State and AI-Driven Advances in EV Battery Ecosystems: Road to 2030
authors: Pooja Choudhary, Anubhav Agrawal, Shivani Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Due to rapid growth of electric vehicles (EV) in India and on global front the advancements in battery technology and smart control systems have become critical for achieving sustainable mobility. The paper presents a comprehensive overview of EV classifications, battery technologies, and emerging innovations on solid-state batteries (SSBs) and artificial intelligence (AI)-driven EVs enhancing communication. Comparative analysis shows that SSBs achieve energy densities up to 450-500 Wh/kg, nearly twice that of conventional Li-ion batteries, with enhanced safety and reduced thermal instability. AI-based Battery Management Systems (BMS) improve charging efficiency by 15-20% and extend lifespan by 25-30% under extreme conditions. The paper integrates technological insights, Indian market data, and global developments to highlight challenges and future directions in developing a sustainable and intelligent EV ecosystem. The research finding aims to support researchers, policymakers, and industry stakeholders in fostering a resilient, intelligent, and environmentally responsible EV ecosystem in India.

title: A Computational Study on CT-CV Charging of Lithium-Ion Batteries with Temperature Regulation
authors: Sahil Yadav, Subham Khange, Vivek Chaudhary, Ashwini Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Fast and safe charging of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) is essential for high-performance applications such as electric vehicles and portable electronics. However, conventional Constant Current-Constant Voltage (CC-CV) charging lacks temperature feedback, leading to excessive heat generation, accelerated degradation, and safety concerns during high-rate charging. This study introduces a novel Constant Temperature-Constant Voltage (CT-CV) charging strategy, incorporating a Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controller to dynamically adjust the charging current in response to real-time cell temperature. A coupled pseudo two-dimensional (P2D) electrochemical-thermal model was developed in COMSOL Multiphysics to simulate the proposed method at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \mathrm{C}-5 \mathrm{C}$</tex> charging rates across multiple temperature setpoints. The results demonstrate that the CT-CV strategy achieves up to 40.8% reduction in total charging time while maintaining thermal stability, with an optimal temperature setpoint of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$32{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> offering the best trade-off between charging speed and safety. The controller further exhibited robust performance under fault conditions, effectively preventing thermal runaway. These findings establish CT-CV charging as a practical and thermally safe alternative to conventional CC-CV, with strong potential for integration into advanced battery management systems.

title: BiLSTM-Based Load Forecasting for Individual Appliances in Smart Homes
authors: Ravivarma Kamireddy, Lokeshgupta Bhamidi, U Ramanjaneya Reddy
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study develops an intelligent energy predictor using a Bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) neural network model for predicting energy demand response within a smart home. To implement this proposed model, the real-time data from five different appliances such as air conditioner, TV, computer, fan, and lamp were monitored via smart meters within a Home Energy Management System (HEMS). In addition to the smart meters, an extra meter has been installed to monitor overall energy consumption data. This real-time collected data setup enables the collection of precise and comprehensive energy consumption data, which in turn supports data analysis and confirms the system's effectiveness before its large-scale deployment. The data normalization and feature selection plays a crucial role in the effectiveness of the proposed model. Various performance metrics are used to validate the proposed model and compare with LSTM model. The proposed Bi-LSTM method achieved superior performance over the LSTM model by reducing errors interms of performance metrics such as MAE, RMSE, NRMSE, MSE, and improving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of individual appliances in a smart home. The results highlight the model's potential in smart-home energy management and suggest future work in integrating contextual data and enhancing real-world applications.

title: Design and Analysis of Coreless Double-D Coil for Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer in Electric Vehicles Under Misalignment
authors: Ngangoiba Maisnam, Maria Loukrakpam, Shaik Affijulla, Ksh Milan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) systems are gaining significant attention as a promising solution for charging Electric Vehicles (EVs) due to their enhanced convenience, safety, and potential to alleviate range anxiety associated with conventional plug-in methods. Among various magnetic coupler topologies, the Double D (DD) coil stands out for its superior coupling performance, lower leakage flux, and inherent tolerance to misalignment, making it particularly well-suited for dynamic charging scenarios. This paper presents the design and analysis of a coreless DD coil specifically developed for EV wireless power transfer applications. A DD coil with 7 turns was meticulously designed and modeled using ANSYS Maxwell 3D. Comprehensive simulations were conducted to extract critical parameters, including self-inductance, mutual inductance, and coupling coefficient, under a 5A input current excited solely to the transmitting coil. The performance of the designed coil was rigorously evaluated under misalignment conditions, vertical and longitudinal displacements. The findings provide valuable insights into the magnetic field distribution and transfer characteristics of DD coils, demonstrating their robust behavior and efficiency in mitigating the challenges posed by dynamic operating environments.

title: Scalar Control of Induction Motor with Current Limiting Feature
authors: Manju Bhashini R, Ragavan K, Pallavi Bharadwaj
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Enhanced safety and reliability in induction motor drive is achieved using a scalar control for open loop operation by ensuring the current does not exceed the rated value under a wide operating conditions, and for motors designed for any voltage rating. A ramp control mechanism is incorporated into the generation of pulse width modulation (PWM) signals. This ensures a gradual increase or decrease in the motor speed by slowly varying the applied voltage. The ramp duration is determined based on the motor's mechanical parameters, allowing the stator voltage's RMS value to rise until it reaches the level required for the desired reference speed. The proposed control is applied to two three-phase induction motors with differing voltage ratings. Both simulation and experimental results are presented for various operating speeds.

title: Modeling and Integration of Regenerative Braking for Motor Through SCMIC-Based Hybrid PV/ Battery System
authors: Parinita Mondal, Jayati Dey, Tapas Kumar Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a Single Capacitor Based Multi-Input Converter (SCMIC) fed by two inputs, PV and battery. The SCMIC simultaneously supplies power to a resistive load and an electrical motor through an H-bridge converter, which is operated with the unipolar PWM switching topology. A control scheme is proposed here to integrate the regenerative braking operation of the motor, during which charging of the battery through SCMIC takes place. Power sharing between PV and battery is accomplished in accordance with the instantaneous load requirements, which leads to an improvement in battery life cycle. To minimise the power and energy strain on the battery, a maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm is used to draw the most power possible from PV, which serves as an auxiliary source to supply power to the load during regular operation. When there is low load, the extra PV power is also utilised to recharge the battery. Additionally, the output voltage of the SCMIC is continuously maintained at the desired reference level by regulating the duty ratio of the switches linked to the battery. The performance of the SCMIC combined with motor load via the H-bridge converter is evaluated under several operating situations in a simulated environment in order to examine the effectiveness of the suggested topology and the control strategy. The simulation results demonstrate that the regenerative braking and its associated control integrated with the PV/battery-fed SCMIC work efficiently at a fast speed of response.

title: Efficient Frequency Regulation of Shipboard Microgrid Utilizing One-Third Method Based Tuning Rules
authors: A. V. Waghmare, V. P. Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Shipboard microgrid systems for all-electric vessels have emerged as a significant technological trend in the advancement of both commercial and military ship development. While these systems integrate renewable energy sources for greener and cleaner energy generation, this integration may give rise to undesired frequency deviation. To solve this issue, this paper proposed one-third method based tuned PI controller for mitigating the frequency deviation occuring in shipboard microgrid systems. Considered shipboard microgrid model integrated renewable sources and energy storage systems along with the conventional sources. An overall transfer function is developed by considering first order transfer functions of all the components. The obtained overall transfer function is then reduced to an FOPDT model using step response method. This FOPDT model facilitated parameters, which are further utilized in tuning of proportional integral controller. For tuning one-third method based tuning rules are utilized. The tuned PI controller is implemented to shipboard microgrid model, and simulations are performed for performance evaluation. For performance evaluation, frequency deviation responses along with error indices are presented.

title: Stability and Power Quality Reinforcement in Offshore Wind Farms Using Battery-Supported E-STATCOM
authors: Vivek Gedam, S. P. Gawande, Mukesh Yadav, Ajinkya Sonawane, Pradyumn Chaturvedi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of offshore wind energy integration into contemporary power grids has intensified the challenges associated with maintaining voltage stability, ensuring power quality, mitigating harmonic distortion, and providing dynamic reactive power support. Conventional Static Synchronous Compensators (STATCOMs) are extensively utilized for reactive power compensation; however, their inability to supply active power and their limited responsiveness to fast-changing system dynamics constrain their effectiveness. To overcome these shortcomings, this work introduces an Enhanced STATCOM (ESTATCOM), which incorporates a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) into the traditional STATCOM framework. This integration substantially enhances the device's transient and steady-state performance, enabling rapid voltage regulation, effective harmonic suppression, and simultaneous support for active and reactive power exchange under variable load and generation profiles. The proposed hybrid configuration is particularly advantageous in offshore wind farm grid connections, where abrupt power fluctuations and transient disturbances are prevalent. Comprehensive simulation studies carried out in MATLAB/Simulink confirm that the E-STATCOM outperforms conventional designs in improving voltage stability, reducing harmonic distortion, and enhancing overall power quality. Results further indicate that incorporating energy storage not only accelerates system response but also ensures consistent and reliable operation of the wind energy conversion system across a broad range of grid operating conditions.

title: Enhanced Modeling and Implementation of Incremental Encoder-Based FOC for PMSM Drives
authors: Brijendra Sangar, Shilpa Ranjan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Incremental encoders are critical in electric drive systems for delivering precise rotor position and speed feedback. This study develops a detailed MATLAB/Simulink model to simulate an incremental encoder, focusing on accurate signal processing. A novel algorithm determines rotation direction within a quarter of the encoder's angular resolution, ensuring robust quadrature signal (A, B), and Z) interpretation. The simulation computes rotor position from encoder pulses, validated through experimental tests to confirm accuracy and performance under dynamic conditions. This paper proposes a sensor-based control approach for Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) using a Field-Oriented Control (FOC) algorithm deployed on an FPGA controller. FOC is chosen for its superior efficiency in electric vehicle speed regulation. The implementation, detailed using MATLAB and dSPACE 1104, integrates encoder feedback to enhance torque and speed control precision, addressing challenges like signal noise and computational demands for optimal PMSM drive performance.

title: A High-Efficiency Broadband Rectifier With a Coupled-Feedback Network
authors: Tianqi Wang, Wenbo Liu, Tao Wang, Jinghang Qin, Jiazhen Li
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter introduces a novel broadband rectifier integrated with an initial coupled-feedback network (CFN), designed for the application in the realm of wireless power transmission. The rectifier’s architecture employs a transmission line, which has been bent into an L-shape in conjunction with the CFN to form an impedance-matching network. The CFN serves dual purposes: it not only achieves impedance matching but also recycles the rectified energy back to the source for reiterated rectification, effectively broadening the rectification bandwidth. Experimental fabrication and testing of the rectifier demonstrate that at an input power of 8 dBm and a load of 0.9k<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula>, the conversion efficiency exceeds 50% within the 0.7–2.8-GHz frequency range, with a peak efficiency of 70.3% and a relative bandwidth reaching 120%.

title: Integrated Sensing and Communications for Unsourced Random Access: Fundamental Limits and Practical Model
authors: Mohammad Javad Ahmadi, Rafael F. Schaefer, H. Vincent Poor
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This work addresses the problem of integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) involving a massive number of unsourced and uncoordinated users. In the proposed model, known as the unsourced ISAC system (UNISAC), all active communication and sensing users simultaneously share a short frame to transmit their signals without requiring scheduling by the base station or the need to announce their identities. Consequently, the received signal from each user is heavily affected by interference from numerous other users, making it challenging to extract individual transmissions. UNISAC is designed to decode the message sequences from communication users while simultaneously detecting active sensing users and estimating their angles of arrival, regardless of the senders’ identities. We establish a second-order achievable bound for UNISAC that explicitly quantifies performance deviations due to finite resources, and we show that it outperforms ISAC approaches built on traditional multiple access methods, including ALOHA, time-division multiple access (TDMA), treating interference as noise (TIN), and a TDMA-based scheme combined with multiple signal classification for sensing. Additionally, we propose a practical model that validates the feasibility of the achievable result, showing comparable or even superior performance in scenarios with a small number of users. Through numerical simulations, we demonstrate the effectiveness of both the practical UNISAC model and the achievable result.

title: LumaDiff: A Luminance-Aware Diffusion Model for Maritime Image Restoration
authors: Jiajun Yang, Haiwen Yuan, Kuanlei Zheng, Li Cheng, Xuan Li, Changshi Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Glare is particularly severe in maritime environments, causing the loss of image high-frequency information, such as ship names and other critical structural components. A luminance-aware diffusion-based model, termed LumaDiff, is proposed for maritime image restoration under intense glare conditions. As one of the main contributions, a luminance-mask-guided attention mechanism (MG-MHA) is developed to dynamically learn feature representations within glare regions by adaptively modulating attention scores based on the luminance mask. Then, a spatial-frequency multi-branch fusion module is introduced, which enhances fine-grained structural coherence by dynamically fusing spatial and frequency features in parallel. In addition, a new multi-level loss function that minimizes both pixel and perceptual discrepancies is defined to optimize the learning process. Quantitative and qualitative experiments on Singapore maritime dataset (SMD) and maritime vessel dataset (MVD) have been conducted. The multi-branch fusion module performs better than other modules in SSIM, which is of great significance for maritime image restoration. For maritime image restoration from glare interference, the proposed model LumaDiff achieves an SSIM of 0.943 and 1.3% improvement over the baseline.

title: A Microwave Sensing System Based on Transmitarray Antennas for Detecting Low-Density Contaminants in Food Packaged Products
authors: Yuhan Zuo, Qijian Lu, Weiliang Yu, Rui Chen, Pengfei Gu, Mengmeng Li, Francesca Vipiana, Dazhi Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A novel microwave sensing (MWS) system for detecting low-density contaminants in food packaged products is presented in this work. The proposed MWS system is featured by a pair of transmitarray antennas designed at 10 GHz with electrically reconfigurable unit cells. The focus spot of the transmitarray antennas can be scanned and focused at the desired positions along the horizontal or vertical direction in their near-field region. Compared with existing standard MWS systems with fixed waves, the proposed MWS system has a larger and more flexible near-field detection coverage. Moreover, the electric field is enhanced by the transmitarray, which contributes to a better detection. Then, we design a machine learning model, based on the proposed transmitarray antenna, to detect the different low-density contaminants in static oil-filled food jars. Experimental results demonstrate improved performance in the detection accuracy where 100% and 99% are observed with different scanning strategies with the proposed system, while less than 90% with the existing standard ones.

title: Near-HBM Tensor Core Acceleration for Fine-Grained Sparse Matrix-Matrix Multiplication
authors: Lihan Hu, Shiju Li, Hoshik Kim, Jongryool Kim
publish: IEEE Computer Architecture Letters
abstract: Fine-grained structured sparsity, especially the 2:4 pattern, is a practical way to reduce the computation and memory cost of deep learning workloads while preserving model accuracy. NVIDIA Sparse Tensor Cores support such sparsity through metadata-driven sparse matrix-matrix multiplication (SpMM). However, the compressed sparse format introduces indirect and irregular memory accesses, which disrupt data locality and limit performance. We propose TC-AIA, a processing-near-HBM framework for accelerating structured SpMM on Tensor Cores. TC-AIA offloads metadata-driven data gathering to logic near HBM, where scattered accesses are reorganized and packed into dense Tensor-Core-compatible inputs before computation. By removing most indirect accesses from the GPU-side execution path, TC-AIA improves Tensor Core utilization and memory efficiency. Experimental results show that TC-AIA achieves significant speedup and reduces memory traffic across a wide range of structured sparse workloads.

title: A Novel Ultra High-Gain DC–DC Converter for Fuel Cell Electrical Vehicles
authors: Yasir Bashir, Prakash Ji Barnawal, Amarendra Edpuganti
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: To integrate the inherently low output voltage of fuel cell (FC) stacks with the dc-bus or auxiliary energy storage units in fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEVs), high-gain dc-dc converters are often utilized. To address this requirement, this paper proposes an ultra-high gain converter derived from a combination of switched-capacitor cells and a quadratic boost converter, specifically tailored for FCEV applications. The proposed converter reduces voltage stress across semiconductor devices, and it maintains a common ground configuration, ensuring compatibility and simplified integration with the existing vehicle power train. This paper presents the converter's operating principles, design methodology, and a comparison with the state-of-the-art high-gain converter topologies to highlight the merits of the proposed converter. The performance of the proposed converter is verified using a simulation model developed in MATLAB/SIMULINK.

title: Kalman-Based Forecasting of Stock Market Trend
authors: Neelanshu Garg, Amit Kumar Naik, Abhinoy Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a Kalman filter (KF)-based algorithm to predict the stock market price. The financial data of certain companies listed in the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) India for the year 2012 from the official website of BSE were used. The prices of the stock for the past days along with certain technical indices such as the moving average convergence/divergence (MACD), relative strength index (RSI), and stock momentum (SM) were used for the prediction of the price for the next day. The authors first propose a new model wherein the highest stock price is obtained as the linear combination of the MACD, RDI, and SM. Following this, the KF recursively updates the parameters upon the completion of the prediction for the corresponding day. The performance of the algorithm is validated with real-data of stock market for five Indian companies i.e., NTPC, IOCL, Coal India limited, GAIL, and BHEL.

title: Strategic Bidding for Virtual Power Plant in Energy Markets Using New Hybrid MGFO Technique for Energy Scheduling
authors: Choudhary Om Prakash, Altaf Q.H. Badar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The increasing penetration of DERs such as solar, wind, microturbines, and storage has added complexity to power system operations, exposing the limitations of traditional grid tools in handling their variability. Virtual Power Plants (VPPs) tackle this issue by integrating various distributed energy resources (DERs), flexible loads, and storage systems into a unified framework that can actively engage in electricity markets. This work introduces an intelligent bidding approach aimed at optimizing VPP revenue across Day-Ahead (DA), Real-Time (RT), and Balancing Markets, while accounting for the operational limitations of resources such as solar power plants (SPPs), wind power plants (WPPs), and microturbines (MTs) A hybrid metaheuristic algorithm, MGHO (Manta-Gorilla Foraging Optimization), is developed and compared with MILP, MRFO, and GTO. MGFO outperformed all benchmark methods, achieving the highest profit of 41, 311.14, an increase of 12.86% over MRFO and GTO, and 15.08% % over the MILP method over the 24-hour horizon. The optimization problem was modeled and solved in MATLAB, demonstrating that strategic bidding can significantly enhance VPP profitability even in the presence of renewable uncertainty.

title: Effect of Inertia on Power System with Renewable Energy Integration
authors: Pandi K., Ram Jethmalani C. H.
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study investigates the effects of inertia within a microgrid that has high penetration of renewable energy. The research focuses on the frequency response of a power system when the inertia values of a steam turbine generator are varied, specifically in a setup integrated with solar photovoltaic sources. For this analysis, two distinct microgrid power systems were used: a four-bus system and an IEEE 9-bus system. The results demonstrate that a higher inertia constant significantly improves system stability. This improvement is seen through a reduction in frequency changes and a faster recovery time after a disturbance occurs. Furthermore, a side-by-side comparison of cases with and without PV integration sheds light on the complex role of renewables, showing how they can both stabilize and destabilize the system. These findings highlight a critical point: tuning inertia is essential for mitigating transient disturbances and boosting the overall resilience of a power system. As more renewable energy comes online, it often displaces traditional generators that provide rotational inertia, leading to a lower overall system inertia. This decline in inertia makes the grid more vulnerable to frequency deviations caused by sudden load or generation changes. The study ultimately emphasizes that proper inertia tuning, even in systems with a high share of renewables, is a key strategy for maintaining frequency stability and enhancing microgrid resilience during transient events.

title: A Novel Lightweight Symmetric Encryption and Decryption Hardware for CPS Application Security
authors: Priyanka Ram, Vikas Mittal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: With the growing reliance on wireless communication in modern technologies, security remains a critical concern, especially in applications like Remote Keyless Entry [1] (RKE) and smart door systems. This paper presents a secure text encryption and decryption system implemented in Verilog, designed specifically to prevent replay attacks in applications such as smart door locks. The system utilizes symmetric key encryption enhanced with a dynamically generated salt and a counter-based mechanism to ensure message uniqueness. Each character of an input message is encrypted using an XOR operation with a symmetric key and then combined with a salt value and a message counter to form a unique encrypted packet. During decryption, the receiver verifies the counter to detect and reject any repeated or previously used messages, effectively mitigating replay attacks. Simulation results demonstrate that the system successfully encrypts and decrypts messages while identifying and blocking repeated unlock commands based on stale counters. This lightweight and hardware-friendly approach provides a robust security layer for smart locks and other embedded IoT systems, ensuring secure communication with minimal resource overhead..

title: New Scheme of Health Monitoring for SiC Based Three-Phase Inverter
authors: Jegurupati Gowri Prasanth, Priyabrata Shaw, Manoranjan Sahoo
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The thermal management of power semiconductor devices is critical for the reliability and efficiency of modern power electronics systems. This paper presents an optimal method for accurate estimation of the junction temperature (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T_{j}$</tex>) of Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs in a Three-Phase inverter under varying load and switching conditions. Using measured parameters and datasheet parameters such as RMS current, switching energy losses (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E_{o n}, E_{o f f}$</tex>) and a thermal impedance model, the proposed method calculates the total power loss and their corresponding thermal impact. By comparing the estimated junction temperature with the measured <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R_{D S(o n)}$</tex>, it is possible to dynamically adjust the thermal parameters for increased accuracy. The approach can be integrated with real-time monitoring systems in power conversion units and electric drives and offers a reliable diagnostic to monitor the condition of switching devices. The results demonstrate that the algorithm correctly calculates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T_{j}$</tex>, ensuring safe operation and extending the useful life of the device.

title: ZA-ASFE Based Advanced Technique for 1-Phase Grid Connected SPV System
authors: Dheeraj Kumar, Sanjeev Singh, Shailendra Kumar, Rahul Arora
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a single phase grid connected solar photo-voltaic (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 P-G C-S P V$</tex>) system without any battery storage for effective operation under various dynamic condition using the zero attracting (ZA) and Andrew sine function estimation (ASFE). The combined controller represented as ZA-ASFE controller has an excellent performance in various dynamic working scenarios with linear/non-linear loads. The complete system is modeled and simulated for rating of 2.5 Kw and 750 VA in the MATLAB/SIMULINK software. The simulated performance is presented and validated on a laboratory scale hardware setup to demonstrate effective control as desired.

title: Fusion-Based Switched Kalman Filter for State-of-Charge Estimation in Electric Vehicles Under Dynamic Conditions: A Real-Time Validation
authors: D S R S L Avanthika, Ramanjaneya Reddy Udumula, Bhamidi Lokeshgupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The battery is a critical component in Electric Vehicles (EV's), and accurate State of Charge (SoC) estimation is essential for the efficient functioning of Battery Management Systems (BMS). Traditional methods like Coulomb Counting (CC) are prone to integration drift over time, while Open Circuit Voltage (OCV) methods offer limited accuracy under dynamic load conditions. To address these challenges, this study proposes a Switch Mode Kalman Filter (KF) approach, which intelligently switches between CC during load conditions and OCV during rest periods. To simulate the dynamic behavior of EV operations, a Hybrid Pulse Power Characterization (HPPC) test was conducted on a Lithium-ion (Li-ion) cell. Real-time validation using OPALRT confirms the improved performance of the proposed method. The traditional Kalman Filter achieved an RMSE of 0.1085 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.7077, whereas the proposed SM-KF achieved a lower RMSE of 0.0548 and a higher <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.9253, demonstrating superior estimation accuracy. By utilizing OCV data during rest phases, the switch mode KF effectively corrects CC drift, making it highly suitable for real-world EV applications that frequently alternate between driving and idle conditions. The results highlight the robustness and adaptability of the proposed approach for real-time SoC estimation.

title: Dual-Loop PD Control for Time Delayed Double Integrating Processes Using a Smith Predictor
authors: Prabir Singha, Dipjyoti Das, Sudipta Chakraborty
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Double integrating processes with large time constants, common in industries like chemical processing and bioreactors, are challenging to control due to their instability and sensitivity to disturbances. This work proposes a simple yet effective PD-PD control framework based on the Smith Predictor, incorporating inner and outer loops for servo tracking and disturbance rejection. Controllers are tuned using analytical and IMC-based methods with the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M_{s}$</tex> criterion. The approach, requiring minimal tuning parameters, demonstrates improved stability and performance on benchmark models.

title: Design and Implementation of a Multi-Protocol Architecture for Scalable IoT Systems
authors: V Kavin Ishwar, Suresh Kumar Gawre
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative multi-protocol communication architecture for IoT systems that is designed to integrate heterogeneous protocols within a single scalable network. With the growing prevalence of sensor-based IoT devices across various sectors, the ESP32 platform is highlighted as a popular and cost-effective solution for developing versatile IoT applications from home automation to industrial monitoring. The proposed architecture combines a static-channel peer-to-peer communication protocol (ESP-NOW) for low-latency specific communication, HTTP for web-based user interfaces, and wired bridge (UART) connectivity for seamless integration, creating a robust multi-protocol system capable of supporting future expansions. To demonstrate its applicability, a preliminary sensor-based module for an Automatic Electricity Cutoff System (AECS) is presented. This system detects potential non-requirement of energy by sensing latch positions, which enables energy savings and enhanced safety. The architecture also provides a web interface for direct user interaction with the hardware network.

title: Optimal Placement of PMUs Using AO Algorithm by Considering Data Traffic and Cost Installation Indices in Smart Grid
authors: Vedik Basetti, Chandan K Shiva, Sachidananda Sen, Sruthi Ankam, Shriram S. Rangarajan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) with 120 samples per second generates huge amount of data that can sometimes leads to network congestion when transmitting this data in bulk quantities over the network lines. To address this issue, by placing the PMU optimally across the system can reduce the data traffic and help alleviate any traffic overload. To undertake this challenge, a Wide Area Monitoring System (WAMS) data traffic model has been considered. The main contribution of the paper is to propose a new binary Artemisinin optimization (AO) technique to minimize the number of PMUs required to make the system observability by simultaneously considering data traffic index and the installation cost using data traffic and cost installation indices. The suggested method is tested by implementing it on benchmark systems of IEEE 14 and IEEE 30 bus systems. The results obtained are compared with other techniques suggested in the previous studies, which show the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: A Novel Single-Stage AC-AC Converter Topology with Reduced Voltage Stress for Solid-State Transformers
authors: Rupesh Kumar Yadav, Raj Moyal, Mukesh Kumar Pathak
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel single-phase, singlestage, high-frequency isolated AC-AC converter topology for solid-state transformer (SST) applications based on the LLC resonant converter. The proposed topology incorporates a rectifier bridge connected to the AC input, after which the rectified signal is converted into a high-frequency alternating signal by an active half-bridge circuit. A series resonant tank is placed in series with the transformer's primary winding to achieve soft switching. A semi-active rectifier is implemented at the secondary terminal, where one leg comprises diodes and the other consists of controlled switches to eliminate the back-power flow. This configuration generates a pulsating DC output. After that, an unfolding bridge transforms it into a synchronized AC waveform. The converter replaces electrolytic capacitors with film capacitors. The steady-state analysis is carried out using the Fundamental Harmonic Approximation (FHA) method, and unified soft switching conditions are analysed. The theoretical analysis is further validated through a 10 kW simulation and 1 kW experimental results, which offer more reliable and highpower density.

title: Crowd Monitoring and Control Using Joint Face Super-Resolution, Head Pose Estimation and Face Verification
authors: Ramakant Soni, Pawan K. Ajmera, Ayush Srivastava, Yatharth Agarwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In large-scale public events, effective crowd control is very critical to ensure safety, optimize movement flow and enable secure access. Most of existing surveillance systems operate with low-resolution imagery and lack the ability to extract detailed identity and behavioral information under such constraints. This paper presents a combined deep learning framework that jointly performs face super-resolution (FSR), head pose estimation (HPE) and face verification to enable intelligent, real-time crowd monitoring and control. Unlike traditional methods that treat these components independently, this approach integrates them into a multi-task architecture which is optimized via pose-aware and identity-aware feedback. The system enhances low-resolution facial inputs, estimates head orientation for movement direction inference and identifies individuals for access control and tracking. It monitors sector-wise population density and issues redirection signals when overcrowding is detected. Extensive evaluations on benchmark datasets (AFLW2000, CASIA-WebFace, CelebA, LFW, and VGGFace2) demonstrate substantial technical advancements: super-resolution performance improves PSNR from 11.24 dB to 17.91 dB and SSIM from 0.12 to 0.35; head pose estimation achieves mean absolute errors of 3.04° (yaw), 1.06° (pitch), and 0.76° (roll); and facial verification attains 93.26% in-domain (CelebA) and 86.15% cross-domain (LFW) accuracy. The proposed framework offers scalable and modular solution for real-world applications enabling smart surveillance, public safety and efficient crowd management.

title: A LiDAR-Inertial Mapping and Voice-Driven Goal-Setting Framework for Autoware-Based Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Akshat Tambi, Aditya Jambhale, Prerna Sharma, Yashowardhan Singh, Manikandan Ganesan, Sivanathan Kandhasamy, Bharatiraja Chokkalingam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Accurate and real-time localization is a critical requirement for autonomous vehicle navigation, especially in the context of large, complex environments such as university campuses. In this paper, we present a mapping and simulation framework that incorporates FAST-LIO (a tightly coupled LiDAR-inertial odometry system) integrated with the Autoware autonomous driving stack. We generate a high-resolution point cloud map of the campus using FAST-LIO and a Livox Mid360 LiDAR with an inertial measurement unit (IMU). The point cloud map is subsequently processed into a form usable in Autoware and incorporated into a planning and simulation workflow. Our framework for integrated mapping and simulation takes advantage of the point cloud data for localization and global path planning. We evaluate the system by performing a series of simulation experiments demonstrating aspects of localization accuracy and planning robustness. The results indicate that integrating maps generated with FAST-LIO into Autoware-based planning systems is feasible and effective, offering a reproducible workflow that can support future research in campus-scale autonomous navigation. In addition, we describe a voice-command module that supports user natural language navigation commands that are interpreted with a large language model and transformed into goal positions in the Autoware simulation. This allows a more interactive user experience, highlighting the potential of LLMs in the autonomous vehicle control interface.

title: Model-Free Variable Impedance Control of Redundant Manipulators for Soft Tissue Puncture
authors: Hongde Liao, Xin Wang, Zhijun Zhang, Ning Tan
publish: IEEE/CAA Journal of Automatica Sinica
abstract: Robotic-assisted medical technology has long been a key area of research in modern surgical medicine. Robotic-assisted puncture techniques, both theoretically and practically, hold significant potential to improve puncture precision and overall surgical outcomes in clinical practice. This paper presents a model-free variable impedance control (MFVIC) method for robotic soft tissue puncture tasks, enabling high-precision puncture of soft tissues using variable impedance control without requiring model information. Conventional position- or force-based control methods often fail to ensure the precision of puncture or maintain an appropriate puncture force, both of which are critical for the task. The proposed variable impedance control approach allows for accurate puncture to the desired location while maintaining low puncture force throughout the puncture process, thus effectively meeting the demands of the puncture task. Additionally, a Jacobian matrix estimator is designed to estimate the Jacobian matrix of the redundant robotic arm in real-time during operation. This enables precise robot control using sensor data, without the need for prior knowledge of the robot model.

title: Architectural Strategies for Automotive Predictive Maintenance: Balancing System Efficiency and Security through Advanced Risk Analysis
authors: Constantin-Laurentiu Paraschiv, Franco Cirillo, Christian Esposito, Rahamatullah Khondoker, Mohammed Salman, Marius Constantin Vochin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Vehicles
abstract: The increasing complexity of modern vehicles calls for advanced predictive maintenance strategies to ensure safety, reliability, and security. While preventive maintenance improves vehicle lifespan, growing connectivity exposes systems to cybersecurity threats that may compromise operational integrity. This paper proposes architectural strategies for automotive predictive maintenance, balancing system performance with safety and cybersecurity. The Holistic Vehicle-Cloud Network Architecture (HVNA) integrates Internet of Things (IoT) devices, cloud computing, and machine learning to predict and mitigate failures in both in-vehicle and over-the-air communications. Cybersecurity assessment follows the International Organization for Standardization/Society of Automotive Engineers (ISO/SAE) 21434 standard, combining Medini Analysis with Threat Analysis and Risk Assessment (TARA) and the Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service and Elevation of Privilege (STRIDE) model to identify, classify, and mitigate vulnerabilities across system components and communication channels. Results reveal 162 threats in the vehicle architecture and 198 in the external communication architecture, including a notable share of high-level threats (1.8% and 5.6%, respectively). These findings highlight the importance of integrating predictive maintenance with robust cybersecurity measures to ensure resilience in connected and autonomous vehicles.

title: Development of an infrared trace gas detection system adapted to a six degree-of-freedom rotary Stewart parallel robot
authors: Bin Li, Shiwen Wang, Chenghao Han
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: A near-infrared trace gas detection system based on TDLAS-WMS technique is presented in this paper. It has been developed and is integrated to work together with a six degree-of-freedom rotary Stewart parallel robot to carry out remote and highly sensitive trace gas detection tasks in missions including disaster relief and firefighting. The DFB laser at 1654 nm is used as the light source in the system, and circuits such as the laser temperature control circuit, laser drive circuit, and phase-locked amplifier are independently developed. These electrical and optical devices are integrated in an instrument case and connected to the gas cell fixed on the robot through optical fibers. This article tests the performance of the independently developed circuits described above through experimental tests. The test results verify the reliability and effectiveness of the system, and it can meet the requirements of the gas detection system and replace commercial instruments. Compared with other forms of gas detection systems, the detection system proposed in this paper has the advantages of non-contact detection, a large detection range, high sensitivity, and a fast response speed. Through the testing of methane gas, various performance aspects of the detection system have been verified, including detection stability and the lower detection limit. The experiment results verified the feasibility of the proposed detection system.

title: Time-Varying Graph Signal Reconstruction with Temporal Filter Identification
authors: Shuyang Feng, Rui Yang, Xintong Shi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Graph signal reconstruction aims to recover signals from incomplete or noisy observations by exploiting correlations induced by the underlying graph topology. Although recent works have extended reconstruction methods from static or band-limited graph signals to time-varying settings, many existing approaches rely on a priori smoothness assumptions on temporal signal differences, which correspond to restrictive temporal filter structures. To enhance flexibility and applicability, a temporal filtering framework that does not rely on a predefined temporal filtering model is proposed for the joint reconstruction of time-varying graph signals and the estimation of temporal filters. To address the practical challenge of unknown filter order, a joint signal reconstruction and filter identification method is further developed to simultaneously estimate both filter coefficients and their effective order. The resulting optimization problems are efficiently solved using the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM). Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in capturing both nodal characteristics and the temporal evolution of graph signals.

title: CC-SDSN: a Collaborative Control Plane for Software Defined LEO Satellite Networks
authors: Yihua Peng, Wei Su, Lisheng Ma, Shuoyu Mao, Zhuojia Yang, Xiaohong Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Available control plane designs for Software Defined LEO Satellite Networks (SDSN) suffer from the problems of high network update latency, large flow setup time, and low network throughput. This paper proposes a new collaborative control plane architecture for SDSN (CC-SDSN), which consists of a Network Control Center (NCC) and multiple Ground Relay Stations (GRSs) in the terrestrial networks, as well as multiple Satellite Group Controllers (SGCs) in the LEO satellite networks. Each SGC maintains the local view of a group of LEO satellites and supports the quick intra-group flow setup. The NCC takes advantage of the powerful computation resources in the terres trial nodes to maintain a global view of the whole LEO satellite network and is responsible for the rapid inter-group flow setup, while the GRSs are used to ensure the real-time communication between the NCC and SGCs and thus to reduce the network update latency. The designs of the protocol for handling data f low and synchronization operations in CC-SDSN, and the pre caching scheme for inter-group flow tables are addressed as well. Finally, extensive experiments based on Satellite Tool Kit (STK) and Mininet are conducted to demonstrate the efficiency of CC SDSN in terms of network update latency, flow setup time, and network throughput.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Spectrum

title: Early Functional Safety and PPA evaluation for faster digital design development
authors: Michelangelo Bartolomucci, David Kingston, Teo Cupaiuolo, Alessandra Nardi, Riccardo Cantoro
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The use of semiconductor devices in safety-critical applications is increasing in both volume and complexity. Applications in markets such as automotive, data centers, and aerospace have dependability requirements that impose additional safety and reliability metrics beyond the traditional Power, Performance, and Area (PPA). Semiconductor devices for safety-critical applications incorporate protection logic to enhance resilience against random failures (i.e., they are hardened). Although automation to optimize PPA and Time-to-Market (TTM) is well established, further effort is required to incorporate safety as a first-class metric in the design process. This paper presents a novel approach that supports safety requirements from Register Transfer Level (RTL) exploration through implementation, thereby minimizing costly design iterations. A Safety Specification Format (SSF) is used to explore different protection levels and safety mechanisms at the RTL stage and to seamlessly implement the selected configuration. The approach is evaluated on the CV32E40P and CVA6 open-source RISC-V processors and on ISCAS89 benchmarks. Validation is performed through trend comparison and quantitative correlation against traditional implementation methodologies across multiple designs.

title: Distance-Domain Degrees of Freedom in Near-Field Region
authors: Son T. Duong, Tho Le-Ngoc
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Extremely large aperture array operating in the near-field regime unlock additional spatial resources, which can be exploited to simultaneously serve multiple users even when they share the same angular direction. This work investigate the distance-domain degrees of freedom (DoF), which are the DoF when the user only varies its distance to the base station and not the angle. To obtain the distance-domain DoF, we investigate a line-of-sight (LoS) channel between a base station (source) and observation region representing users. The base station is modeled as a large two-dimensional transmit (Tx) array with an arbitrary shape. The observation region is modeled as an arbitrarily long linear receive (Rx) array, where elements are collinearly aligned but located at varying distances from the Tx array. We assume that both the Tx and Rx array has continuous aperture with an infinite number of elements and infinitesimal spacing, which establishes an upper bound for the distance-domain DoF in the case of finite number of elements. First, we assume an ideal case where the Tx array is a single piece and the Rx array is on the broad side of the Tx array. By reformulating the channel as an integral operator with a Hermitian convolution kernel, we derive a closed-form expression for the distance-domain DoF via the Fourier transform. Our analysis shows that the distance-domain DoF is predominantly determined by the extreme boundaries of both the Tx and Rx arrays rather than its detailed interior structure. We further extend the framework to non-broadside configurations by employing a projection method that converts the problem to an equivalent broadside case. Finally, we extend our analytical framework to the modular array, which shows the distance-domain DoF gain over the single-piece array given the constraint of the physical length of the array.

title: Optimal Design of a High-Efficiency Permanent Magnet Assisted Synchronous Reluctance Motor Considering Anti-Demagnetization Performance
authors: Xiao Liu, Wei Zhao, Meng Lu, Dongpei Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: To address the issues of low efficiency and susceptibility to demagnetization in ferrite permanent magnets for Permanent Magnet Assisted Synchronous Reluctance Motors(PMaSynRMs), an optimal design method that comprehensively considers anti-demagnetization performance is proposed. Based on anti-demagnetization analysis, design variables are determined under the constraint of a 5% maximum fault-current demagnetization rate. Moreover, a self-iterative Expected Improvement (EI) criterion-based Kriging model is proposed, which automatically concludes the adding points process and improves prediction accuracy of the surrogate model. Subsequently, multi-objective optimization focused on improving efficiency and torque is performed using a multi-objective particle swarm optimization (MOPSO) algorithm. The optimized motor achieves an efficiency of 94.9%, representing a 2.9% improvement over the initial design, while the maximum demagnetization rate of the ferrite under fault conditions is controlled within 4.91%. Experiments confirm an efficiency of 94.8% under rated operating conditions, a wide high-efficiency operating region, and satisfactory anti-demagnetization performance.

title: NVC-1B: Scaling up Neural Video Coding Models
authors: Chuanbo Tang, Xihua Sheng, Li Li, Dong Liu, Feng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Emerging large models have achieved notable progress in the fields of natural language processing and computer vision. However, large models for neural video coding are still unexplored. In this paper, we try to explore how to build a large neural video coding model. Based on a small baseline model, we gradually scale up the model sizes of its different coding parts, including the motion encoder-decoder, motion entropy model, contextual encoder-decoder, contextual entropy model, and temporal context mining module, and analyze the influence of model sizes on video compression performance. Then, we explore using different architectures, including CNN, mixed CNN-Transformer, and Transformer architectures, to implement the neural video coding model and analyze the influence of model architectures on video compression performance. Based on our exploration results, we design the first neural video coding model having more than 1 billion parameters – NVC-1B. Experimental results show that our large model achieves a significant video compression performance improvement over recent state-of-the-art neural video compression models. With the continuous advancement in hardware and the successful on-device deployment of large models, we anticipate that our proposed large neural video coding model can bring video coding technologies to the next level.

title: An automatic identification method for linear discontinuities on high-steep slopes based on thermal infrared imaging: A case study in the Jinsha River suture zone
authors: Wen Zhang, Qing Liu, Jia Wang, Han Yin, Yaoyao Wang, Junqi Chen, Xiaohan Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Discontinuities are critical factors that influence slope stability. While planar discontinuities can be reliably identified, linear discontinuities remain challenging to identify because of their concealed nature and limited surface exposure. This study introduces an improved neighborhood search algorithm that utilizes thermal infrared (TIR) images to identify linear discontinuities. The method identifies discontinuities based on their thermal characteristics, using temperature differences between intact rock and discontinuities. To evaluate the effectiveness of the algorithm, a typical high-steep rock slope in the upper Jinsha River region was selected. High-resolution visible and TIR images were acquired using an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV), and the method was applied to identify and validate discontinuities. Comparison with the results of manual identification indicated a positive correlation between discontinuity scale and identification rate. For linear discontinuities exceeding 10 m in trace length, the identification rate reached 70.5%. The method also demonstrated potential advantages in capturing temperature difference boundaries caused by hot air movement and in delineating unstable areas, as well as open discontinuities and rockfalls. This research provides a new idea for the automatic identification of linear discontinuities in complex slope conditions and offers valuable insights for rock mass engineering evaluation.

title: DHTM: Decrypting Hashing Training Method for valid localization of encrypted images
authors: Jingpeng Han, Jiajing Wu, Che Liu, Qian Ma, Zhiqiang Wei, Qingliang Li, Tiejun Cui
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Deep hashing algorithms have been widely adopted in remote sensing image localization tasks, benefiting from their superior storage efficiency and computational speed. Despite their convenience and high efficiency, the reliance on convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for feature extraction and hash code generation renders them vulnerable to noise and various forms of encryption interference. This vulnerability significantly degrades the localization performance of such algorithms in remote sensing scenarios, posing a critical challenge to their practical deployment. To address this pressing issue, we propose a novel anti-interference decryption method for remote sensing image hashing. The core insight of our approach is to construct adversarial samples that closely mimic real-world interference patterns, which are then utilized to retrain the original hashing network, thereby enhancing its robustness against encryption interference. Specifically, guided by class center hash codes generated from carefully screened high-quality samples, our method trains the deep hashing network by constructing adversarial interference samples embedded with distinct category information. This design ensures that the retrained decryption network can accurately identify and decrypt encrypted interference images, enabling reliable and effective remote sensing image localization. Compared with traditional anti-interference algorithms, the proposed method exhibits greater targeting and effectiveness in the field of remote sensing image hashing, owing to its tailored design for the unique characteristics of remote sensing data and interference patterns. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the network trained with our method can maintain accurate localization judgments even when confronted with encrypted interference images, achieving efficient and robust remote sensing scene localization performance.
