title: Dual-wavelength Generation in NPR Mode-locked Fiber Laser Relying on Active Insertion Loss Control
authors: Jianing Tao, Churan Zhang, Minglie Hu, Youjian Song
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: In this work, an easily triggered dual-wavelength nonlinear polarization rotation (NPR) mode-locked fiber laser enabled by insertion loss control (ILC) is implemented. Dual-wavelength pulse operation is obtained by simply tuning intra-cavity loss. Without the need for the periodic filtering effect typically employed in dual-wavelength lasers, ILC also allows reproducible switching among various single-wavelength and dual-wavelength mode-locking states at its specific angle. Our work demonstrates a novel approach for reproducibly achieving dual-wavelength in spatial NPR mode-locked lasers. This flexible dual-wavelength laser source may be used for various high-precision applications, such as absolute ranging and molecular spectroscopy.

title: Finite-Horizon Distributed Optimal Consensus Control of Discrete-Time Homogeneous Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Wei Wang, Ke Wang, Chaoxu Mu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: This paper addresses a critical gap in multi-agent systems control by introducing a model-free approach for finite-horizon distributed optimal consensus control of discrete-time homogeneous leader-following systems. While existing model-free optimal consensus methods focus exclusively on infinite-horizon scenarios with asymptotic convergence, they prove inadequate for time-sensitive applications requiring consensus within predetermined time frames. We propose a time-varying Q-function (TVQfn) framework that uniquely addresses the time-varying nature of finite-horizon optimal control without requiring system dynamics knowledge. Through local consensus error formulation, we transform the finite-horizon optimal consensus problem into a global consensus error-based regulation problem and demonstrate that its solution is equivalent to solving time-varying Riccati equations via distributed control protocols. Our key theoretical contribution establishes that global optimal consensus performance can be achieved through purely local control actions with appropriately designed coupling gain. The proposed TVQfn methodology enables direct estimation of time-varying Riccati equation solutions using linear algebraic computations, eliminating iterative updates and function approximation errors common in existing learning approaches. Simulation results demonstrate superior finite-horizon consensus performance compared to established infinite-horizon methods.

title: Theoretical and experimental study of the freestanding-mode triboelectric nanogenerators for humidity sensors
authors: Haosen Li, Wenjing Ju, Zhinan Zhong, Xiaofeng Liu, Qidi Fu, Xi Lu, Zhiyong Wei
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Triboelectric nanogenerators exhibit considerable potential for energy harvesting and sensing applications; however, their output performance is notably impacted by variations in environmental humidity. This study presents the design and fabrication of a humidity sensor utilizing a free-standing triboelectric nanogenerator. The theoretical modeling and experimental evaluation of its output characteristics are conducted. A finite element model of the freestanding-mode triboelectric nanogenerators is also established to perform transient simulation via the dynamic mesh method. It is demonstrated that the proposed electrodynamic theoretical model, which incorporates edge effects, can accurately predict the output voltage across varying rotational speeds and humidity levels. We also investigated the output characteristics of freestanding-mode triboelectric nanogenerator based on three distinct triboelectric materials under varying humidity conditions. It is shown that the linearity of the sensor can be enhanced to R² = 0.996 through a material combination strategy. Moreover, the study effectively decoupled the influences of rotational speed and humidity on output voltage by employing continuous wavelet transform on the time-domain signal. The approach facilitated the simultaneous extraction of both humidity and rotational speed from a single output voltage measurement. The proposed freestanding-mode triboelectric nanogenerator-based humidity sensor exhibits rapid response characteristics (response time = 3.3s) and facilitates real-time, self-powered monitoring of environmental humidity, presenting potential applications in the field of humidity monitoring.

title: Resource-Efficient Adaptive Security for Rolling-Shutter VLC Sensors Using Lightweight Reinforcement Learning
authors: Puneet Pandey, Sandeep Joshi
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Rolling-shutter visible light communication (RS-VLC) enables data reception on commodity complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS) image sensors; however, it is highly sensitive to exposure-time variations, motion-induced distortion, signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) jitter, and geometry-dependent eavesdropping. Battery-powered camera receivers therefore face a coupled energy-security trade-off: selecting higher security modes accelerates battery depletion, whereas under-provisioning increases vulnerability to RS-aware attacks. Existing VLC security strategies are predominantly static and cannot adapt to rapidly changing optical conditions, whereas neural network-based reinforcement learning incurs prohibitive computational overhead for lightweight embedded receivers. This work develops a lightweight adaptive physical-layer security controller for RS-VLC based on tabular Q-learning over a compact discretization of battery level and received SNR. The controller requires no offline pre-training, learns entirely online, and maintains a negligible memory and computational footprint, making it compatible with camera-based embedded platforms. Using monotone dynamic programming arguments, we derive sufficient conditions under which a stationary policy admits a two-threshold structure in the joint battery-SNR domain. Both expected-environment dynamic programming and model-free reinforcement learning are observed to produce policies consistent with this monotone two-threshold form across the discretized state space. A physically grounded simulation framework incorporating rolling-shutter exposure dynamics, Peukert-inspired battery discharge, retransmissions, RS-induced noise, and a phenomenological logistic eavesdropper model is used for evaluation. Simulation results show that the learned policy achieves battery lifetime comparable to an expected-environment dynamic programming (DP) benchmark (approximately 102 decision slots) while yielding lower average attacker success probability than static and heuristic baselines. Across stochastic channel realizations and quantization ablations, adaptive policies maintain attack-success probabilities in the 5-6% range without sacrificing energy efficiency. These results indicate that simple, structure-consistent tabular reinforcement learning can provide a practical and deployable approach to adaptive physical-layer security in battery-limited RS-VLC receivers under realistic operating conditions.

title: Scattered-Power Substructure Characteristic Mode Formulation via Hybrid Vector Spherical Wave–MoM Method: Comparative Analysis of Scattering-Matrix- and Impedance-Matrix-Based Formulations
authors: Hongwei Ren, Yuanxi Cao, Penghao Feng, Sen Yan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In this paper, we introduce an alternative scattered-power substructure characteristic mode (CM) formulation based on a hybrid method combining vector spherical wave expansions and the method of moments (MoMs). Compared with the impedance-matrix-based eigenvalue problem, the proposed formulation involves matrices of smaller size, thereby reducing the computational burden associated with the eigenvalue decomposition. By applying the proposed hybrid approach, we also establish an equivalent form of the power-operator-based generalized eigenvalue equation. To further demonstrate the necessity of the hybrid method, this article investigates the impact of passive loading on several existing substructure CM formulations. The algebraic relations verify that these formulations are equivalent when the passive loading is lossless. In lossy passive-loading scenarios, however, the scattering-matrix-based formulations do not admit corresponding impedance-matrix-based formulations. In contrast, the CM formulations developed via the hybrid method remain equivalent to those based purely on the MoM. Several numerical examples are presented to validate the proposed formulations.

title: A Sector-Grounded Metasurface Windshield Antenna with Uniform Downtilted Beam for Wide-Azimuth V2X Coverage
authors: Wen Jun Zhou, Bai Hao Zhang, Zhi Xing Chen, Jia Jia Chen, Shao Zhang, Gu Ying Deng, Huafeng Su, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: At V2X frequencies, automotive windshields behave as electrically thick dielectric waveguides due to their high permittivity and substantial thickness. When used as antenna substrates, they support strong surface waves, leading to radiation distortion and reduced efficiency. This paper presents a sector-grounded metasurface antenna for inclined windshields that manages the supported surface wave modes rather than simply suppressing them. Modal analysis shows that about 68.1% of the input power is trapped in the TM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> surface wave on the grounded windshield. A half-wavelength slotline, fed by a coaxial-to-CPW transition and etched in the sector ground, acts as a surface wave launcher (SWL) to excite a cylindrical TM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> surface wave, while the sector ground regularizes its wavefront over wide azimuthal ranges. A radially modulated metasurface then converts the bound TM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> surface wave into a uniformly downtilted beam with wide-azimuth coverage. When the unconverted waves reach the finite ground edges, the surface impedance discontinuity at the grounded-to-ungrounded transition scatters the remaining energy and excites TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> surface waves in the ungrounded region. To suppress these harmful waves and stabilize the radiation in large windshield implementations, a peripheral spatial bandstop filter is introduced around the sector ground. Measurements of a prototype implemented on a 40°-inclined windshield with a 300 × 300 mm² aperture demonstrate stable wide-azimuth coverage, with a peak gain of 5.61 dBi, a 90° half-power beamwidth in the azimuth plane, and radiation efficiency exceeding 60% over 5.8-6.0 GHz, thereby validating the proposed antenna for large-scale windshield integration.

title: Gas Component Detection and Calibration Technology for GIS Based on Optical Frequency Comb
authors: Zheng Liu, Yiming Zang, Haoran Wang, Wei Liu, Jingzheng Huang, Xiuchen Jiang
publish: IEEE Instrumentation & Measurement Magazine
abstract: Detection of SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf> Decomposition Components in Gas-Insulated Switchgear (GIS) is of significant importance for fault diagnosis. While infrared spectroscopy is a commonly used gas detection method with good stability and fast response, it still faces challenges such as unstable light sources, low [resolution, and insufficient interaction between the gas and the optical path. This study employs an optical frequency comb (OFC) light source centered near 1550 nm. Leveraging its uniform frequency spacing and high-resolution characteristics, the source effectively enhances the detection accuracy of gas absorption spectra. An enhanced long-path gas absorption cell was designed to achieve fifty round-trip reflections of the light beam within the chamber, resulting in an effective optical path length of 50 m, which was further validated by Monte Carlo ray-tracing simulations. This design significantly improves the gas-light interaction effect and consequently increases the gas absorption rate. By preparing CO and H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S gas mixtures at various concentrations, absorption spectrum measurements were conducted for both gases across different concentration levels. The corresponding absorbance values at each concentration were calculated by fitting the data with the Voigt line shape function to more accurately account for Doppler and collisional broadening effects. A positive correlation between gas absorbance and concentration was established by fitting the experimental data based on the Lambert-Beer law. Uncertainty propagation analysis was conducted to quantify measurement errors. This methodology enables the precise calibration of SF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf> decomposition-related components (CO, H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S) in GIS, providing robust support for the detection and diagnosis of power equipment fault types.

title: QoS-Aware UAV Service Scheduling for Timely IoT Data Aggregation in Dynamic Environments
authors: Pushpak Shukla, Shailendra Shukla
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: In dynamic IoT environments, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) serve as mobile service agents collecting data from dispersed mobile devices, such as wearables/smartphones and BLE-tagged assets in indoor warehouses and pop-up events, and sensors on UGVs/responders in disaster-response perimeters. Ensuring the timeliness of collected information is crucial for Quality of Service (QoS), with Age of Information (AoI) as a key metric. This paper presents a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) based framework for adaptive UAV service scheduling that jointly optimizes data freshness, trajectory efficiency, and energy usage. We model the service scheduling problem under realistic constraints, including device mobility, limited UAV battery, and inter-agent diversity. We model indoor IoT mobility as Brownian motion and optimize a multi-objective reward (multi-level AoI, energy, spatial diversity). ACDQN augments DQN with residual blocks and contrastive learning, improving representation quality and policy stability. Experiments across diverse mobility patterns demonstrate that ACDQN improves data aggregation performance, achieves more efficient trajectories, and significantly lowers AoI. Results show Brownian motion induces up to 7 times higher device displacement than random walk, a 16% reward gain with multi-level AoI modeling, over 8% improvement in dynamic settings versus baselines, and measurable benefits from incorporating diversity bonuses.

title: Attitude-Position Integrated Control for Spacecraft Formation With Predefined-Time Convergence
authors: Yu-Yao Wu, Yaoyuan Zeng, Yuanyuan Zhang, Hui-Jie Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: This article investigates the attitude-position integrated control of spacecraft formation in the presence of inertia uncertainties and external disturbances. The mathematical model of the spacecraft formation attitude-position integrated control system is established using dual quaternion representation. A novel predefined-time extended state observer (ESO) is developed to estimate the total perturbation, which includes both inertial uncertainties and external disturbances in the system model. Compared with existing predefined-time ESOs, the proposed observer eliminates redundant differential terms and provides a more rigorous theoretical proof. Based on the estimates obtained from this predefined-time ESO, a predefined-time attitude-position integrated control scheme for spacecraft formation described by dual quaternions is proposed for the first time. Furthermore, the closed-loop system’s predefined-time stability is proven using Lyapunov stability theory. Finally, a series of comparative simulations validates the performance and effectiveness of the proposed predefined-time ESO and the presented control strategy.

title: GKit-SSRDecoder: an open-source C/C++-based PPP-B2b and HAS decoding and formatted product generation software
authors: Zhiyuan Wu, Pan Li, Jiahuan Hu, Jingkai Yuan, Mingbao Wei, Yang Sun, Bao Shu, Bobin Cui, Guanwen Huang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With the rapid development of Real-Time Precise Point Positioning (RT-PPP), the BeiDou-3 PPP-B2b and Galileo High Accuracy Service (HAS) have become pivotal for providing free, global, satellite-based precise correction services. However, most existing open-source decoding tools are developed based on the Python language, which limits their integration into embedded systems and real-time C/C++ positioning frameworks. In addition, these raw data streams lack standardized input formats and product generation, and do not take into account user-side satellite antenna calibration, which hinders offline analysis and cross-platform verification. To bridge these gaps, this study develops and presents the GKit-SSRDecoder, an open-source, high-performance C/C++-based software designed for the end-to-end processing of PPP-B2b and HAS messages. The software features a modular architecture that supports multi-receiver raw data stream parsing, State Space Representation (SSR) message decoding, and the automated generation of RINEX-compliant precise orbit, clock, and code bias products. Comprehensive evaluations were conducted using data from diverse GNSS receivers and compared against post-processed products. The results demonstrated that the Signal-In-Space Range Errors (SISRE) for the PPP-B2b service is 0.093 m and 0.086 m for GPS and BDS-3, respectively, while the HAS service achieves 0.057 m for GPS and 0.048 m for Galileo. Positioning tests showed that the generated products enable RT-PPP to reach centimeter-to-decimeter-level accuracy in static and kinematic modes. Specifically, in real-time kinematic scenarios, the positioning performance of the PPP-B2b and HAS services achieves RMS values of 0.13/0.11/0.12 m and 0.18/0.12/0.23 m in the East/North/Up directions, respectively. This software serves as a useful and portable tool for the GNSS community, facilitating the industrial application and scientific research of satellite-based augmentation services.

title: A Measurement-Oriented Spatio-Temporal Subspace Network for Monitoring Nonstationary Industrial Processes
authors: Jian Huang, Jianbo Yu, Lorenzo Ciani, Bing Song, Yu Sun, Xiaofeng Yang, Yuping Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: A fundamental challenge in industrial instrumentation is the reliable measurement and monitoring of nonstationary processes, where the sensitivity and accuracy of traditional fault detection systems are frequently jeopardized by changing spatial and temporal variations. This paper introduces the Extended Temporal and Spatial Stationary Subspace Network (ETS<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>N), a novel framework designed to enhance measurement reliability by jointly modeling temporal homogeneity and spatial heterogeneity in multivariate process signals. In order to maintain the time-space-dependent coherence necessary for local stationarity assumptions, the architecture incorporates three essential units: (1) the Temporal Homogeneity Enumeration Unit (THEU), which reinforces intrinsic temporal coherence to support robust feature extraction; (2) the Spatial Heterogeneity Extraction Unit (SHEU), which constructs a triple-branch convolutional structure with functional independence and diversity constraints to decouple heterogeneous sensor responses; and (3) the Spatio-Temporal Amalgamation Decoupling Unit (STADU), which separates stationary and nonstationary components for interpretable monitoring. With the help of this deep measurement model, latent process dynamics can be accurately extracted, improving fault detection, measurement, and process state comprehension. Validation on both simulated and real-world industrial datasets, such as the blast furnace iron production process and the Secure Water Treatment (SWaT) system, shows that ETS<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>N outperforms current techniques in terms of sensitivity, stability, and fault isolation. The suggested method aids in the creation of future measurement systems for intricate, dynamic industrial settings.

title: Life Extension of Lithium-ion Battery for Automotive Applications using Hybrid Energy Storage Systems
authors: Shiv Shankar Sinha, Pallavi Bharadwaj
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: This study addresses one of the most pressing concerns of Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), namely energy-power imbalance, to enhance the reliability and efficiency of energy storage technology employed in battery electric vehicles (BEVs). In this regard, a hybrid energy storage system (HESS), consisting of a power-dense Lithium-Titanate-Oxide (LTO) LIB in conjunction with an energy-dense Nickel-Manganese-Cobalt (NMC) LIB is developed. The primary focus is to investigate the efficacy of HESS technology in prolonging the lifespan of the main energy source, NMC battery, by suppressing internal aging and minimizing peak current stress. The LIB life cycle analysis is performed using a novel electro-thermal-aging model wherein the aging state is estimated without extensive experimentation. The effectiveness of the proposed HESS technology and electro thermal-aging model is experimentally validated through two case studies. The findings demonstrate a 21.25% and 15.94% reduction in electrical and thermal stresses, respectively, resulting in a 54.46% lifespan extension of NMC battery under standard electric vehicle driving profile.

title: Satellite-Ground Collaborative Learning: An Empirical Study on Remote Sensing Vision-Language Model
authors: Yisheng Zhao, Hongyi Chen, Hao Zhou, Yaoqi Liu, Haoyu You, Yinhe Han, Jianyu Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Severe on-orbit computing and power constraints necessitate aggressive compression of ground foundation models for satellite deployment, causing degradation of representation capability. This leaves compressed models vulnerable to out-of-distribution (OOD) scenarios arising from factors like dynamic earth systems, the diversity of subcategories, and unpredictable user queries. However, current learn-then-deploy paradigm lack mechanisms for autonomous OOD adaptation. To address this, we propose the satellite-ground collaborative learning (SGCL) framework, enabling asynchronous, lifelong updates between ground and satellite models. SGCL achieves this through three core mechanisms: (1) on-orbit OOD detector for bandwidth-efficient data downlink; (2) ground-based LoRA fine-tuning triggered by satellite-to-foundation output divergence; (3) adaptive distillation that aggregates multi-satellite knowledge. For comprehensive evaluation, we propose the SGCL-Benchmark that simulates cyclical workflows across 7 satellites with specialized protocols for domain/fine-grained/format OOD robustness. Experiments demonstrate SGCL’s effectiveness in mitigating compression-induced degradation. Our codes and dataset will be released after acceptance to promote the development of satellite intelligent applications.

title: Task Graph Maximum Likelihood Estimation for Procedural Activity Understanding in Egocentric Videos
authors: Luigi Seminara, Giovanni Maria Farinella, Antonino Furnari
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Humans engage daily in procedural activities such as cooking a recipe or fixing a bike, which can be described as goal-oriented sequences of key-steps following certain ordering constraints. Task graphs mined from videos or textual descriptions have recently gained popularity as a human-readable, holistic representation of procedural activities encoding a partial ordering over key-steps, and have shown promise in supporting downstream video understanding tasks. While previous works generally relied on hand-crafted procedures to extract task graphs from videos, this paper introduces an approach based on gradient-based maximum likelihood optimization of edge weights, which can be used to directly estimate an adjacency matrix and can also be naturally plugged into more complex neural network architectures. We validate the ability of the proposed approach to generate accurate task graphs on the CaptainCook4D and EgoPER datasets. Moreover, we extend our validation analysis to the EgoProceL dataset, which we manually annotate with task graphs as an additional contribution. The three datasets together constitute a new benchmark for task graph learning, where our approach obtains improvements of +14.5%, +10.2% and +13.6% in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$F_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> score, respectively, over previous approaches. Thanks to the differentiability of the proposed framework, we also introduce a feature-based approach for predicting task graphs from key-step textual or video embeddings, which exhibits emerging video understanding abilities. Beyond that, task graphs learned with our approach obtain top performance in the Ego-Exo4D procedure understanding benchmark including 5 different downstream tasks, with gains of up to +4.61%, +0.10%, +5.02%, +8.62%, and +15.16% in finding Previous Keysteps, Optional Keysteps, Procedural Mistakes, Missing Keysteps, and Future Keysteps, respectively. We finally show significant enhancements to the challenging task of online mistake detection in procedural egocentric videos, achieving notable gains of +19.8% and +6.4% in the Assembly101-O and EPIC-Tent-O datasets, respectively, compared to the state of the art. The code for replicating the experiments is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/fpv-iplab/Differentiable-Task-Graph-Learning</uri>.

title: DSIGAN: A Dual Branch Style Injection Generative Adversarial Network for Pan-sharpening
authors: Yajie Feng, Yongzhi Wang, Yigao Cheng, Zhijie Zeng, Zheng Ji
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Pan-sharpening aims to fuse the spectral information from a low-resolution multispectral (LRMS) image with the spatial details from a high-resolution panchromatic (PAN) image to generate a high-resolution multispectral (HRMS) image. Despite the significant progress of deep learning in the field of pan-sharpening, early supervised methods are limited by the domain bias resulting from downsampling-based training. In recent years, although unsupervised learning has effectively alleviated this issue, its fusion strategies are often relatively static. The network architectures typically have fixed biases toward either spatial details or spectral fidelity during the design phase, making it difficult to adaptively find the optimal balance. To address these issues, we propose a novel unsupervised pan-sharpening framework, namely DSIGAN, which remodels the task as a content-style fusion problem. Specifically, we construct a dual-branch generator to decouple and extract the spatial structure (content) from the PAN image and the spectral information (style) from the LRMS image, respectively. We then propose a content-driven style injection mechanism to adaptively inject the spectral style into the content features, thereby achieving a synergistic optimization of spatial details and spectral consistency. Furthermore, we design a QSS (Quality-Structure-Style) loss function to simultaneously constrain both spatial structure and spectral fidelity in an unsupervised manner. Extensive experiments on two public and two self-built datasets demonstrate that DSIGAN achieves superior performance compared to several state-of-the-art methods without requiring ground-truth labels. Overall, the proposed method also strikes an excellent balance between enhancing spatial details and preserving spectral consistency.

title: Few-shot Remote Sensing Image Scene Classification Based on Zigzag Scanning Feature Sequence and Riemannian Kernel Fisher Discriminant Analysis
authors: Xiliang Chen, Longwei Li, Chengzhi Lv, Zhenyu Wang, Mingqing Liu, Guobin Zhu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Few-shot remote sensing image scene classification aims to use annotated data to achieve efficient classification, which has important applications in environmental monitoring, urban planning, agricultural yield estimation and other fields. However, due to the significant intra-class variability and inter class similarity in remote sensing scenes, traditional methods struggle to simultaneously capture the spatial correlation and discriminability of features, resulting in limited performance in few-shot scenarios. This paper proposes a few-shot remote sensing image scene classification method based on zigzag scanning feature sequence and Riemannian kernel Fisher discriminant analysis (ZSFS-RKFDA). Firstly, deep features are extracted through a convolutional neural network (CNN), and a zigzag scanning (ZS) process reorganizes the two-dimensional feature maps into one-dimensional sequences that preserve spatial relationships. Secondly, the feature sequence is mapped to non-Euclidean geometric space through the Riemannian manifold space embedding module (RieMSEM). The main steps include constructing an autoregressive moving average (ARMA) model to capture the spatial correlation of features and constructing class prototypes on the Riemannian manifold space. Finally, Riemannian kernel Fisher discriminant analysis (RieKFDA) is employed to optimize the metric distance, enhancing intra-class compactness and inter-class separability. A joint loss function is designed to enable end-to-end training. Experiments on three public datasets demonstrate that the proposed method outper forms existing mainstream approaches in both 5-shot and 10-shot few-shot classification scenarios.

title: VecComp: Vector Computing via MIMO Digital Over-the-Air Computation
authors: Saeed Razavikia, José Mairton Barros da Silva Junior, Carlo Fischione
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Recently, the ChannelComp framework has proposed digital over-the-air computation by designing digital modulations that enable the computation of arbitrary functions. Unlike traditional analog over-the-air computation, which is restricted to nomographic functions, ChannelComp enables a broader range of computational tasks while maintaining compatibility with digital communication systems. This framework is intended for applications that favor local information processing over the mere acquisition of data. However, ChannelComp is currently designed for scalar function computation, while numerous data-centric applications necessitate vector-based computations, and it is susceptible to channel fading. In this work, we introduce a generalization of the ChannelComp framework, called VecComp, by integrating ChannelComp with multiple-antenna technology. This generalization not only enables vector function computation but also ensures scalability in the computational complexity, which increases only linearly with the vector dimension. As such, VecComp remains computationally efficient and robust against channel impairments, making it suitable for high-dimensional, data-centric applications. We establish a non-asymptotic upper bound on the mean squared error of VecComp, affirming its computation efficiency under fading channel conditions. Numerical experiments show the effectiveness of VecComp in improving the computation of vector functions and fading compensation over noisy and fading multiple-access channels.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems

title: Performance Comparison of High-Gain DC-DC Converters for PV Fed EV Off-Board Chargers
authors: Paul Mathew, Prabhakar. M
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this manuscript, three high gain DC - DC converters (HG-DCCs) are proposed for charging the electric two (E2W) and three wheelers (E3W) from solar photovoltaic (PV) panels. The HG-DCC functions as the power electronics bridge, matching the low-voltage PV array to the high-voltage DC bus. All the three HG-DCCs employ coupled inductors to achieve a voltage gain of more than 13. For ripple-free input current operation, these converters are synthesized from the traditional interleaved boost converter (IBC). Several gain extension mechanisms like voltage-lift capacitor, coupled inductor (CI), diode-capacitor multipliers, and an X-shaped cell aid to enhance the IBC's voltage gain. The main research contribution is the manner of coupling the secondary windings of the CIs for achieving a voltage gain ranging from 13.33 to 15.83. The CIs along with gain extension networks significantly reduce the voltage stress on the switches. The performance attributes of all the three HG-DCCs are thoroughly compared for determining the most suitable converter. The converters are compared through the circuit structure and simulation outputs.

title: A Comprehensive Study of Battery Health Indicators Extraction Using Partial Charging Curve
authors: Shrinidhi Ganesh, Varshini R, Shubhashri V P, Ilango Karuppasamy
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive review of existing methods for the estimation of key battery health indicators: State of Charge (SOC), State of Health (SOH), and Remaining Useful Life (RUL), highlighting their limitations in real-time applications. A data-driven approach utilizing multiple condition indicators is proposed to enhance real-time health monitoring of lithium-ion batteries, particularly in electric vehicle systems. Health indicators such as partial charging curves and multistage charging curves are defined and extracted to support advanced diagnostic techniques including Incremental Capacity/Differential Voltage (IC/DV) analysis and Differential Thermal Voltammetry (DTV). Using a real-life battery dataset, the proposed method involves pre-processing, health indicator extraction, and application to SOC and SOH estimation, while referencing prior data-driven RUL estimation results. Comparative analysis of two extraction methods is presented, concluding that the selection of a suitable health inducators extraction technique should be application-specific.

title: Smart Thermal Regulation in EV Batteries Using Real-Time Digital Twin Feedback for Enhanced Performance
authors: Vinay Kumar R, Pavan Kumar K.K, D. Ramasamy, M.M. Noor, W.S.W. Harun, L. Samylingam, Navid Aslfattahi, Maryam Sadat Kiai, Chee Kuang Kok
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The growing demand for high-performance electric vehicles (EVs) has intensified the need for intelligent battery thermal management systems (BTMS) to ensure safety, reliability, and energy efficiency. This study presents a novel digital twin (DT)driven framework for smart thermal regulation in lithium-ion battery packs, integrating real-time sensor data with a hybrid physicsinformed and data-driven model. The DT architecture synchronizes virtual battery simulations with real-world measurements, enabling predictive thermal control under transient operating conditions such as fast charging and aggressive driving. A reduced-order thermal model, calibrated against CFD simulations, is implemented within a MATLAB-Simulink environment to support rapid thermal forecasting and control decision-making. The system dynamically adapts cooling strategies including air, liquid, and Phase Changing Material (PCM)-assisted methods, based on predicted spatiotemporal heat fluxes, thereby enhancing thermal uniformity and reducing parasitic energy losses. Results demonstrate a 50 % reduction in cell-to-cell temperature gradients and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18-22 \%$</tex> savings in cooling energy consumption compared to conventional static BTMS. Furthermore, predictive control improves thermal response latency by up to 35 %, contributing to improved battery lifespan and overall energy efficiency. This work highlights the potential of DTenhanced BTMS as a scalable solution for next-generation EV platforms, bridging the gap between cyber-physical systems and real-time thermal optimization.

title: A Control Strategy for Input Voltage Unbalance in Three-Phase PWM Rectifiers Based on Adaptive Periodic Current Fluctuation Suppression
authors: Hongda Song, Li Liu, Xiaolei Che, Yimin Gong, Yang Yang, Jiannan Zhang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Three-phase pulse width modulation (PWM) rectifiers are crucial in industrial, civil, and transportation applications. Due to the diversity of grid loads, the three-phase input voltage may be unbalanced, which consequently leads to unequal three-phase current sharing and increased current harmonic distortion, severely risking the overheating of cables and circuit breakers. When the three-phase voltage unbalance reaches 20%, the three-phase current deviation can reach 13.7%, and the 3rd harmonic content of the current increases by 3.8%. Therefore, the focus of this paper is to control the current uniformly and reduce harmonics under three-phase voltage unbalance conditions. Based on the Recursive Gauss-Newton (RGN) algorithm, this paper proposes two algorithms: a Negative Sequence Component Elimination (NSCE) algorithm, which is capable of extracting the effective positive-sequence voltage component during voltage unbalance for precise control, and an Adaptive Periodic Current Fluctuation Suppression (APCFS) algorithm. The APCFS method serves the dual purpose of achieving uniform current control and harmonic reduction, while mitigating the harmonics introduced by the dead-time effect. The current deviation is reduced from 13.7% to 2%, and the total harmonic distortion (THD) is decreased from 5.18% to 2.07%. The efficacy of these proposed algorithms is comprehensively validated through theoretical analysis, current harmonic comparison, and transient response tests, including load step and AC voltage step changes. Experimental results obtained from a 10-kW prototype confirm the theoretical analysis and demonstrate that the combined NSCE and APCFS algorithms effectively resolve the negative effects arising from both three-phase voltage unbalance and dead-time.

title: DFOGI-RLS Lattice-FLL Based Synchronization Technique for Wind-PV-BESS Based Standalone Distributed System With Local Non-Linear Load
authors: Divyansh Shailly, Prakash Chittora, Madhusudan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study illustrates an advanced synchronization and control approach for a distributed generation system that incorporates photovoltaic (PV) systems, battery energy storage systems (BESS), and wind energy conversion systems (WECS). To achieve precise synchronization and control, the system employs a frequency-locked loop (FLL) combined with a DFOGI and recursive least square (RLS) Lattice-based control technique. This technique enhances system stability and response time even in the presence of distortion and imbalance. To ensure a reliable power supply and frequency regulation, the distributed generation setup merges wind energy through an SEIG with PV and BESS. A voltage source converter (VSC) facilitates efficient operation by managing load fluctuations, mitigating harmonic distortions, and balancing voltage and frequency. To preserve power balance between the generation and load, a bidirectional DC-DC converter (BDC) is connected to the DC-link side of the voltage source converter (VSC) through the battery energy storage system (BESS). An incremental conductance algorithm, augmented with an integral regulator, is applied for maximum power point tracking (MPPT). The effectiveness of the MG is confirmed through MATLAB/Simulink simulations, which evaluate dynamic oscillations, microgrid frequency, and harmonic distortion across different load and energy source conditions.

title: Low-Power High-Speed Approximate Ternary Half-Adder and Multiplier Circuit Design Using Carbon Nanotube Field-Effect Transistors
authors: Murali Krishna Peddachowdappa, Vishnuvardhan D
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper explores modern mitigation techniques as in ternary logic and approximate computing, to reduce power consumption in electronic circuits. Three-level logic Ternary logic, which has three voltage levels, is also useful to minimize circuit area and power. However, there is a trade-off between energy usage, area, and precision when using approximate computing. Based on carbon nanotube field- effect transistors (CNTFETs), which perform better in terms of latency and power consumption than their exact counterparts, the research presents a novel design for approximate ternary half adders and multipliers. The performance of CNTFETs with operation diameters of nanotubes that can be adjusted to offer different levels of threshold voltage is strategically used by selectively pruning the circuit size of the blocks of Sum and Product. This controlled compromise aims to trade-off high performance improvement with a regulated trade-off in accuracy of certain outputs with overall consistency of computation reliability. Overall, this study introduces CNTFET-based approximate ternary half adders and multipliers, which provides a sophisticated way of optimizing performance and controlled decrease in accuracy for low-power circuit design using new technologies and customized approximations.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Threephase Multilevel ANPC Switched-Capacitor
authors: Dipanwita Ganguly, Ranjit Kumar Barai
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper introduces an enhanced three-phase five-level Active Neutral-Point-Clamped Switched-Capacitor (ANPC-SC) inverter that employs interleaved pulse width modulation (PWM) to achieve efficient and reliable operation for renewable and industrial power conversion systems. The proposed configuration merges the voltage-boosting benefits of switched-capacitor (SC) networks with the low common-mode voltage (CMV) and fault-tolerant characteristics of ANPC inverters. A chronological review of recent studies (2020-2023) highlights advancements in PWM control, capacitor selfbalancing, and total standing voltage (TSV) reduction, which have inspired the present design. The interleaved PWM approach minimizes switching stress, suppresses current ripple, and distributes power uniformly among switching devices, leading to improved harmonic performance and reduced losses. The inverter ensures natural capacitor voltage equalization and soft-charging behavior, thereby lowering device voltage stress and enhancing operational stability. A 4 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{kW}, 400 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> DC input generating 220 V RMS at 50 Hz was simulated in PSIM to verify system performance. The results demonstrate a voltage gain of approximately 1.8, overall efficiency exceeding 96%, total harmonic distortion (THD) below 3%, and capacitor voltage imbalance within 5% during dynamic conditions. These outcomes confirm that the proposed interleaved ANPC-SC inverter offers a compact, highefficiency, and robust alternative for modern medium-power grid-connected energy systems.

title: Optimized Power Flow Management in High Renewable and PEV Penetration Scenarios Using Multi-Verse Optimizer
authors: Ankur Maheshwari, Supriya Jaiswal, Himanshu Raj
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources (RESs), such as wind and solar power plants, along with plug-in electric vehicles (PEVs), poses challenges in optimal power flow (OPF) management due to their inherent intermittency and the unpredictable charging/discharging behaviour of PEVs. Ensuring efficient and cost-effective OPF is crucial as power systems transition toward low-carbon, decentralized networks. This paper presents an OPF management framework for the IEEE 57-bus system under two contrasting operational scenarios: a conventional configuration with only thermal generators and a high-penetration configuration incorporating largescale RESs and PEVs. The Multi-Verse Optimizer (MVO), a metaheuristic algorithm inspired by cosmological theories, is employed to solve the OPF problem with the objective of minimizing total operating cost over a 24-hour scheduling horizon. The simulation results show that integrating renewables and plugin electric vehicles (PEVs) into the 57-bus system substantially enhances economic efficiency. Compared to the conventional case, the high-penetration scenario achieves lower peak operating costs, smoother dispatch patterns, and improved cost stability, particularly during mid-day and evening demand peaks. The study confirms that MVO-based OPF offers a powerful tool for managing modern, renewable-rich systems while maintaining grid reliability and economic performance.

title: A 5G-Enabled IoT Architecture for V2V and V2I Communication in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Sk. Momin, G. Jawahar Sagar, Sachidananda Sen, Chandan Kumar Shiva, B. Vedik, Tarkeshwar Matho
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The integration of 5G networks with autonomous vehicles is poised to revolutionize the field of Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), offering low-latency, high-bandwidth communication necessary for real-time vehicle and infrastructure interactions. This paper proposes a novel Internet of Things (IoT) framework designed to leverage 5G technologies for optimizing Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) and Vehicle-to-Infrastructure (V2I) communication. The framework aims to enhance the safety, traffic flow, and energy efficiency of autonomous vehicles by enabling faster, more reliable communication among vehicles and between vehicles and infrastructure. By utilizing 5G's ultra-low latency and high data throughput, the proposed framework supports real-time data exchange, allowing for timely decision-making in critical situations such as collision avoidance, traffic signal optimization, and dynamic route planning. The design of the IoT framework is validated through simulations that demonstrate significant improvements in traffic efficiency, safety, and energy consumption compared to conventional communication systems. The results highlight the potential of 5G -enabled V2V and V2I communication to transform urban mobility, making it safer, more efficient, and environmentally friendly. Future work will explore the integration of machine learning models for adaptive traffic management and further refinement of the framework to accommodate large-scale deployment in real-world environments.

title: Design and Simulation of an Electronic on-Load Tap Changer Transformer Under Transient and Steady-State Conditions
authors: Narayan Prasad Bhusal, Jeetendra Chaudhary, Rajesh M. Pindoriya, Apsara Adhikari, Akhileshwar Mishra, Shailendra Kumar Jha
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Nepal's electricity sector is moving towards an unbundled market structure, where separate companies will handle generation, transmission, and distribution. This change will encourage competition in all three sectors. In the distribution sector specifically, future competition among DISCOs will likely focus on delivering better quality and reliability of power supply. One effective method for maintaining voltage within acceptable limits—regardless of changes in supply or demand—is the On-Load Tap Changer (OLTC), which adjusts the transformer's turn ratio. In Nepal's electricity distribution network, voltage imbalance is a significant concern in urban areas, while undervoltage and voltage sags are common in rural regions. With the growing integration of Distributed Energy Generators (DEGs) and the adoption of innovative grid technologies, issues such as overvoltage and voltage swell are expected to become more frequent shortly. According to Electricity Regulation 2050, distribution voltage must be kept within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5 \%$</tex>. However, traditional mechanical OLTC systems face challenges such as arc formation, slow response time, wear and tear of mechanical components, and frictional losses. To address these limitations, mechanical components can be replaced with electronic switches such as TRIACs, creating an Electronic (E-OLTC). This paper presents the design of an E-OLTC using MATLAB simulation and demonstrates how it works through a hardware prototype.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Tuning of a Bidirectional Wireless Charging System to Zero Phase Angle Under Misalignment
authors: Akhil Yadav Mudiminchi, P. C. Sekhar, K. Satya Prakash
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Wireless Power Transfer offers a convenient and safe charging method for electric vehicles by eliminating the need for physical connectors. However, coil misalignment between the transmitter and receiver can detune the system from its resonant frequency, causing significant reactive power circulation and reducing efficiency. To alleviate these ill effects, this paper proposes a control scheme for an series-series compensated WPT system, where a reinforcement learning (RL)-based pri-mary-side phase-shift modulation strategy is realized to minimize reactive power flow and maintain zero-phase-angle operation under misalignment. In addition, the secondary converter is controlled to charge the battery at a reference current. In this work, the traditional PI controller is compared with the RLbased controller at the primary side, for their transient and steady state responses with misalignments under constant current operation. The relative phase shift and the internal phase shift of the converters are modulated to achieve ZPA operation at the desired reference current.

title: Anomaly Detection in Industrial Time Series Using LSTM: A Case Study on Solar Panel Monitoring
authors: Mukesh Azad, Sandeep Raghuwanshi, Monika Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In industrial systems, timely and accurate detection of anomalies is critical for ensuring operational efficiency, safety, and predictive maintenance. This paper presents a deep learningbased approach using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to detect anomalies in multivariate time series data collected from solar panel installations. The proposed method captures temporal dependencies and nonlinear patterns in sensor data to identify abnormal behavior indicative of faults, degradation, or environmental impact. We conduct extensive experiments using a real-world solar panel dataset, comparing the LSTM-based model with traditional techniques such as Isolation Forest and statistical thresholding. The LSTM approach outperforms baselines in terms of precision, recall, and F1-score, demonstrating its robustness and adaptability in an industrial energy context. The results highlight the potential of LSTMbased models in enhancing the reliability of renewable energy systems through intelligent monitoring.

title: Boosting Energy Storage Performance: Ni-Doped $\text{ZnMn}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}$ as a Promising Electrode for Supercapacitors
authors: Kalpana Kukreti, Shivam Kumar Mittal, Kanhaiya Lal Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The creation of high-performance, scalable, and environmentally friendly electrode materials is essential for maximizing the potential of supercapacitors. Herein, we present a calcination-free co-precipitation technique for creating Ni doped <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{ZnNi}_{x} \text{Mn}_{2-x} \mathrm{O}_{4}(x=0.0,0.1,0.2$</tex>, and 0.3) nano-spheres. Environmentally friendly production processes are supported by this low-temperature synthesis approach, which significantly reduces energy consumption and processing time. X-ray diffraction (XRD) analysis is used to confirm the successful formation of the spinel phase. Furthermore, the effects of Ni doping on the structural and electrochemical properties of ZMO are thoroughly examined. Among the synthesized materials, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{ZnNi}_{0.2} \text{Mn}_{1.8} \mathrm{O}_{4}$</tex> composition (denoted as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{ZMO10)}$</tex> demonstrates superior electrochemical performance and remarkable cycling stability. The optimized composite, ZMO10, exhibits a high specific capacitance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$640 ~\mathrm{F} ~\mathrm{g}^{-1}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5 \text{Ag}^{-1}$</tex> and retains 98 % of its initial capacitance after 5,000 charge-discharge cycles at a high current density of 5 Ag <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$^{-1}$</tex> underscoring its potential as a robust and efficient electrode material for next-generation supercapacitor applications.

title: A Dual Input Single Output Nonisolated DC/DC Converter for Low/High Voltage Sources with Step Down/Up Capabilities
authors: Avisha Tah
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: A dual input single output (DISO) DC-DC conversion system which can integrate two renewable energy sources of different voltage levels, is proposed in this paper. The proposed converter can act as an interface between the renewable generations such as solar photovoltaic, fuel cell etc. to the load. The advantage of the proposed converter is that both the power sources can supply the load simultaneously or individually through the converter. Hence, in case of a failure of one of the sources, the other source can maintain the supply to the load, enhancing the reliability of the power system. Another advantage of the proposed converter is that both the sources can supply the load using the same circuit components, thus, reducing the size and cost of the system. The operating principle and different modes of operation of the DISO DC-DC converter is discussed. The steady-state analysis, deriving the input-output voltage relationship, of the proposed DISO DC-DC converter is presented. A pulse width modulation (PWM) control strategy is proposed to operate the proposed DISO converter in buck, boost and buck-boost mode as per the source availability and load demand. The results are presented to validate the proposed switching strategy on the proposed converter.

title: Design and Hil Validation of a Closed-Loop Controlled 5 KW LLC Resonant Converter for 400 V DC Output Using OPAL-RT
authors: Uditveer Singh Rajput, Annu Kumari, Sanjeev Singh, D. Giribabu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents design and validation of a high-performance LLC resonant converter for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$400 ~\mathrm{V}, 5 ~\text{kW}$</tex> DC output application, suitable for isolated charging systems, and other industrial applications. The designed converter achieves high efficiency at 10 khz switching frequency due to soft-switching operation and optimized resonant tank circuit. The presented control strategy ensures desired voltage regulation under varying input and load. The entire system is modeled and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink for its performance evaluation. The obtained simulated performance is validated in real-time through Hardware-in-Loop (HIL) OPAL-RT platform. For varying input voltage between 240 330 volts and varying output power the designed topology provides soft switching with efficiency above 90 % for both active and reactive loads without changing the resonant tank parameters. The proposed design demonstrates robustness, accuracy, and suitability for high-voltage DC applications.

title: A Closed Form Fault Location Method for Double-Circuit Series Compensated Lines
authors: Neethu George, O. D. Naidu, Ashok Kumar Pradhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Fault location on transmission lines is a crucial function for utilities which is widely implemented in commercial protection intelligent electronic devices by major manufacturers. Fault location on double-circuit series compensated lines is a well-researched problem, but remains complex due to the compensator's non-linear behavior, limited model availability, and zero-sequence mutual coupling. Existing model-free methods rely on complex optimization or iterative solvers. These are difficult to integrate into intelligent electronic devices and face limited acceptance by utilities due to initialization and convergence issues. This paper presents a closed-form, non-iterative approach for faulted section identification and fault location on double-circuit series compensated lines. The method analytically linearizes the reactive power flowing into the fault path using distributed line model, while eliminating the need for compensator model. Faulted section is identified, and fault location is pinpointed where the linearized quantity approaches zero. The key highlight of the method is the industry-accepted level of accuracy, direct formulation, and non-iterative nature - enabling seamless integration into intelligent electronic devices and ensuring practical applicability in real-world power systems. Simulation tests and field validations further confirm its reliability and effectiveness.

title: A Transformer Less Zeta Converter with Reduced Voltage Stress for Hybrid Electric Vehicle Applications
authors: G. Ramanathan, C. Bharatiraja, Sanjeevikumar Padmanaban
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study proposes a modified ZETA converter tailored for hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) applications with the objective of reducing ripple currents. The conventional ZETA topology is enhanced by integrating two additional switching devices and an extra inductor, by creating a direct power transfer path between the input and output stages, the modified configuration improves the converter's operational performance. This arrangement allows the system to achieve reduced current ripple and enhanced voltage gain through proper duty cycle regulation. In addition, the proposed converter exhibits lower switching and conduction losses compared with the conventional ZETA converter topology. The work thoroughly investigates the mechanism and features of the proposed converter, based on simulation and experimental findings for validation. The study highlights the performance as well as efficiency of the proposed converter in hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) applications, demonstrating considerable potential for improving power delivery systems in electric vehicles. This novel method resolves specific problems concerning ripple currents in HEV systems and also aids in the overall progress of power electronics technology for sustainable transportation solutions.

title: A Seven-Level Triple-Boost Common-Ground Multilevel Inverter with Reduced Voltage Stress
authors: Deepak Singh, N. Sandeep, Piyush Kant
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: A novel seven-level triple-boost common-ground multilevel inverter (7L3BCGMLI) topology is proposed with significantly reduced component count and enhanced power conversion efficiency, which is highly suitable for grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) applications. The proposed inverter employs only nine power semiconductor switches, two switched-capacitors (SCs), and one power diode to generate a seven-level output voltage waveform with a threefold voltage gain, while simultaneously eliminating the leakage current using common-ground technique. Compared to existing similar structures, the proposed topology achieves the lowest total standing voltage of 5.33 p.u., minimal maximum blocking voltage of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 V_{\text{dc}}$</tex>, and the lower capacitor voltage diversity factor, enabling the use of lower-rated and cost-effective semiconductor devices and capacitors. Detailed comparative analysis against state-of-the-art topologies confirms the superiority of the proposed inverter in terms of efficiency (98.17%), component optimization, and practical implementation feasibility. These features establish it as a promising solution for compact, efficient, and reliable PV-integrated multilevel inverter systems.

title: Enhancing the Life Expectancy of Storage Device in a Solar Pv Based Microgrid
authors: Saqib Ahmad Wani, Sofi Jan, Rabiya, Mubashar Yaqoob Zargar, Ikhlaq Hussain
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Photovoltaic based Stand-alone DC microgrids typically operate at maximum power point tracking (MPPT). However, continuously operating the solar panel at MPPT reduces the lifetime of battery energy storage system (BESS) due to the high charging when battery is fully charged. This paper proposes a hybrid control strategy combining MPPT & variable power point tracking (VPPT) for a photovoltaic based stand-alone DC microgrid with constant DC link voltage. The proposed control strategy effectively minimizes the charging current of the battery when the load demand is lower than the PV generation & battery is fully charged. The system performance is simulated through MATLAB.

title: Generative Artificial Intelligence and Corporate Risk-Taking: A Stylized Theoretical Framework with Evidence
authors: Yuanlin Wu, Huajun Zheng, Danyang Li
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 2nd International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Digital Media
abstract: None

title: Mitigating Repetitive Generation of Motion Tokens in Motion-Language Model via Knowledge Distillation
authors: Ruilin Wang
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 2nd International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Digital Media
abstract: None

title: Generative AI-Driven Digital Media Platforms for Interactive Art Communication
authors: Hongyu Ren
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 2nd International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Digital Media
abstract: None

title: Converging Technologies and Their Human Impact: Ethical and Societal Reflections on Nanotechnology, Biotechnology, Information Technology, and Cognitive Science (NBIC) Systems — A Systematic Review
authors: Sikozho Vengesai, Clopas Kwenda
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: The convergence of Nanotechnology, Biotechnology, Information Technology, and Cognitive Science (NBIC) signifies a transformative shift in science and technology, bringing in unmatched innovation and redesigning human life across multiple dimensions. As these disciplines combine, they create powerful, harmonious systems which are able to enhance human abilities, transforming medicine, and shifting social structures. However, these benefits come with complex ethical and societal implications. This paper critically examines the human impact of NBIC convergence, focusing on concerns such as equity, privacy, identity, autonomy, and the potential for socio-economic divides. It reflects on both the pros and cons of NBIC systems, emphasizing on the need for inclusive, forward-looking governance frameworks that are ethically and culturally grounded. The study found out that existing ethical frameworks are fragmented and Inadequate and that NBIC pose threats to privacy, autonomy and mental integrity. Future research should focus on practical tools and frameworks that support responsible, inclusive, and just innovation. Drawing on ethical theories and global perspectives, the paper proposes a balanced discourse that seeks to harness the benefits of converging technologies while mitigating their risks to humanity and social cohesion.

title: Toward Digital Transformation 4.0 in the Global South: A Zimbabwean Framework for Policy, Inclusion, and Industrial Resilience
authors: Samuel Musungwini, Susan Madanhi
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Digital Transformation 4.0 (DT4.0) relies on Artificial Intelligence (AI) and is viewed as a key element of economic competitiveness, institutional modernisation, and social inclusion. However, in the Global South, especially in Zimbabwe, the paths to DT4.0 are uneven, underexplored, and limited by structural barriers. This paper presents a framework for AIenabled Digital Transformation 4.0 (DT4.0) designed for Zimbabwe, critically addressing the opportunities, risks, and governance challenges that influence digital transitions in developing economies. The study uses a qualitative approach, mixing a systematic literature review with evidence from 15 key informant interviews. These interviews include operations managers, data protection officers, strategy consultants, and cloud architects. Additionally, a focus group of 12 members comes from government, industry, academia, and civil society. The analysis follows the Technology-Organisation-Environment (TOE) framework, Socio-Technical Systems (STS) theory, the Diffusion of Innovation (DOI) model, and the Capability Approach. It brings together expert insights on infrastructure readiness, organisational capacity, regulatory dynamics, and inclusivity. The findings reveal that while AI-enabled DT4.0 is seen as essential, Zimbabwe's readiness varies significantly. There are opportunities in sectors such as fintech, agriculture, and healthcare. However, challenges such as frail infrastructure, skills gaps, poor governance, and urban-centred projects disrupt widespread adoption. Without targeted investments in skills, rural connectivity, responsive regulation, and cross-sector collaboration, Digital Transformation 4.0 (DT4.0) could increase rather than reduce inequality. The paper presents a framework for DT4.0 in the Global South that is sensitive to local contexts and offers practical pathways for policymakers, practitioners, and researchers. It emphasises that Zimbabwe's digital future relies not only on technology but also on creating fair, resilient, and trust-based systems for lasting transformation.

title: Meta-Analysis of Climate-Smart Livestock Management and Greenhouse Gas Emissions
authors: Sibusisiwe Dube, Nikodemus Angula, George Mufungulwa, Ronald Karon, David Vuyelwa Ruwodo, Sibonile Moyo
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Livestock production is important for food security, economic development, and social well-being, particularly in developing countries. Milk and beef from livestock are major sources of protein. However, along them are gases such as carbon dioxide (CO2), methane (CH4), and nitrous oxide (N 2 O) which are harmful to the environment. There is therefore a need for climate-smart livestock production and management practices, which while promoting livestock productivity, have potential to reduce the emission of the unwanted gases. This meta-analysis presents synthesized evidence from peer-reviewed studies published within the year 2020-2025 regarding the effectiveness of climate-smart livestock production and management practices in reducing greenhouse gas emissions. Practices evaluated included dietary modifications, feed additives, manure management strategies, improved animal health and genetics, and optimized grazing systems. The results of this meta-analysis show that the climate-smart livestock production and management practices particularly certain feed additives and improved forage quality intensity led to the reduction of greenhouse gas emission by up to 40 % when combined practices were used and by up to 80 % in manure management. It is worth noting that the effectiveness of the different climate-smart livestock production and management practices varied depending on the specific practice, animal type, baseline management, geographic context, and production level. This meta-analysis reveals the most promising interventions that can inform the scientific community, policy makers and stakeholders on decisions, guiding investment in effective greenhouse gas mitigation technologies, at the same time promoting the adoption of sustainable livestock production methods essential for achieving the climate goals.

title: A Systematic Review of Feature Modelling Challenges in Multimodal Learning
authors: Prince Ndaruza, Gideon Mazambani, Tavengwa Masamha, Kudakwashe Dube
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: This systematic PRISMA-compliant review identifies critical methodological gaps in multimodal learning and proposes a unified framework for feature modelling. An analysis of 75 peer-reviewed studies from 2020 to 2025 revealed growing scholarly interest in multimodal learning methodologies. However, these studies exhibit systemic methodological inefficiencies such as the underutilization of both feature disentanglement (42.7% adoption) and contrastive learning (16% adoption); and the widespread fusion features within shared latent spaces (69.3 % of studies). These methodological inefficiencies result in a suboptimal downstream task performance. To address these methodological shortcomings, the study proposed an integrated multimodal learning framework that conducts disentanglement before shared-space projection; utilizes contrastive loss-driven semantic alignment during projection; and retains features in the shared latent space in their semantically aligned form without fusion them. The proposed multimodal learning framework can help address the prevalent loss of complementary modality information in existing methodologies.

title: Frequency-Domain Signatures for Proactive Defense Against Model Poisoning Attacks in Federated Learning
authors: Fangjie Hu, Aiqing Zhang, Meng Li, Chen Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Federated Learning enables decentralized model training without exposing raw data, but remains fundamentally vulnerable to poisoning attacks from malicious clients. Existing defenses rely heavily on passive anomaly detection, honest majority assumptions, or unrealistic statistical priors, making them ineffective against adaptive and stealthy adversaries. In this paper, we propose SpecShield, a proactive defense mechanism that actively probes client models through calibrated adversarial perturbations. By leveraging the Fast Gradient Sign Method on the server side, SpecShield elicits dynamic response patterns from each client. These responses are then analyzed in the frequency domain using the Discrete Wavelet Transform. These frequency-domain features uncover distinctive response patterns between benign and malicious clients, enabling robust detection of model poisoning attacks in both non-IID environments and Byzantine majority scenarios. We further derive theoretical upper bounds on perturbation magnitudes to guarantee detection accuracy while preserving benign client performance. Through extensive experiments conducted on real-world datasets under six state-of-the-art poisoning attacks, SpecShield consistently outperforms existing defenses in both detection accuracy and model robustness. Our results demonstrate that active perturbation-induced profiling provides a new dimension for securing federated learning against sophisticated adversarial threats.

title: Reducing Annotation Dependency in Low-Resource NLP Using Bootstrap Techniques: A Shona Case Study
authors: Dean Shumbanhete, Zelalem Sintayehu Shibeshi, Nomusa Dlodlo
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: This research explores bootstrap techniques (ensemble masking, Bayesian LSTM, variational inference, sampling with replacement -through dependency tree morphing in the paraphraser-, deep k-means clustering for synthetic labelling, self-supervised training with data-augmentation) as a means by which to overcome the dependency on manual annotation for Natural Language Processing (NLP) in Machine Learning (ML). The study focuses on Shona, an agglutinative language that is under-represented in ML. Unlike high-resource languages, African languages face data scarcity and agglutinative complexity, which limits the deployment of technology. The study employs bootstrapping, a statistical learning method, in a neural network that iteratively improves NLP models using minimal labelled training data through self-supervised learning. By using Bayesian Variational Inference in the weight calculations, a neural network model can effectively represent the relationships between data points as posteriors and priors. As such, Bayesian Variational Inference allows a neural network to represent weights as distributions, allowing for more sophisticated modelling of a language as variables/nodes in a bidirectional, cyclical graph. Results show that bootstrap techniques can help mitigate class imbalances; however, this comes at the cost of a volatile model in the pre-training phase. The model appears to perform best in the first 5-10 epochs, which is expected given its origins in risk-sensitive one-shot learning. When combined with regularisation, Bayesian models stabilise and appear to approximate the performance of a transformer more effectively while maintaining significantly shorter training times. Shona's agglutinative traits mirror isiXhosa and isiZulu, allowing the results of this project to advance NLP in Southern Africa. By prioritising adaptive techniques, this study aims to bridge the gaps in global language technology access.

title: VLM-Guard: Defending Jailbreaks by Monitoring Only Hundreds of Safety-Critical Neurons
authors: Jinyin Hu, Jiawei Zhou, Minshan Xie, Zhonghao Yang, Jing Li, Huadi Zheng, Jie Shi, Daojing He, Yu Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Large Vision Language Models (VLMs) are shown to be vulnerable to jailbreak attacks. Current attack detection methods often fall short due to their inability to comprehensively monitor the large input-output space or to accurately monitor safety-critical neurons associated with harmful semantics, resulting in high false positives and false negatives. In this paper, we introduce VLM-Guard, a highly effective detection framework that defends against jailbreak attacks by precisely identifying critical neurons linked to unsafe behaviors. Leveraging a tailored differential analysis over a large corpus of activation values, VLM-Guard isolates a compact set of neurons—just a few hundred, comprising less than 0.2% of the total—that are strongly correlated with harmful semantics. This enables the design of an attack detector that is not only effective at monitoring adversarial behavior but also lightweight and training-free (i.e., no parameter updates or model fine-tuning), making it well-suited for practical deployment. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that VLM-Guard excels in detecting jailbreak attacks while preserving benign performance in attack-free settings, offering an effective and efficient solution for safeguarding VLMs.

title: DC Bus Voltage Control Strategy for Hybrid Energy Storage System with Renewable Energy System
authors: Sheikh Mohammad Sajid, Pradyumn Chaturvedi, Saquib Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this article, the hybrid energy storage system (HESS) is integrated with a renewable energy system (RES) for the design of a standalone DC microgrid. The HESS consists of a battery energy storage system and a supercapacitor (SC) energy storage system. The RES consists of a solar photovoltaic (PV) system. The behavior of RES depends on the environmental conditions and requires an improved control strategy for tight DC bus voltage regulation. This paper proposes an effective DC bus voltage control strategy for HESS with RES for a standalone DC microgrid. In HESS, the battery goes into deep charge and discharge during dynamic changes like changes in PV irradiation and load demand. Then the SC energy storage system is going to supply the high frequency component of current during dynamic changes and extend the life cycles of the battery energy storage system. The stability of a designed controller is verified using frequency response based bode plots. This additional feature is added to it and reliable supply to critical loads with this control strategy. The proposed control strategy is verified using the MATLAB environment.
