title: Joint Shape-Position Optimization Enhanced 2D DOA Estimation in Movable Antenna Systems
authors: Chengzhi Ye, Ruoyu Zhang, Lei Yao, Wen Wu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Movable Antenna (MA) technology is emerging as a promising advancement with the potential to significantly enhance the performance of future wireless communication and sensing systems. In this paper, we address two-dimensional (2D) direction of arrival (DOA) estimation via joint shape-position optimization. Specifically, we formulate an optimization problem aimed at minimizing the Cram´er-Rao Bound (CRB) based on a 2D DOA estimation model for MA systems. To tackle the highly non-convex nature of this CRB minimization, we investigate the spatial utilization of the movable region (MR) under minimum antenna spacing constraints. By demonstrating that an equilateral triangle yields the minimum overlap area, we strategically design an equilateral triangular MR. This specific geometric configuration enables the exploitation of structural symmetry to simplify the geometric constraints, which effectively reduces the complexity of solving the optimization problem. Subsequently, we derive the optimal MA positions by selecting the candidate locations farthest from the centroid of MR. The results demonstrate that the proposed joint shape-position optimization substantially enhances 2D DOA estimation performance.

title: SSM-DETR: A High-Fidelity Fisheye Perception Framework for Autonomous Driving Scenarios
authors: Qingfeng Ge, Dongping Jia, Pei Lin, Chen Cui
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: In intelligent transportation systems (ITS) and autonomous driving applications, object detection systems based on fisheye vision sensors face significant challenges in complex traffic scenarios. Traditional sensor data processing methods have limited capabilities in handling fisheye visual information degraded by factors such as radial distortion, complex lighting variations, and coexisting multi-scale targets. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an end-to-end sensor data processing framework for fisheye image object detection, termed SSM-DETR. This framework integrates three collaborative sensor data enhancement modules: Selective Adaptive Feature Extraction Network (SAFE-Net), which collaboratively models geometric distortion features of fisheye images in both spatial and frequency domains; Spectral-Polar Adaptive Encoding Layer (SPAEL), which employs a bipolar efficient attention mechanism to decompose attention into positive and negative polarity components while enhancing fisheye image radial geometric perception capability; and Multi-scale Adaptive Feature Fusion Pyramid Network (MAFFP-Net), which constructs a cross-stage multi-receptive field enhancement architecture based on frequency-spatial domain collaborative attention mechanism to achieve adaptive fusion of multi-scale semantic information. Comprehensive experiments on the Amsterdam Urban Fisheye Vision Dataset (AUFV-Dataset) demonstrate that SSM-DETR achieves 60.36% mAP50 and 38.67% mAP50-95, which represent gains of 4.4% and 3.96% over the RT-DETR baseline, respectively, while maintaining a real-time inference speed of 70.1 FPS. Extensive validation across multiple datasets further verifies that the framework possesses strong generalization capability.

title: A Ka-Band Splash Hat-Fed Reflector Antenna With Ultra-Wideband and ETSI Class IV Radiation Patterns for 5G Backhaul
authors: Yi-Xiang Li, Bing-Zhong Wang, Lin Peng, Jun-Wei Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Wide bandwidth and high sidelobe suppression are critical for the application of 5G backhaul networks. However, the reported hat-fed reflector antennas show limitations in these performance aspects. In this communication, several techniques are incorporated into a Ka-band splash hat-fed reflector antenna to achieve ultra-wideband and ETSI Class IV radiation patterns for 5G backhaul application. To suppress edge diffraction and obtain wide impedance matching, a novel splash hat feed, consisting of an upward-tilted corrugated brim, a zigzag-shaped sub-reflector, a multi-step dielectric head and a multi-step dielectric rod, is proposed. The proposed multi-step dielectric rod functions not only as an impedance matching device, but also as a TE11-HE11 mode converter to improve radiation performance. To further suppress far-out sidelobes, a metal cylinder with a foam absorber covering its inner surface is proposed for the reflector. Moreover, a self-developed Visual Basic (VB) code is designed to convert sub-efficiencies and additional specific criteria into optimization objectives, which helps to suppress the far-out sidelobes while ensuring high antenna efficiency within a wideband. A prototype was fabricated and measured. The measured relative impedance bandwidth (|S11| < -17.7 dB) is approximately 56.7% (25.8-46.2 GHz). Within desired band (26.5-43.5 GHz), the antenna efficiency ranges from 0.6 to 0.7, with co- and cross-polar radiation patterns in E-and H-planes compliant with the strictest ETSI envelopes, Class IV.

title: FISformer: Replacing Self-Attention with a Fuzzy Inference System in Transformer Models for Time Series Forecasting
authors: Bülent Haznedar, Levent Karacan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: Transformers have achieved remarkable progress in time series forecasting, yet their reliance on deterministic dot-product attention limits their capacity to model uncertainty and nonlinear dependencies across multivariate temporal dimensions. To address this limitation, we propose FISformer, a Fuzzy Inference System-Driven Transformer that replaces conventional attention with a FIS Interaction mechanism. In this framework, each query-key pair undergoes a fuzzy inference process for every feature dimension, where learnable membership functions and rule-based reasoning estimate token-wise relational strengths. These FIS-derived interaction weights capture uncertainty and provide interpretable, continuous mappings between tokens. A softmax operation is applied along the token axis to normalize these weights, which are then combined with the corresponding value features through element-wise multiplication to yield the final context-enhanced token representations. This design fuses the interpretability and uncertainty modeling of fuzzy logic with the representational power of Transformers. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that FISformer achieves superior forecasting accuracy, noise robustness, and interpretability compared to state-of-the-art Transformer variants, establishing fuzzy inference as an effective alternative to conventional attention mechanisms.

title: Towards Long-Tailed Federated Edge Learning: A Strategic Data Selection Framework
authors: Lianhong Zhang, Shunpu Tang, Chao Li, Lisheng Fan, Arumugam Nallanathan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Federated edge learning (FEEL) has emerged as a privacy-preserving paradigm for collaborative intelligence in mobile edge computing (MEC) networks, where edge servers (ESs) coordinate edge devices (EDs) for local model training while a central server (CS) executes global model aggregation. However, practical FEEL systems often suffer from biased model training due to the typical long-tailed data collected at the edge side, which degrades model performance. Moreover, this issue is further aggravated by unreliable update delivery over fading channels, since the loss of updates from rare (tail) data further increases model bias and degrades the global model. To address this issue, we propose a joint optimization framework for data selection and transmission. Specifically, we design distribution-oriented priority assessment, including a weight-based method tailored for local distribution-aware scenarios, and a dynamic weight-based method for cases lacking local information, both of which actively prioritize EDs with greater volumes of tail data. Based on these priorities, we further develop channel state information (CSI)-based bandwidth allocation, including an instant channel state information (ICSI)-based dynamic allocation scheme and a statistical channel state information (SCSI)-based one-time allocation scheme, to guarantee that the high-priority tail-rich EDs can successfully transmit their data. Simulation results show that the proposed framework improves model accuracy over random selection, especially under severe data imbalance. In particular, under the most extreme data condition with an imbalance factor of 100 and a non-IID degree of 0.1, the proposed framework achieves an overall accuracy gain of approximately <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> compared to the baseline.

title: ThermalGaussian++: Improving Alignment and Resolution for ThermalGaussian
authors: Rongfeng Lu, Chi Zhu, Quan Chen, Le Zhang, Ming Lu, Tingyu Wang, Haofan Ren, Yitian Xue, Yunfei Guo, Chenggang Yan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Thermography is especially valuable for the military and other users of surveillance cameras. Some recent methods based on Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) have been proposed to reconstruct thermal scenes in 3D from a set of thermal and RGB images. However, unlike NeRF, 3D Gaussian splatting (3DGS) prevails due to its rapid training and real-time rendering. In this work, we propose ThermalGaussian, the first thermal 3DGS approach capable of rendering high-quality images in RGB and thermal modalities. We first calibrate the RGB camera and the thermal camera to ensure that both modalities are accurately aligned. Subsequently, we use the registered images to learn the multimodal 3D Gaussians. To prevent the overfitting of any single modality, we introduce several multimodal regularization constraints. We also develop smoothing constraints tailored to the physical characteristics of the thermal modality. Besides, we contribute a real-world dataset named RGBT-Scenes, captured by a handheld thermal-infrared camera, facilitating future research on thermal scene reconstruction. Based on ThermalGaussian, we further introduce ThermalGaussian++ to improve the alignment and resolution of ThermalGaussian. To improve multimodal alignment, we design a multimodal pose optimization module. This module enables direct processing of non-aligned multimodal image pairs, reducing the need for professional calibration before each use. To improve thermal resolution, we also propose a multimodal joint super-resolution reconstruction module, which enhances the quality of low-resolution thermal fields. Additionally, we contribute a new dataset: RGBT-Scenes++, which offers higher-resolution thermal images. We conduct comprehensive experiments demonstrating that ThermalGaussian++ achieves photorealistic thermal rendering and improves RGB rendering quality. It significantly enhances both alignment and resolution, enabling better practical deployment. In addition, our multimodal regularization constraints reduce the model's storage requirements. The code and datasets will be released.

title: Advancing In-Context Learning for Efficient and Stable Medical Report Generation
authors: Mingjie Li, Rui Liu, Zeyi Shi, Mingfei Han, Lina Yao, Zhihui Li, Xiaojun Chang, Kilian M. Pohl, Md Tauhidul Islam, Lei Xing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) have shown strong generalization across multimodal tasks, but adapting them to medical report generation (MRG) often demands extensive paired image-text data that are limited due to data privacy and annotation cost. In-context learning (ICL) offers a promising training-free alternative, yet standard ICL approaches rely on long demonstration prompts that are computationally inefficient and often yield inconsistent or clinically inaccurate descriptions. To address these challenges, we propose Principal In-Context Vectors (PCVs), a compact latent-guidance framework that distills multimodal demonstrations into stable semantic representations. By extracting hidden states from auto-regressive VLMs and applying principal component analysis (PCA), we identify robust semantic directions that remain stable under input perturbations. These PCVs are then injected into new queries to steer generation toward accurate and clinically meaningful outputs without any model tuning. Extensive experiments on four MRG benchmark datasets show that our approach can enhance both zero-shot and fully supervised generation quality across diverse settings, including cross-center, cross-disease, and longitudinal scenarios. This work provides a lightweight and scalable approach to adapt pre-trained VLMs for practical clinical deployment.

title: Improved Residual Learning in Diffusion Models
authors: Junyu Zhang, Daochang Liu, Eunbyung Park, Shichao Zhang, Chang Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Diffusion models (DMs) have profoundly transformed the field of generative modeling, delivering exceptional image generation quality. Nevertheless, residuals in the generated images, arising from modeling errors that accumulate along the practical sampling trajectories of pre-trained DMs, remain an inevitable challenge. To mitigate this, we propose a novel residual learning framework built upon a parameterized correction function, designed to improve modeling performance through residual correction. Most notably, our framework exhibits remarkable transferable residual correction capabilities, enabling a correction function optimized for a specific pre-trained DM on a given dataset to enhance the performance of other DMs trained on the same dataset. To further improve our framework, we introduce an advanced training approach that designs an ODE sampler bank to refine residual simulation and incorporates a score consistency maintenance technique to enhance model convergence. Building on this approach, the optimized correction function achieves substantial improvements in residual correction. Extensive experiments performed on four widely used datasets and multiple pre-trained DMs validate the effectiveness and superiority of our residual learning framework.

title: Constrained Harmonization of Optical Satellite Imagery for Surface Reflectance Bias Removal
authors: Lino Garda Denaro, Chao-Hung Lin
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Surface reflectance derived from optical satellite images is a fundamental and important material for remote sensing applications. The advanced atmospheric corrections have achieved a great progress in removal of atmospheric effects by using complicated image- and physical-based models. Those algorithms are processed in each image data acquisition and using additional in-situ data information. However, the process of image-by-image atmospheric correction may cause biases for multitemporal images, which produce temporal inconsistencies and discontinuities over the surface reflectance of invariant ground objects. In this study, a surface reflectance bias removal is proposed for multispectral and multitemporal satellite images using a constrained harmonization. The constrained harmonization is applied by using pseudoinvariant features (PIFs) instead of an entire image. The PIFs are determined by using iterative reweighted multitemporal and multivariate alteration detection (IRMMAD). With using PIFs, the removal of surface reflectance bias in multispectral and multitemporal images is achieved through solving a constrained least-squares linear system simultaneously. Those constraints are called temporal and spectral consistency constraints which can maintain the temporal and spectral consistencies, respectively. To test the robustness of the proposed method, surface reflectance derived from six atmospheric correction (AC) models were tested. Qualitative and quantitative analyses on the surface reflectance of multitemporal images have been conducted. The experimental results show that the surface reflectance biases can be significantly removed while the spectral signatures can be preserved. In addition, the proposed method outperforms related paired-wise harmonization and temporal-constrained harmonization methods in terms of consistency measurements.

title: Beyond SCR: A Comprehensive Approach to System Strength Assessment in IBR-Rich Networks
authors: Sudipta Ghosh, Surour Alaraifi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: High levels of inverter-based resources (IBRs) reduce synchronous inertia and influence traditional indicators of “system strength,” such as the short circuit ratio (SCR). This letter introduces a novel inertia-focused composite metric that can complement or replace strength-only screening in weak grids. It integrates three coherent components: (i) accelerating-bus inertia HB, which measures the inertial support at any bus; (ii) effective distribution inertia (EDI), which accounts for the system-level inertial support; and (iii) a Redundancy Factor (RF) that considers the diverse inertia paths owing to network topology and sources. These three elements can be normalized and combined into a System Strength Index (SSI) designed for online monitoring using PMU/SCADA data. The SSI provides the system strength through a single-number that quantifies the system strength by considering the inertias' magnitude, distribution, and network topology. Its multiplicative structure rewards concurrent magnitude and deliverability while penalizing low-inertia or path redundancy.

title: Decision-Making in Controlled Environment Agriculture: Producer Perspectives on Digital Decision-Support Tools in the US_supp1-3677667.docx
authors: Ziynet Boz
publish: N/A
abstract: Controlled environment agriculture (CEA) has gained popularity due to its ability to deliver high yields and improved resource use efficiencies. These outcomes are attributed, in part, to its enhanced operational control capabilities. However, CEA systems require detailed management decisions for all conditions, necessitating significant horticultural and technological knowledge that many incoming operators may lack. This creates a demand for effective decision-support tools (DSTs). The objective of this study was to investigate the decision-making processes of CEA farmers in the United States, identify key areas where operators require the most support, and understand their perceptions of DSTs. An explanatory mixed-methods research approach was employed. Data were collected from CEA farmers across the United States to determine production challenges, decision-making factors, and attitudes toward DSTs. The study found that labor management, integrated pest management, and climate control were the most significant challenges faced by CEA operators, although these challenges varied by climate zones. Farmers emphasized consumer needs and schedules as key factors in their decision-making process. While DSTs were generally viewed positively, concerns were raised about their cost and the difficulty of use. These findings underscore the need for DSTs that are both cost-effective and user-friendly, addressing critical production challenges. This study provides valuable insights for DST developers by identifying priority focus areas for CEA systems and considerations for designing decision-making algorithms. Addressing identified challenges will help improve the adoption and effectiveness of DSTs, ultimately supporting more efficient and successful CEA operations.

title: Unified Multimodal Industrial Anomaly Detection via Few Normal Samples
authors: Shuai Jiang, Yunfeng Ma, Jingyu Zhou, Yaonan Wang, Min Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Multimodal industrial anomaly detection (MIAD) is the process of integrating multiple sensor data and utilizing visual intelligence to identify abnormal states in industrial production. In this article, we focus on two main practical but challenging issues in MIAD, i.e., a unified model for multiclass anomaly detection, and model training with only few normal samples. The current mainstream “one-for-one” paradigm requires training time that grows exponentially, and it relies on a sufficient number of samples (even just normal samples), which cannot adapt to practical industrial scenarios with rich abnormal classes. To this end, we offer a Unified MIAD model that trained using only Few (e.g., 1, 2, and 4) normal samples, termed UniMF. Specifically, we propose a fusion-guided prompt engineering process that generates paired antithetical instance-specific prompts with the assistance of multimodal fusion at both query and token levels. To enable cross-modal prompt learning under multimodal conditions, UniMF performs multi-proxy pairwise matching that involves alignment among multimodal feature patches, embeddings, and tokens of antithetical prompts. Experimental results show that UniMF stands state-of-the-art performance while remaining “one-for-all” paradigm, and even outperforms “one-for-one” methods under certain settings. Cross-dataset evaluation between MVTec 3D-AD and Eyecandies datasets also shows the transferability of UniMF.

title: Mitigating Attacks on Web Applications via Fine-Grained Adaptive Control-Flow Integrity
authors: Min-Chieh Wu, Yu-Sung Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: While control-flow integrity (CFI) has been extensively applied to binary programs to ensure correct code execution, its application to web applications remains largely at the research stage. A key challenge lies in the inherent flexibility of web application programming and the extensive customizations it allows, which make optimizations based on static code analysis far less effective. To achieve practical performance, existing systems are limited to coarse-grained control-flow integrity, which leaves in trinsic detection blind spots. We propose fine-grained adaptive control-flow integrity (fgaCFI), the first web application CFI system that achieves basic-block level CFI with practical performance overhead. The Lightweight Dynamic Bytecode Monitor (LDBM) prototype, targeting PHP-based applications, supports three CFI monitoring granularity levels and adjustable monitoring scopes. Our results showed that basic-block level CFI achieved 100% detection coverage, outperforming function-level CFI with 83.33% detection coverage and request-level CFI with 58.33% detection coverage. By adapting to the uneven distribution of vulner abilities in the software modules, LDBM was able to maintain an 8.77% performance overhead on average.

title: Lightweight Spiking U-net: An Energy-Efficient Network for Radar LSS Target Detection
authors: Yingying Wu, Rui Guo, Caiyong Lin, Shiyou Xu
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Detection of low, slow, and small (LSS) targets in complex ground clutter remains a critical challenge in radar surveillance. While convolutional neural network (CNN)-based methods have significantly improved detection performance, they perform dense computations uniformly across the range-Doppler map, creating a severe mismatch with the intrinsic spatial sparsity of LSS targets. This redundancy prohibits deployment on battery-powered edge radar systems with strict power constraints, typically below 10 mW. To resolve this, we propose an LSS-UNet, a lightweight spiking neural network (SNN)-based framework for energy-efficient, end-to-end LSS target detection. We formulate the task as a semantic segmentation problem in the R-D domain, leveraging the sparse spatiotemporal dynamics of spiking neurons to extract weak target features from strong clutter. Experiments on two public radar LSS datasets demonstrate that the LSS-UNet achieves an F1-score exceeding 0.92 comparable to full-precision ANN baselines. By utilizing accumulation-only operations via an average firing rate of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\approx$</tex-math></inline-formula> 12%, it achieves an estimated computational energy reduction of approximately 21× compared to ANN baselines, based on standard CMOS operation-energy constants, alongside significantly lowered memory bandwidth requirements. This validates SNNs as a viable solution for high-reliability, low-power edge sensing.

title: Financial Services Information Security Culture: The Effect of Strategy, Top Management Involvement, Knowledge and Knowledge Sharing
authors: Joseph Mupokosera
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Financial services firms are one of the prime targets of information security violations due to the high probability of the assailants realizing huge pecuniary returns from initiating the attacks on them. It is forecast that the assortment of socio-cultural procedures implemented by firms, that is, information security culture, is an effective safeguard to information security breaches. Information security culture aims to safeguard firms' information assets by way of swaying human behaviour to be attuned to the protection of the firm's information assets. Information security strategy, top management involvement, information security knowledge and information security knowledge sharing are predicted to be amongst the determinants of financial services firms' information security culture. Therefore, this research examines the effect that information security strategy, top management involvement, information security knowledge and information security knowledge sharing exert on financial services firms' information security culture. This research is a quantitative multiple case study of four financial services firms in Zimbabwe wherein 386 respondents were purposefully selected to respond to a questionnaire whose constructs were obtained from the review of extant literature on financial services information security culture. Reliability, confirmatory factor analysis, correlation and regression analysis were performed on the data obtained from the questionnaires to determine whether or not the constructs outlined above are significant predictors of financial services information security culture in the Zimbabwean context. The results of the study show that information security strategy, top management involvement and information security knowledge sharing exert on financial services firms' information security culture are all significant predictors of financial services firms' information security culture. On the contrary, the study established that, information security knowledge is not related to information security culture in the financial services.

title: Security Engineering with AI for Purple Teaming: Multi-Case Study of Cybersecurity Engineering Practices in Zimbabwe, Zambia and Botswana
authors: Lynet Tsitsi Svotwa, Arnold Ganga, Tinashe Kachote
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Cybersecurity threats in Southern Africa are growing in both complexity and frequency, placing increasing pressure on organizations to strengthen their defense mechanisms. With the agility around Digital transformation a lot of technologies are rapidly changing through continuous software releases as well as integrations of Artificial Intelligence in modern business operations, The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into security operations is now a necessity is it presents promising opportunities for iteratively improving threat detection, incident response, and overall cyber resilience. However, the adoption of AI in the region remains inconsistent due to infrastructural limitations, financial constraints within various organisations, a lack of localized datasets, insufficient awareness, and ethical concerns. This study investigates the integration of AI into key security tools and Security Operations Center (SOC) functions, specifically focusing on Endpoint Detection and Response, Extended Detection and Response (XDR) and automated Penetration Testing using AI driven tools such as Horizon3.ai. The aim is to assess how these technologies contribute to strengthening cybersecurity capabilities across organizations in Southern Africa. Employing a qualitative case study methodology, the research draws on insights from cybersecurity practices in Zimbabwe, Zambia, and Botswana. Data collection involved interviews with security professionals, analysis of implementation documentation, and evaluation of AI-driven tools used in SOC centers for incident investigations and threat hunting. The findings indicate that AI technologies significantly enhance the effectiveness of cybersecurity operations, particularly in terms of automation and real-time response. Red teaming tools powered by AI are particularly effective in simulating attacker behavior, uncovering misconfigurations, and providing remediation guidance for purple teamers. However, critical challenges persist, including limited infrastructure, skill shortages, regulatory gaps, and resistance to AI adoption due to ethical and governance concerns. Despite these barriers, organizations that have successfully implemented AI report marked improvements in their ability to detect and respond to cyber threats. The study recommends the development of region-specific AI and cybersecurity policies, investment in technical infrastructure and capacity-building programs, and the creation of centralized anonymized datasets that reflect local cybersecurity contexts. It also emphasizes the need for collaborative efforts among governments, industry stakeholders, and academic institutions to ensure the ethical and effective deployment of AI in security engineering. These measures are essential to cultivating a secure, adaptive, and resilient digital ecosystem in Southern Africa.

title: Teletext on the Radio: Co-Opting Some of the Best Tech from the 1980s
authors: Stephen Cass
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: Once upon a time in Europe, television remote controls had a magic teletext button. Years before the internet stole into homes, pressing that button brought up teletext digital information services with hundreds of constantly updated pages. Living in Ireland in the 1980s and '90s, my family accessed the national teletext service—Aertel—multiple times a day for weather and news bulletins, as well as things like TV program guides and updates on airport flight arrivals.

title: A Simulation-Based AI-Assisted Framework for Beam Steering in Reconfigurable Metasurface Antennas
authors: Mohammad H. Ahmad
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Millimeter-wave communication systems require highly directive and dynamically steerable antennas to mitigate the severe propagation losses and blockage effects. Reconfigurable metasurface antennas have emerged as a promising solution for adaptive beam control. However, conventional beam synthesis methods rely on computationally intensive full-wave electromagnetic optimization or exhaustive parameter sweeps, limiting their suitability for rapid reconfiguration and large configuration spaces. This paper presents a simulation-based artificial intelligence-assisted framework for efficient beam steering in reconfigurable metasurface antennas. The proposed approach formulates beam synthesis as an inverse control problem, in which a supervised learning model is trained on full-wave electromagnetic simulation data to directly infer metasurface configuration states corresponding to the desired beam directions. Once trained, the model enabled fast and direct prediction without repeated electromagnetic optimization. The numerical results demonstrate accurate beam steering over a ±60° angular range, achieving mean beam-pointing errors below 2.0°, while maintaining stable gain and controlled sidelobe levels across multiple operating frequencies. Furthermore, the proposed framework achieves a reduction of more than two orders of magnitude in the online inference latency compared with conventional full-wave optimization. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of learning-assisted inverse synthesis for reducing the computational burden of metasurface beam control and establishing a scalable, simulation-driven foundation for future intelligent and experimentally realizable electromagnetic surface systems.

title: CraftGround: A High-Performance Reinforcement Learning Environment Based on Minecraft
authors: Hyeonseo Yang, Minsu Lee, Byoung-Tak Zhang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Recent trends in reinforcement learning have focused on developing agents that can operate in open-ended, multimodal environments and can be generalized to real-world tasks. Minecraft offers a rich, procedurally generated world that supports hierarchical skill learning, long-term planning, and complex decision-making, making it a compelling testbed for advancing generalizable reinforcement learning research. However, existing Minecraft-based frameworks such as Malmö, despite their early contributions, suffer from slow simulation speeds, outdated dependencies, and limited extensibility–posing significant challenges for modern, large-scale reinforcement learning research. To address these limitations, we introduce CraftGround, a next-generation high-performance Minecraft-based reinforcement learning benchmark environment. Rather than simply improving upon Malmö, CraftGround is designed from the ground up for scalability, multimodal interaction, and experimental flexibility. It achieves up to 2.79× faster simulation performance and supports sound subtitles for richer perception. It also provides a unified action configuration that allows seamless switching between discrete and human-like controls without reconfiguration. These advancements make CraftGround a robust and future-ready platform for complex and realistic reinforcement learning research. We further assess its usability through two RL experiments, showing that CraftGround supports practical learning in representative settings. The environment is open-sourced under the GNU General Public License v3.0 and publicly available at https://github.com/yhs0602/CraftGround.

title: A Step-size Adaptation Golden Eagle Optimizer-Based K-Means Method for Maritime Search and Rescue Clustering
authors: Xu Zhang, Yuan Zhao, Zhixiong Huang, Yangyang Liu, Baowang Lian, Zesheng Dan
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Traditional clustering algorithms often face limitations in solving the problem of survivor clustering and rescue platform deployment in large-scale maritime search and rescue, due to their limited global exploration ability and tendency to be trapped in local optima. To address this problem, this paper proposes a step size adaptation Golden Eagle Optimization KMeans clustering algorithm (SAGeo-KMeans). The proposed algorithm combines the advantages of the global exploration capability of the Golden Eagle Optimization algorithm (GEO) and the local exploration capability of the KMeans algorithm through a novel step size adaptation factor. The factor considers the exploration completeness of the cluster centers, the spatial distribution of survivors, and the adaptive adjustment strategy of GEO, enabling dynamic adjustment of the search step size. Simulation results indicate that, compared with existing methods, the proposed algorithm can reduce the total rescue distance within a limited number of iterations, effectively improving search and rescue efficiency.

title: Fixed-Time Sliding Mode Control of a Thrust-Vectored Planar VTOL UAV with Strongly Coupled Dynamics
authors: Jhonatan-Fernando Eulopa-Hernandez, Jairo Olguin-Roque, Diego-Alfredo Padilla Pérez, Pablo-Alejandro Arizpe-Carreón, Carlos Couder-Castañeda
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents the design and numerical validation of a robust fixed-time sliding mode controller (FTSMC) for a thrust-vectored planar vertical takeoff and landing (PVTOL) system with strongly coupled nonlinear dynamics. Unlike conventional PVTOL models assuming aligned thrust, the proposed system explicitly incorporates thrust vectoring, resulting in significant inter-axis coupling. A fixed-time sliding-mode control law with smooth nonlinear terms is developed to ensure practical fixed-time convergence to a predefined boundary layer, independently of initial conditions, while mitigating chattering. Numerical simulations under bounded disturbances demonstrate that the proposed FTSMC outperforms a non-terminal sliding mode controller (NTSMC), achieving a reduction of approximately 38% in settling time, more than 80% reduction in pitch overshoot, and up to 60% decrease in control effort. These results highlight the robustness and efficiency of the proposed control strategy for safety-critical UAV and rocket-based platforms that require rapid, precise maneuvering.

title: TSUE+: an Efficient Update Framework with Swift Recycling Mechanism for Erasure-Coded Cluster File Systems
authors: Zheng Wei, Jing Xing, Yida Gu, Wenjing Huang, Yili Ma, Dong Dai, Guangming Tan, Dingwen Tao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Compared to replication-based storage systems, erasure-coded storage incurs significantly higher overhead during data updates. To address this issue, various parity logging methods have been proposed. Nevertheless, due to the long update path and substantial amount of random I/O involved in erasure code update processes, the resulting long latency and low through put often fail to meet the requirements of high performance applications. To address this challenge, we propose TSUE+, an efficient update framework with a swift recycling mechanism. TSUE+ divides the update process into two distinct stages: in the synchronous stage, data updates are stored in the format of replica data logs, eliminating random I/O by trading space for time; in the asynchronous stage, the recorded update logs are recycled and merged into original data and parity blocks, thereby reclaiming the storage overhead incurred in the synchronization phase. By converting random I/O operations into sequential ones based on data logs, TSUE+ effectively reduces update latency; furthermore, it significantly minimizes recycling overhead using a three-layer log structure and by leveraging the spatio-temporal locality of access patterns. We evaluated TSUE+ and other state of-the-art (SOTA) update mechanisms under diverse encoding schemes, using heterogeneous storage devices—including HDDs, SATA SSDs, NVMe SSDs, and PMEM—and multiple real-world and synthetic workloads: the MSR Cambridge trace, the Alibaba Cloud trace, the Tencent Cloud trace, and multiple synthetic worst-case workloads. Among all the platforms, TSUE+ has achieved significant performance improvements compared to other update methods, it also indicates that TSUE+ can be applied to various storage devices. Additionally, we provided percentile-based tail latency tests and update tests under the worst-case environment, which further demonstrated the ro bustness of TSUE+. Moreover, by enabling prompt log recycle and avoiding unnecessary overwrites and improving update granularity through locality-aware recycling, TSUE+ not only improves update performance but also mitigates write wear on SSD devices, thereby extending their operational lifespan.

title: Design Factors Affecting IMU-based Joint Torque Estimation Using Deep Learning
authors: Hongbo Wang, Qingyao Bian, Hang Zhou, Quanbo Ge, Zhenyu Lu, Ziyun Ding
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurate lower limb joint torque estimation is important for gait analysis, rehabilitation planning, and the control of assistive devices. While inertial measurement units (IMUs) provide a portable alternative to laboratory-based motion capture systems, the influence of key design choices on deep learning-based joint torque estimation remains insufficiently understood. This study systematically evaluated five factors affecting lower limb joint torque estimation across five degrees of freedom: hip flexion, hip adduction, hip rotation, knee flexion, and ankle dorsiflexion. Using a public dataset of 20 healthy adults during treadmill walking, we examined model architecture, sampling frequency, sliding window size, IMU sensor configuration, and data normalisation method. Each configuration was evaluated over 10 independent training runs. Recurrent neural networks outperformed the other architectures, with LSTM achieving the strongest overall performance. Among the tested settings, 100 Hz sampling, a 64-sample sliding window, a four-sensor configuration, and Z-score normalisation gave the best overall results. The resulting benchmark configuration achieved an average Pearson correlation coefficient (R) of 0.97 across five lower limb joint torque estimation tasks. These findings provide an evidence-based reference for IMU-based joint torque estimation and support the development of wearable biomechanics for clinical gait analysis, rehabilitation, and assistive technology.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences

title: CCAT: Silicon-Platelet Feedhorns for Submillimeter Wavelengths
authors: Jason Austermann, James Beall, James R. Burgoyne, Scott Chapman, Steve Choi, Cody J. Duell, Anthony I. Huber, Johannes Hubmayr, Matthew A. Koc, Michael D. Niemack, Joel N. Ullom, Jeffrey van Lanen, Anna Vaskuri, Michael Vissers, Jordan Wheeler
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Silicon-platelet feedhorn arrays are an established technology at millimeter wavelengths that, for some applications, can provide significant advantages over traditional direct-machined metal feedhorns. The Prime-Cam focal planes operating in the 350 GHz (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>860 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m) and 850 GHz (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>350 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m) bands are anticipated to carry the first silicon-platelet feedhorn arrays to operate fully at submillimeter wavelengths, representing a significant step forward in the application of this technology. In particular, the feedhorns designed for operation in the 850 GHz band represent a 3x increase in frequency compared to previously demonstrated and deployed devices of this type. Here we present a demonstration of silicon-platelet feedhorns at these submillimeter wavelengths, including in-lab performance characterization. We present fabrication metrology, room-temperature beam maps, and cryogenic optical efficiency measurements where the feedhorns are coupled to prototype CCAT Prime-Cam detectors. We show that feedhorn performance measurements are well matched to simulation and compare that performance directly to traditional, direct-machined metal feedhorns.

title: Highly Integrated Electrically Small Magneto-Electric Dipole Rectenna for Simultaneous Wireless Information and Power Transfer
authors: Shaopeng Wan, Wenhong Chen, Ping Lu, Chaoyun Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: To enhance compactness in IoT devices, wireless power and information transmission can be integrated into a unified system. In this work, a rectifying circuit is highly integrated with an electrically small magneto-electric dipole antenna, forming the RF front-end of a simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) system. The rectenna comprises an electric dipole and a pair of split rings, with the rectifying circuit directly embedded within the structure. Specifically, the rectifying circuit is connected to the open slot of one split ring, facilitating efficient energy harvesting and power conversion. Furthermore, the miniaturized electric dipole is well-suited for data communication. To achieve high isolation, a periodic rectangular-patch structure is designed to reduce mutual coupling between the two ports of the dipole and split rings. The electric dipole antenna receives low power but ensures a satisfactory signal-to-noise ratio (SNR>59 dB in the input power of 0 dBm) for signal demodulation/decoding. On the other hand, the split ring with embedded rectifying circuit achieves a maximum power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 66.8% for wireless power transfer. The proposed rectenna has merits of electrically small (ka = 0.99), low profile and high isolation, making it suitable for remote powering of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and wireless information.

title: On the Availability Risks of Production LLM Services under Unbounded Inference
authors: Yunzhe Li, Jianan Wang, Hongzi Zhu, James Lin, Shan Chang, Minyi Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become foundational components in a wide range of applications, including natural language understanding and generation, embodied intelligence, and scientific discovery. As their computational requirements continue to grow, these models are increasingly deployed as cloud-based services, allowing users to access powerful LLMs via the Internet. However, this deployment model introduces a new class of threat: denial-of-service (DoS) attacks via unbounded reasoning, where adversaries craft specially designed inputs that cause the model to enter excessively long or infinite generation loops. These attacks can exhaust backend compute resources, degrading or denying service to legitimate users. To mitigate such risks, many LLM providers adopt a closed-source, black box setting to obscure model internals. In this paper, we propose ThinkTrap, a novel input-space optimization framework for DoS attacks against LLM services even in black-box environments. The core idea of ThinkTrap is to first map discrete tokens into a continuous embedding space, then undertake efficient black-box optimization in a low-dimensional subspace exploiting input sparsity. The goal of this optimization is to identify adversarial prompts that induce extended or non-terminating generation across several state-of-the-art LLMs, achieving DoS with minimal token overhead. We evaluate ThinkTrap across multiple commercial, closed-source LLM services and observe that it can consistently induce abnormally long outputs and noticeable response-side degradation under black-box access. To further quantify the system-level impact of the attack, we conduct controlled experiments on private LLM deployments, where ThinkTrap reduces service throughput to as low as 1% of its original capacity and, in extreme cases, induces complete service failure due to resource exhaustion.

title: Research of Radiation Tolerance via Polyimide Matrix Multilayer Dielectric Structures
authors: Dong Yue, Tangming Geng, Jialin Liu, Jialu Qi, Yuxin Wang, Wei Kong, Yuanhang Yao, Dongyu Hou, Yu Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Polyimide (PI), characterized by exceptional mechanical properties, high breakdown strength and excellent electron radiation resistance, are ideal substrates for spacecraft antennas in space applications. However, conventional PI film used in aerospace inevitably suffer from the harsh electron radiation environment in space, leading to performance degradation. This work designs a method to enhance the breakdown strength and electron irradiation resistance by constructing a sandwich structure incorporating mica nanosheets (MNNs). The PI-based composite film exhibited a significantly enhanced breakdown strength (657.07 kV/mm), a markedly improved tensile strength (129.8 MPa), and minimal performance loss after high-fluence electron irradiation (breakdown strength decreased by 5.9%, tensile strength decreased by 17.2%). The enhanced electron irradiation resistance can be attributed to the presence of high atomic number elements (Al, Si, K) in MNNs, which enhancing electron scattering, and the establishment of a synergistic "scattering-trapping-enhancement" protective network via the sandwich structure. These work presents a design strategy for developing PI-based composite film with enhanced electron irradiation resistance.

title: Mitigation of Corona Discharge Breakdown in ESIW Bandpass Filters
authors: Antonio Seller Rueda, Stephan Marini, Darío Herraiz, Ángel Belenguer, Héctor Esteban, Vicente E. Boria
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: In this work, corona discharge breakdown thresholds in empty substrate integrated waveguide (ESIW) bandpass filters are investigated and experimentally validated for the first time. The objectives are the analysis and subsequent enhancement of the peak power handling capability (PPHC) of inductive ESIW filters operating in low-pressure environments, where gas breakdown may limit the maximum admissible RF power level. First, the critical regions where the electric field (EF) reaches its maximum amplitude are identified by full-wave electromagnetic (EM) simulations. Then, an enhancement strategy based on the introduction of drilled apertures in the ESIW covers is applied, in order to reshape the field distribution in the most critical zones without compromising the in-band response. The results show a consistent increase in the corona discharge threshold at low pressure, between 2 and 3.1dB, with respect to a reference ESIW bandpass filter of the same electrical specifications.

title: Enhancement of Partial Discharge Performance and Insulation Stability of XLPE/SiR Heterointerfaces in Cable Accessories by Plasma Silicon Deposition
authors: Yuyao Zhong, Qing Xie, Jingli Liu, Ming Yan, Jun Xie, Qijun Duan, Yan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: In cable accessories, the heterogeneous interface between cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) and silicone rubber (SiR) is a critical weak point for partial discharge (PD). This study employs dielectric barrier discharge plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition (DBD-PECVD) to in-situ construct a SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sub>H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</sub> functional layer on XLPE, thereby enhancing the interfacial dielectric strength. The evolution of surface morphology, chemical composition, dielectric strength and trap distribution is investigated under different interfacial pressures. Moderate plasma treatment smooths the XLPE surface and optimizes the trap distribution, while keeping the interfacial conductivity within the insulating range of 1.2×10-<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">15</sup>–6.2×10-<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13</sup> S/m. At an interfacial pressure of 0.4 MPa, the sample treated for 3 min exhibits the highest electrical strength among the investigated conditions. The corresponding partial discharge inception voltage (PDIV) and breakdown voltage (BDV) are 9.79 kV and 21.25 kV, respectively, representing increases of 30.88% and 37.45% compared with the unmodified interface. After thermal aging, the BDV of the plasma treated interface decreases by only 7.62%, less than that of a silicone grease coated interface. These results indicate that a solid SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sub>H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</sub> functional interlayer can enhance the dielectric performance of XLPE/SiR interfaces and improve the retention of interfacial insulation performance under thermal aging.

title: HAPS- and UAV-Based 6G NTN Architecture for Rural Vehicular Connectivity
authors: Muhammad Asghar Khan
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: High-altitude platform stations (HAPS) and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are key elements of non-terrestrial networks (NTNs) for rural vehicular connectivity and emergency response in sixth-generation (6G) systems. In regions where terrestrial infrastructure is sparse or slow to restore, HAPS provide long-endurance, wide-area coverage from the stratosphere, while UAVs enable rapid, localized access. This article presents a layered NTN baseline that integrates satellites (GEO/MEO/LEO), HAPS, UAVs, and selective terrestrial infrastructure with mobile edge computing (MEC). In the proposed design, HAPS operate as control and backhaul anchors, whereas UAVs function as relay and access nodes, maintaining session continuity along road corridors through make-before-break (MBB) handovers. We evaluate three standards-aligned key performance indicators (KPIs): (i) end-to-end latency for safety messaging, map updates, and incident video; (ii) distance-based service availability over a 100-km route using joint SINR and deadline constraints; and (iii) backhaul goodput under clear, moderate, and heavy weather conditions for GEO, MEO, LEO, HAPS–FSO, HAPS–mmWave, and micro-wave links. The reported results, derived under stated assumptions, provide practical guidance on design trade-offs, including tier selection, MEC placement, handover strategy, and weather-aware backhaul. This article also highlights key challenges and outlines future research directions.

title: Spectrum-Efficient Coherent Photonic Terahertz Transmission System Based on Subcarrier Multiplexing with Offset Quadrature Amplitude Modulation
authors: Zhuoxin Li, Jiao Zhang, Yutong Jiang, Siyu Zhu, Meng Liu, Jiaxuan Li, Ruizhe Xu, Xiaodong Wei, Shuang Gao, Junkai Mao, Min Zhu
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: To overcome the spectral efficiency (SE) limitations of conventional subcarrier multiplexing (SCM) caused by guard bands, a spectrum-efficient coherent photonic terahertz (THz) transmission system based on SCM with offset quadrature amplitude modulation (SCM-OQAM) is proposed and validated. By modeling intrinsic interference in spectrally compact configurations, we reveal that adjacent-channel interference exhibits an alternating real-imaginary structure. Leveraging this property, the proposed SCM-OQAM scheme achieves interference suppression in the real domain without requiring guard bandwidth. A coherent transmission architecture together with the corresponding digital signal processing (DSP) workflow is developed. Simulation results demonstrate that, under comparable performance conditions, the proposed system achieves approximately 15% improvement in SE compared with SCM-QAM. In addition, owing to the high tolerance of OQAM to truncation effects, the critical truncation length can be reduced from 7T to 3T, leading to a 57% reduction in the number of filter taps and a corresponding reduction in computational complexity. Furthermore, the proposed scheme is particularly well suited to short-reach photonic THz access and fronthaul scenarios, where spectrum resources are scarce and implementation efficiency is critical. These results highlight the potential of SCM-OQAM as an effective approach for jointly improving spectral utilization and DSP efficiency in future high-capacity THz communication systems.

title: “Optimizing Interconnection Calls Routing: A Machine Learning Approach for Cost Efficiency.”
authors: Ivy Anesu Mudari, Mainford Mutandavari, Kenneth Chiworera
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: This study aims to design and develop a machine learning-based automated least-cost routing (LCR) model for telecommunications interconnection calls. The suggested model uses a Random Forest Regressor to estimate the lowest-cost call path based on charge per minute. We used interconnection data from TelOne's Interconnection Department to train and test the model. The results demonstrate improved call cost efficiency. This research contributes practical insights and guidelines for developing intelligent call routing systems, supporting cost reduction in telecommunications networks.
