title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Adaptive Critic Control Design for Leader-Followers Multi-Agent Systems in Zero-Sum Games
authors: Yuna Oh, Jinyoung Lee, Jun Moon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper proposes the reinforcement learning (RL)-based adaptive critic controller for multi-agent systems with one leader and N followers. The overall problem is formulated by the zero-sum game framework, where the followers minimize while the leader maximizes the same cost to capture the formation problem between the leader and followers. The optimal value function is approximated using the RL approach, and an adaptive critic network with an additional stabilization term is employed to derive the weight update rule. By incorporating the additional stabilization term, the need for decomposition in the controller design is eliminated, and the analysis for the closed-loop system stability in the sense of uniformly ultimately boundedness is simplified. In addition, an asymmetric barrier Lyapunov function is utilized to effectively handle output constraints and enhance the control performance. The proposed RL-based adaptive controllers for the leader and followers do not require the exact modeling information, since it is estimated by the neural network-based identifier. The performance of the proposed method is demonstrated through various simulations, including the leader-follower vehicle tracking problem using the CarSim environment.

title: A 22–39-GHz Wideband High-Gain Active Phase Shifter Using Improved QAF and Current-Reused Technique in 28-nm Bulk CMOS
authors: Cheng Cheng An, Li Gao, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a vector-sum phase shifter (VSPS) with low power and high gain based on a 28-nm bulk CMOS process. Informed by an analysis of the load characteristics of the quadrature all-pass filter (QAF), this circuit introduces and implements an innovative asymmetric-resonance structure. The improved QAF significantly reduces the quadrature amplitude error from 2 to 0.3 dB. Moreover, the circuit employs a current-reuse technique by cascading a 6-bit vector synthesis circuit with the common-source stage circuit at the DC level, effectively reducing DC power consumption while maintaining high gain. The proposed phase shifter (PS) realized a 1-dB bandwidth of 22–39 GHz (55.7% fractional bandwidth) and 360° phase shift with a phase resolution of 5.625°. The measured average gain is −1.5 dB, and the calculated corresponding root mean square (rms) gain and phase error are 0.37–0.64 dB and 0.32°–2.7°, respectively. The total DC power consumption is only 15 mW. This PS is particularly suitable for massive element phased-array beamforming, demonstrating over a 50% improvement in the figure of merit (FOM) compared to prior broadband designs.

title: A 150-Mbps Low-Latency Small-Size Fault Digital Isolation System in 180-nm CMOS Process for Aerospace Applications
authors: Isa Altoobaji, Ahmad Hassan, Mohamed Ali, Yves Audet, Ahmed Lakhssassi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Digital isolation systems for high-speed sensor interfaces are key components in aerospace applications. However, working in harsh environments such as high-voltage (HV) common mode (CM) noise and extreme temperature variations limits the speed and increases the latency of the design. In this paper, a 150 Mbps fault digital isolation system is reported. The circuit architecture is based on the pulse amplitude modulation scheme for capacitively coupled isolated links. It enables signal transmission between the transmitter (Tx) and the receiver (Rx) through one isolation capacitor instead of using two elements per channel to form the two complementary paths as in conventional edge-based capacitive methods. It also eliminates the need for an additional channel to transfer low-frequency signals, which require an enlarged capacitor. The proposed approach is simulated using Cadence tools, and a prototype has been manufactured using a 180 nm CMOS process occupying a core silicon area of 24200 μm2. Two identical dies were assembled in a single package to construct the isolation system. Experimental results show that the implemented design achieves a common mode transient immunity (CMTI) of 250 V/μs while operating at a transmission speed of 150 Mbps. The measured propagation delay is 4 ns within a variation rate of 14.2 ps/°C, over temperatures ranging from –40°C to 100°C.

title: Towards Comprehensive Information-theoretic Multi-view Learning
authors: Long Shi, Yunshan Ye, Wenjie Wang, Tao Lei, Yu Zhao, Gang Kou, Badong Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Information theory has inspired numerous advancements in multi-view learning. Most multi-view methods incorporating information-theoretic principles rely an assumption called multi-view redundancy which states that common information between views is necessary and sufficient for down-stream tasks. This assumption emphasizes the importance of common information for prediction, but inherently ignores the potential of unique information in each view that could be predictive to the task. In this paper, we propose a comprehensive information-theoretic multi-view learning framework named CIML, which discards the assumption of multi-view redundancy. Specifically, CIML considers the potential predictive capabilities of both common and unique information based on information theory. First, the common representation learning maximizes G´acs-K¨orner common information to extract shared features and then compresses this information to learn task-relevant representations based on the Information Bottleneck (IB). For unique representation learning, IB is employed to achieve the most compressed unique representation for each view while simultaneously minimizing the mutual information between unique and common representations, as well as among different unique representations. Importantly, we theoretically prove that the learned joint representation is predictively sufficient for the downstream task. Extensive experimental results have demonstrated the superiority of our model over several state-of-art methods. The code is released on CIML.

title: Screening Robust Cover for JPEG Steganography
authors: Jin Li, Kai Zeng, Kejiang Chen, Weiming Zhang, Nenghai Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Online social networks (OSNs) offer an abundant and freely available source of images, providing fertile ground for steganographic communication. However, the mandatory lossy operations applied by these platforms—primarily JPEG recompression— make robustness a pressing challenge. Existing robust steganographic methods focus on improving the embedding process, but inevitably compromise security. In this paper, we break this trade-off by proposing, for the first time, a robust cover screening method that enables successful message extraction after JPEG recompression, even when combined with non-robust steganographic methods. To ensure that the screened covers are compatible with arbitrary steganographic settings—including distortion functions, coding schemes, and messages—we introduce Robustness-Minimizing Modification (RMM), which simulates the worst-case impact of steganographic modifications on cover robustness. Images that remain unchanged under JPEG recompression after RMM are screened as robust covers. Our experiments reveal that such robust covers exist widely in both natural and generated images. Therefore, recent advances in generative modeling enable cost-effective and scalable expansion of candidate covers, addressing potential limitations of the screening method in practice. Our experiments also demonstrate that these screened covers can achieve 100% message extraction even with non-robust steganography at high embedding rates, while maintaining security comparable to other covers.

title: An Intelligent Joint Access Control and Resource Allocation Scheme in Multi-User LEO Satellite Networks
authors: Chong Xu, Feng Liu, Junyi Yang, Haobin Mao, Zhenyu Xiao, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The low earth orbit (LEO) satellite communication network has recently been proposed by 3GPP as a new paradigm of infrastructure to enhance the capacity and coverage of existing terrestrial wireless networks. However, the mobility of LEO satellite nodes leads to a dynamic environment, which introduces unique challenges for handover and throughput optimization in multi-user access control for LEO networks. We formulate an optimization problem of joint access control and resource allocation to maximize the long-term system throughput and avoid frequent handovers, which is non-deterministic polynomial-time hard. To overcome this challenge problem, we propose a multi-agent deep reinforcement learning algorithm and design the proximal policy optimization (PPO) network structure with the long short-term memory (LSTM) layers. In our proposed algorithm, the centralized trainer node is responsible for training the parameters of all networks, and then each ground user independently makes its own access decisions based on its local observation. We deploy a policy network on each ground user that is able to intelligently access a proper LEO satellite node to maintain high system throughput and avoid frequent handovers over a long period. The simulation results have demonstrated the effectiveness and superiority of our proposed algorithm compared to benchmark schemes in addressing the access control and resource allocation issue for the multi-user LEO satellite network.

title: Rapid and Selective Microplastics Detection in Water Using a Cost-Effective CS–TA-Functionalized E-SMS Optical Fiber Sensor
authors: Arturo Gaviria-Calderón, Brayan Patiño-Jurado, Rodrigo Acuna Herrera, Pedro Torres, Jorge Garcia-Sucerquia
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Microplastic contamination in aquatic environments poses a persistent analytical challenge due to the minute size and low environmental concentrations of the plastic particles originating from various sources, including the degradation of large plastic debris, intentionally manufactured microbeads, and synthetic fibers. Among the different types of polymers, polyethylene (PE) stands out as one of the most widely produced plastics around the world and one of the main contributors to microplastic pollution in aquatic environments due to its extensive use in packaging materials and disposable products. To contribute to the development of practical and accessible monitoring tools, in this work we present a cost-effective optical fiber sensor for the selective detection of polyethylene (PE) microplastics. The sensor device is based on an etched singlemode–multimode–singlemode (E-SMS) fiber structure, functionalized with a chitosan–tannic acid (CS–TA) conjugate on the fiber surface to exploit the chemical affinity between the CS–TA matrix and PE. The E-SMS structure was fabricated by splicing two single-mode fibers to a 15 mm multimode fiber segment and etched with hydrofluoric acid. Experimental evaluations over a broad concentration range (0.001 to 1000 ppm) revealed a robust, concentration-dependent response. Performance metrics include a sensitivity of 3.92 nm/μg/mL and a detection limit of 0.01 μg/mL (10 ppb). By combining high selectivity with a simplified fabrication process, this CS–TA-functionalized the sensor provides a fast, sensitive, selective, and cost-effective platform for microplastic monitoring in water.

title: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences Publication Information

title: A Swept-Frequency Eddy Current Linear Response Model Based on a Magnetoelectric Sensor for Wide-Range Metal Coating Thickness Measurement
authors: Yang Qiu, Ziyun Huang, Aijun Song, Yan Li, Jiawei Wang, Guoliang Yu, Mingmin Zhu, Haomiao Zhou
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Aiming at the problem that it is difficult to achieve non-destructive and accurate evaluation of metal coating thickness, a new measurement model based on swept-frequency eddy current is proposed to realize the thickness measurement of metal coatings in the order of nanometers to millimeters. The model reveals that the total magnetic field has a linear relationship with the logarithm of coating thickness at a specific excitation frequency. At the same time, the slope of this linear relationship is also linearly correlated with the logarithm of excitation frequency at different substrate thicknesses. Consequently, the inversion process for coating thickness is simplified by circumventing the need to solve complex coupled parameters. Finally, a measurement system was constructed using a magnetoelectric (ME) sensor, achieving thickness measurement of aluminum coatings from 500 nm to 2 mm with a maximum relative error of less than 3%. This research provides a new method for the wide-range nondestructive testing of metal coatings.

title: Longitudinal Evaluation of Child Fingerprint Recognition: Time Interval up to Eight years
authors: Mst Rumana Sumi, Stephanie Schuckers
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: Child fingerprint recognition systems face unique challenges due to the rapid physiological changes associated with growth, making long-term recognition difficult. This study is focused on a comprehensive evaluation of fingerprint recognition performance over time, utilizing a longitudinal dataset comprising approximately 40K fingerprint images collected every six months over 8 years from 267 children aged 4 to 18 years. With the dataset, this study focused on the impact of aging, i.e, the variations in accuracy due to the increasing time gap between reference and probe samples. Additionally, the study assessed the effectiveness of customized Preciozzi’s scaling and VeriFinger’s fingerprint scaling methods, designed to adjust a fingerprint to compensate for changes related to the growth of a fingerprint. Despite significant aging effects in the 4–8-year age group, our findings reveal that a 4-year-old child remains recognizable for up to 48 months with a TAR of 97.4% at 0.1% FAR and up to 60 months with a TAR of 90.9% at 0.1% FAR without any scaling due to growth. Scaling techniques effectively mitigated decreases in performance, maintaining a TAR of 99.3% at 0.1% FAR for up to 90 months. Additionally, the study highlights that scaling is not strictly essential for children over 8 years of age up to 96 months but remains beneficial for achieving near-perfect accuracy (99–100%) over extended periods, especially for the 9–11-year age group. Linear Mixed-Effects Models revealed key predictors of comparison score variability, including time gap, enrollment age, and image quality, while accounting for their interactions and contributions to recognition performance. This study offers valuable insights into the longitudinal performance of child fingerprint recognition systems, bridging gaps in biometric research and paving the way for policy development and advanced technical solutions to enhance child safety and identification worldwide.

title: Tackling a Resource-Sharing Hybrid Disassembly Line Balancing Problem Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Shujin Qin, Wenjing Zeng, Xiwang Guo, Jiacun Wang, Shixin Liu, Liang Qi, Bin Hu, Jun Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Driven by accelerated product obsolescence and frequent consumer replacements, electronic waste is growing rapidly. Waste recycling, as a core component of resource reuse, has become an important means of alleviating resource scarcity and reducing environmental pollution. In the process of recycling discarded products, the efficiency of disassembly operations is crucial. To improve disassembly efficiency and maximize resource utilization, this work proposes a hybrid disassembly line structure that incorporates both linear and U-shaped workstations. Shared labor is introduced between adjacent disassembly lines, allowing workers to flexibly execute tasks across lines. This resource-sharing mechanism enhances task coordination and reduces idle time, contributing to improved system efficiency. Using a precedence relationship graph to model dependencies among tasks, we develop a mathematical model aimed at maximizing profit. We use an exact solver to verify the model and adopt a variant of dueling deep Q-network, called PER-Dueling DQN (PDDQN), which incorporates prioritized experience replay to enhance sampling efficiency and solve the model optimally. A simulation environment aligned with this problem is constructed for the reinforcement learning agent. We compare the proposed method with other reinforcement learning approaches, including advantage actor-critic, proximal policy optimization, and trust region policy optimization. Through experiments on disassembling products of different sizes, the feasibility and effectiveness of PDDQN are demonstrated, exhibiting significant advantages over other methods.

title: DIP-Watermark: A Double Identity Protection Method Based on Robust Adversarial Watermark
authors: Yunming Zhang, Dengpan Ye, Caiyun Xie, Sipeng Shen, Ziyi Liu, Jiacheng Deng, Yueyun Shang, Zhihong Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The wide deployment of Face Recognition (FR) systems poses privacy risks. One countermeasure is adversarial attack, deceiving unauthorized malicious FR, but it also disrupts regular identity verification of trusted authorizers, exacerbating the potential threat of identity impersonation. To address this, we propose the first double identity protection scheme based on traceable adversarial watermarking, termed DIP-Watermark. DIP-Watermark employs a one-time watermark embedding to deceive unauthorized FR models and allows authorizers to perform identity verification by extracting the watermark. Specifically, we propose an information-guided adversarial attack against FR models. The encoder embeds an identity-specific watermark into the deep feature space of the carrier, guiding recognizable features of the image to deviate from the source identity. We further adopt a collaborative meta-optimization strategy compatible with sub-tasks, which regularizes the joint optimization direction of the encoder and decoder. This strategy enhances the representation of universal carrier features, mitigating multi-objective optimization conflicts in watermarking. Extensive experiments on two large-scale facial datasets demonstrate that DIP-Watermark achieves significant attack success rates and traceability accuracy on state-of-the-art FR models and commercial APIs. It also exhibits superior robustness against a wide range of real-world simulated distortions, outperforming existing privacy protection methods based on adversarial attacks, deep watermarking, or their simple combination. Our work potentially opens up new insights into proactive protection for FR privacy.

title: R$^{2}$Net: 2D Deep Residual Learning with Height Embedding for 3D Radio Map Estimation
authors: Huiting Rao, Junyuan Wang, Huiling Zhu, Cheng-Xiang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Acquiring channel knowledge is required by many applications. For instance, handover in cellular networks is mainly decided based on the knowledge of pathloss. In contrast to traditional statistical distance-determined models that might provide misleading pathloss estimates, researchers started to explore deep learning methods recently to accurately estimate the radio map that characterizes the spatial distribution of pathloss according to the specific physical wireless propagation environment. However, existing works mainly focused on 2D radio map estimation by assuming that all receivers are at the same height. In fact, radio maps could be significantly different at different receiver heights, highlighting the importance of 3D radio map estimation. In this paper, we first propose a method to embed height information into 2D images, and then propose a general 2D radio residual network (R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>Net) for 3D radio map estimation. Since pathloss exhibits different characteristics in indoor and outdoor scenarios, we specifically propose R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>Net-In for indoor scenarios and R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>Net-Out for outdoor scenarios to better capture penetration loss and diffraction loss, respectively. Extensive experimental results show that our R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>Net significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art benchmarks in terms of estimation accuracy, computational and storage costs, and inference speed. In addition, due to the lack of publicly available 3D radio map datasets, a 3D indoor radio map dataset (3DiRM3200) is created, which took more than 1,000 labour hours. The dataset and codes will be available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/lighttime2023/3DiRM3200.git</uri>.

title: Near-field Channel Estimation Based on Super-resolution Sparse Reconstruction for THz Massive MIMO Systems with Coprime Array
authors: Wenjie Zhang, Zhen Gong, Penglu Liu, Lingli Dai, Xin Wang, Dun Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Coprime arrays are a promising array structure for near-field THz massive MIMO communication. However, they pose a significant challenge for traditional channel estimation methods to acquire high-precision channel state information (CSI) and user location information (ULI). To address this problem, a super-resolution sparse reconstruction based near-field channel estimation method is proposed in this paper. First, leveraging the sparsity of the channel, a high-resolution location parameter estimation algorithm based on fourth-order cumulants is developed to acquire the angles and distances of users. In the algorithm, an observation matrix comprising the largest continuous virtual elements is constructed from the fourth-order cumulant matrix, after which the spatially smoothed MUSIC technology is applied to estimate super-resolution ULI. Then, to enhance the robustness under challenging conditions such as low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and weak path gains, a channel reconstruction algorithm based on iterative path separation is proposed for channel estimation by utilizing the estimated user location parameters. The algorithm employs successive cancellation of the strong path signals to sequentially estimate the gain of each path, and then reconstructs the MIMO channel matrix based on the estimated path parameters. Finally, simulation results show that the proposed method can achieve excellent performance in user positioning and channel estimation with moderate pilot overhead, verifying its effectiveness and advantages in near-field terahertz massive MIMO systems.

title: A Self-Assemble W -Band Multilayer Silicon-Based Waveguide With Dumbbell-Shaped Holey EBG Structures and Its Antenna Applications
authors: Jing Cai, Gan Chen, Zhiyong Yang, Yunting Zhu, Yongcan Hua, Yuhang Dou, Miao Zhang, Weiyi Lin, Qing Huo Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article develops a novel holey electromagnetic bandgap (EBG) structure comprising dumbbell-shaped (DS) holes. They enable the self-assembly of multilayer metallic plates constituting the waveguide and cavity-based components. Compared to conventional circular holey EBG structures, the proposed DS irregular holes feature a compact size, a wide stopband, and insensitivity to misalignment. Moreover, the methodology of connecting multiple holes based on the DS holey EBG structure significantly reduces the number of holes, thereby simplifying their distribution in different layers and enhancing the integration of complex devices. For demonstration, straight rectangular waveguides integrated with DS holey EBG structures are designed in the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$W$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-band first. Furthermore, two <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$W$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-band over-mode cavity-backed <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8\times 8$</tex-math> </inline-formula> slot arrays, integrated with and without DS holey EBG structures, are also designed for comparison. The constitutive multilayer metallic plates are fabricated by applying the deep reactive ion etching (DRIE) and physical vapor deposition (PVD) processes. Abroadband measurement bandwidth is obtained. According to measurement, the transmission loss per unit length of the rectangular waveguide is evaluated as low as 0.012dB/mm at 100GHz. The <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8\times 8$</tex-math> </inline-formula> slot arrays achieve wideband operation and low sidelobe levels (SLLs). The measured −10-dB impedance-matching bandwidth is 90–109.5GHz (19.5%), while the 3-dB down boresight gain bandwidths are 90–108GHz (18%) with in-band SLLs less than −12.9dB. An improvement of about 2dB has been observed in the antenna gain by comparing the antennas with and without DS holey EBG structures.

title: Novel Channel Estimation Based on Enhanced Diffusion Posterior Sampling for mmWave MIMO Systems
authors: Jinwook Kim, Seongwoo Lee, Joonho Seon, Soo Hyun Kim, Young Ghyu Sun, Hyowoon Seo, Dong In Kim, Jin Young Kim
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this letter, a novel diffusion model (DM)-based channel estimation method is proposed to mitigate abrupt performance degradation in millimeter wave (mmWave) multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems under severe pilot scarcity. To address this issue, the proposed method jointly exploits first- and second-order posterior moments using the moment matching principle and Tweedie’s formula. This method improves estimation accuracy and robustness under high uncertainties, while maintaining a comparable computational burden. From the simulation results, it is confirmed that the proposed method successfully mitigates the abrupt performance degradation. The proposed method also substantially outperforms state-of-the-art DM-based methods in estimation accuracy under severe pilot scarcity, while achieving comparable computational complexity.

title: A Prediction Model Integrating Adaptive-Network-Based Fuzzy Inference System and Fuzzy C -Mean Clustering
authors: Rongtao Zhang, Xueling Ma, Weiping Ding, Witold Pedrycz, Jianming Zhan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: Multivariate prediction is a crucial tool for control system design, allowing for accurate prediction and control of system states by modeling the relationships between multiple input and output variables. However, existing models often rely on dimensionality reduction techniques to manage high-dimensional data, which can lead to the loss of valuable information. Combined prediction models (CPMs) have gained significant attention for their ability to integrate the strengths of multiple predictors, thereby improving prediction accuracy. Nevertheless, most current CPMs overlook the relationship between each predictor’s output and the predicted sequences, resulting in suboptimal performance. To address these challenges, we propose an adaptive-network-based fuzzy inference system CPM (ANFIS-CPM) enhanced by an improved fuzzy <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-means (FCM) clustering, termed ANFIS-CPM-FCM. Our approach defines a similarity metric to quantify feature relationships, enhances traditional FCM to automatically determine the optimal number of clusters based on a density clustering model, trains separate ANFIS models on each cluster, and aggregates predictions by considering the relationships between each predictor’s output and the predicted sequences. Extensive comparative and ablation experiments on six publicly available datasets demonstrate that our ANFIS-CPM-FCM outperforms existing methods in terms of prediction accuracy and robustness, highlighting the benefits of integrating improved clustering with adaptive fuzzy inference systems.

title: Intense TeraHertz Time Domain Spectroscopy of Water-based Liquid Crystals
authors: Fabio Novelli, Lorenza Spina, Andrea Rubano, Laura Cattaneo, Domenico Paparo, Federica Ciuchi, Maria Penelope De Santo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: Intense terahertz time-domain spectroscopy enables the characterization of highly absorbing and optically thick media by combining bright terahertz sources with off-focus probing conditions that suppress nonlinearities. Here, we apply it to cromolyn sodium salts dissolved in pure water, across the isotropic and nematic phases. Transmission measurements performed on half-millimetre thick liquid samples kept at 20 °C and 40 °C reveal systematic reductions in the terahertz absorption when compared to pure water, which is consistent with effective-medium predictions for isotropic solutions. A distinct excess absorption, however, emerges exclusively for the nematic phase, indicating a novel spectroscopic signature of supramolecular ordering. Polarized optical microscopy confirms the presence of domains with sizes comparable to the terahertz wavelengths used, suggesting that scattering could contribute to the anomalous terahertz response recorded in the nematic phase. Our results demonstrate that intense terahertz time-domain spectroscopy provides a non-contact probe of mesophase formation in lyotropic chromonics without the need to apply external bias. In turn, this offers a route to studying opaque systems wherein the microstructural organization lacks the strong optical signatures of liquid crystals.

title: Generalized Spatial Modulation FH-MIMO for High Data-Rate Dual-Function Radar Communication Networks
authors: Bingsheng Shen, Suyang Li, Yang Yang, Zhengchun Zhou, Pingzhi Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The dual-function radar-communication (DFRC) network has emerged as a pivotal solution for integrating radar sensing and wireless communication within a unified platform, significantly improving spectral efficiency and hardware utilization. This field has evolved from basic DFRC architectures to more sophisticated frequency-hopping multiple-input multiple-output (FH-MIMO) DFRC networks. Such networks leverage frequency agility and spatial diversity to substantially enhance resilience against interference and increase the achievable communication data rate. However, fixed frequency-hopping patterns assigned to each transmit antenna restrict the independent and flexible optimization of resources across the spatial and frequency domains. In this paper, we introduce a novel waveform structure that incorporates generalized spatial modulation (GSM) into the FH-MIMO DFRC network. The proposed GSM-FH-MIMO DFRC facilitates a more efficient and dynamic utilization of the available time, frequency, and spatial resources. Communication information is intelligently embedded through a synergistic combination of FH codes, phase modulation, and the activation states (indices) of transmit antennas, thereby creating a multi-dimensional modulation scheme. For information decoding at the communication receiver, a method based on the discrete Fourier transform (DFT) is developed. Furthermore, we present a rigorous analysis of the achievable data transmission rates under different system configurations and derive the optimal number of active antennas to maximize overall network efficiency. Simulation results verify that the proposed GSM-FH-MIMO DFRC network significantly enhances the communication data rate without degrading radar functionality. Moreover, in certain scenarios, the proposed waveform also yields advantages in terms of radar waveform ambiguity and target detection performance.

title: Function-Space ADMM for Decentralized Federated Learning: A Control Theoretic Perspective
authors: Akihito Taya, Yuuki Nishiyama, Kaoru Sezaki
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Decentralized federated learning (FL) is a promising approach for training machine learning models on sensor networks, Internet of Things (IoT) devices, and other edge systems where no central server exists. While federated learning offers advantages such as preserving data privacy, it often suffers from non-independent and identically distributed (IID) data distributions across devices, which cause significant performance degradation. This issue is particularly severe when directly optimizing model parameters, because neural network training is inherently non-convex and standard convergence guarantees for convex optimization do not apply. Unlike existing decentralized FL methods that primarily operate in parameter space, we propose federated function-space alternating direction method of multipliers (FedF-ADMM). FedF-ADMM exploits the convexity of loss functionals within function space to derive alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM)-based update directions, which are subsequently projected onto the parameter space via knowledge distillation. We further introduce a stabilization coefficient to enhance robustness under severe non-IID settings and analyze its behavior from a control-theoretic perspective by interpreting it as a proportional-integral (PI) term. Experiments under challenging non-IID scenarios, including settings where each device has data from only a single label, demonstrate that FedF-ADMM achieves faster and more stable convergence than existing decentralized FL methods, while attaining higher accuracy and better consensus among devices.

title: Optimal Network Reconfiguration using BPSO Technique with EVCSS and DGs
authors: Akash Kumar, Anish Sharma, Abhishek, Divya Bharathi Raj, Venkatakirthiga Murali
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the development and evaluation of an optimal network reconfiguration approach for minimizing total real power losses in radial distribution systems with the integration of Distributed Generators (DGs) and Electric Vehicle Charging Stations (EVCS). The proposed method employs Binary Particle Swarm Optimization (BPSO) to identify the optimal combination of tie and sectionalizing switches that should be closed and opened, respectively, to achieve the most efficient network topology. The approach is implemented in MATLAB and tested on the standard IEEE 33-bus radial distribution system (RDS). Simulation results demonstrate a substantial reduction in total real power losses, from 202.68 kW to 49.83 kW, corresponding to a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 4 2 \%}$</tex> improvement. Compared to existing methods in the literature, the proposed BPSO-based technique effectively identifies globally optimal switching configurations, overcoming the limitations of traditional heuristic methods. Owing to its significant loss reduction capability, the developed BPSO-based reconfiguration (RN) strategy can serve as a reliable and practical tool for power system operators tasked with optimizing the performance of Radial Distribution System (RDS).

title: An Optimal Compensation Topology to Improve Efficiency in Bidirectional Wireless Power Flow Systems
authors: Venkata Madhava Ram Tatabhatla, Ravulakari Kalyan, Nindra Sekhar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Most of the contemporary cars in today's world possess only Plug-in charging facility which has problems such as range anxiety and electrocution risk during charging. Wireless power transfer system is superior to conventional Plug-in charging due to its capability to run Electric vehicle through dynamic charging and to create an electrical isolation during charging. However, WPT has few limitations too such as low efficiency, high infrastructural cost and risk of human exposure to radio frequency radiation. The low efficiency of WPT is the result of high leakage reactance of transmission coils. To reduce the leakage reactance of the transmission coils, reduce the Volt-Ampere capacity of the inverter and to increase the available active power to the load, it is required to compensate the transmission coils. Conventional topologies hold few demerits like high voltage operation and inverter current spikes. This manuscript provides a comprehensive analytical review of the methods proposed in the literature along with their associated equations and output waveforms.

title: A Low Device Count Single-Phase Five-Level Boost Inverter
authors: Rage Ravitheja, Priyabrata Shaw, Manoranjan Sahoo, Jegurupati Gowri Prasanth
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Improved power quality is one of the key requirements along with high efficiency for single-phase inverters in solar photovoltaic (PV) applications. However, improving power quality without forgoing power density and efficiency of the converter is the top priority. In order to achieve better power quality along with high efficiency and power density, a single-phase five-level boost inverter (SFBI) with reduced semiconductor devices is proposed in this paper. Detailed analysis of the operational modes and steady-state characteristics of the proposed topology is presented, highlighting its functional behavior under various operating conditions. The design expressions for the passive elements of the SFBI are derived to facilitate component selection. The theoretical analysis and high boost factor of the proposed SFBI are validated through MATLAB simulations. Simulation findings confirm the effectiveness of the proposed circuit, including high voltage gain and enhanced power quality with a five-level output voltage.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of an Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor for EV Applications
authors: Dilsha Purushothaman, Sanjeev Guhan K S, Nagarajan V S, C. Bharatiraja
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and performance analysis of an Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (IPMSM) developed for electric vehicle applications. The motor is designed to deliver 25 kW continuous power and 40 kW peak power, with a maximum speed of 8000 rpm and efficiency greater than 95%. The motor features a compact design with a 240 mm outer diameter and incorporates a rotor with variable orientation magnets and a 48-slot stator. Finite element analysis (FEA) was carried out using MotorSolve to evaluate the electromagnetic performance, including flux distribution, torque production, and efficiency. The winding layout and coil parameters were optimized to support high current operation while maintaining thermal and structural integrity. The results confirm that the proposed design meets the required performance targets and is suitable for electric mobility applications.

title: Oil-Paper Insulation Diagnosis Using Time-Varying Capacitance Profile of Modified TVM
authors: Somesh Ganguly, Arijit Baral, Chandra Madhab Banerjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Dielectric spectroscopy-based techniques are reported to be effective in the insulation diagnosis of oil-paper insulation. Dielectric responses are usually modelled and analyzed using insulation models for identifying certain insulation sensitive parameters. However, the accuracy of these models in estimating the insulation sensitive parameters depends significantly on their formulation methodology. In the present work, the time varying capacitance profile obtained from the modified Time Varying Model (TVM) has been utilized for the diagnosis of laboratory prepared oil-paper insulation samples. The proposed methodology is capable of estimating the value of paper moisture with utmost accuracy and thus helpful in efficient insulation diagnosis of oil-paper insulation samples.

title: Guidelines for Relay Configuration to Handle Mutual Coupling Effect of Parallel Lines
authors: Ravindra Kumar, Manoj Tripathy
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Transposed circuits effectively eliminate the unbalance in mutual impedances between the phases of a line. However, transposing does not eliminate the zero-sequence mutual coupling effect between parallel lines during phase-toearth faults. Distance relays effectively determine the fault point impedance during phase-to-earth faults in mutually coupled parallel lines when accounting for the mutual coupling compensation factor, IED connections, and the mutual impedance present between the parallel lines. When configuring distance protection relays for parallel lines, it is essential to consider zero-sequence mutual coupling. This consideration ensures accurate and reliable results. This paper can help configure wiring relays for mutually coupled parallel transmission lines. It studies the impact of parallel line mutual coupling on the reach of ground distance elements and the effectiveness of single-ended fault detection algorithms. Mutual coupling in transmission lines can cause ground distance elements to underreach and overreach. In this paper, cases of underreach and overreach have been examined. Taking these factors into account can improve the reliability and accuracy of distance protection systems.

title: Flexible Mode Totem Pole PFC Rectifier Using Sic-MOSFET
authors: Karatlapellysrinivas, Deepak Sai Duvvapu, BarryVenugopal Reddy
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In This paper describes an electric vehicle (EV) onboard charge based on bridgeless totem pole boost power factor correction converter. The BLTBPFC, which serves as a front-end Power Factor Correction (PFC), and an isolated DC-DC converter make up the EV onboard charger. When a bidirectional AC-DC converter has constant output power, the input current increases as the input voltage decreases. This lowers the converter's work efficiency by increasing conduction losses. An adaptable modethis study proposes a Totem Pole power factor correction (PFC) converter. Depending on the input voltage, the converter modifies its topology, using a three-level PFC topology at low-line input and a totem pole PFC topology at high-line input. This converter offers the benefit of better efficiency across a broad range of inputs when compared to traditional totem pole PFC. The converter's control principle and method of operation are examined in MATLAB, simulation is used to confirm the converter's viability and efficacy.

title: Smart Energy Management System for a Solar-Powered EV Charging Station
authors: Shubham Singh, M.R. Sindhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a Smart Energy Management System (SEMS) designed for a solar-powered EV charging station, integrating advanced control strategies to maximize economic benefits and technical advantages. By leveraging solar energy as the primary power source and seamlessly integrating with the grid as a backup, the SEMS ensures optimal energy utilization, reduces dependency on the grid during peak hours, and minimizes operational costs through real-time energy optimization. The proposed SEMS incorporates dynamic pricing models and adapt to market fluctuations and optimize energy exchange, ensuring sustainable operations and profitability. The system performance is analysed under various operating conditions and seen that system efficiency varies from 80% to 98.19%, and Grid power reduction varies up to 100%.

title: AI-Powered Adaptive Battery Model for Fast Charging Scheduling Incorporating SoC-Dependent Dynamics and Degradation Forecasting
authors: Vikram Kumar Saxena, Kundan Kumar, Benjamin A Shimray, Sanjeet Kr. Dwivedi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel AI-powered adaptive framework for fast electric vehicle (EV) charging that integrates lithium-ion battery degradation modelling with realtime state of charge (SoC) dependent power constraints. Unlike traditional charging systems that rely on linear models or static scheduling, the proposed methodology employs a hybrid architecture that fuses empirical battery behavior with an artificial neural network (ANN) trained on historical chargedischarge cycles. The model estimates state-of-health (SoH) dynamically and enforces a nonlinear, SoC-dependent charging envelope to prevent thermal and electrochemical stress. A model predictive control (MPC) algorithm minimises both charging time and degradation cost while ensuring grid stability. Simulation results demonstrate a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12 - 15\%$</tex> increase in energy throughput, a 27% reduction in SoH degradation, and complete observance of grid power constraints across a heterogeneous EV fleet. This integrated framework represents a step forward in degradation-aware, smart charging infrastructure for largescale EV deployments.

title: AI-Driven Adaptive FOPID Control for Predictive Maintenance with Experimental Analysis
authors: Ankush Madhukar Gund, Gajanan Madhavrao Malwatkar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The paper includes the new framework of lightweight reinforcement learning based adaptive control that combines Fractional Order PID (FOPID) controller with Q-learning to be used in real-time industrial automation in low-cost embedded systems. In contrast to the conventional PID/FOPID control or the simulation-based learning, the proposed system can be deployed on resource-limited edge devices like Raspberry Pi 4 and ESP32. It uses a five-layer architecture of sensing, control, learning, and actuation all in an online manner. The reinforcement learning agent uses a reward-penalized Q-learning algorithm to dynamically tune all of the five FOPID parameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{Kp}, \text{Ki}, \text{Kd}, \lambda, \mu)$</tex> in reaction to environmental disturbances, with actuator-safe output clipping to ensure real-time safety. The controller was tested by the use of simulations in MATLAB and hardware-in-the-loop experiments on a BLDC motor. The results show large gains compared to static FOPID: With decrease in Mean Absolute Error (MAE), overshoot, and energy consumption with minimum latency overhead. The modular and energy-aware design of the system is suitable to apply predictive control in the industry 4.0 applications, especially in the cases of low-cost, adaptive, and resilient automation.

title: Development of Distinct Third-Order Inverse Filters Employing Single OTRA
authors: Shubhojit Ghosh, Aniket Mazumder, Shuvadeep Biswas, Ram Bhagat
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper introduces three innovative thirdorder inverse filter circuits that utilize a single Operational Transresistance Amplifier (OTRA) along with a minimal set of passive components. The circuits that have been proposed can perform inverse low-pass (ILP), inverse band-pass (IBP), and inverse high-pass (IHP) filtering functions. The proposed configurations are evaluated through performance analyses, which include Monte-Carlo and sensitivity analyses. Simulations are carried out using the AD844 library in PSpice.

title: Optimal Allocation and Hosting Capacity Analysis of EV Charging Stations and Solar PV Systems in Bhopal City
authors: Priyanshu Gupta, Vivek Mohan, Mrudula Murukan G, Reshmi A. R, Aedna Kurian
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a methodology for assessing the hosting capacity and optimal integration of solar photovoltaic (PV) systems and electric vehicle charging stations (EVCS) in a 11 kV radial distribution network in Bhopal, India. The analysis is conducted in three stages: (1) determination of PV hosting capacity (without EVCS) using particle swarm optimization (PSO), (2) evaluation of bus-wise EVCS hosting capacity (without PV) through iterative load flow analysis to identify maximum permissible charging stations at different power levels, and (3) joint optimization of co-located PV-EV systems using PSO to minimize power losses and improve voltage regulation. The framework incorporates linear regression-based load forecasting (projected to 2030) and probabilistic modeling of uncertainties, including beta distributions for solar irradiance, normal distributions for state-of-charge (SoC) and battery capacity, and binomial distributions for charger level selection. Vehicle arrival patterns are statistically modeled in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex>-minute intervals. The computation utilizes MATLAB for probabilistic modeling and PSO, while DIgSILENT PowerFactory is employed for load flow simulations. The results provide benchmarks for the integration of distributed energy resources (DER) in urban distribution networks, offering practical insights for system planning under technical constraints.

title: Scalogram-Histogram Driven CNN Approach for Classifying Power System Disturbances in PMU Data
authors: Pradeep Kumar Khatik, Punjan Dohare
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In contemporary power systems, phasor measurement units (PMUs) play the leading roles. It provides high-resolution, synchronized digital data which support real-time monitoring, stability assessment as well as fault detection. In this paper, a new PMU anomaly detection framework was suggested as per IEEE 1159-2019 power quality standards. The normal, Voltage sag, frequency fluctuation, transient and oscillatory event types of signals PMU synthetics they are both sampled at 1000 Hz and tropical noise is added to them to resemble real life situations. A complex morlet wavelet is used as continuous wavelet transform (CWT). Generate scalograms, features of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0}$</tex>-bins of the histograms are obtained in order to classify with the help of a convolutional neural network (CNN). A lightweight CNN model was designed to classify these anomalies on a sample size of 250, qualitative and quantitative analysis of the histograms and confusion matrix respectively containing clear classes signatures and misclassified regions of transient versus oscillatory event classes. These results indicate the possibility of wavelet augmented feature extraction and CNNs in raising power system reliability. It is advised that it should be validated using real-world PMU data.

title: Grey Target Approach for Estimating the Remaining Life of Power Transformers
authors: Mulpuru Gopi, Chilaka Ranga, Kushal M Jagtap
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Remaining life estimation of the power transformer provides the information of the asset how long been in service. Transformers are operated with several stresses like thermal, electrical, etc. In general, operational life of the transformer depends on its degradation of insulation, ambient and loading conditions. From the last two decades, transformer life is analyzed through thermal modelling, load variation, and experimental studies has been conducted with limited attributes. In present paper, a novel grey relational approach (GRA) is proposed to estimate the remaining life of the power transformers. GRA is the potential tool in estimating degradation of each insulation attribute and provides the ranking. Fifteen on-field diagnostic data, collected from the Himachal Pradesh State Electricity Board, India, were used to test and validate the proposed model. Further, collected ten attributes are normalized in linear range to maintain consistency between attributes. Additionally, a new life estimation approach is introduced based on the degradation of insulation of each insulation grades of the transformer. The proposed model effectively overcomes the limitations of conventional methods. Moreover, the evaluated results of proposed model are compared with fuzzy logic model in [13] and with analytical method. The ranking provides valuable insights to implementing appropriate maintenance actions, ultimately enhancing transformer longevity. This approach is beneficial for diagnostic experts and maintenance staff in ensuring the reliable operation of transformers.

title: Design and Development of a Single-Stage LED Driver Using Boost Converter with Dimming Control Algorithms
authors: Vijayalakshmi A L, Kundan Krishn, Suprabha Padiyar U., Vedavyasa Kamath, Lokesh J
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a Light Emitting Diode (LED) driver circuit based on a closed-loop DC-DC Boost converter that ensures both constant current and constant voltage regulation. This closed loop system utilizes a dedicated controller to maintain the LED operating point across a wide input voltage range of 10 V to 25 V. The feedback system facilitates dynamic control of the brightness of the LED lamp. Two control strategies, Proportional-Integral (PI) control and Sliding Mode Control (SMC), were implemented and analyzed for dimming regulation in the proposed LED driver. The system is designed to deliver 60 W at 40 V and 1.2 A and is simulated using the MATLAB SIMULINK environment. Additionally, a hardware prototype of the LED driver is developed and tested under dynamic input and output conditions, with control logic implemented on an STM32 Nucleo development board. The simulation and experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed driver circuit in achieving stable current and voltage operation.

title: Sensorless Speed Control of Dual Stator Induction Machine Using Enhanced Rotor-Flux Based Model Reference Adaptive System with Stator Command Voltage Estimation
authors: Abhijit Das, Shantanu Chatterjee, Amarendra Edpuganti
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This manuscript presents a novel enhancement to the conventional Rotor-Flux based Model Reference Adaptive System (RF-MRAS) for sensorless speed control of Dual Stator Induction Machines (DSIMs). The proposed improvement utilizes stator command voltages, rather than line voltages, for the calculation of rotor flux vectors. This modification effectively mitigates the impact of low signal-to-noise ratios introduced by voltage sensors, which typically deteriorate drive performance-particularly in the low-speed operating region. The efficacy of the suggested methodology is substantiated by conducting a comparative analysis with the traditional RF-MRAS technique. In addition, stability evaluations are carried out to examine performance across all four quadrants of operation. The entire system has been rigorously modeled and simulated using the MATLAB/Simulink environment, and an experimental test bench has been developed utilizing a TMS320F28335 FPGA controller to corroborate the simulation results with experimental findings.

title: Resilience Enhancement Methods for Power Distribution Network Under Extreme Events Review, Gaps and Future Directions
authors: Mohd Shahrukh, Ranjana Sodhi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Rapidly occurring climate changes and greater reliance on digital infrastructures around the globe has given rise to extreme natural and/or man-made events like hurricanes, earthquakes, heat waves, floods, cyber-attacks in recent times. Under such unpredictable operating landscapes, the traditional idea of reliable power system operation is getting replaced with the resilient power system operation, which, in turn, demands the modelling of the extreme events, techniques for quantifying and enhancing resiliency of the system. This paper presents a literature survey on the said topics, with a special emphasis on how Distributed Energy Resources, Mobile Power Sources, and Electric Vehicles(EVs) can play a key role in enhancing the power system resiliency (PSR). The literature review concludes with the identified research gaps, indicating the future research trends in the discussed field of research.

title: PVsyst-Based Design and Performance Evaluation of a 100 kWp Grid-Connected Solar PV System in Egypt
authors: Mohamed Moustafa, Mohamed Swillam, Mohamed Mahmoud, Samar Akef
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a feasibility study of a 100 kWp grid-connected photovoltaic system designed to meet the energy demands of a local factory near the Oil Libya gas station on Alex Road, about 94 kilometers from the city center. The system, installed with a fixed tilt mounting structure, was analyzed using PVsyst software, considering geographic location, solar irradiance, weather patterns, and load consumption profiles. A comprehensive techno-economic analysis was conducted, evaluating parameters such as net present value of ~87,060$, levelized cost of energy 0.0415$/kWh for a Capital Expenditure total of 55000$, and operational costs. Simulation results show an expected annual energy production of 160.7 MWh, with a potential 20% reduction in monthly electricity bills. The study demonstrates the technical and economic viability of the proposed system and highlights the effectiveness of PVsyst as a simulation tool. It offers a practical reference for similar industrial-scale solar projects in comparable settings.

title: Fuzzy-Driven Electro-Mechanical Control for Stable and Efficient Electric Car Operation
authors: Buddhadeva Sahoo, Sarthak Mohanty, Pravat Kumar Rout, Manoj Kumar Debnath, Prakash Chandra Sahu, Subhasis Panda
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The present study provides a unified design for electrical and mechanical control for the multi-converter, multimachine (MCMM) set-up used in the operation of electric cars. The electrical control design (ECD) involves a fuzzy logic-based robust controller with the capacity of improving power quality for the electric car. It estimates the machine's actual speed and rotor angle based on real-time processing of the voltage and current signals. Active and reactive current components are controlled by monitoring machine and wheel torque, and inverter DC-link voltage, ensuring the smooth flow and quality of power. The mechanical control design (MCD) seeks to enhance the dynamic stability of the vehicle, especially under adverse conditions such as on slippery or uneven road surfaces. This design is based on an accurate mathematical model of the electric car's mechanical system that considers its tire-road interaction, gearbox behavior, and various external forces, as well as any mechanical coupling of drivetrain components. A detailed torque modeling (TM) is employed for an accurate tracking mechanism of the torque generated at the wheels for precise torque control. The proposed control framework is implemented and tested under a variety of road conditions. Simulation results demonstrate the capability of the integrated control strategy in improving power quality and stability for electric car applications.

title: CKF-Based Sensorless Control of PMSM Drives Integrating with GH-Framed DTC-SVM
authors: Shibaji Mondal, Priyankar Roy, A Bandyopadhyay, Ujjwal Mondal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: For permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drives, a hybrid sensorless control approach is proposed that combines a cubature Kalman filter (CKF)-based estimator with space vector modulation-based gh-frame direct torque control (DTC-SVM). The approach ensures accurate rotor position and speed estimation without position sensors even at low speeds and dynamic operating conditions. The gh-DTC-SVM architecture improves torque control accuracy and reduces torque ripple through seamless voltage vector transitions. The CKF estimator provides robust state tracking during steady-state operation, speed reversal, and startup. First, the approach is validated in four relevant scenarios using MATLAB/Simulink simulations, and the results show good control accuracy and low estimation error. Experimental results further demonstrate the robust performance of the approach over a wide speed range including reversal as well as accurate speed tracking. This sensorless approach provides a small, reliable, and efficient solution for PMSM drives in electric vehicles and industrial applications.

title: Smart Wireless Numerical Relay with Real-Time Fault Isolation for Transmission Lines
authors: Afthab Seyed, Prince Bharti, Priyadarshni S, Roopa Sri A E, Venkatesh Muniyandi, Raja Pitchaimuthu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a wireless numerical relay for real-time fault detection and isolation in medium-voltage transmission lines. The system employs Hall-effect current sensors and an ESP32 microcontroller to acquire and process three-phase current signals using Clarke transformation. Fault conditions are detected by analyzing the resultant current magnitude and the determinant of a fault detection matrix (FDM), while tripping commands are transmitted wirelessly via the ESP-NOW protocol. A laboratory-scale setup emulating a 150 km Pi-model line was developed to test LG and LL fault scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate high accuracy, fast response time, and reliable communication performance of the system. The proposed system provides a cost-effective and scalable approach for smart grid protection applications.

title: Orthogonal Deep Convolutional Neural Network for Incipient Inter-Turn Fault Diagnosis in Electric Drive System
authors: Fatimatelbatoul Husari, Jeevanand Seshadrinath
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Early detection and diagnosis of inter-turn fault in electric drive system (EDS) is a vital topic to investigate in manufacturing systems. Also, identification the failure at an early stage can save the maintenance cost and invaluable time. This work proposes a methodology which is orthogonal deep convolution neural network (ODCNN) with soft orthogonality constraints (SOC) for stator turn fault detection at an incipient stage in EDS, in presence of high harmonics content and for a wide range of speed and load variations. The proposed technique has been examined on two different applications, viz: the dataset of stator turn fault in electric drive system and the CIFAR-10 dataset, to validate the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed method. The results of the proposed ODCNN have been compared to the normal convolution neural network (NCNN) without any orthogonality constraints, and the comparisons show that the proposed ODCNN has achieved a significant improvement in the field of fault detection in EDS at an early stage and image classification as well.

title: Sustainability Challenges and Economic Impact of Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure Deployment
authors: Joynath Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In recent years, there has been a growing focus on minimizing the environmental impact of transportation by promoting electrification, with an emphasis on shifting from gasoline-powered vehicles to electric vehicles (EVs) powered by renewable energy sources. This research explores the challenges associated with EVs and their charging infrastructure, along with the implications for consumers and the broader economic impact in India. An exploratory approach is adopted to review developments in e-mobility, existing technical barriers, policy enhancements, and advancements aimed at achieving the goal of zero emissions by 2070 in India. Recent data are used to represent the current status and progress toward the projected targets, followed by trend analysis of EV adoption, charging infrastructure deployment, and renewable energy capacity needed to support these targets, and discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges arising from this transition period. Furthermore, the study models the adoption of EVs as a new technology using the Gompertz Curve and recommends frequent monitoring to achieve the desired goals. Residual plots are used to understand market scenarios and target deviations, enabling policymakers to adopt improved suggestions and recommendations. The study concludes that treatment over deviations can drive enhancements and increased adoption, aligning market demand with sustainability goals.

title: An Enhanced Fox Optimization Algorithm for Optimal Reactive Power Dispatch: A Case Study on the Indian 62-Bus System
authors: Prachi Nagarkar, Bimal Kumar Dora, Sunil Bhat, Manoranjan Sahoo
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The Fox optimization Algorithm (FOXOA) is a swarm-based optimization method inspired by the intelligence and behavior of Fox. FOXOA has achieved significant acceptance among the research community and is now employed to address many optimization problems. Nevertheless, evidence indicates that the exploration and utilization of this resource are inadequately balanced. This research employs a unique non-uniform mutation and balanced state of Exchange market algorithm based Fox optimization Algorithm (EFOXOA) method to address Optimal Reactive Power Dispatch (ORPD) problems. ORPD is a power system optimization tool that regulates control variables, to decrease active power loss and improve voltage deviation. The proposed method is assessed utilizing the Indian Utility 62 bus system. The algorithm's efficacy is assessed by comparing the results from FOXOA and EFOXOA with previously published result presented in literature. The simulated outcomes validate the efficacy and resilience of EFOXOA in addressing ORPD problems.

title: Applying a Schema-Constrained LLM Verification Pipeline to the Evaluation of Complex System Designs
authors: Bo-Shuo Zhang, Tung-Ting Tsai, Wen-Pinn Fang
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 8th Artificial Intelligence and Cloud Computing Conference
abstract: None

title: Faithful and Realistic Image Compression At Extreme-Low Bitrate Via Pretrained Discrete Priors
authors: Min Woo Kim, Nam Ik Cho
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Image compression is fundamentally governed by the distortion-perception trade-off, where achieving high perceptual realism often necessitates a sacrifice in pixel-wise fidelity. This challenge is significantly magnified at extreme low bitrates due to severe information constraints, creating a critical bottleneck for data representation. To address this representation bottleneck, we propose a framework that leverages pretrained discrete priors derived from large-scale vision encoders. Rather than training from scratch, we employ clustering-based initialization to map high-capacity discrete priors into a bitrate-constrained space. This ensures that reconstructed images achieve enhanced content fidelity while preserving superior perceptual realism. Furthermore, to overcome the practical bitrate overhead where arithmetic coding occasionally exceeds the theoretical uniform bound in discrete latent spaces, we introduce a dynamic entropy coding scheme. By deterministically switching between arithmetic and uniform coding on a per-image basis, our method ensures near-optimal compression efficiency. Extensive experiments confirm that our method improves content preservation while maintaining high-fidelity perceptual quality.

title: Efficient and Secure Distributed Informational Interaction Algorithm for Current Sharing and Voltage Regulation in DC Microgrids: An Event-Triggered Differential Privacy Approach
authors: Wenbin Yue, Hongjun Chu, Yutao Qiu, Chunxia Dou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Although information interaction is essential for achieving group goals of distributed systems, the information interaction consumes lots of communication resources, and also leads to the privacy disclosure of individual information. Therefore, how to design an efficient and secure distributed information interaction algorithm naturally becomes a crucial issue. In this article, we propose a differential privacy consensus algorithm based on an event-triggered communication mechanism for current sharing and voltage regulation in DC Microgrids. The main idea is to, at each event-triggered instant, first release some sporadic current data, and then mask the data with additive Laplace noises with an invariant variance. By virtue of a stochastic approximation technique, time-varying control gains are designed to compensate for the adverse effect of the stationary noises. As a result, not only the execution efficiency of the network is improved, but the privacy of initial currents can be well protected. The advantage of the proposed algorithm lies in its ability to prevent the gradual leakage of privacy caused by noise attenuation. That is, it provides stronger privacy protection than the commonly adopted approach using exponentially decaying noise. Furthermore, we carry out rigorous convergence analysis for current sharing and average bus voltage regulation, and also evaluate the level of differential privacy achieved. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed algorithms is validated by simulation results from a detailed switch-level microgrid model.

title: A Booth-Based Digital Compute-in-Memory Macro With Bitwise-Efficient Multiphase Accumulation
authors: Yiying Jiang, Xiaoqin Wang, Heng You, Qiang Li, Shushan Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: This brief presents a Booth-based all-digital SRAM compute-in-memory (CIM) macro designed for high-efficiency multiply-and-accumulate (MAC) operations in artificial intelligence applications. The architecture incorporates three key innovations: 1) a pre-encoding (PENC) scheme utilizing a Booth-based consolidated truth table, designed to reduce encoder complexity and computing unit overhead; 2) a minimalist bitwise processing technique, employing only two transistors for bit retention and shifting, aimed to minimize computational complexity within the subcomputing array; and 3) a multiphase product summation approach, integrating unified negative compensation with global sign bit expansion, intended to lower power consumption during partial product accumulation and final addition. The proposed architecture is readily adaptable to multiplications across diverse bit widths and accommodates flexible CIM array sizes, ensuring high scalability. Measurement results from a 55-nm-CMOS process demonstrate that the proposed CIM macro achieves an energy efficiency of 16.8 TOPS/W under 8b/8b MAC operations.

title: IEEE Computational Intelligence Society Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems

title: DCFNet: Doppler Correction Filter Network for Integrated Sensing and Communication in Multi-User MIMO-OFDM Systems
authors: Hyeonho Noh, Hyeonsu Lyu, Moe Z. Win, Hyun Jong Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a headline feature for the forthcoming IMT-2030 and 6G releases, yet a concrete solution that fits within the established orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) family remains an open problem. Specifically, Doppler-induced inter-carrier interference (ICI) destroys subcarrier orthogonality of OFDM sensing signals, blurring range–velocity maps and severely degrading sensing accuracy. Building on multi-user multi-input-multi-output (MIMO) OFDM systems, this paper develops a model-driven ISAC framework that jointly optimizes transmit and receive beamforming to maximize multi-user communication sum-rate under sensing-performance constraints, while mitigating Doppler ICI. To this end, we propose a Doppler-correction filter network (DCFNet), an AI-native ISAC model that achieves fine-grained range-velocity estimation precision with minimal complexity and without altering the legacy frame structure. A bank of DCFs derived from the Doppler physics first shifts and suppresses dominant ICI components, and a subsequent deep neural network cancels the residual interference to yield a clean radar sensing image. To further enhance the range and velocity estimation precision, we propose DCFNet with local refinement (DCFNet-LR), which applies a generalized likelihood ratio test (GLRT) to refine target estimates of DCFNet to sub-cell precision. Simulation results show that DCFNet-LR runs 143 times faster than a maximumlike-lihood search and achieves significantly superior performance, reducing the range and velocity RMSE by factors of 2.7 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup> and 6.7 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup> compared to conventional detection methods.

title: EyeKey: Self-Supervised Keypoint Detection and Description Network Based on Local Feature Saliency for Retinal Image Global Registration
authors: Yanchao Liang, Ding Ma, Xiangqian Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Retinal image registration (RIR) plays an important role in the diagnosis and long-term monitoring of retinal diseases. Retinal image global registration (RIGR) is usually the first step of RIR. Traditional methods often struggle to achieve robust keypoint detection and description when faced with high-resolution, fine-textured retinal images. Deep learning-based methods for this task have not been widely developed. Therefore, we propose a keypoint detection and description network based on local feature saliency, EyeKey, for RIGR. EyeKey uses the “Detect While Describing (DWD)” design. Specifically, two proposed UDPAM++ modules are embedded into the feature description network to enhance its feature description capability. Concurrently, these modules detect distinctive keypoints based on local feature saliency, combined with a Mapping Module featuring only three learnable parameters. Moreover, we achieve self-supervised feature description network training on high-resolution, fine-textured retinal images through the Random Local Hardest Example Mining strategy. Additionally, we realize robust unsupervised keypoint detection network training based on the High Matching Probability Defines Keypoints strategy and the proposed Cumulative Salient Keypoint Expansion, which, together with the DWD design, mutually reinforce the training of the keypoint detection and description network. Finally, combined with the feature-based RIGR pipeline, our method achieves outstanding performance while maintaining excellent inference speed on monomodal and multimodal RIGR evaluation datasets.

title: Complementary Reconfigurable Field-Effect Transistor-based hybrid 6T SRAM Cell as a Multibit Dot-Product Engine In-Memory-Computing
authors: Saroj Satapathy, Juhan Ahn, Jaydeep Kulkarni
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: In-Memory Computing holds the promise to break the memory wall challenge; however, established memory device scaling cannot keep pace with growing architectural demands. We propose a complementary reconfigurable field effect transistor (CRFET) based hybrid 6T SRAM bitcell that holds potential for IMC architectures. established memory device scaling cannot keep pace with growing architectural demands. CRFET devices can be configured as n-type or p-type transistors during runtime using an additional programmable gate (PG) bias. Leveraging its CMOS compatibility, we combine conventional MOS transistors with CRFET transistors to propose a hybrid 6T bitcell. We exploit the current tuning capability of the CRFET device pass gate in the bitcell to achieve the multibit dot-product computations. We analyze and discuss the area efficiency through proposed mask designs, better noise margin, and energy efficiency through simulation using a Verilog-A-based spice model developed with the help of the Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD) simulations of a 7nm equivalent process design kit. We also demonstrate the robustness of the dot product engine (DPE) IMC in terms of output current and voltage characteristics, bitline non-linearities of multiple row arrays, and error percentage. The analysis shows that without area penalties compared to a conventional 6T memory array, it provides 24% better read noise margin and 18% average energy per operation efficiency versus conventional multibit DPE IMC implementations.

title: The Stealth Signals Bypassing Iran's Internet Blackout: Files Hidden in Satellite TV Broadcasts Keep Information Flowing
authors: Evan Alireza Firoozi
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: On 8 January 2026, the Iranian government imposed a near-total communications shutdown. It was the country's first full information blackout: For weeks, the internet was off across all provinces while services including the government-run intranet, VPNs, text messaging, mobile calls, and even landlines were severely throttled.
