It was an unprecedented lockdown that left more than 90 million people cut off not only from the world, but from one another.

title: The MAN in the Machine: Robert Woo has Spent 15 Years Testing and Improving Exoskeletons from the Inside
authors: Eliza Strickland
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: Technologies that merge with the human body are often judged by the discrete tasks they can perform: restore movement, enable communication, control robotic tools. But their true test is in how they work in daily life, on and within the people who use them. In this special report, we look at exoskeletons and brain-computer interfaces from the user's perspective, where performance is measured not just in capability but in effort, trade-offs, and lived experience.

title: News
authors: Benjamin Skuse, Lawrence Ulrich, Dina Genkina, Alexander C. Kaufman
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: In July 2022, Ukrainian mathematician Maryna Viazovska received a Fields Medal—widely regarded as the Nobel Prize for mathematics—for breakthrough work in solutions to the problem of packing spheres in higher dimensions. Sphere-packing asks, as the name implies, how densely identical spheres can be packed in n-dimensional space. In two dimensions (a circle-packing problem, in other words), a honeycomb layout is the best solution. In three dimensions, spheres stacked in a pyramid are optimal. But in four, five, or more dimensions, it becomes exceedingly difficult to find the best solution, and to prove that it is in fact the best.

title: Modeling Deep Fusion of Intra- and Inter-Modal Incongruity for Multimodal Sarcasm Detection
authors: Fengmao Lv, Junlin Fang, Guosheng Lin, Wenya Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Multimodal sarcasm detection receives increasing attentions due to people's growing interest in posting multimodal information. The key factor of multimodal sarcasm detection is to leverage incongruity information across different modalities. Existing works are mainly based on the late fusion strategy by simply concatenating the intra- and inter-modal incongruity features, which are prone to learning surface patterns. In contrast, this work mainly focuses on modeling the deep fusion of intra- and inter-modal incongruity information. To this end, this work first discusses the incompatibility between the two kinds of incongruity features within existing multimodal frameworks. Under this motivation, we further propose an end-to-end cooperative framework dubbed Cooperative Multimodal Incongruity Learning (CoMIL). Specifically, our approach incorporates a primary module to model the deep fusion of intra- and inter modal incongruity information. To prevent the integrated inter modal visual information from disturbing the modeling of intra text incongruity, CoMIL introduces a cooperative mechanism incorporating a reference module which focuses on token-level correlations as a structural guidance to the primary module. Based on the proposed cooperative mechanism, the intra- and inter-modal incongruity information can be compactly and compatibly integrated into deep features of neural models. Extensive experiments are conducted to validate the effectiveness of our proposed CoMIL approach.

title: Experimental Analysis of Coupled Nanoring Resonator for Photonic Integrated Circuits
authors: P. Agilandeswari, G. Thavasi Raja
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: In this paper, the coupled asymmetrical nanoring resonators are fabricated with narrow bandwidth, high-Q factor, and high ON-OFF ratio for ultracompact optical integrated circuits. This nanoscale device is used to realize the dual function of narrowband filter and 1×2 optical switch. The coupled asymmetrical photonic crystal ring resonators play a vital role in wavelength filtering and switching operation. The 100 keV electron beam lithography and reactive ion etching method is used to fabricate a nanoscale photonic device with low input power, high extinction ratio of 36.55 dB, narrow bandwidth of 0.5 nm and high-Q factor of 3097. This nanophotonic platform is highly suitable for optical communication system and photonic integrated circuits.

title: Uncovering a De-biased Latent Proxy of Teaching Quality from Heavily Skewed Evaluation Data: A Latent Space Reconstruction Framework via ED-VAE
authors: Jingcan Liu, Minghao Huang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The psychometric validity of Student Evaluations of Teaching (SETs) is severely compromised by the ‘‘ceiling effect’’, where negative skewness compresses variance and attenuates the sensitivity of conventional linear models. To address this, we propose the Educational De-biasing Variational Autoencoder (ED-VAE), a deep generative framework with a skewness-aware loss function designed to estimate a debiased latent proxy of teaching effectiveness. Leveraging a rare quasi-experimental dataset (N = 22, 429) collected over the 2019–2024 period from 26 application-oriented institutions in China (65% of the 66 instructors taught largely identical curricula across more than one institution), we were able to mitigate confounding from instructor heterogeneity in a way that is seldom feasible in standard SET studies. Experimental results show that the extracted latent quality (Z1) achieves high internal consistency (r ≈ 0.96) and consistent cross-context behaviour (r ≈ 0.16). To ensure external validity, a 5-fold grouped cross-validation on unseen instructors confirmed out-of-sample generalizability (r ≈ 0.156). Furthermore, mixed-effects modelling (HLM) indicates that the latent construct primarily captures individual instructor variance rather than mere institutional response styles. While the OLS fixed-effects baseline failed to detect institutional impact (p = 0.173), ED-VAE reveals a modest but statistically stable correlation (r = 0.126, 95% CI [0.111, 0.140]) between the latent proxy and institutional resources. A Monte-Carlo simulation calibrated to our empirical response distribution (P(score=5) ≈ 92%) shows that ceiling-induced range restriction attenuates Pearson correlations by roughly 44%; under this attenuation, an observed r = 0.126 is consistent with a small-to-moderate construct-level effect of ρ ≈ 0.22. We therefore interpret ED-VAE as a proof-of-concept computational auxiliary, suitable for macro-level research and diagnostic institutional analysis, rather than as a deployable tool for high-stakes individual decisions.

title: Real-Time Vehicle Speed Gun with LiDAR–Camera Fusion
authors: Liem H. T. Nguyen, Moditha Reddy Jagannatha Reddy, Chulwoo Pack, Sungyong Jung, Hyusim Park
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a dual-sensor speed measurement system that fuses LiDAR-based distance ranging with depth-camera tracking through a Kalman filter. We quantitatively evaluate the separate contributions of per-modality filtering and dual-modal fusion via outdoor vehicle experiments encompassing acceleration, deceleration, and steady-state driving conditions. Per-sensor filtering reduces distance error and suppresses high-frequency measurement jitter, while sensor fusion further lowers the root-mean-square error (RMSE) and produces speed profiles that closely match ground-truth measurements. The proposed system operates in real time on a Raspberry Pi platform. Time-stamped CSV speed logs and synchronized, annotated video from real vehicle trials are provided to enable independent validation. The results demonstrate that complementary sensing combined with probabilistic sensor fusion offers a practical and deployable solution for accurate speed measurement under real-world conditions. The fused system achieves a speed root-mean-square error (RMSE) of 0.4993 mph and a distance RMSE of 0.4339 m, representing improvements of 55% and 56%, respectively, over the raw single-sensor baselines. The primary novelty of this research is the development of a three-stage processing architecture that systematically isolates and quantifies these performance gains achieved by per-modality Kalman filtering and subsequent probabilistic sensor fusion. Furthermore, this architecture is implemented and empirically validated on a resource-constrained Raspberry Pi platform under dynamic, real-world driving conditions.

title: A Discretized Reduced-Order Model for MEMS Microwave Power Detection Chips
authors: Ruifeng Li, Debo Wang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: To accurately predict the nonlinear deformation behavior of the cantilever beam under electrostatic actuation and enhance the microwave performance of the capacitive MEMS microwave power sensors, a discretized reduced-order model (DROM) is proposed in this work. By applying the Galerkin method, the governing differential equation is reformulated as a discretized integral formulation, thereby facilitating the derivation of an approximate analytical solution. Compared to simulation results, the model is demonstrated to substantially simplify computation without sacrificing accuracy, showing an average relative error of only 5.01% in predicting deflection. Moreover, the model allows for detailed analysis of both static and dynamic mechanical responses. A sensitivity model is also developed to quantify output performance. Three sensor chips of different sizes are fabricated and characterized. Measured results show an excellent microwave performance across the X-band, with the return losses reaching a minimum of –60.5 dB at 9 GHz. The output capacitance exhibits a linear dependence on input power, yielding a high sensitivity of 144.4 fF/W at 10 GHz for the 400 μm device. Furthermore, the measured resonant frequency of the cantilever beam is 115.7 kHz, which is orders of magnitude below the operating frequency, ensuring structural stability under typical operating conditions. This work establishes a comprehensive theoretical framework for the modeling of cantilever-based microwave power detection chips.

title: Exploiting Label-aware Knowledge from Heterogeneous Clients for Hierarchical Federated Learning
authors: Minh-Duong Nguyen, Quoc-Viet Pham, Dinh Thai Hoang, Diep N. Nguyen, Long Tran-Thanh, Won-Joo Hwang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: In real-world applications, Federated Learning (FL) faces two challenges: (1) scalability and (2) heterogeneous data. To address the first problem, we design a novel FL framework named Full-stack FL (F2L). More specifically, F2L provides a hierarchical network architecture, making extending the FL network accessible without reconstructing the whole network system. Moreover, leveraging the advantages of hierarchical network design, we propose a new Label-driven Knowledge Distillation (LKD) technique at the centralized server to address the second problem. Unlike the current knowledge distillation techniques, LKD is capable of training a student model, which consists of good knowledge from all teachers' models. Therefore, our proposed algorithm can effectively extract the knowledge of the regions' data distribution (i.e., the regional aggregated models) to reduce the divergence between clients' models when operating under the FL system with non-independent identically distributed data. Extensive experiment results reveal that: (i) our F2L method can significantly improve the overall FL efficiency in all global distillations (i.e., accuracy is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7-20\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> higher in non-IID settings), and (ii) F2L rapidly achieves convergence as global distillation stages occur instead of increasing on each communication cycle.

title: Nonasymptotic Oblivious Relaying and Variable-Length Noisy Lossy Source Coding
authors: Yanxiao Liu, Sepehr Heidari Advary, Cheuk Ting Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: The information bottleneck channel (or the oblivious relay channel) concerns a channel coding setting where the decoder does not directly observe the channel output. Rather, the channel output is relayed to the decoder by an oblivious relay (which does not know the codebook) via a rate-limited link. The capacity is known to be given by the information bottleneck. We study finite-blocklength achievability results of the channel, where the relay communicates to the decoder via fixed-length or variable-length codes. These two cases give rise to two different second-order versions of the information bottleneck. Our proofs utilize the nonasymptotic noisy lossy source coding results by Kostina and Verdú, the strong functional representation lemma, and the Poisson matching lemma. Moreover, we also give a novel nonasymptotic variable-length noisy lossy source coding result.

title: A Novel Dual-Frequency Phase Control Network for Reconfigurable Dual-Band Filters and Diplexers With Independent Control of Insertion Phase and Center Frequency
authors: Yuhang Ning, Yuxi Zhou, Chengfei Yi, Bin Liu, Pei-Ling Chi, Tao Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: A novel dual-frequency phase control network (PCN) is proposed for the flexible design of reconfigurable dual-band filters and diplexers with independent, continuous phase shift and frequency control. The dual-frequency PCN consists of a dual-frequency resonator for independent frequency control of both bands, a dual-frequency external coupling circuit, and a parallel-to-ground admittance circuit for separate control of admittance. By tuning these components, the network enables a 90° phase shift at each frequency band while maintaining a consistent amplitude response across both passbands. To validate this approach, a reconfigurable dual-band filter in conjunction with the coupled-resonator topology with the proposed dual-frequency PCN was designed, achieving a continuous 340° phase shift in each band. In addition, a reconfigurable diplexer was designed, fabricated, and measured, integrating a common resonator topology with two dual-frequency PCNs, resulting in a continuous 360° phase shift for both bands. Experimental results from both prototypes confirm the effectiveness of the dual-frequency PCN, supported by a detailed theoretical analysis. This design demonstrates significant potential for compact, multifunctional, and integrated devices in frequency division duplex (FDD) and multiband time division duplex (TDD) systems.

title: Optimizing Coexisting Satellite and Terrestrial Networks via Contrastive Generative Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Abuzar B. M. Adam, Eva Lagunas, Mostafa Samy, Dinh-Hieu Tran, Symeon Chatzinotas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The coexistence of terrestrial (TN) and non-terrestrial networks (NTN) in the Frequency Range 3 (FR3) spectrum enables wide-area connectivity but also introduces severe cross-network interference and rapidly varying channels, making resource-block (RB) assignment and receive beamforming essential for maintaining reliable ESIM performance. These challenges stem from mixed discrete-continuous decision variables, strong interference coupling, and stringent quality-of-service (QoS) constraints. Conventional optimization becomes intractable at scale, while standard deep reinforcement learning (DRL) struggles with hybrid action spaces and fast temporal dynamics. We propose a contrastive-enhanced generative primal-dual PPO (CG-PD-PPO) framework that reformulates the joint allocation problem as a constrained Markov decision process (CMDP). A primal-dual proximal policy optimization backbone enforces QoS guarantees, while a diffusion-based action generator and contrastive representation learning improve exploration and policy stability. The system model incorporates ESIM mobility, Loo fading, and Doppler effects in a Ku-band downlink, reflecting practical TN-NTN coexistence conditions. Simulation results show that CG-PD-PPO outperforms successive convex approximation (SCA-MA) and PD-PPO in sum throughput, QoS satisfaction, and interference robustness, while maintaining lower computational complexity, highlighting its practicality and scalability for future TN-NTN coexistence in FR3 scenarios.

title: Strunkmap: An Abstract Approach to Understand Spatiotemporal Density Distribution
authors: Xin Wang, Jingyuan Zhang, Lei Yu, Zhirong Huang, Jiajia Ma, Shiqi Cheng, Ruize Zhou, Xiaoxiao Ma, Jia Xu, Peng Wang, Li Yang, Fengjun Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Visual analysis of spatiotemporal density distributions is crucial for understanding spatiotemporal dynamics. However, existing methods suffer from visual occlusion and information loss when simultaneously displaying multiple density distributions. We present Strunkmap as an abstract approach to address these challenges. We introduce anisotropic kernel density estimation to enhance the accuracy of density generation. We extract the trunks of density distributions to identify the overall spatial patterns. Path scanning and trunk-outline matching strategies are employed to preserve local spatial structure. We design a stacked trunk plot that enables lossless density representation while conserving substantial screen space. Based on the visual design, Strunkmap integrates multiple heatmaps within a single map to effectively display temporal evolution of density distributions without visual occlusion. Ablation studies and comparative experiments validate the superiority of Strunkmap in accuracy and efficiency for hotspot identification and trend exploration. Theoretical analysis demonstrates Strunkmap's scalability, which we further verify through large-scale spatiotemporal data visualization. Color encoding schemes and scaling ratios are discussed to illustrate the flexibility. Our evaluations with user feedback demonstrate that Strunkmap is a viable solution with significant potential to real-world applications.

title: Design and Analysis of Full-Metal Omnidirectional Filtenna With Improved Selectivity and Low Profile
authors: Wenlei Wang, Chengjie Yang, Huayan Jin, Weiliang Yu, Ke Ming Chen, Leilei Liu, Guo Qing Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Two typical equivalent circuit models for filtering antennas (filtennas) are investigated to facilitate the optimization of frequency selectivity. The circuit analysis guides the design of a full-metal omnidirectional filtenna with improved selectivity and a low profile for vehicular communications. This filtenna is primarily made of a cylindrical metal block with an inner air cavity and a circular side groove. The cavity top has four slots for independent cavity mode control, while the bottom of the groove includes another four slots to improve frequency selectivity. This custom-designed metal block centrally fed by a coaxial probe can generate two radiation nulls to synthesize the bandpass filtering characteristic, along with three resonant poles working in patch TM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">02</sub>, patch half-TM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">02</sub>, and cavity TM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">010</sub> modes, respectively. The full-metal omnidirectional filtenna is fabricated, and experimental results show a–10-dB impedance bandwidth of 23.3%, a peak gain of 8.81 dBi, and excellent gain-suppression indexes of 0.13 and 0.21 for lower and upper frequency bands, respectively. In addition, a counterpart fabricated by the printed circuit board (PCB) technology is tested for comparison, which demonstrates that the low-cost full-metal design has a 3.85 dB gain improvement.

title: Intelligent IoT Poultry System for Automated Feed and Environmental Monitoring
authors: Willard Dhliwayo, Monica Madyembwa, Tinoziva Hungwe, Blessing Mlongoti, Jameson Madzana, Itai Nyerai
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Poultry farming continues to be a vital contributor to food security and employment, particularly in developing countries such as Zimbabwe, where small-scale farmers dominate the sector. However, traditional poultry feeding practices are labour-intensive, time-consuming, and prone to feed wastage, all of which negatively affect poultry productivity and farmer profitability. This study presented the design, implementation, and evaluation of an Internet of Things (IoT)-based automated poultry feeding system. The core objective of this research was to develop a smart system for automating the feeding process and environmental monitoring of poultry systems. The system was built using an ESP32 microcontroller, coupled with a DHT11 sensor for temperature and humidity, an MQ135 gas sensor for air quality, a Light Dependent Resistor (LDR), and ultrasonic sensors for feed level monitoring. Through a purposive sampling procedure, data was collected using interviews, focus group discussions and questionnaires. The data collected was on feed management, environmental parameters and challenges facing the poultry sector. The prototype was developed using IoT linked to the android mobile application to monitor the changes. The experimental results showed that the prototype was able to dispense the feed once the level dropped to a set minimum. The model also monitored environmental variables including temperature, humidity and ammonia. Furthermore, feed wastage was minimized through controlled dispensing cycles. The model proved efficient for small- and medium-scale farmers.

title: Limitations of Wave Field Synthesis Part II: Low-Frequency Limits
authors: Gergely Firtha
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: This paper establishes explicit low-frequency validity limits for 2.5D Wave Field Synthesis (WFS) derived from the Kirchhoff approximation. We show that the breakdown at low frequencies is governed by the applicability of the stationary phase approximation to the 2.5D Kirchhoff integral. This is quantified via an effective window (aperture) length that compresses the influence of secondary loudspeaker array size, tapering, referencing, and source/receiver proximity into a single quantity. We derive closed-form cutoff conditions for arbitrary source models and listening positions, yielding a cutoff wavenumber that scales with the inverse square of the effective length and recovers classical Fresnel-number/zone theory in frontal directions. The analysis predicts markedly higher cutoff for lateral positions and for sources/receivers near the SSD, and quantifies the impact of tapering and referencing schemes. Simulations with linear and circular loudspeaker arrays for plane, point, and focused sources validate the theory, including the intermediate transition band bounded by the two asymmetric effective lengths.

title: ST-GLNet: A Spatiotemporal Graph Learning Network for Accurate Lightning Prediction Using Multimodal Meteorological Data
authors: Jinlong Wang, Xingtao Yang, Haoran Zhao, Yuanyuan Zhang, Danfeng Hong, Xin Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Lightning strike, as a highly destructive natural phenomenon, causes significant casualties and economic losses every year. Accurate lightning prediction is crucial for disaster warning. However, existing lightning prediction methods predominantly rely on single-source historical patterns or numerical weather models, which struggle with limited spatial and temporal resolutions. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel framework, named Spatiotemporal Graph Learning Network (ST-GLNet), which integrates multi-source heterogeneous data including radar, lightning location, and meteorological data into a unified spatiotemporal learning architecture. The framework combines a Topology-Aware Spatiotemporal Hierarchy (TASH) and a Spectral Graph Regularization (SGR) for feature extraction, along with a Dynamic Adaptive Multimodal Fusion (DAMF) module for dynamic feature interaction. Through extensive evaluation on a custom-built multi-modal lightning prediction dataset (MLPD), the results show that ST-GLNet outperforms existing methods, achieving higher accuracy in lightning detection and lower false alarm rates, particularly in short-term predictions. Notably, the model demonstrates superior performance in long-term forecasting as well, making it a robust solution for real-time lightning nowcasting. This study’s contributions are pivotal for advancing predictive models in atmospheric science, offering a more reliable and accurate approach to lightning forecasting by leveraging multi-source data integration and innovative machine learning techniques. Dataset is available at https://github.com/ YMui9527/MLPD.git.

title: Channel-adaptive Semantic Communication for Stereoscopic Media: Design and Prototype Implementation
authors: Jingxuan Men, Mahdi Boloursaz Mashhadi, Ning Wang, Yi Ma, Mike Nilsson, Rahim Tafazolli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Stereoscopic media have garnered attention due to their widespread applications in 3D visualization, metaverse, virtual reality, and immersive media. By leveraging the disparity between views, stereoscopic media can facilitate 3D structure construction and enhance spatial perception. However, considering the correlation between stereo media pairs, stereoscopic media communication will introduce redundancy. To address this, we propose a novel dual-view deep joint source-channel coding scheme that efficiently extracts and transmits semantic features representing inter-view correlations, thereby reducing redundancy while improving the reconstruction quality. Furthermore, as the transmission channel quality varies, we design an adaptive semantic rate control method for stereoscopic media, based on a self-attention model, which uses inter-view correlations, feature importance, and channel conditions to apply an adaptive mask to control the transmitted data volume. This approach maintains reconstruction quality under varying conditions and improves Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) compared with benchmarks. Finally, leveraging 3D rendering, we demonstrate the practical effectiveness of the proposed schemes for immersive media transmission in real-world scenarios.

title: Impact of High Communication Latency on Teleoperated Driving Performance Independent of Driver Experience
authors: Donghyun Kim, TaeYoon Lim, Eugene Kim, Sungjun Park
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study examines how communication latency affects human driving performance in a MATLAB Vehicle Dynamics Blockset environment (double lane change, ISO 3888-1) and tests whether prior experience protects drivers from latency induced degradation. Twenty participants, grouped by experience into A/B/C, completed the task under latencies of 0, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, and 900 ms. Performance was quantified by path error, completion time, steering effort, vehicle speed, and cone contacts. To ensure rigor, we used repeated measures generalized estimating equations (GEE) modeling within participant correlation (subject ID as panel; robust sandwich standard errors), with fixed effects for Delay, Group, and Delay × Group; when assumptions were doubtful, we used Kruskal-Wallis with Dunn’s post-hoc and Holm correction adjustment. Performance equivalence was assessed via TOST for mean path error (δ = 0.50 m) and proportion TOST for the failure rate (error > 0.5 m) with margin Δ = 0.20. Path error increased monotonically with latency for all groups, with a steeper rise near 500 ms. GEE confirmed a strong latency effect with a non-significant Delay×Group interaction. Equivalence generally held up to 500 ms, but not at 900 ms; failure rate equivalence held at 0 - 200 ms only. In conclusion, the present findings did not provide evidence that driving experience alone guarantees robustness under high latency conditions. Therefore, when latency cannot be sufficiently reduced, assistive control and fail safe strategies should be established.

title: Design of Fault-Tolerant Dual Active Bridge for Solid-State Transformer
authors: S. Sarangi, Arunesh Kumar Yadav, Surya Narayan Gangolu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Solid-state transformers (SSTs) represent an advancing technology delivering superior efficiency and gridintegrated capabilities, enabling compact, lightweight solutions for applications such as offshore power transmission. SSTs interface medium-voltage (MV) three-phase alternating current with low-voltage (LV) alternating or direct current networks. As distributed energy resources and charging infrastructure expand, power distribution systems encounter voltage and current constraints. SSTs offer a solution by enabling adaptive power-flow management and direct-current compatibility, potentially circumventing costly grid upgrades. Nevertheless, since SSTs rely on power electronic components, they remain vulnerable to internal malfunctions. This investigation addresses the development of a resilient SST capable of tolerating internal faults. The study presents a fault-mitigation strategy targeting the Dual Active Bridge (DAB) stage, utilizing fast Fourier transform-based fault detection. Numerical simulations in MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate that the proposed approach enables the SST to operate reliably under all internal fault conditions.

title: Solar Integration by Topological Reconfiguration of on-Board Charger
authors: Dilip Kumar Patel, Ramanuja Panigrahi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are a promising solution to reduce emissions and consumption of fossil fuels. However, their adoption is impeded by short driving range and a long charging time. The impact of heightened load on the power grid is also a point of worry. One promising solution to these issues is to integrate onboard solar generation to EVs. The effective advancement of SEVs necessitates the development of suitable power electronic systems that can transfer energy from onboard solar PV (OBPV) to the drive and auxiliary batteries. This paper proposes an innovative topological reconfiguration of the standard onboard charger that enables OBPV integration without requiring an additional power converter. The Active Front End Converter (AFEC) of the onboard charger is reconfigured as a back-to-back buck-boost converter that can charge the auxiliary battery of the EV from the OBPV. The proposed topological reconfiguration is validated through simulation and experimentally on a lab-developed prototype.

title: Transportation Electrification in Crisis: A 2025 Review of Electric Vehicle Mobility and Infrastructure Resilience During Disruptive Events
authors: Puli Tarun, G. Jawahar Sagar, Harshitha Puli, Sariki Murali, Lokesh Mani Sai Balaji Loya, Tarkeshwar Matho
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Transportation electrification continues to evolve as a cornerstone strategy in global climate action. However, the increasing integration of Electric Vehicles (EVs) into urban mobility systems introduces new vulnerabilities during disruptive events such as wildfires, floods, cyberattacks, and grid failures. This 2025 review critically evaluates the state-of-theart advancements in EV mobility resilience, focusing on recent developments in charging infrastructure, disaster planning, and evacuation logistics. We extend previous findings by incorporating insights from real-world disruptions (e.g., 2023-2024 climate-related events), advancements in vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technologies, and the emergence of artificial intelligence (AI)driven evacuation planning. Particular emphasis is placed on the interplay between smart grids, decentralized charging networks, and adaptive routing during emergencies. The study identifies persistent gaps related to congestion management, mixed fleet behavior, and equity in access to emergency infrastructure. The findings aim to support policy development, infrastructure planning, and technological innovation that enhance the role of EVs as both mobility assets and energy resources during future disruptive events.

title: Market Design for Short-Term Power Trading Through Electric Power Exchange
authors: Vankadara Sampath Kumar, Saibal Chatterjee, Devnath Shah
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The restructuring and liberalization of electricity markets worldwide have fostered competitive environments conducive to efficient energy trading. A pivotal innovation in this domain is the establishment of Electric Power Exchanges (PX), which provide transparent and standardized platforms for short-term power trading. This paper explores the design and operational aspects of PXs, focusing on their core mechanisms such as day-ahead, intraday, and real-time balancing markets. It highlights the evolution of trading products, bidding strategies like auction-based and continuous bilateral trading, tailored for grid stability and market efficiency. Emphasis is placed on the role of PXs in managing increasing renewable penetration, forecasting errors, and market volatility through innovative tools like complex market orders, integrated power-emission trading, and auction mechanisms in intraday markets. A comparative analysis of major global PXs such as EPEX Spot, Nord Pool, GME, OMIE, EXIST, JEPX, IEX, and OPCOM is presented, showcasing trends in cleared volumes, price behaviors, and the influence of renewable energy shares on market prices. The paper underscores the importance of aligning PX design with regional technical and economic conditions to maximize social welfare and liquidity. Insights derived here aim to guide future PX developments, especially in emerging markets, toward resilient, inclusive, and sustainable power trading frameworks.

title: Smart Accident Prevention System for Electric Vehicles Using Speed Regulation and Drowsiness Detection
authors: Alok Vardhan, Dinanath Prasad, Mahendra Dutt Dwivedi, Souvik Ganguli, Neeraj Maurya, Muchkund Choudhary, Vaishnavi Singh, Riya Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a smart accident prevention system specifically designed for electric vehicles (EVs), aiming to reduce accidents caused by over speeding and driver drowsiness. The system integrates a microcontroller-based platform with wireless communication (ZigBee XBee S2C), IR sensors for drowsiness detection, and regenerative braking for automatic speed control. The vehicle receives zone-specific speed limits via wireless signals, allowing it to automatically reduce speed in sensitive or accident-prone areas. In parallel, the IR-based eye blink sensor detects prolonged eye closure, triggering alerts and initiating speed control if the driver is unresponsive. The prototype demonstrates the effectiveness of this dual-layered safety mechanism through various simulated conditions and graphical results, showcasing its real-time response capability, accuracy, and potential for future integration in autonomous EV platforms.

title: PSO and Fuzzy Based Optimal Allocation of Wind Turbines
authors: Ritika Anand, Divya Bharathi Raj, Venkatakirthiga Murali
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The deliberate placement of wind farms has become essential to optimizing wind energy potential while guaranteeing adherence to topographical and ecological restrictions, as the requirement for renewable energy continues to rise. This project aims to identify optimal locations for wind turbine installation within a designated 5 km x 5 km area, utilizing both 1500 kW and 2000 kW turbine models. The Fuzzy Mamdani logic is employed to assess soil quality and evaluate terrain characteristics, facilitating the identification of suitable sites for turbine placement. Each potential location within the area is assigned a soil quality suitability score ranging from 0.1 to 1. Sites with scores below 0.3, indicating poor soil quality or irregular terrain, are excluded from consideration for turbine installation. To enhance turbine efficiency, a minimum spacing of 400 meters between turbines is enforced as a key constraint. Additionally, the project directly addresses wake effects to better manage turbulence and improve energy generation efficiency. Furthermore, the total output power of the wind farm is calculated in accordance with the Betz limit, which defines the maximum efficiency of wind energy conversion. The implementation of Fuzzy Mamdani logic significantly enhances the evaluation process and turbine placement outcomes.

title: Analysis of Inner Current Loop with PI and Hysteresis Controller for DC/AC VSC in Microgrid Applications
authors: Sheikh Mohammad Sajid, Pradyumn Chaturvedi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The microgrid is developed with a solar photovoltaic (PV) array, a battery energy storage system (BESS) and a traditional three phase ac grid with a linear or non-linear AC load connected at the point of common coupling (PCC). This article presents the comparative analysis of the inner current control loop with proportional integral (PI) and hysteresis controllers for DC/AC voltage source converter (VSC) in microgrid applications. The control strategy is developed for a smooth transition from grid-connected mode (GCM) to islanded mode (IM) and IM - GCM and charging and discharging algorithm for BESS based on state of charge (SOC). The addition of a voltage and current loop with a maximum power point tracking (MPPT) in solar PV array provides extra layer of controller parameters. The effectiveness of the developed system is verified with three different cases: (a) transition from GCM IM & IM - GCM, (b) exchange of power with the grid at PCC, and (c) reliable supply at PCC under IM for solar intermittency. At PCC, the measured total harmonic distortion (THD) is less than 5% as per IEEE standard 519. The developed system is analyzed and simulated in the MATLAB environment.

title: Reliability-Weighted Adaboost Regression Model for Distribution System State Estimation
authors: Himani Mattoo, Anirban Dasgupta, Muneeb Mushtaq Sheikh, J.G Sreenath, Praveen Tripathy
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: State estimation in distribution systems is often complicated by nonlinearities, limited sensor coverage, and the presence of noise and outliers. Traditional model-based techniques such as the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) rely on linearized system models and Gaussian noise assumptions, which limit their accuracy under practical conditions. On the other hand, data-driven methods such as k -Nearest Neighbors (kNN) and decision trees are model-free but tend to struggle in noisy and partially observed environments. To overcome these challenges, we propose a weighted AdaBoost regression framework that incorporates the reliability of measurements as a weighting factor during training. The model adaptively emphasizes more trustworthy samples and downplays unreliable or noisy inputs, while also adjusting the contribution of individual weak learners. This dual mechanism enhances robustness and allows more accurate estimation of system states. The framework is evaluated on the IEEE 33-bus distribution test system under partial observability, where measurements are available at only a subset of nodes. Results demonstrate that the proposed estimator significantly outperforms the EKF, kNN, decision tree, and support vector regression (SVR) models in estimating voltage magnitudes and angles, particularly under noisy and incomplete measurement conditions.

title: Zero Sequence Voltage Control for Handling Unbalances in Multiport Charging Station Based Transformerless Series Hybrid Converter and DAB
authors: Jayant Kumar, Ibhan Chand Rath, Siba Kumar Patro
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this paper, a medium voltage transformerless series hybrid converter (TSHC) and dual active bridge (DAB) based multiport charging station (MCS) is proposed, which can recharge multiple electric vehicles (EVs) battery. It is also capable to mitigate the power quality issue of grid by supplying reactive power. The TSHC composed of parallel half bridge chain links (PHBCLs) and series full bridge chain links (SFBCLs), having <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M$</tex> number of sub modules (SMs). A DAB is connected across each SM of PHBCLs and SFBCLs of TSHC. The TSHC requires lesser switching devices and energy storage components, occupies a smaller space, and requires fewer SMs compared to traditional modular multilevel converter (MMC). Furthermore, it produces a greater number of AC output voltage levels in comparison to MMC for same number of SMs. In this MCS, total <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3(N+M)$</tex> DABs are used as DC-DC converter, owing to its attributes such as soft switching commutation, high efficiency, power density, galvanic isolation, elevated voltage conversion ratio, reliability, and bidirectional power transfer capabilities. Practically, there is unbalance loading at EV charging ports/DC side and also unbalance at AC grid/load causing divergence to the SMs capacitor voltages of TSHC. This issue of capacitor voltage divergence is taken care by proposed zero sequence voltage control technique. A comprehensive mathematical analysis is conducted for the proposed control, which address the power balancing in imbalanced scenarios such as vacant charging ports, an uneven battery power and AC grid unbalance. The functionality and regulation of the TSHC and DAB system is validated through PSCAD simulation of case study.

title: Integration of Solar PV System with Electric Grid for Reactive Power Control
authors: Rahul Kumar, Mukesh Kumar Kirar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Advancements in technology have led to the widespread use of non-linear loads in utility networks. Additionally, the growing integration of solar power into distribution systems has intensified power quality issues due to the intermittent and variable nature of solar energy. The prevalence of non-linear loads introduces significant challenges such as low power factor, load unbalancing, and harmonic distortions. To mitigate these issues and improve power factor, Active Power Filters (APFs) have emerged as an effective solution. This article presents the system architecture and performance evaluation of a 3-ϕ Solar Photovoltaic-based Shunt Active Power Filter (PV-SAPF). The proposed system draws power from the solar PV array and compensate harmonic distortions from load current. A Proportional-Integral (PI) controller is employed to generate reference currents and Hysteresis Current Controller is used for switching signals of the inverter. The system's dynamic performance, including both transient and steady-state responses, is analyzed using MATLAB-SIMULINK simulations. The effectiveness of the PV-SAPF system, enhanced by its simplified and innovative control strategy, is further validated through real-time testing with the OPAL-RT digital simulator.

title: Co-Adaptive Reinforcement Learning-Based Attacker-Defender Model for Secured Smart Distribution System
authors: Atharva Rathi, Akshaiy Thinakaran, Dipanshu Naware
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Smart distribution systems are witnessing increased deployment due to improved grid efficiency and reliability. These systems rely heavily on accurate meter readings for real-time monitoring and control, but the growing dependence on communication networks makes them vulnerable to cyber-physical threats such as false data injection attacks (FDIAs). Moreover, the need for data-driven technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), and deep learning (DL) in various sectors has been rising for over a decade, specifically in the energy sector. To address this challenge, this work proposes a reinforcement learning (RL)-based framework for FDIA injection and detection. In the proposed approach, there is an agent that acts as an adversary, learning to simulate and discover stealthy FDIA strategies, while the other agent serves as a detector, tasked with detecting and localising these attacks. This continuous adversarial training enables the defender agent to adapt dynamically to new and previously unseen FDIA patterns. Simulation results show that the RL-based defender provides a detection rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 6 1 \%}$</tex> when compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5. 4 5 \%}$</tex> with DL-based defender. The overall findings demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework in building resilient smart distribution systems and highlight the importance of adaptive, data-driven defence strategies in securing modern power grids.

title: Adaptive Multi-Objective Storage Planning for Flexibility Enhancement Under High Renewable Variability
authors: Vijay Naga Mohan Krishna Pothabathula, Hemanth Kumar Raju Alluri
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: High shares of wind and solar amplify variability and forecast uncertainty, straining the flexibility of the system, and threatening adequacy. This paper proposes a novel, adaptive multi-objective storage planning framework that co-optimizes investment and operations to enhance flexibility during the energy transition. Our key contribution is a Flexibility Enhancement Index (FEI) that integrates source, demand, and storage side flexibility into a single, operationally meaningful metric. A twostage framework is developed: (i) a stochastic MILP for capacity expansion and siting of grid-scale energy storage and demandside flexibility enablers; (ii) an evolutionary NSGA-II-based operational layer that refines dispatch and reserve schedules with feedback from FEI. Uncertainty is represented via scenario bundles generated from wavelet-ARIMA and GARCH residual modeling of RES forecast errors. The adaptive loop re-weights FEI components online using reliability signals (LOLP, unserved energy), curtailment, and ramping stress. We provide a tractable chance-constrained reformulation for reserve sufficiency and frequency nadir proxies, and a decomposition strategy (Benders + temporal splitting) to address scalability. Case studies on a modified Southern Indian region with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5 - 6 0 \%}$</tex> instantaneous RES demonstrate (a) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24-38 \%$</tex> curtailment reduction, (b) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$31-47 \%$</tex> LOLP reduction, and (c) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 - 2 7 \%}$</tex> ramping stress reduction for comparable cost, yielding superior Pareto fronts.

title: Design and Optimization of a Low-Power Hybrid Solar–Micro Hydro Microgrid with ANN-Based Voltage and Frequency Control for Remote Electrification
authors: Somila Hashunao, R K Mehta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a hybrid solar-micro hydropower system tailored for electrification in remote communities with kilowatt-scale demand. The hybrid microgrid combines the intermittent daytime energy supply from solar PV with the continuous generation potential of micro-hydro to improve overall system stability and reduce reliance on storage. To address challenges in voltage and frequency regulation under variable load and resource conditions, an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)-based controller is implemented using a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) architecture. Trained on multiple simulated operating scenarios in MATLAB/Simulink, the ANN outperforms traditional PID and GA-optimized controllers in minimizing voltage/frequency deviations <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\pm 3 ~\mathrm{V}, \pm 0.05 ~\text{Hz})$</tex>, reducing average daily power loss <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sim 6.5 \%)$</tex>, and achieving faster system response (settling time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<5 ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>). The simulation includes five distinct operating scenarios reflecting real-world conditions such as peak load, cloudy operation, and hydro outage. The results confirm the robustness and practicality of this low-voltage, cost-efficient hybrid configuration for decentralized electrification.

title: Small Signal Modelling and Closed Loop Control of Series LC Partial Resonant Current-Fed Half-Bridge DC-DC Converter
authors: Swati Tandon, Akshay Kumar Rathore, Sheron Figarado
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: A systematic approach for small signal modelling of partial series LC resonance based current-fed converter is presented. A series-resonance pulse during overlap conduction time is exploited for Zero-current switching (ZCS) of the devices, enabling natural decay of the outgoing semiconductor switch current. Small signal modelling is carried out through averaging method and a cascaded multi-loop controller with a minor loop for current control is proposed for variable frequency fixed duty cycle modulation to achieve stable operation of the converter. Dynamic analysis of the system against wide range of input voltage variations as well as load disturbances is corroborated using PSIM 11.1.5 simulations. Test results for step changes in source voltage and load current are presented to prove the efficacy of the proposed approach.

title: Enhancing Speed Response in PMSM-Fed Electric Vehicles Through Active Disturbance Rejection Controller-Based Vector Control Method
authors: Sanjay Kumar Kakodia, Giribabu Dyanamina, Amarendra Edpuganti
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Permanent magnet synchronous machines (PMSMs) are controlled using a proportional-integral (PI) controller-based vector control scheme, which exhibits poor performance in terms of load disturbances and speed ripples. Frequent disturbances are common in electric vehicle (EV) applications; therefore, active disturbance rejection control is a promising solution. In this study, the vector control scheme incorporates the ADRC within the speed loop because the outer speed loop significantly degrades performance in response to load disturbances. Therefore, an ADRC using an extended state observer (ESO) estimates the unknown load disturbance and compensates for it, thereby making it robust. Furthermore, a MATLAB/Simulink environment using an OPAL-RT 5600 real-time simulator was used for real-time control. The effectiveness of the ADRC and PI controllers was compared by using a real-time controller with different speeds and load profiles.

title: String Level Arc Fault Detection and Localization in Large-Scale Solar PV Array
authors: Shubham Kumar, Paresh Kumar Nayak
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Arc faults are the main cause of fire hazards in solar <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P V$</tex> array. Therefore, detection and location estimation of arc faults are of uttermost importance in a large-capacity solar PV array. Conventionally, arc fault circuit interrupter (AFCI) is used for detection and isolation of arc faults using array voltage and current. However, AFCI has limitation of detecting arc faults during low irradiance conditions and also, localization of arc faults is not possible by AFCI. In the present work, arc faults in soar PV array are precisely located at string level using only string current. The cumulative sum (CUSUM) of change in the string current is employed for detection and localization of arc faults in solar PV array. The efficacy of the proposed method is validated on different arc faults simulated on a grid-connected 3 MW solar PV plant under different operating conditions using MATLAB ® /Simulink.

title: Cross-Tied BIPV Hybrid Interconnections with Enhanced GMPP and Reduced Mismatch Loss for Extreme Shading Resilience in Smart Buildings
authors: Nikhil Arunrao Bodkhe, Debayan Sarkar, Risha Mal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Extreme non-uniform shading patterns (NU-SPs) significantly hinder the power yield of distributed solar photovoltaic (PV) and building-integrated photovoltaic (BIPV) systems. This research evaluates the effectiveness of five hybrid BIPV module interconnection topologies—three existing (Alternate Bridge Link Honey Comb [A-BL-HC], Series-Parallel Triple-Tied Cross Link [SP-TT-CL], and Total Cross-Tied–Series Parallel–Total Cross-Tied [TCT-SP-TCT]) and two proposed (Proposed Total Cross-Tied–Bridge Linked–Total Cross-Tied [Prop. TCT-BL-TCT] and Proposed Bridge Link–Total Cross-Tied–Bridge Link [Prop. BL-TCT-BL])—under high, low, and very low NU-SP conditions. Using MATLAB R2023b simulations, key parameters such as Global Maximum Power Point (GMPP), Local MPPs (LMPP1 & LMPP2), mismatch losses (ML), and the number of cross ties (CTs) are examined. The proposed configurations demonstrate superior performance, with Prop. TCT-BL-TCT and Prop. BL-TCT-BL achieving higher GMPP values (up to 858.97 W and 682 W, respectively) and reduced mismatch losses (as low as 13.98%). The increased cross ties enhance current distribution and shading tolerance. These results confirm that intelligent modifications in array topology significantly mitigate shading effects, improve energy yield, and enhance the practical deployment of BIPV systems in real-world conditions.

title: Electromechanical Equivalent Circuit Modelling of PMSM with Stator Winding Faults Analysis
authors: R. Raja Singh, T. V. Brindavan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machines (PMSMs) are widely preferred for high-performance applications which demand high precision control and efficiency. Accurate electromechanical modelling is crucial for analysing the behaviour of the machine under both normal and abnormal operating conditions. Insulation degradation is one of the major causes that leads to some electrical discharge, which may initiate the inter-turn short circuit faults. If undetected in early stages, it leads to phase-to-phase fault and phase-to-ground fault. This paper develops a 3 kW PMSM electromechanical model in Matlab/Simulink to analyse stator winding faults under varying load conditions. The model enables detailed investigation of fault dynamics such as inter-turn, phase-to-phase, and phase-to-ground faults. To evaluate the impact of faults on PMSM, both electrical and mechanical subsystems are incorporated and analysed to determine the behaviour. The winding faults, including single-phase, two-phase and three-phase faults, were created with 90% severity.

title: Cascadable Second-Order Universal Filter with CMOS-Based DX-MOCCII for Control Engineering
authors: Aamir Suhail Taray, Ashok Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: A novel electronically tunable higher-order currentmode universal analog filter is proposed, employing the Dual-X second-generation multi-output current conveyor (DX-MOCCII) as its core active element. The design uses fully grounded passive components, ensuring compatibility with CMOS IC fabrication. It supports all five standard filtering responses—low-pass, highpass, band-pass, all-pass, and notch—without requiring internal structural changes or input matching. Both the natural frequency and quality factor are electronically controllable, due to which the proposed current-mode universal filter holds significant promise in modern control system applications. The electronically tunable nature of the proposed design makes it especially suitable for adaptive and reconfigurable platforms, where real-time parameter adjustment is essential. This capability is especially valuable in control systems, where precise frequency tuning, realtime signal conditioning, and dynamic compensation are essential. Applications include analog PID controllers, active noise suppression, automatic gain control, and real-time preprocessing in sensor-based feedback loops. Its current-mode operation offers wide bandwidth, high slew rate, and improved linearity, while the Cascadable structure simplifies multi-stage integration. Simulation results using Cadence OrCAD with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.18 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> TSMC CMOS process validate the design's low power consumption and reliable performance, making it well-suited for analog front-end blocks in control-oriented applications.

title: Value of Lost Load Computation Framework for Vehicle to Load Applications Considering Life Cycle Techno-Economic Analysis
authors: N S Ramanathan, Pallavi Bharadwaj
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Transportation electrification has emerged as an intelligent choice to curb carbon emissions. To further accelerate net-zero transition, ways are sought to use electric vehicles as a potential transportation source. An electric vehicle (EV) battery could be used to power the critical loads during source outages, based on the replacement cost scaled to its usage. The economic benefits of their use on non-curtailment of the critical loads are expressed in terms of value of lost load (VOLL). This work proposes a novel approach to evaluate VOLL for EV batteries as a potential candidate for vehicle-to-load applications. This techno-economic analysis critically examines whether the battery should be used to cater to the loads. Further, the methodology is analyzed using sensitivity analysis based on hours of outages, battery cycling and replacement costs, fuel costs and inflation. This study justifies the efficacy of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) as an economical energy source during power outages over leadacid batteries by 52% based on the associated replacement cost. Furthermore, LIBs offer 15% cost benefits over diesel generators for 50% of source outage per day.

title: State-of-Charge Estimation for EV Li-Ion Batteries using ResNet: A Robust Deep Learning Approach with Real-World Driving Data
authors: N. Vigneswar, R. Manivannan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Accurate estimation of the state of charge (SOC) is essential for ensuring the efficient, safe, and reliable operation of electric vehicles (EVs), particularly under the dynamic and non-stationary conditions characteristic of real-world driving scenarios. This study proposes a deep learning-based SOC estimation framework employing an advanced residual neural network (ResNet) architecture, specifically designed to capture the complex nonlinearities inherent in real-world EV datasets. The model is trained, validated, and tested using real-time operational data acquired from a BMW i3 equipped with a 60 Ah lithium-ion battery (LIB) pack. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed ResNet model achieves a root mean squared error (RMSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 1 2 7 5 \%}$</tex> and a mean absolute error (MAE) of 4.5779 % on an entirely unseen test data set. These performance metrics surpass those of several conventional machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches, including feedforward neural networks (FFNN), long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, gated recurrent units (GRU), and extreme learning machines (ELM). The superior accuracy and generalization performance of the ResNet model underline its robustness and practical suitability for integration into realtime battery management systems (BMS), offering a promising solution for next-generation EV applications.
