title: PathSim: Graph Similarity Learning via Centrality-Driven Path Matching
authors: Yin-Peng Jiang, Ke-Jia Chen, Yu-Sheng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Graph similarity computation (GSC) is a fundamental research problem in the field of graph learning evolving from early A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">*</sup>-based approximation algorithms to more efficient methods based on graph neural networks. The latter predicts similarity scores presented in the form of graph edit distance or maximum common subgraph by generating embeddings of interaction information between graph pairs. However, current methods often struggle to represent and match the inherent sequential structures in graphs, e.g., paths, which are crucial for the performance of GSC task. To address this issue, we propose a novel learning-based GSC method that incorporates path sampling, encoding, and matching in an end-to-end framework. By selecting critical paths via a centrality-based sampling strategy and learning path-level representations through cross-graph interactions, our method enables effective sequential structure matching between graphs. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets show that our method achieves lower mean squared error on structure-sensitive datasets compared to existing leading methods and also achieves state-of-the-art performance on key ranking metrics including Spearman’s <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\rho$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, Kendall’s <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\tau$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and Precision@10. Ablation studies further confirm the effectiveness of each proposed component.

title: Research on Low Timing Jitter Silicon Single-Photon Avalanche Diodes
authors: Yanling Ren, Jiangteng Xia, Xianzheng Lang, Ming Zhang, Yuelin Wang, Qiaoli Liu, Anqi Hu, Xia Guo
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: Single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD) is a critical device in the laser time-frequency transfer system based on time-of-flight measurement of ultra-short laser pulses. However, it is difficult for SPADs to achieve low timing jitter with a large photosensitive area. Generally, the technique of reducing the neutral layer of the structure was utilized to improve the timing performance. In this paper, the effect of the multiplication electric field on the timing performance of SPADs is investigated by tuning the implantation energy of the charge layer to engineer the electric field distribution, thereby accelerating carriers more rapidly, shortening the time required to reach the ionization threshold, and reducing the statistical spread of avalanche triggering time. Silicon SPADs with a diameter of 200 μm, fabricated in a 6-inch CMOS technology, achieve a peak PDP higher than 54.4% at 635 nm, and a timing jitter of 28.9 ps at 532 nm under an excess bias of 2.35 V with an RMS value of 12.3 ps.

title: Data-Driven Modeling and Optimization of Discrete Waveform Relaxation Parameters for RLC Circuits
authors: Marwa E. Alrefai, Mohamed Kamel Riahi, Mohammad Al-Khaleel
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a large-scale numerical and data-driven framework for analyzing the optimized transmission parameter of the discrete Optimized Waveform Relaxation (OWR) method applied to RLC transmission-line (TL) circuits. Although the discrete convergence factor admits a rigorous analytical formulation, deriving explicit asymptotic expressions that capture the dependence of the optimized parameter on the time-step size Δt and the minimum frequency ωmin becomes increasingly challenging when multiple circuit parameters vary simultaneously. To address this limitation, a high- dimensional parameter sweep is performed using high-performance computing (HPC) resources to compute optimized parameters across a five-dimensional grid. The resulting curves exhibit structured sigmoidal behavior with distinct frequency regimes. A region-wise modeling strategy based on Richards-type sigmoid functions and regression-based models is introduced to obtain accurate and computationally efficient predictors for the optimized parameter.

title: Liver Couinaud Segmentation: A Review on Existing Segmentation Techniques of the Couinaud Classification of Liver_supp2-3623914.xlsx
authors: Gajendra Kumar Mourya
publish: N/A
abstract: Liver Couinaud segmentation is crucial in biomedical image processing for treatment planning and surgical interventions of the liver parenchyma. This is because the liver Couinaud segments help provide clinicians with a clearer and specific diagnosis of the location of the diseased areas within the liver tissue. Technological advancements in the form of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models have greatly enhanced the process of liver Couinaud segmentation. The efficiency of ML and DL models showcases their performance with incredible results. Hence, this article discusses the state-of-the-art techniques for liver Couinaud segmentation from 2006 to 2024. The segmentation techniques are divided into semi-automatic and automatic. Additionally, the online medical imaging datasets suitable for developing algorithms and frameworks for liver Couinaud segmentation are presented with their descriptions, advantages, and limitations. Furthermore, the performance metrics that are commonly used for evaluating the performance of segmentation algorithms are thoroughly discussed. Finally, the research challenges and recommendations are accentuated for future researchers who intend to bridge the highlighted gaps in liver Couinaud segmentation.

title: Energy-Efficient Beam Generation in RIS-Assisted Systems with Discrete Amplitude–Phase Coupling
authors: Jinghe Wang, Junwei Zhang, Wankai Tang, Weicong Chen, Jiajia Guo, Junyan Dai, Xiao Li, Shi Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) have emerged as a promising solution to enhance wireless coverage and energy efficiency. However, practical RIS implementations inevitably suffer from hardware constraints, where the reflection amplitude is coupled with the discrete phase states, and the power consumption depends on the PIN-diode bias/activation states. This paper investigates energy-efficient RIS beam generation under amplitude–phase coupling constraints. A realistic system model is first established, incorporating discrete reflection coefficients and power-aware coding to capture the trade-off between beamforming gain and hardware power consumption. Based on this model, we develop a Phase Rotation (PR) method and a Power-Aware Greedy Neighborhood Search Algorithm (PG-NSA) to efficiently synthesize low-power beam patterns. Furthermore, a multi-objective optimization framework based on the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) is introduced to obtain a Pareto front balancing beam pattern distortion and RIS power consumption. Simulation results on a 16 × 16 2-bit RIS demonstrate that the proposed methods outperform the conventional direct method based on the general RIS model, achieving up to 30% improvement in energy efficiency while maintaining high-quality beam patterns under practical RIS hardware limitations.

title: An Autoencoder-Kurtogram Approach to Transient Feature Extraction and Fault Diagnosis of Rolling Bearings
authors: Na Zhe, Pan He, Shiwen Liu, Yufeng Bao, Tangfan Xiahou
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Fault diagnosis is critical to modern mechanical systems to enhance their operational reliability by enabling early fault detection via feature extraction of signals. Kurtogram has been extensively implemented to feature extraction and fault diagnosis. Traditional kurtogram methods face the challenge in terms of detecting transients in signals heavily corrupted by noise, especially encountered in complex operating environments. This article introduces a novel autoencoder-kurtogram method, which incorporates Feature Mode Decomposition (FMD) and an autoencoder for extracting transient features and diagnosing faults in rolling bearings. In the proposed approach, the spectrum is adaptively split by taking into account periodicity and impulsiveness. A composite metric for transient characterization is then constructed using an autoencoder model, and the frequency band dominated by transients is obtained based on a continuous 2D colormap. Simulated signals with composite noise and real experiment data are leveraged to examine the performance of the proposed approach. The proposed approach proves effective as it successfully isolates weak transient features even in signals dominated by heavy noises.

title: Enhancing Integrity Verification of Convolutional Neural Network Predictions in a Malicious Model_supp1-3687141.pdf
authors: Lingling Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: The widespread deployment of neural networks has raised significant concerns regarding the integrity and privacy of model predictions, especially in malicious environments. Current approaches have explored zero-knowledge proofs for integrity verification. However, they suffer from inefficiency in proving runtime and a lack of rigorous integrity verification for non linear operations. To address these issues, we present a trustwor thy framework for Enhancing Integrity Verification of Convolutional Neural Network predictions (EIV-CNN) in a malicious model, whose key contributions are an efficient optimized sum check protocol and a robust enhanced verification mechanism. Specifically, we first propose an algorithm that enables efficient proving of both batch and collaborative CNN predictions by com bining sumcheck claims of multiple matrix multiplications into one. Moreover, we introduce a non-interactive sumcheck protocol with malicious security (NM-Sumcheck) to serve as a building block for publicly verifying matrix multiplication operations. Furthermore, we introduce a verifiable method for transforming nonlinear operations into matrix operations, enabling their sub sequent evaluation with the NM-Sumcheck protocol. Our EIV CNN provides malicious security, guarantees public verifiability, and preserves model privacy. Empirical results demonstrate that our sumcheck framework achieves constant prover time, verifier time, and proof size. Compared to the state-of-the-art, it achieves up to a 128.56× reduction in prover time, along with significant reductions in communication overhead and enhanced scalability.

title: Audible Focal Spot Generation via a Metasurface-enabled Parametric Array Loudspeaker_supp1-3689544.pdf
authors: Yun Jing
publish: N/A
abstract: Parametric array loudspeakers (PALs) produce highly directional sound beams but are constrained by poor low-frequency performance and unwanted low-path attenuation. Existing beam focusing engineering alleviates these limitations but relies on active phased arrays, which are costly and requires complex hardware for practical applications. Here, we present a passive acoustic metasurface (AMS) lens that enables broadband focusing of the audible beam generated by an ordinary PAL. The AMS is constructed from Fabry-P´erot-based annular unit cells, whose width is smaller than the carrier ultrasound wavelength, allowing fine spatial phase control for achieving tight focusing. Both simulations and experiments confirm the generation of strongly localized audible focal spots, with measured full-width at half-maximums (FWHMs) below 30mm axially and 6mm laterally across a wide frequency range from 500 Hz to 4 kHz. Our passive AMS design delivers stronger low-frequency output and steeper path attenuation than conventional PALs, supporting private delivery in public installations, vehicles, healthcare, and immersive environments. Unlike phased-array PALs that rely on computationally intensive beamforming and complex control hardware, the passive AMS offers a compact and low-complexity alternative for direct applications.

title: Synthesis and Design of Quasi-Elliptic Bandpass Filters on Resonating/Nonresonating Node With Transmission Zero Pair
authors: Lin Yan, Jing-Yu Lin, Hong-Ji Li, Sai-Wai Wong, Yi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Two novel low-pass prototype topologies for realizing quasi-elliptic bandpass filter (BPF) responses are discussed here. Each topology can generate a pair of transmission zeros (TZs), configurable at either a resonating or nonresonating node (NRN). In the case where the TZ pair is associated with a resonating node, the corresponding filter section is composed of one bandpass resonator (BPR) and two bandstop resonators (BSRs), denoted as BPR = 1 and BSR = 2. Alternatively, when the TZ pair is assigned to an NRN, the section employs two BSRs, represented as BPR = 0 and BSR = 2. These modular sections can be cascaded to create compact filters with multiple TZs. Meanwhile, to overcome the computational complexity of traditional synthesis on the configuration of proposed two low-pass prototypes, a filter synthesis framework based on surrogate-assisted optimization is proposed. This framework is capable of designing filters integrating different resonant sections, and each section can carry a TZ pair at either a resonant or nonresonant node. To validate the proposed approach, the synthesis and design of two BPFs with distinct topological arrangements are demonstrated: one is a 5th-order inline BPF constructed using three BPR = 1 and BSR = 2 sections and two BPR = 0 and BSR= 2 sections; the other is a 5th-order filter that incorporates one BPR = 1 and BSR = 2 section along with two BPR = 0 and BSR= 2 sections. Additionally, a manifold quadruplexer is implemented, utilizing two BPR = 1 and BSR = 2 sections and one BPR = 0 and BSR = 2 section to achieve the third-order channel filtering response. The synthesized, simulated, and measured results exhibit excellent agreement, confirming the effectiveness and accuracy of the proposed methodologies.

title: Head-to-head Textures Stabilization through Local Anisotropy Modifications in Large Aspect Ratio Stripes
authors: R. Fernández-Jiménez, M. Sánchez-Agudo, P. Ochoa, A. Gómez, M.T. Magaz, J. Soler-Morala, J. Rubio-Zuazo, F.J. Palomares, J.M. González
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: We report on the identification, from micromagnetic simulations data, of the types of stabilized head-to-head textures appearing at large aspect ratio stripes incorporating local anisotropy modifications (transverse easy axes). Our results show the occurrence, depending on the stripe thickness and width, of transverse symmetric (smaller stripe transverse dimensions) and transverse asymmetric (larger stripe transverse dimensions) textures.

title: High-Voltage Circuit Breakers in Extreme Electrical Conditions: USING THE NEW AMPERE-SECOND CRITERIA IN NONSTANDARD SYSTEMS
authors: John Webb, Jan Weisker, Victor Savulyak
publish: IEEE Industry Applications Magazine
abstract: CIRCUIT BREAKERS DESIGNED AND TESTED UNDER IEEE Standard C37.04/C37.09 and International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) 62271-100 assume electrical networks with a time constant of 45 ms or less and minimum relay delays of one half cycle and that are electrically “far” from generators or large synchronous machines. Increasingly, systems have evolved that exceed these parameters, and users struggle to properly apply circuit breakers with catalog and test data conforming only to these more moderate conditions. The most recent revisions to the cited IEEE and IEC standards have changed the testing methodology to provide results that assist in these applications, and the IEEE guide for applying high-voltage circuit breakers, IEEE Standard C37.010, encompasses this methodology. This article explains the basis for these changes and provides practical examples for employing this new methodology.

title: SuperScribble: A Super-pixel and Pixel Dual Constraint Remote Sensing Image Semantic Segmentation Network With Scribble Annotation
authors: Anqi Zhao, Hongjin Wang, Xinghua Li, Huanfeng Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Semantic segmentation is a crucial task in remote sensing field. With the rapid advancement, most learning-based methods rely heavily on pixel-level annotation data for training. To reduce annotation burden, more and more weakly supervised semantic segmentation (WSSS) methods are developed. Among these WSSS annotations, scribble is flexible and easily obtainable during practical labeling. Since there are relatively few multiclass WSSS methods for remote sensing images based on scribble annotation, a super-pixel and pixel dual constraint remote sensing image semantic segmentation network with scribble annotation (SuperScribble) is proposed. SuperScribble consists of three models: super-pixel feature extraction (SFE) model, pixel feature extraction (PFE) model and multi-head attention feature fusion (MAF) model. SFE and PFE are designed to extract super-pixel features and pixel features, respectively. On the one hand, they jointly generate more accurate pseudo labels. On the other hand, they are fused in MAF to achieve better segmentation. To further guarantee the quality of pseudo labels, a dual constraint strategy (DCS) for pseudo label generation is adopted, which is proved that it’s highly compatible with dual-branch networks. Experimental results demonstrate that compared with other 8 methods, SuperScribble could achieve competitive results on three public datasets, namely Potsdam, Vaihingen and Zurich Summer datasets. The implementation code and data will be available at: https://github.com/lixinghua5540/SuperScribble.

title: EvoSR-LLM: Evolutionary Symbolic Regression Guided by Large Language Models
authors: Yuchen Li, Handing Wang, Yaochu Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Mathematical equations describe fundamental laws across various disciplines, yet discovering concise and effective mathematical expressions from data remains a challenging task. Traditional symbolic regression methods often make limited use of the domain-specific prior knowledge, while large language model (LLM)-based symbolic regression approaches can more effectively utilize it. However, the existing LLM-based symbolic regression methods require substantial computational resources to generate equations while still suffering from low efficiency in producing effective ones. To address this issue, we propose EvoSR-LLM, a prompt-guided evolutionary search algorithm for equation discovery. EvoSR-LLM represents each equation as an individual composed of knowledge, insights, and code, integrating an evolutionary algorithm for iterative refinement. By leveraging an adaptive prompt mechanism, EvoSR-LLM constructs diverse prompts to guide the LLM in generating more effective equations. Experimental results demonstrate that EvoSR-LLM outperforms state-of-the-art symbolic regression baselines on 24 scientific benchmark problems, discovering equations with higher accuracy, stronger generalization, and lower complexity.

title: An Ultra-Compact 50-Gbaud × 16-Channel CPO Transceiver employing a 1060-nm Single-Mode VCSEL array and Multicore Fibers
authors: Wataru Yoshida, Tomonori Azuma, Liang Dong, Kazuya Nagashima, Sho Yoneyama, Kensho Nishizaki, Jumpei Miike, Makoto Miyoshi, Xiaodong Gu, Satoshi Ide, Hideyuki Nasu, Fumio Koyama
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: This paper describes the detailed design of an ultra-compact 50-Gbaud NRZ/PAM4 × 16-channel Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) transceiver to realize an energy-efficient spatial division multiplexing (SDM) optical link. The CPO transceiver features a 50-Gbaud 1060-nm coupled-cavity single-mode (SM) vertical cavity surface emitting laser (VCSEL) and photodiode (PD) arrays as well as SM 19-core multicore fibers (MCFs). Double-sided flip-chip bonding and direct butt-coupling to MCFs enable an extremely small mechanical size of 7.7 mm × 15.9 mm × 7.95 mm. A 50-Gbaud × 16-channel testing station to evaluate a 2-km MCF-based SDM optical link exhibits 3-dB bandwidth of 34 GHz at the worst channel. When operating 16 channels simultaneously, measured bit error rate (BER) characteristics are sufficiently lower than the KP4-FEC threshold for both 50-Gb/s NRZ and 106.25-Gb/s PAM4 operations. The 2-km MCF-based SDM optical link demonstrates record low link energy of 3.95 pJ/bit with 106.25-Gb/s PAM4 signals.

title: A Digital Twin-Enhanced Cloud-Edge-End Collaboration Scheme for Intelligent Resource Orchestration
authors: Tianyu Li, Xingwei Wang, Rongfei Zeng, Zhi LiuSenior, Yuxin Zhang, Qiang He, Liang Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: Cloud-Edge-End Collaboration (CEEC) computing has emerged as a promising paradigm for supporting computation-intensive applications within Digital Twin (DT) networks, achieving effective coordination of abundant computational resources and low-latency responsiveness. Deploying DT systems directly on Physical Entities (PE) significantly reduces communication overhead by minimizing data exchanges among Cloud Servers (CS), Edge Servers (ES), and PE. Additionally, pre-deploying computation replicas for specific task categories on ES effectively mitigates cold-start overhead in task scheduling. Nonetheless, due to the limited storage and computational capabilities of PE, efficient DT modeling and timely updates remain challenging. To address these challenges, we propose A digiTaL twin-enhAnced edge-cloud-eNd collaboraTIon sCheme (ATLANTIC) for intelligent resource orchestration. Specifically, we formulate the Two time-scale Intelligent Resource Orchestration Problem (TIROP), decomposing it into short-term computational task scheduling and long-term resource orchestration. A hierarchical optimization framework is designed to iteratively optimize resource allocation, effectively adapting to dynamic computational demands while satisfying diverse Quality of Service (QoS) requirements. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that ATLANTIC significantly improves service efficiency by dynamically responding to spatiotemporal variations in computational workloads.

title: Guide for the redesign of websites not accessible to blind people: MiUV study case
authors: Jorge Domínguez-Herrera, José Rafael Rojano-Cáceres
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: This research proposes a guideline that outlines the redesign process of a web system by compiling a set of tools and techniques aimed at improving accessibility for users with visual functional diversity (blindness). It is worth mentioning that the study adheres to the W3C (World Wide Web Consortium) standards using automatic evaluation programs and screen readers. The paper presents the selection process for these tools and techniques, which constitute the guideline composed of five stages: (1) training on accessibility issues, (2) initial system exploration, (3) evaluation with users and experts, (4) requirements analysis, and (5) recoding. It also specifies the artifacts to be produced at each stage and how they are used in subsequent phases. The guideline was applied to the institutional system “MiUV” which is used by the Universidad Veracruzana community, including faculty members, administrative staff and students—some of whom have visual functional diversity. The results showed that the proposed process was effective in guiding the redesign to improve accessibility. However, opportunities for improvement were also identified in each phase of the redesign process.

title: DeepSecure: Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Detecting Security Vulnerabilities in Python Code_supp1-3689776.pdf
authors: Reem Abdulrazaq Almutairi
publish: N/A

title: Multi-Level Conditional Disturbance Rejection Control for Satellite Attitude Tracking
authors: Gwanyeon Kim, Jonggeon Lim, Sesun You, Youngwoo Lee, Daehee Won, Yonghao Gui, Wonhee Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Recently, the conditional disturbance rejection controller (CDRC) was proposed to improve control performance by leveraging disturbances that have beneficial effects on the system. However, it only considers disturbances acting on the state variable directly influenced by them. Although fast convergence of this state can be achieved with the CDRC, it may unintentionally affect the convergence of the output (i.e., the primary state). In this paper, a multi-level CDRC (ML-CDRC) is proposed to enhance satellite attitude control performance by accounting for the effect of disturbances on both attitude (output) and angular velocity. The extended state observer is employed to estimate the lumped disturbance, including the modeling errors and external disturbances. Then, a backstepping-based controller with the multi-level disturbance rejection law (ML-DRL) is designed for attitude tracking. The ML DRL is developed to improve the control performance by using a disturbance with a damping effect on both attitude and velocity. Faster convergence of attitude and velocity can be achieved by conditionally compensating for the disturbance. The stability of the proposed control method is analyzed by demonstrating that the errors are bounded as time tends to infinity. The attitude control performance of the proposed method is evaluated through numerical examples conducted using the MATLAB/Simulink Multibody tool.

title: Outliers-Resistant State Estimation for Integrated Electricity-Gas Energy Systems Under Sensor Resolution Effects
authors: Xingzhen Bai, Guhui Li, Suoyue Wang, Chunlei Hao, Zhe Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In this paper, the dynamic state estimation problem is investigated for integrated electricity-gas systems (IEGSs) subject to measurement outliers, where measurement signals are obtained from sensors with specified resolution. The consideration of sensor resolution is a crucial aspect that reflects real-world engineering practices, as it directly influences the reliability and accuracy of measurements. To address the challenges posed by measurement outliers, an innovation saturation mechanism is employed to constrain the innovation terms affected by these outliers. The main objective is to develop a recursive estimation algorithm tailored for the IEGS, which considers nonlinear measurements, sensor resolution effects, and measurement outliers. This approach ensures that an upper bound on the estimation error covariance is established, and subsequently minimized by designing the suitable estimator gain. The effectiveness of the proposed outlier-resistant recursive estimation algorithm is validated through extensive simulation experiments conducted on an IEGS consisting of a 14-node electrical power system and three 7-node natural gas systems.

title: Beamforming With a Phased Array Interleaved by Coupled Subarrays
authors: Ke Huang, Bin Zhang, Lixin Ran
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A novel beamforming method is proposed to address mutual-coupling-induced beam misalignments in phased arrays interleaved by coupled subarrays (PAICS). By establishing a signal-coupling model, subarray port currents are related to decoupled incident voltages via a generalized impedance matrix (GIM), which is used to correct the ideal steering vector (ISV) in beamforming. Compared with optimization-based methods, this analytical method notably reduces computational overhead and response time. A C-band PAICS comprising seven subarrays was designed and implemented with integrated measurement-and-control circuitry, enabling efficient data acquisition required for GIM determination. Experiments on the prototype demonstrate high angular accuracy in direction-of-arrival (DoA) estimation and beam steering over ranges of ±55° and ±50°, respectively. The tested 1.5-ms latency of beamforming-weight calculation proves its potential for real-time embedded implementation.

title: Robust Controller for Single-Stage Isolated Power Conditioning Unit on-Board Charger
authors: Mohammad Asif Ali, Aman Keshri, Dogga Raveendhra, Suneel Raju Pendem, B L Narasimha Raju, Pradyumn Chaturvedi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This manuscript presents robust control design for Single-stage on-board charger for Plugin Electric Vehicle. Bidirectional interleaved totem pole configuration has been selected for this purpose. In several literature the conventional linear controllers are used for designing the control aspects of the power converter. However, this paper presents the robust control technique using sliding mode control. These controllers provide better dynamics performance and stability during the case of uncertainty and disturbance. Sliding surface used is decoupled for primary and secondary bridge. Detailed mathematical explanation has been provided in this paper for the derivation of reaching law for controller. Lyapunov theory is utilized in this paper for control law derivation, stability analysis and sliding coefficient estimation.

title: SiC based DC-DC Regulated Power Supply using LLC Resonant Converter
authors: Samerthya Kumar Pandey, Naveen Yalla, Narendrababu A
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design, analysis, and hardware validation of a Silicon Carbide (SiC) based LLC resonant converter for high-efficiency isolated DC-DC power supply applications. The converter is designed to operate with a 100 V input and deliver a regulated 48 V output at 500 W, targeting applications such as electric vehicle chargers and telecom supplies. The LLC topology is selected for its superior softswitching characteristics, high power density, and efficient lightload performance. A cascaded PI control strategy is implemented along with a voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO) to modulate switching frequency and maintain output voltage regulation across a wide operating range. The proposed design is validated under various load and frequency conditions, demonstrating robust closed-loop regulation and peak hardware efficiency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2. 6 4 \%}$</tex>. The converter maintains stable performance in resonant, above-resonant, and below-resonant modes, confirming the effectiveness of the control approach and the benefits of integrating wide bandgap SiC devices.

title: Data-Driven Multi-Objective Optimization of Perovskite Solar Cells Using SCAPS-1D, Gaussian-Process Surrogates & NSGA-II
authors: Velpuri Leeladevi, Piyush Kuchhal, Debasis De
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Achieving a balance between high power conversion efficiency (PCE) and low cost of manufacture continues to be a significant challenge to the commercialization of perovskite photovoltaics. A data-driven, multi-objective optimization framework is introduced, combining SCAPS-1D device simulations, Gaussian Process surrogate modelling, and the NSGA-II evolutionary algorithm. A dataset of 221 simulations systematically varying perovskite absorber thickness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{5 0 - 1 0 0 0 ~ n m})$</tex> and bulk defect density <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathbf{1 0 1 0 - 1 0 2 0} \text{cm}^{-\mathbf{3}}\right)$</tex> under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AM} 1.5 \mathrm{G}\left(1000 ~\mathrm{W} ~\mathrm{m}^{-2}, 300 ~\mathrm{K}\right)$</tex> conditions was generated. The surrogate model, trained on 80 % of the data, predicts PCE with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{>} \mathbf{0. 9 9}$</tex> on held-out samples. Manufacturing cost is represented by a weighted sum of layer thickness and defect-density contributions to capture material usage and processing complexity. NSGA-II applied to the surrogate identifies a Pareto front of design points achieving up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{3 0} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> predicted PCE while minimizing cost. The resulting Pareto-optimal parameter combinations reveal regimes where modest efficiency trade-offs yield significant cost savings. This approach offers actionable guidelines for experimental fabrication and establishes a generalizable workflow for rapid, cost-effective development of advanced photovoltaic devices.

title: Design and Analysis of Ultra-Gain Bi-Directional DC-DC Converter
authors: Vansh Sharma, S Porpandiselvi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Renewable energy sources are essential for achieving sustainable development. However, their inherent intermittency and unpredictability pose a significant challenge to the continuous and reliable power supply. To address this issue, energy storage systems (ESS) such as electrochemical batteries and supercapacitors are employed. These systems store excess energy when generation is more than demand and supply the power during low or no generation, or when load requirements surpass available generation. This paper proposes an ultragain bidirectional DC-DC converter designed to facilitate the efficient integration of energy storage systems with high-voltage applications such as electric vehicle (EV) charging and DC grid systems. The converter offers a quadratic voltage gain in both buck and boost modes, enabling versatile operation for charging and discharging scenarios. The operating principles, switching strategy, and mode-by-mode characteristics of the proposed converter are thoroughly analyzed. In addition, MATLAB/Simulink-based simulations are conducted to validate the theoretical design. The results validate mathematical analysis with the simulated performance, demonstrating the converter's suitability for modern renewable energy and energy storage applications.

title: Enhanced Sensorless Control of Pmsm Using Torque-Aided Extended Kalman Filter for Robust Speed Operation
authors: Raja Gandhi, Deigratia Sutnga, Edi Sankararao, Ajay Anand, Rakesh Roy
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper represents the sensorless control of PMSM by estimating the rotor position and speed of the motor with the help of EKF. The EKF algorithm is modified using the general speed torque equation of the motor. This equation uses load torque information applied to the motor which is estimated by a separate load torque estimation block. The load torque estimation block uses instantaneous input voltage and current to the motor. With this the motor can be run easily at different speed with any amount of load torque. The proposed method is validated in experiment on a 3.7 kW PMSM which current vector is controlled using field oriented control method. No speed and position sensore is used in the set up and estimated position and speed are given as feedback in the close loop control. The estimated speed, position, load torque and current are analyzed for different cases to validate the proposed method's performance. The cases are no load clockwise operation, no load anti clockwise operation and load torque operation. In all the cases the motor runs very smoothly and the calculated speed error is found very less which shows the satisfactory performance of the proposed method.

title: A Reduced Switch Count Three-Level Inverter for Wind Energy System Using ANN-Based Vector Control
authors: Navin Kumar, Giribabu Dyanamina
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this paper, vector control with an artificial neural network (ANN) controller has been proposed for the machineside power converter(MSPC) and grid-side power converter(GSPC) for improving the power quality of the gridconnected self-excited induction generator (SEIG) based wind energy system. Further, a reduced device-count three-phase three-level switching capacitor (SC) type converter has been proposed for the grid-side converter. SC voltage helps to synchronize a five-level line voltage output. The suggested converter generates pulses using the SPWM approach. MATLAB/SIMULINK has been used to simulate the suggested model, and the WAVECT wcu300 FPGA-based controller has been used in the lab to construct the prototype model of the suggested grid-connected SEIG-based WES in order to verify its functionality In terms of reducing undershoots, overshoots, ripples, and complication in the steady state during unpredictable wind speed, the ANN with the SPWM technique beats the PI with the SPWM technique.

title: Design and Implementation of Smart Road Management System Using PLC and HMI
authors: Alok Vardhan, Dinanath Prasad, Mahendra Dutt Dwivedi, Souvik Ganguli
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The growing challenges of traffic congestion, urbanization, and safety in modern cities necessitate intelligent and adaptive traffic control systems. This paper presents the design and implementation of a Smart Road Management System that integrates a Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) with a Human–Machine Interface (HMI) to achieve efficient, feedback-based traffic regulation. The system employs photoelectric sensors to detect real-time traffic density and dynamically adjust signal durations to minimize waiting and clearance times. The HMI provides an intuitive control interface for real-time monitoring, mode selection, and emergency operations. The prototype, developed using a Mitsubishi FX1S-30MR-DS PLC and GOT1000 HMI, was tested on a scaled fourjunction traffic network. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in average vehicle waiting time (38.7%), junction clearance time (38.8%), and emergency vehicle response (57.1%) compared to traditional fixed-timing systems. Additional features, including green corridor activation, burglary alert response, and advertisement consultancy based on vehicle density, enhance system functionality and practical applicability. The proposed PLC–HMI-based architecture offers a robust, scalable, and intelligent solution for next-generation urban traffic management.

title: Lag-Aware Ensemble Learning for Generalized Hourly GHI Forecasting Across India
authors: Anirudh D, Jezer Nirmal J, Jineshwar S D, O V Gnana Swathika
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a physics-based stacked ensemble model for the hourly forecasting of Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI). The ensemble is comprised of XGBoost, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Ridge Regression, along with Ridge Regression as the meta-learner. The model includes meteorological and solar geometric inputs along with lag features and moving averages trained on historical data from 2014 to 2023 across sixteen geographically diverse cities in India. A wide range of metrics including RMSE, NRMSE, MAE and R2 were used to test the models individually and the ensemble. The results indicate that the model that incorporated lag features and moving averages demonstrates high levels of predictive accuracy when compared with the model without any lag features. The model has shown great spatial and temporal generalization when tested on 6 new unseen cities and the unseen year data from 2024.

title: Design and Simulation of a Coupled Inductor Interleaved Buck Converter for High-Efficiency DC Power Application
authors: Chandika Pratap Singh, Narendrababu A, Harsh Prajapati, Naveen Yalla
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a two-phase interleaved buck converter using a magnetically coupled inductor for high-efficiency DC power applications. The coupled structure improves ripple performance and reduces component count. A practical magnetic design is carried out using the area product method, and a prototype is fabricated to confirm inductance targets. A small-signal model supports PI controller development for closed-loop regulation. Comparative simulation results highlight a significant reduction in phase current ripple compared to separate inductors, while maintaining stable output under transient conditions. The converter design is validated through measurements of fabricated components, and a complete test setup is prepared for future hardware evaluation. This work combines analytical modeling, physical design, and control integration, offering a compact and efficient solution for high-efficiency power systems.

title: Intelligent Time-Domain Protection of Gridforming Microgrids Enabled by IEC 61850 Goose Communication
authors: Aditi Gupta, Soumya R Mohanty, Nand Kishor
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper introduces a real-time fault identification framework for microgrids comprising gridforming inverter-based resources (GFM-IBRs), where three photovoltaic (PV) plants operate in parallel at the point of common coupling (PCC) under grid-forming control. The proposed approach leverages the transient characteristics of relay current signals by employing advanced signal decomposition techniques, which approximate wavelet-based multiresolution analysis using Butterworth filtering. A novel feature extraction metric-the Superimposed Identification Factor (SIF), is formulated to encapsulate the dynamic deviation of sequence component behavior under faulted conditions. The scheme demonstrates high sensitivity in distinguishing between high- and low-impedance faults across grid-connected and islanded operational modes. The methodology is modeled within the RSCAD/RTDS environment, and its real-time implementation validates its reliability, selectivity, and effectiveness for practical protection applications in inverterdominated microgrids. Integrating the IEC 61850 GOOSE protocol using GTNETx2 and Virtual Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs) shows that the scheme is compatible with contemporary substation communication standards and supports external relay coordination. This reinforces its suitability for implementation in advanced protection architectures.

title: Design and Analysis of a Uni-Switch Quadratic Boost Converter with Three-Winding Coupled Inductor for Efficient Solar-Powered EV Battery
authors: K Karthik Kumar, A S Kamaraja
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study presents a novel Uni-Switch Quadratic Voltage Step-up Boost Converter (USQVSC), integrating a three-winding coupled inductance (CI), tailored for efficient solar PV-based electric vehicle (EV) battery charging. The topology achieves substantial voltage gain at low duty ratios, significantly lowering voltages stresses on the switches. It ensures continual source current with minimal ripple (lower <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{d i} / \mathbf{d t}$</tex>) and suppresses reverse recovery losses by regulating the diodes current fall rates. Notably, dual output capacitors reduce dependence on bulky energy storage components, enabling a more compact design. The converter also enhances design adaptability while minimizing electromagnetic stress. Comprehensive analysis elaborates on voltage/current stresses, the impact of leakage inductance on gain, and critical design aspects. The system's performance, validated through MATLAB simulations, confirms continuous conduction mode operation with remarkable efficiency of 98.2 %, and a minimal loss of 6.4 W in a 320 W prototype. These features collectively establish the USQVSC as a promising solution for high-efficiency, compact EV charging systems.

title: Single Switch Based Quadratic Gain Buck-Boost PFC Charger with DCM Operation
authors: Rahul Kumar, Bhim Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper introduces a quadratic gain AC-DC converter operating in discontinuous conduction mode for a battery charging application. The configuration presented in this work uses a single active switch and can achieve output voltage regulation at wider variation with a correspondingly low variation in the duty ratio. The converter operation under various operating modes is presented. The voltage gain equation for both continuous along with discontinuous conduction modes, as well as design equations for passive components used in suggested converter, are developed for discontinuous conduction mode. In constant current charging mode, the DC side uses a single current sensor to control the battery charging current. A good power quality on grid current is achieved along with unity power factor operation. Presented configuration is designed for 800 W power output and universal AC input supply. The converter design, operation, and control are validated using simulation in MATLAB Simulink environment.

title: Calibration of Photo Resistive Sensor Using Optimization Algorithms
authors: T. Bhanu Teja, Rushali Pandit, Yadav Rinki Harinath, E. Mallikarjun
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The purpose of this work is to calibrate an LDR sensor to measure light intensity by comparing its output with the reference sensor, BH1750. The optimization of gain and offset, which is crucial for converting the ADC values of the LDR into lux values, is achieved through the use of the steepest descent optimization method and the conjugate gradient optimization technique. A decrease in error between normalized ADC and lux values is achieved through an iterative calibration process. The results are visualized through the calibration curve. The calibrated parameters are displayed, indicating successful optimization and improved alignment between the LDR and the reference sensor. This approach provides a low-cost alternative for improving the accuracy of LDR sensors for light intensity measurement applications.

title: ML Based Approach to Mitigate the Effect of Thermal Transients on the Dielectric Response data for Dry-Type Insulation
authors: Somesh Ganguly, Praveen Kumar, Arijit Baral, Sivaji Chakravorti
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Temperature of the insulation system of a transformer during normal operation is relatively higher than that of the ambient. Polarization-Depolarization Current (PDC) measurement, being an offline technique, necessitates switching off the target transformer and allowing sufficient time to cool it to the ambient temperature. However, such a procedure is vastly time-consuming and hence not always feasible for field measurements. Commencing PDC data measurement before the targeted unit attains thermal equilibrium, gets invariably affected by thermal transients. Use of such data for prediction of insulation conditions is likely to create ambiguity in the results obtained. The present work proposes a Wavelet Neural Network (WNN) regression-based approach to obtain the polarization current profile of dry-type insulation free from the effect of thermal transients. The proposed technique utilizes the data affected from thermal transients to obtain the polarization current profile at a particular steady state temperature. The results obtained from the present investigation justify the performance of the WNN regression-based methodology in obtaining the polarization current profile from the data measured during temperature transients. The performance of the WNN based methodology has also been compared with other available regression algorithms.

title: Estimation of Storage Depletion Outage Time for Energy Harvested Microgrid Systems
authors: Ritwik Hakdar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Renewable-integrated microgrids are a promising solution for providing sustainable and resilient power to local loads. However, such microgrids may experience storage depletion outages (SDOs) when renewable generation is insufficient and the energy storage is depleted due to frequent peak load events, causing a temporary outage resulting from energy scarcity. These SDOs degrade system reliability. In this paper, a novel absorbing discrete-time Markov chain (DTMC) model of the energy storage state-of-charge is developed to estimate the expected time-to-first SDO in an islanded microgrid, thereby relating outage behavior to system reliability.

title: A Novel High-Gain Switched Capacitor-Based Quadratic DC–DC Converter for Fuel Cell Electrical Vehicles
authors: Yasir Bashir, Prakash Ji Barnawal, Amarendra Edpuganti
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Most of the fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEVs) demand a high-gain converter to step up the inherently low output voltage of fuel cell (FC) stacks for integration with the DC link bus or auxiliary energy storage systems, such as batteries or supercapacitors. This paper proposes a high-gain switched capacitor structure-based quadratic boost dc-dc converter designed specifically for FCEV applications. The converter employs a switched capacitor structure combined with a quadratic circuit to achieve high gain and minimize voltage stress on components, all while maintaining a common ground configuration. Detailed analyses of the converter's operation, component design, and simulation validation are presented in this paper. Performance is benchmarked against existing highgain converters through extensive MATLAB simulation.

title: A Modified Notch Filter SOGI-Controlled Solar-Battery Interface for Power Quality Improvement in EV-Integrated Grids
authors: Sarthak Mohanty, Shubhranshu Mohan Parida, Pravat Kumar Rout, Buddhadeva Sahoo
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The integration of a solar photovoltaic conversion system (SPCS) and a battery energy conversion system (BECS) into a three-phase weak AC distribution grid subjected to nonlinear loads presents significant challenges in maintaining power quality and system stability. During standalone operation, voltage distortion and imbalance at the point of common coupling (PCC), exacerbated by harmonics introduced by electric vehicle (EV) charging loads, deteriorate system performance and complicate grid synchronization. To address these issues, this paper proposes a filter-based control strategy that accurately extracts the fundamental components of PCC voltage and load current. These components serve as reference signals for controlling the switching of the SPCS-BECS voltage source converter (VSC). A three-phase modified notch filter second-order generalized integrator (MNFSOGI)-based algorithm is employed to perform real-time harmonic filtering and facilitate precise reference signal generation. These reference signals are utilized by a hysteresis current controller and an MNFSOGI-based phase-locked loop (PLL) to achieve robust synchronization and seamless transition between islanded and grid-connected modes. The proposed framework enhances current waveform quality, effectively manages unbalanced and non-linear loads, and ensures stable operation under dynamic environmental conditions. Simulation results obtained from MATLAB/SIMULINK validate the effectiveness of the proposed control scheme in improving power quality and ensuring compliance with grid standards.

title: Performance Analysis of a Modified Single-Phase Split-Source Inverter Achieving Reduced Commutation Loss and Enhanced Voltage Gain
authors: Muppidi Angel Babita, K. Vinoth, Hari Charan Nannam, Majji Sai Manohar, A. Surya
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this paper, a single-phase split-source inverter with a passive network is introduced. This inverter can deliver double-boosted AC output with only one inductor and a common H-bridge structure. The proposed system is well compared to the existing literature, and its operational traits are well noted. The proposed topology eliminates traditional commutation problems and reduces control complexity through a constant duty cycle during inductor charging. The performance analysis of the proposed system for various load conditions includes a continuous, dynamic analysis at various frequencies is verified using MATLAB/Simulink, and the experimental prototype is designed in the laboratory to determine the real-time analysis.

title: Decentralized Interval Type-2 Fuzzy Logic Fractional Order PID Controller Design for a Liquid Level System
authors: Soumya Ranjan Mahapatro, Manmohan Sharma, Bidyadhar Subudhi, Sudipta Chakraborty
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents design and realization of an interval type-2 fuzzy logic fractional order PID (IT2-FLFOPID) controller for a multivariable liquid level system. The performance of this controller is compared with the conventional interval type-2 fuzzy logic PID (IT2-FLPID) controller. The time response of the proposed controller was compared with conventional IT2-FLPID controller. From the comparative assessment it was confirmed that the proposed controller exhibits superior performance in terms of faster rise time, settling time, less overshoot while maintaining superior control performance as evaluated using different error performance measures.

title: Effect of Anode Thickness on Internal Short Circuit Behavior of Li-Ion Battery
authors: Narendra Babu Ch, Ashish. Paramane, Pitambar R. Randive
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The present study discusses the influence of anode thickness on the internal short circuit (ISC) behavior of lithiumion batteries. A pseudo-two-dimensional (P2D) model is used to analyze the numerical behavior. The anode with different thickness configurations is considered. To identify the optimal anode thickness that enhances the electrochemical performance, a comparison is made among the different anode thickness configurations. Results indicated that a thicker anode enhances the voltage and capacity compared to the thinner anodes. Further, the C-rate strongly impacts the cell performance during the ISC. At low C-rates, stable discharge profiles are observed; however, at higher C-rates, severe voltage drop is observed for all anode thickness configurations. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of anode thickness and C-rate in enhancing the Li-ion performance and safety and mitigating the ISC conditions.

title: Detection, Classification and Phase Localization of Cyber Attacks in $3-\phi$ Standalone Inverter Systems
authors: Tushita Das, Mitikiri Sagar Babu, Suprabhath Sriranga Koduru, Vedantham Lakshmi Srininvas, Sukumar Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This work presents a method to detect and classify cyber attacks in a three-phase standalone inverter-based AC microgrid. The considered attack types are delay attack, in-phase false data injection attack (FDIA), and out-of-phase FDIA. Detection is performed at the load side using feeder-side voltage magnitude and phase angle deviation changes. The method identifies the attacked phase and classifies the attack type based on specific patterns in these changes. The proposed logic is tested in MATLAB/Simulink 2024b under each attack scenario with load changes during the attack period. Results show accurate detection and classification of all attack types with correct phase identification. The proposed method uses voltage magnitude and phase angle deviation changes to reliably detect and classify delay and FDIA attacks in standalone inverter-based AC microgrids.

title: A Time-Varying COA-TVECM Enumeration for Modelling the Demand-Cost Nexus in EV Battery Recycling Towards Circular Economy
authors: Uttam Saha, Parimal Acharjee, Tushar Kanti Bera
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Apart from standardized cell chemistry, levitated production cost, safety concerns are the refugee for sluggish growth of Electric vehicle (EV) in India. The constrained availability of battery protein materials like Li, Co and Ni underscore the strategic necessity of embedding circular economy principles within the EV value chain. The oligarchic nature of rare-earth materials in the global market accentuates establishment of robust battery recycling blueprint. Taking key attributes such as Recycling expenditure and EV demand into consideration, long - term sustainability can be availed. This paper rigorously examines the economic viability by Employing a Threshold Vector Error Correction Model (TVECM) analysis along with Cheetah Optimization Algorithm (COA) to denouements an optimal threshold value, effectuates as a tipping point on which the recycling system commutes its dynamic behavior. The model has pugnacious responses towards high deviation system, enabling early-stage recycling adjustment. This behavior makes the model pragmatic for policy building, where incentives, tech upgrades, or penalties kick in stronger only after particular threshold value. The econometric model illustrates how external disturbance- stemming from economic conditions or policy inventions-disseminates within the system, the green signal towards a sustainable and closed loop circular economy.

title: Performance Analysis of Distribution Systems Under Different Electric Vehicle Penetration Levels
authors: Sapna Verma, Bhavnesh Kumar, Arjun Tyagi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Increasing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) increases load and congestion on existing distribution networks. The situation forces researchers to analyze the impact of EVs on the distribution system for operational implications. This paper presents an in-depth analysis of the impacts of electric vehicle charging stations at various penetration levels. Additionally, distributed solar photovoltaic (PV) systems are included to assess their cumulative effect with EV loads. An Improved Grey Wolf Optimization (IGWO) algorithm is applied to determine the optimal locations and sizes of Electric Vehicle Charging Stations (EVCS) and PV units for the IEEE 69-bus distribution system. The proposed work suggests a smart approach to identify the size and placement of charging stations, leading to reduced active power losses and improved voltage profiles at various levels of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E V}$</tex> and PV penetration. The proposed method demonstrates five different scenarios, including a base case, multiple EV penetration levels, and hybrid scenarios with distributed solar integration. Results demonstrate that the proposed strategy enhances system performance by integrating EVCS with solar PV. The approach achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 1. 1 \%}$</tex> reduction in power losses while maintaining the most stable voltage profile.

title: Exploring Cyber Risks in Grid-Integrated Electrical Vehicle on-Board Charger (OBC)
authors: Nikhil B Sardar, Prashant Surana, Gaurav Choudhary, Ehsan Jamshidpour
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The growing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) within today's power grid infrastructure brings substantial advantages, yet it also introduces serious cyber security challenges. As electric vehicle (EV) usage grows and these vehicles interact with advanced charging networks equipped with advanced technologies like vehicle-to-grid (V2G) and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G), their reliance on digital communication exposes them to malicious cyber activities. This paper conducts a detailed investigation into the security vulnerabilities arising from the interface between the EV infrastructure and the electrical grid. It emphasises potential weaknesses in cyber-links and control systems that regulate energy exchange and charging operations. Furthermore, the study examines three specific cyber attack scenarios that could disrupt the interactions between V2G and G2V: gate pulse interference, intentional frequency alterations, and manipulation of reference signals. The analysis also considers how such attacks could affect battery performance metrics, including state of charge (SoC) and other critical operational parameters like Voltage and current in grid-connected EV charging systems.

title: Integrated Control Strategy for BLDC Motors Using a High-Gain DC-DC Converter with Sensorless Feedback
authors: Konda Naresh, D. Suresh, Swapnajit Pattnaik
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This research offers a unified control approach for BLDC motor drives that combines sensorless control with a high-gain DC-DC converter. The proposed control solution incorporates high performance, small size, and lower cost. The spatially distributed commutation technique offers a cost-effective solution since it eliminates the need for physical position sensors. The proposed systems offer high voltage gain with a low component count, reduced switching losses, and overall better performance for low voltage applications without a switched inductor. Sensorless mode is achieved through back-EMF zero crossings in the freewheeling phase. Rotor position is estimated using a six-step commutation table. A dual-loop control structure with an outer speed loop and a hysteresis current loop provides a fast, dynamic response. The estimated rotor position for VSI switching provides the needed commutation control across a large operating range. Simulation results demonstrate the proposed system high performance BLDC motor applications with reliable performance across a range of speeds and loads.

title: Reconfigurable Bidirectional Zeta–SEPIC Converter for Solar and Battery Integration with Fault-Tolerant Mode Switching
authors: Niwin M J, S Thangavel
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: A dual-input combination with solar PV and battery pack through a bidirectional Zeta-SEPIC converter, along with fault-tolerant reconfigurability is introduced. When the seriesconnected input side switch undergoes a fault or a disturbance, the proposed topology reconfigures to a buck converter or boost converter depending on the power flow condition. The proposed system leads to overcharging of energy storage components, such as inductors and capacitors during reconfiguration. To mitigate this issue, a component protection circuit is integrated with a two-position relay and a resistor, which allows for the discharge of the stored energy. Fault detection is carried out using an open-switch diagnosis based on inductor current prediction, which offers a fast and reliable fault identification. The main advantage of the proposed system is its adaptive control strategy. The reconfigurable control strategy allows continuous operation without system shutdown, enhances the system uptime, and is fault-tolerant. This makes the proposed topology highly reliable for energy conversion applications.

title: Sensorless Position Estimation and Speed Control of IPMSM using High Frequency Oscillating Voltage Injection
authors: Sourabh Paitandi, Subhajit Dey, Arnab Bose, Mainak Sengupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper proposes an innovative sensorless position and speed estimation method for an IPM drive. The proposed method utilises the inherent saliency of the IPM. High frequency (HF) oscillating voltage signals along with the fundamental voltages, have been injected and a modified signal processing technique has been used here. The developed system includes a high-pass filter, a 3-phase PLL and a low-pass filter to extract the rotor position from the phase currents. The important feature of the proposed method is that it uses the variation of inductance with rotor position and does not use the absolute value of inductances. This makes the method reliable. Here, the accuracy of position estimation does not depend on the accuracy of the determination of inductance values under different operating conditions. The method can be used even in the low-speed region where back emf or flux-based estimation methods lack accuracy. The simulation results of the proposed sensorless method establish the accurate operation even at zero speed. The experimental validation has also been initiated. The experimental waveforms of HF currents and signals at different stages are included here which are in good agreement with analytical and simulated results.

title: Intelligent Constrained Zero-Sum Game Design With Advanced Critic Learning Including a Two-Area Power System Application
authors: Menghua Li, Ding Wang, Junfei Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: In this article, an advanced critic learning technique is established to handle the continuous-time (CT) multiplayer zero-sum game (MZSG) problem with asymmetric constraints. To begin with, a novel asymmetric constraint algorithm is presented, which relaxes the restrictions on the control matrices compared to prior related studies. Ulteriorly, the Hamilton–Jacobi–Isaacs equation, the optimal controls, and the worst disturbances are deduced for asymmetric constrained MZSGs (ACMZSGs). Since the acquired Hamilton–Jacobi–Isaacs equation is intractable to solve, an advanced critic learning scheme is built to attain the approximations of the optimal controls and the worst disturbances. It is noteworthy that this article develops a new weight tuning rule to lower the need for the initial admissible controls. Immediately after that, the stability analysis of the control system is given. In the end, the load frequency control problem for a two-area power system with linear dynamics is considered, and the simulation for a nonlinear system is performed to test the feasibility of the suggested control scheme. In particular, comparative experiments are established to further demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed weight tuning rule and the applicability of the proposed asymmetric constraint algorithm.

title: Multi-view Graph Attributed Learning Data Imputation Method for Residential Load Analysis
authors: Zhiping Lin, Weihao Hu, Di Cao, Pengfei Zhao, Zhe Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: With the proliferation of smart grids, residential load data has become vital for user behavior modeling and demand forecasting. However, real-world load curves frequently suffer from substantial missing values due to sensor or communication failures, making accurate imputation crucial. Existing data imputation methods encounter two main challenges. On the one hand, they often suffer significant performance degradation under high missing rates. On the other hand, they lack generalization across users, limiting their ability to accurately impute load curves for households with limited daily load curves. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes a multi-view graph-attributed imputation framework. It constructs an observation-aware similarity graph using mask alignment to ensure structural reliability under sparse observations and introduces higher-order temporal gradient features to represent load dynamics. These views are fused through self-expressive learning and graph embedding to reveal latent user structures and guide robust imputation. Experiments on real-world residential data demonstrate the method’s superior performance, particularly under severe missingness and limited target load data.

title: MADDPG Based Path Planning for Multi-UAV Information Collection in IoT Networks
authors: Liangbin Zhu, Kai Yang, Jinglei Li, Yuxuan Yang, Qinghai Yang, Xiaozheng Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Multi-UAV-assisted information collection is an effective solution for large-scale Internet of Things (IoT) networks, but it is challenged by limited onboard energy, charging requirements, and the need for coordinated path planning among multiple UAVs. This paper investigates a multi-UAV information collection problem in which UAVs cooperatively collect data from distributed IoT devices under energy constraints, charging operations, and collision avoidance constraints, with the objective of minimizing the total mission completion time. The considered problem is formulated as a multi-agent Markov decision process. To address the coupled and high-dimensional decision-making nature of the system, a multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient (MADDPG) framework with centralized training and decentralized execution is developed for cooperative UAV path planning. An energy-aware reward function is designed to jointly account for information collection efficiency, energy consumption, and charging behavior. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves shorter mission completion time and better scalability compared with DDPG-based and MAPPO-based methods.

title: An On-Site Equipment Defect Recognition Method for UAV Inspection Scenarios via Spatial Reasoning Learning with Vision–Language Multimodal Large Models
authors: Fengyuan Dong, Xing Xu, Zeyang Dou
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Power drone inspections have become a crucial method for monitoring the status of transmission lines and substation equipment. However, in real-world scenarios, defect targets typically exhibit features such as small scale, weak texture, and complex backgrounds with strong interference. The traditional model struggles to meet the demand for rapid on-site closed-loop resolution. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an on-site defect identification method for drone inspection scenarios based on spatial reasoning learning from multimodal image-text large models. First, we develop a defect image generation method using Variational Autoencoders (VAE) and conditional score matching diffusion. By embedding semantic information into the feature layer of the U-Net decoder via the multi-layer Spatial Adaptive Normalization and Decomposition (SPADE) operator, we precisely control the generation location and morphology of defect features. Second, at the model level, a visual encoder based on Swin Transformer is constructed. A hierarchical window attention mechanism extracts multi-scale defect and scene topological features. A text encoder is built using the Next Token Prediction pre-training method and a large language model, combined with domain-specific fine-tuning using power system terminology databases and equipment ledger knowledge. At the cross-modal fusion layer, a contrastive learning mechanism aligns defect images and textual descriptions within a unified vector space. For spatial reasoning learning, we construct visually-linguistically intertwined regional inference samples. Region-level instruction fine-tuning drives the model to perform dynamic region cropping and multi-step reasoning analysis. Regarding on-site deployment, we combine CPU-NPU heterogeneous co-acceleration with memory optimization strategies. Through dynamic task allocation and data prefetching techniques, we achieve low-power, low-latency real-time edge inference. Experimental results demonstrate that our method delivers high-precision and high-efficiency defect detection in power inspection scenarios, effectively addressing complex backgrounds and resource-constrained field environments.

title: Security Enhancement for RIS in Correlated Eavesdropping Scenario
authors: Runyu Li, Wei Peng, Yangtian Liu, Robert C. Qiu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) has recently emerged as a promising candidate to enhance the physical layer security (PLS) of next-generation wireless communications. However, the vulnerabilities inherent in RIS have not yet been thoroughly investigated. Specifically, the conspicuous deployment of RIS makes it highly susceptible to detection by eavesdroppers. Once the location of RIS is exposed, the eavesdropper (Eve) can physically approach the RIS to conduct efficient passive eavesdropping, especially under high Rician K-factor conditions. Moreover, due to high channel correlation, increasing transmitter (Alice) power in this scenario yields limited improvement in secrecy rate, and the secure precoding techniques become futile. In this paper, we introduce a D2D relay as a secure backup node collaborating with the RIS to reduce the correlation, and then an achievable secrecy rate maximization problem is formulated. To overcome the non-convexity of the formulated problem, an alternating optimization algorithm is proposed to jointly optimize Alice beamforming vector, the D2D relay beamforming matrix and the RIS phase shifts. Furthermore, asymptotic expressions for the maximum achievable secrecy rate under high transmit power are derived. Simulation results validate the superiority of the proposed scheme over benchmark methods. Critically, when Alice is equipped with more antennas than Eve, the secrecy rate of the proposed scheme increases monotonically with Alice’s transmit power without converging to an upper bound.

title: A Comparative Review of Junction Temperature Monitoring for SiC-Based Power Semiconductor Devices
authors: Guangjie Zhu, Xiangshun Li, Song Xiong, Cheng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: Silicon carbide power devices, representing thirdgeneration semiconductors, are widely used in the areas of industrial control, rail transportation, electric vehicles, and renewable energy. As crucial components of power electronic systems, their reliability is vital to the safe operation of entire systems. Temperature is a critical factor affecting device reliability, and junction temperature serves as a key indicator reflecting the thermal state of the device. Accurate monitoring of junction temperature is therefore essential for tracking device conditions and assessing reliability. This paper provides a comparative review of advances in junction temperature monitoring for silicon carbide-based power devices, systematically analyzing four primary methods: thermal network model, temperature-sensitive electrical parameter, physical contact sensing, and non-contact optical methods. The principles, advantages and limitations as well as applicable situations of each method are analyzed in detail. Furthermore, the evolutionary trajectory of the estimation methods and associated validation techniques are summarized. Finally, challenges and future prospects for junction temperature monitoring for silicon carbide-based power devices are presented. This review concludes that temperature-sensitive electrical parameters method represent the most promising technique for future junction temperature monitoring. Nevertheless, significant challenges persist, particularly in relation to aging-induced coupling and the complexities of accurate parameter extraction. The purpose of this review is to provide helpful theoretical support and methodological guidance to researchers in related fields.

title: Sensor Location and Selection for Fault Diagnosis and Detection in Nuclear Power Plant Using Signed Direction Graph
authors: Tianyang Xing, Xiaoliang Zhu, Mengmeng Ji, Mudi Jiang, Yanfeng Zhao, Yajie Jing, Shenghui Liu, Jianqun Xu, Zhigang Su
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Nuclear power plants are expected to achieve comprehensive fault monitoring to ensure secure and stable operation. Sensor placement aims to provide full spatial coverage at target locations within cost constraints. This paper proposes a comprehensive modeling approach to derive the fault-sensor correlation matrix for the deaerator system in nuclear power plants based on SDG theory. The proposed method optimizes sensor placement by effectively integrating the NSGA-II algorithm with the TOPSIS technique, enabling systematic evaluation and ranking of available sensor allocation schemes. Using APROS simulation data, we conducted a comprehensive analysis of fault propagation paths and sensor correlations, establishing a probability model that accurately assesses sensor fault detection performance. In ideal scenarios, sensor placement optimization reveals a theoretical configuration which removes two ineffective locations and identifies about 20 sensors as optimal. Two sensitivity analysis cases assess key placement factors. Sensor cost does not affect placement at specific locations, but failure probability—driven by harsh measurement conditions—significantly influences initial configuration. The SDG-based fault diagnosis and detection method using a compatible pathway accurately classifies different deaerator component failures, showing strong potential for nuclear power system condition monitoring. The sensor placement method proposed in this study demonstrates superior performance over conventional approaches, providing a reference for complex process monitoring and decision-making.
